profile.domain: AI-powered document classification and routing system for a federal agency

project_goal.value: Automatically classify and route inbound federal agency correspondence — citizen letters, FOIA requests, and regulatory filings — to the correct department within 500ms using LLM-based classification
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual triage process handling tens of thousands of documents daily

target_users.value: Federal agency staff, supervisors, and department routing administrators
target_users.rationale: Internal workforce consuming classified documents and managing routing rules

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM-based classification with human-in-the-loop feedback loop

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs, admin_panel, devops
capabilities.rationale: Full backend pipeline with ML, admin oversight, analytics, and batch retraining

complexity_level.rationale: Tens of thousands of documents per day with sub-500ms classification SLA

risk_level.rationale: Federal agency confidential correspondence with FedRAMP Moderate controls required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Citizen FOIA requests and regulatory filings are sensitive federal records

external_exposure.rationale: No external-facing endpoints; all access through agency intranet

access_model.value: RBAC with Keycloak; CAC/PIV smartcard mandatory for all users; roles: classifier_admin, department_supervisor, system_admin
access_model.rationale: FedRAMP and federal identity mandate CAC/PIV for privileged systems

feature_scope.value: Document ingestion pipeline, LLM-based classification, routing rule engine, department queue management, human-in-the-loop review, model retraining feedback, supervisor admin panel, audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Full intake-to-queue workflow with ML lifecycle management

mvp_scope.value: Ingest via secure upload, LLM classification for top 20 document types, routing to department queues, supervisor review UI, immutable audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: Core routing automation before full feedback loop is built

security_baseline.value: FedRAMP Moderate controls, AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, CAC/PIV auth, SIEM logging, no external model API calls — inference on GovCloud-deployed models only
security_baseline.rationale: FedRAMP Moderate and agency ISSM requirements

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; admin panel and supervisor dashboard require rich UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: ML ecosystem integration and async throughput for high-volume document processing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for operational data, Redis 7 for classification job queuing, S3 GovCloud for document storage
data_platform.rationale: Relational records with high-throughput queue and durable document storage

hosting_target.value: AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate with auto-scaling
hosting_target.rationale: FedRAMP-authorized environment with managed containers

privacy_retention_policy.value: Federal records retention per NARA schedules; correspondence items retained 7 years; audit trails retained 10 years; model versions archived indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NARA records management requirements for federal agency correspondence

future_scope.value: Multi-agency rollout, automated response drafting, confidence-based SLA tiering
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap post-pilot

constraints.value: All compute and data must remain in AWS GovCloud us-gov-east-1; no calls to public LLM APIs; Section 508 compliance for admin UI
constraints.rationale: FedRAMP authorization boundary and data sovereignty requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all Fargate tasks, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, CloudTrail for AWS API actions, PagerDuty escalation for classification queue depth > 5000
observability_baseline.rationale: Agency SIEM and operational monitoring standards

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; GitLab CI for all environments
execution_preference.rationale: Agency development methodology

compliance_context.value: FedRAMP Moderate ATO required; Section 508 accessibility for admin UI; NARA records management schedules; agency ISSM approval for AI model deployment
compliance_context.rationale: Federal agency regulatory environment mandates FedRAMP and accessibility compliance

llm_integration.value: GovCloud-deployed fine-tuned document classification model (no external API calls); confidence scores drive human-in-the-loop escalation threshold; feedback from supervisor corrections fed into monthly retraining batch jobs
llm_integration.rationale: Security constraint prohibits public model APIs; on-premise inference required

project.goal: Classify and route federal agency inbound correspondence automatically
project.target_users: Agency staff, supervisors, routing administrators

security.auth: Keycloak with CAC/PIV smartcard
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

open_questions.retraining_cadence: Monthly batch vs. online learning to be confirmed post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The classification and routing automation directly eliminates manual triage bottlenecks that delay citizen correspondence responses. The human-in-the-loop escalation path for low-confidence classifications is essential for maintaining accuracy while building model trust with agency staff. MVP scope covering top 20 document types addresses the highest-volume categories and will demonstrate ROI within the pilot.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async workers with Redis queue is the right pattern for sub-500ms classification SLA at this document volume. The LLM classifier should be deployed as a separate Fargate service with independent auto-scaling tied to queue depth, not co-located with the API tier. Model versioning via S3 with rollback capability is non-negotiable given the feedback loop retraining cycle.
reasoner_reviews.security: FedRAMP Moderate requires encryption at rest with GovCloud KMS, comprehensive CloudTrail audit logging, and CAC/PIV enforcement at every entry point. The constraint against public LLM API calls is architecturally correct and must be enforced at the network boundary via Security Group egress rules. All model inference must occur within the FedRAMP authorization boundary.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AWS GovCloud us-gov-east-1 constraint limits available managed services; verify ECS Fargate GPU support or use CPU-optimized inference for classification. Section 508 for the admin panel requires ARIA compliance and keyboard navigation testing. NARA retention schedules must be implemented as automated S3 lifecycle policies, not manual processes.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Model retraining on supervisor corrections introduces a feedback loop that could degrade classification quality if supervisors make systematic errors. A quality gate requiring minimum correction batch size and statistical validation before model promotion is critical and should be designed in Phase 1, not deferred.

backend.service_design: Three Fargate services: IngestAPI (document intake and validation), ClassifierWorker (async LLM inference via Redis queue), RoutingEngine (rule evaluation and queue assignment). Each scales independently.
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST API with FastAPI; POST /documents/ingest, GET /queues/{department_id}, PATCH /classifications/{id}/review. No public endpoints.
backend.business_modules: DocumentNormalizer (PDF/image to text extraction), ClassificationOrchestrator (model call + confidence gating), RoutingRuleEvaluator (department assignment logic), FeedbackCollector (correction capture for retraining)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly retraining batch job consumes supervisor corrections from feedback_queue; weekly model evaluation job computes classification accuracy per document type against gold-standard labels
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter queue for failed classifications; circuit breaker on model service; fallback to rule-based routing if LLM confidence below 0.4 for more than 100 consecutive documents

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm with CAC/PIV smartcard integration via PIV middleware; mandatory MFA for all roles; session timeout 15 minutes for unattended workstations
security.authorization_model: RBAC with roles: classifier_admin (manage routing rules, promote models), department_supervisor (review and correct classifications), system_admin (user management, audit access)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_trails table capturing every classification decision, correction, and routing action with user identity, timestamp, document_id, and assigned label; forwarded to agency SIEM via CloudWatch

data.entities: correspondence_items (id, ingested_at, source_type, raw_s3_key, extracted_text_s3_key, status), classification_labels (id, label_name, department_id, confidence_threshold), routing_rules (id, label_id, department_queue_id, priority, conditions_json), department_queues (id, department_name, current_depth, sla_hours), processing_jobs (id, document_id, model_version_id, confidence_score, assigned_label_id, status), audit_trails (id, actor_id, action, document_id, old_label, new_label, timestamp), model_versions (id, trained_at, accuracy_score, training_corpus_size, promoted_by, status), escalation_records (id, document_id, reason, escalated_to, resolved_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for operational records; S3 GovCloud for raw and extracted document content; Redis 7 Streams for classification job queue
data.retention_and_deletion: S3 lifecycle policies enforce NARA retention schedules per document type; audit_trails and model_versions retained indefinitely; processed documents archived to S3 Glacier after 2 years

devops.deployment_topology: Three ECS Fargate services in private subnets; ALB in private subnet serving only internal agency network; no internet-facing endpoints; NAT Gateway for package downloads only
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: lint, unit tests, integration tests against staging DB, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Model promotion gated by accuracy threshold check in CI.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecars on all tasks; CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON; custom metrics: classification_queue_depth, model_confidence_p50, routing_accuracy_rate, escalation_rate_per_label
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service rollback to previous task definition; model version rollback via model_versions status flag; automated rollback triggered if error rate exceeds 2% in 5-minute window

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a FedRAMP-constrained AI document routing system where the dominant architectural challenge is keeping LLM inference inside the GovCloud boundary while meeting sub-500ms SLA at high document volume. The three-service split (IngestAPI, ClassifierWorker, RoutingEngine) with Redis Streams decoupling is the right pattern. CAC/PIV via Keycloak and an immutable audit trail are non-negotiable compliance anchors. The feedback-to-retraining loop needs statistical quality gates before model promotion to prevent systematic error amplification.
target_output.title: Federal Agency Correspondence Classification and Routing System
target_output.executive_summary: A FedRAMP Moderate AI system deployed in AWS GovCloud that automatically classifies inbound federal agency correspondence — citizen letters, FOIA requests, and regulatory filings — and routes them to the correct department queue within 500ms using an on-premise fine-tuned LLM. Human-in-the-loop review handles low-confidence classifications, and supervisor corrections feed a monthly model retraining pipeline. CAC/PIV authentication, immutable audit trails, and NARA-compliant retention policies meet federal records management requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with a React 18 admin SPA served from S3/CloudFront within GovCloud, communicating over private ALB to a FastAPI IngestAPI service. Document ingestion feeds a Redis Streams classification queue consumed by a separately scaled ClassifierWorker that calls a GovCloud-deployed fine-tuned LLM inference endpoint. The RoutingEngine service evaluates routing_rules against classification results and deposits items into department_queues in PostgreSQL 16. All services run as ECS Fargate tasks in private subnets with no internet-facing endpoints. Keycloak enforces CAC/PIV authentication at every entry point.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery workers, boto3. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Tailwind CSS. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS), Redis 7 Streams (ElastiCache). Storage: S3 GovCloud with KMS encryption. Auth: Keycloak with CAC/PIV PIV middleware. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB (internal), CloudFront (internal), ECR, KMS, CloudTrail, GitLab CI. Monitoring: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Document Ingestion: secure upload endpoint accepting PDF, TIFF, and MSG formats with virus scanning via ClamAV. Classification Pipeline: text extraction via Textract GovCloud, LLM inference with confidence scoring, rule-based fallback below 0.4 threshold. Routing Engine: configurable routing_rules mapped to department_queues with SLA hour tracking. Supervisor Review Dashboard: queue view with document preview, one-click label correction, and escalation controls. Admin Panel: routing rule editor, model version promotion UI, classification performance analytics. Audit Trail: append-only log of every classification and routing action.
target_output.system_components: IngestAPI (Fargate service, 2-8 tasks): HTTP ingestion, virus scan, S3 upload, Textract job trigger. ClassifierWorker (Fargate service, 4-20 tasks scaled to queue depth): Redis Streams consumer, LLM inference call, confidence evaluation, feedback to RoutingEngine. RoutingEngine (Fargate service, 2-6 tasks): rule evaluation against classification_labels, department_queue assignment, SLA clock start. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: operational data. Redis 7 ElastiCache: classification job queue and session cache. S3 GovCloud: document storage with KMS SSE-KMS. Keycloak cluster: CAC/PIV identity provider. Datadog Agent: APM and metrics collection.
target_output.workflows: Document Ingestion and Classification: Staff or scanner uploads document via POST /documents/ingest; IngestAPI validates file type, runs ClamAV scan, uploads raw file to S3, triggers AWS Textract for text extraction. Extracted text is published to Redis Streams classification_jobs topic. ClassifierWorker consumes job, calls GovCloud LLM endpoint with extracted text, receives label and confidence score. If confidence >= 0.7 the RoutingEngine assigns to target department_queue; if below threshold item enters human_review_queue and a supervisor notification is triggered. Supervisor Correction and Retraining Feedback: Supervisor opens low-confidence item in Review Panel, inspects document preview and proposed label, either confirms or overrides with correct classification_label. Correction is written to escalation_records and feedback_queue. Monthly batch retraining job aggregates corrections from feedback_queue with minimum 500-record threshold, retrains model, evaluates against gold-standard test set, and if accuracy improves > 0.5% the new model_version is promoted via CI pipeline approval gate.
target_output.data_model: correspondence_items (id UUID PK, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, source_type ENUM upload/scanner/email, raw_s3_key TEXT, extracted_text_s3_key TEXT, status ENUM pending/classified/routed/escalated/archived). classification_labels (id UUID PK, label_name VARCHAR, department_id FK, confidence_threshold FLOAT, active BOOL). routing_rules (id UUID PK, label_id FK, department_queue_id FK, priority INT, conditions_json JSONB, created_by UUID). department_queues (id UUID PK, department_name VARCHAR, current_depth INT, sla_hours INT, last_checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). processing_jobs (id UUID PK, document_id FK, model_version_id FK, confidence_score FLOAT, assigned_label_id FK, processing_ms INT, status ENUM queued/processing/complete/failed). audit_trails (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID, action VARCHAR, document_id FK, old_label UUID, new_label UUID, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, trained_at TIMESTAMPTZ, accuracy_score FLOAT, training_corpus_size INT, promoted_by UUID, status ENUM candidate/active/retired). escalation_records (id UUID PK, document_id FK, reason VARCHAR, escalated_to UUID, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API (no public internet exposure). POST /v1/documents/ingest (multipart file upload, returns document_id and status). GET /v1/queues/{department_id}/items?status=pending (department queue view with pagination). GET /v1/documents/{id}/preview (signed S3 URL for document preview). PATCH /v1/classifications/{job_id}/review (supervisor correction: body {corrected_label_id, notes}). GET /v1/routing-rules (admin: list rules). PUT /v1/routing-rules/{id} (admin: update rule). GET /v1/models/versions (admin: list model versions with accuracy scores). POST /v1/models/{version_id}/promote (admin: promote candidate to active). GET /v1/audit?document_id={id} (compliance: audit trail for document). All endpoints require Keycloak JWT with CAC/PIV-verified session.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FedRAMP Moderate controls: all data encrypted with AWS KMS CMKs (SSE-KMS on S3, AES-256 on RDS), TLS 1.3 on all internal connections. CAC/PIV smartcard authentication via Keycloak PIV middleware; session timeout 15 minutes. RBAC enforced at API layer with FastAPI dependency injection. Immutable audit_trails table (INSERT-only, no UPDATE/DELETE permissions for application role). CloudTrail logging for all AWS API calls. Security Groups restrict all Fargate tasks to private subnets with no direct internet ingress. Egress limited to GovCloud endpoints only. ClamAV virus scanning on all ingested documents before S3 write. Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for admin UI validated by axe-core in CI.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Three ECS Fargate services deployed via GitLab CI rolling updates. Environments: dev (single task per service, shared RDS), staging (multi-AZ mirror of production), production (us-gov-east-1, multi-AZ, auto-scaling). ClassifierWorker auto-scales on custom metric classification_queue_depth with target 200 items per task, min 4 max 20 tasks. IngestAPI scales on CPU 60% target. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with automated failover and daily snapshots retained 35 days. Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ with in-transit encryption. Secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation. ECR image scanning on push. GitLab CI gates: lint (flake8), unit tests (pytest, 85% coverage), integration tests, image build, staging deploy, production approval gate.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each Fargate task with distributed tracing across IngestAPI → ClassifierWorker → RoutingEngine. Structured JSON logs with correlation_id, document_id, and model_version_id fields. CloudWatch Metrics custom namespace with: classification_queue_depth (alarm >5000), model_confidence_p50 (alert <0.65), routing_accuracy_rate (alert <90%), escalation_rate_per_label (monitor for drift), document_processing_p99_ms (alert >1000ms). PagerDuty escalation for queue depth breaches and model accuracy degradation. Weekly automated classification accuracy report distributed to agency ISSO.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $8,500/month at 50K documents/day: ECS Fargate ClassifierWorker cluster dominates at ~$4,200/month scaling with queue depth. RDS Multi-AZ ~$900/month. ElastiCache ~$400/month. S3 + Textract ~$1,200/month at document volume. Datadog APM ~$800/month. Cost controls: ClassifierWorker scale-to-zero during off-hours (6pm-6am), S3 Intelligent-Tiering for documents >90 days, Textract batching for lower-priority document types.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): IngestAPI with ClamAV and Textract integration, ClassifierWorker with GovCloud LLM for top 20 document types, RoutingEngine with static routing_rules, Supervisor Review Dashboard with correction UI, immutable audit_trails, Keycloak CAC/PIV setup, GitLab CI pipeline, FedRAMP control documentation. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Feedback collection pipeline, monthly retraining batch job with accuracy gate, model_versions promotion workflow, Admin Panel with rule editor and model analytics. Phase 3 (6 weeks): SLA monitoring and escalation alerts, NARA retention automation via S3 lifecycle, Section 508 accessibility audit and remediation, classification performance dashboards for agency leadership.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type annotations required on all functions; mypy strict mode in CI. pytest with 85% line coverage gate; integration tests use testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis. All LLM inference calls must be wrapped in a ModelGateway abstraction to support model version swapping without API changes. No direct boto3 calls in business logic — use thin service wrappers for testability. GitLab CI merge gates: flake8, mypy, pytest, Trivy image scan, and axe-core accessibility check on frontend builds.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GovCloud LLM inference latency could exceed 500ms SLA under burst load. Mitigation: pre-warm ClassifierWorker pool during business hours; implement synchronous fast-path for urgent document types with dedicated Fargate capacity. Risk: Supervisor correction quality — systematic over-correction of edge-case labels could degrade model accuracy. Mitigation: statistical quality gate requiring minimum batch size and accuracy delta validation before model promotion. Tradeoff: Redis Streams over SQS for classification queue — lower latency and better consumer group control, but requires ElastiCache management overhead vs. managed SQS. Tradeoff: On-premise GovCloud model vs. Bedrock — meets FedRAMP boundary constraint but loses managed model scaling; mitigated by ClassifierWorker auto-scaling.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Redis Streams over SQS for sub-100ms queue latency. Confirmed GovCloud Textract over on-premise OCR for FedRAMP compliance. Chose Keycloak over Cognito for CAC/PIV smartcard support. Monthly retraining cadence selected with 500-correction minimum batch threshold confirmed with agency data science team.

metadata.notes: FedRAMP Moderate AI classification system with on-premise LLM inference, CAC/PIV auth, and NARA retention compliance.

profile.domain: Municipal Cybersecurity Operations Center

project_goal.value: Graph-based threat correlation platform for detecting lateral movement across municipal infrastructure logs spanning water treatment SCADA, traffic management, and city IT networks
project_goal.rationale: Core research objective is cross-domain attack path detection in connected city systems

target_users.value: Municipal SOC analysts and city CISO team
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are trained security analysts conducting threat hunting and incident triage

project_class.rationale: Exploratory prototype validating graph-based correlation approach before production commitment

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, analytics, realtime, devops
capabilities.rationale: Requires stream ingestion, graph analytics, real-time alerting, and secure multi-role access

complexity_level.rationale: Cross-domain correlation at realtime scale with heterogeneous SCADA and IT log formats is architecturally complex

risk_level.rationale: Handles sensitive infrastructure intelligence; unauthorized disclosure could expose attack surface of critical city systems

data_sensitivity.rationale: Log data contains operational topology and vulnerability indicators for critical infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: SOC platform must be air-gapped from public internet; accessible only on city secure enclave network

access_model.value: RBAC with Azure AD MFA; roles: soc_analyst, threat_hunter, incident_commander, ciso_admin
access_model.rationale: Different analyst roles require scoped access to raw logs, correlation rules, and incident escalation

feature_scope.value: Real-time log ingestion from SCADA, traffic, and IT sources; graph-based correlation rule engine; anomaly subgraph visualization; incident escalation workflows; analyst annotation tooling
feature_scope.rationale: Full prototype feature set for validating cross-domain threat detection hypothesis

mvp_scope.value: Ingest from two log sources (IT network + SCADA), build correlation graph for lateral movement detection, basic analyst review interface
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum slice to validate graph correlation approach within 3-month prototype budget

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all internal traffic, AES-256 encryption at rest, Azure AD MFA enforced, no data egress to public internet, network policy restricting pod-to-pod traffic in AKS
security_baseline.rationale: City security policy and CISA ICS guidelines for critical infrastructure systems

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and D3.js for graph visualization
frontend_stack.rationale: Vue 3 Composition API suits the complex reactive graph UI; D3.js provides flexible force-directed layout for attack path rendering

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; Neo4j 5 for graph storage; Redis 7 for correlation rule hot cache
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides throughput for high-volume log normalization; Neo4j native graph traversal accelerates lateral movement pattern matching

data_platform.value: Neo4j 5 AuraDB Enterprise on Azure, Redis 7 for hot cache, Azure Blob Storage for raw log archive
data_platform.rationale: Graph database enables sub-second multi-hop traversal queries required for real-time attack path correlation

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) on Azure Government cloud, city secure enclave
hosting_target.rationale: AKS on Azure Government satisfies FedRAMP and city data sovereignty requirements for critical infrastructure data

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw infrastructure logs retained 90 days in hot storage; correlation graphs and incident reports retained 3 years; analyst annotations retained indefinitely for research continuity
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: City records retention policy for security incident documentation

future_scope.value: ML-assisted anomaly scoring on graph topology changes; integration with city SIEM; automated playbook execution for confirmed incidents
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 research roadmap contingent on prototype validation

constraints.value: Must run entirely on Azure Government; no external API calls to public services; prototype budget $80K total; delivery within 3 months
constraints.rationale: City procurement and security constraints for critical infrastructure tooling

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus metrics scraped from Go services; Grafana dashboards for ingest throughput, correlation rule evaluation latency, and Neo4j query times; Azure Monitor for AKS node health
observability_baseline.rationale: Research prototype requires observable performance to validate scalability assumptions

execution_preference.value: Iterative research sprints with weekly demos to city CISO; milestone-gated approach with explicit go/no-go decisions
execution_preference.rationale: Prototype requires frequent stakeholder validation given research nature

compliance_context.value: NIST CSF v2.0 controls for Identify and Detect functions; CISA Critical Infrastructure Security guidelines; ICS-CERT advisories for SCADA log handling; city cybersecurity policy CY-2024-03
compliance_context.rationale: Critical infrastructure SOC prototype must align to federal and municipal cybersecurity frameworks

project.goal: Validate graph-based lateral movement detection across heterogeneous municipal infrastructure logs
project.target_users: SOC analysts and city CISO team

backend.framework: Gin for REST API; custom log normalization pipeline
backend.stream_processing: Kafka consumer groups for parallel log ingestion

security.auth: Azure AD with MFA via OIDC
security.network_policy: AKS network policies blocking unauthorized pod communication

data.archive: Azure Blob Storage for 90-day raw log retention

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Azure Container Registry

open_questions.ml_scoring: Deferred to Phase 2 after prototype validation

reasoner_reviews.product: The graph-based correlation approach is the right research bet for multi-domain attack detection. The MVP scope of two log sources is appropriately scoped for 3-month validation. Analyst annotation tooling is critical for building the ground-truth dataset needed for Phase 2 ML work.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Neo4j Cypher queries for multi-hop lateral movement detection are well-suited to this problem. The Go ingestion pipeline with Kafka consumer groups will handle heterogeneous log formats at the required throughput. Key risk is Neo4j memory sizing for large graph expansion during active incidents; recommend starting with AuraDB Business tier and profiling query memory before committing to node sizes.
reasoner_reviews.security: The air-gapped deployment on Azure Government is the correct security posture for critical infrastructure intelligence data. Azure AD MFA with RBAC is solid. The outstanding risk is the SCADA log ingestion path: ensure the log forwarder at the SCADA boundary is one-way (syslog-ng with no return channel) to prevent any possibility of command injection back toward control systems.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $80K budget is achievable with AKS spot node pools for non-critical workloads and AuraDB Business tier rather than Enterprise for the prototype phase. The 3-month timeline is tight but viable with the phased MVP scope.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Previous round plan lacked specificity on the Neo4j schema design for heterogeneous node types across SCADA and IT domains. The correlation rule engine design was underspecified -- rule evaluation order and conflict resolution for overlapping patterns must be addressed in this round.

backend.service_design: Three Go microservices: log-ingestor (Kafka consumer, normalizes SCADA CEF and Windows Event Log syslog into canonical LogEvent structs), correlation-engine (evaluates Cypher-based traversal rules against live graph, emits CorrelationAlert events), api-gateway (Gin REST server, serves analyst UI, manages correlation rule CRUD)
backend.api_patterns: REST API with versioned endpoints /api/v1; Server-Sent Events stream at /api/v1/alerts/stream for real-time analyst feed; Cypher query proxy endpoint for ad-hoc threat hunting
backend.failure_handling: Kafka consumer offset management with dead-letter topic for unparseable log events; Neo4j transaction retry with exponential backoff; circuit breaker on AuraDB connection pool
backend.scaling_notes: Log ingestor horizontally scales via Kafka partition assignment; correlation engine is stateless and scales independently; Neo4j AuraDB handles read scaling via read replicas
backend.backend_risks: Graph expansion during coordinated multi-vector attacks could cause Neo4j memory pressure; correlation rule evaluation may fall behind ingest rate during peak incidents

security.auth_design: Azure AD OIDC via Go oauth2 middleware; JWT tokens validated on every request; session tokens stored in Redis with 8-hour expiry matching analyst shift length
security.authorization_model: RBAC: soc_analyst (read log events, create annotations), threat_hunter (full graph query, manage correlation rules), incident_commander (escalate incidents, view all domains), ciso_admin (manage users, export reports)
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for Neo4j credentials, Kafka SASL credentials, and AES-256 encryption keys; AKS workload identity for pod access to Key Vault without static secrets
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit_events collection in Neo4j capturing every analyst graph query, rule modification, and incident escalation with user_id, timestamp, and query_hash
security.security_risks: SCADA log forwarder boundary is highest risk surface; one-way syslog-ng relay with network ACL blocking return traffic to OT network; validate with city network team

data.entities: infrastructure_node (id, node_type ENUM scada_device/it_host/network_device, system_domain, ip, hostname, criticality_tier, last_seen), log_event (id, source_node_id, event_type, raw_payload_hash, normalized_fields_json, ingested_at, vintage), correlation_rule (id, name, cypher_pattern, severity, enabled, created_by, last_fired_at), attack_graph (id, seed_event_id, matched_rule_id, node_ids[], edge_ids[], confidence_score, detected_at, status ENUM open/investigating/closed), incident_report (id, attack_graph_id, analyst_id, summary, severity ENUM critical/high/medium, escalated_to, created_at), analyst_annotation (id, target_node_id, content, analyst_id, created_at)
data.storage_design: Neo4j AuraDB Enterprise for all graph nodes and relationships; Redis for active correlation rule cache and real-time alert deduplication; Azure Blob for raw log archive with 90-day lifecycle policy
data.retention_and_deletion: Hot graph data 90 days with TTL on log_event nodes; attack_graph and incident_report nodes retained 3 years; analyst_annotation retained indefinitely; archive raw logs to Azure Blob Cold tier after 30 days

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster on Azure Government with 3 node pools: system (Standard_D4s_v5 x3), workload-spot (Standard_D8s_v5 spot for log-ingestor and correlation-engine), memory-optimized (Standard_E8s_v5 x2 for Neo4j sidecar and Redis)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (golangci-lint), unit tests, integration tests against dockerized Neo4j and Kafka, build and push to Azure Container Registry, deploy to AKS via Helm chart with image digest pinning
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus operator scraping Go /metrics endpoints; Grafana dashboards for ingest_lag_seconds, correlation_rule_eval_p99_ms, neo4j_active_transactions, alert_backlog_depth; Azure Monitor for AKS node and pod health
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm chart versioned rollback via helm rollback; Kafka consumer group offsets allow reprocessing from last committed position after rollback

ISS-001.title: Neo4j schema lacks multi-domain node type differentiation
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan used a flat InfraNode label for all infrastructure without distinguishing SCADA device topology from IT network topology, making cross-domain Cypher traversal patterns ambiguous and difficult to index efficiently

ISS-002.title: Correlation rule evaluation order and conflict resolution unspecified
ISS-002.detail: When multiple correlation rules match overlapping subgraphs, the previous plan did not specify evaluation priority, deduplication logic, or how conflicting severity assessments are resolved for the same attack_graph

ISS-003.title: SCADA log forwarder boundary security controls underspecified
ISS-003.detail: The ingestion path from OT network SCADA systems to the SOC platform lacked explicit one-way data diode controls and network ACL specifications, leaving open the theoretical risk of lateral command injection back to control systems

focus_issues.title: Correlation rule evaluation order and conflict resolution unspecified

previous_plan.title: Municipal SOC Graph Correlation Platform - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: A research prototype SOC platform on AKS/Azure Government using Go microservices and Neo4j 5 to correlate lateral movement patterns across SCADA and IT infrastructure logs in real time, enabling city security analysts to visualize and investigate cross-domain attack paths.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go log-ingestor consuming Kafka topics for SCADA CEF and Windows Event Log syslog, normalizing into canonical LogEvent nodes in Neo4j; correlation engine evaluating Cypher traversal patterns to detect multi-hop lateral movement; Vue 3 analyst interface for attack graph visualization.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OIDC with MFA; RBAC roles for analyst tiers; SCADA log ingestion via syslog-ng one-way relay (one-way mentioned but ACL rules not specified); TLS 1.3 internal; Azure Key Vault for secrets.
previous_plan.data_model: infrastructure_node differentiated by domain label (ScadaDevice, ItHost, NetworkDevice); log_event, attack_graph, incident_report, analyst_annotation entities present; correlation_rule entity lacks priority field.

best_plan.title: Municipal SOC Graph Correlation Platform - Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: A research prototype SOC platform on AKS/Azure Government using Go microservices and Neo4j 5 to correlate lateral movement patterns across SCADA and IT infrastructure logs in real time.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Go log-ingestor consuming Kafka topics for SCADA and IT logs; Neo4j graph for attack path correlation; Vue 3 analyst UI for subgraph visualization.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OIDC MFA; RBAC; syslog-ng one-way relay without ACL specs; TLS 1.3; Key Vault.
best_plan.data_model: Domain-differentiated infrastructure nodes; log_event, attack_graph, incident_report entities; correlation_rule missing priority ordering.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close ISS-002 (correlation rule conflict resolution) and ISS-003 (SCADA boundary ACLs). For ISS-002: add priority integer field to correlation_rule entity, define lexicographic evaluation order, specify a 30-second deduplication window for overlapping attack_graph creation. For ISS-003: specify network ACL rules blocking all return traffic from AKS SOC namespace to OT network VLAN, document the syslog-ng one-way relay with explicit firewall policy. Neo4j schema is solid from round 2; focus this round on the rule engine and boundary security specifications.

fix_report.action_taken: Added priority integer field to correlation_rule entity; specified evaluation order as ascending priority with lowest integer winning; defined 30-second deduplication window preventing duplicate attack_graph nodes from overlapping rule matches on the same seed event; documented conflict resolution policy in data_model and workflows sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Analysts will observe deterministic alert triage ordering and no duplicate attack graphs for the same lateral movement event, enabling reliable incident escalation workflows

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit network ACL specification: syslog-ng relay runs on isolated DMZ VM with iptables rules ACCEPT on UDP/514 inbound from OT VLAN 10.20.0.0/16, DROP ALL outbound to OT VLAN; AKS SOC namespace network policy blocks all egress to OT VLAN CIDR; firewall rule documented with city network team sign-off requirement
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero possibility of return-channel communication from SOC platform to SCADA control systems; passes CISA ICS-CERT OT/IT boundary review

target_output.title: Municipal SOC Graph Correlation Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A 3-month research prototype deploying on AKS/Azure Government to validate graph-based lateral movement detection across heterogeneous municipal infrastructure. Go microservices ingest normalized SCADA CEF and Windows Event Log syslog into Neo4j 5, where Cypher-based correlation rules evaluate multi-hop traversal patterns spanning water treatment SCADA devices, traffic management controllers, and city IT hosts. SOC analysts investigate flagged attack subgraphs via a Vue 3 D3.js visualization interface with role-scoped access and append-only audit trails.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven pipeline on AKS: syslog-ng DMZ relay receives one-way UDP/514 from OT VLAN and IT network, forwards to Kafka topics scada-logs and it-logs partitioned by source system. Go log-ingestor consumer groups normalize CEF and Windows Event Log formats into canonical LogEvent structs and create infrastructure_node and log_event nodes in Neo4j via Bolt protocol. Go correlation-engine polls Neo4j every 15 seconds executing prioritized Cypher traversal rules to detect multi-hop lateral movement patterns; matching subgraphs materialize as attack_graph nodes. Go api-gateway serves REST and SSE endpoints to Vue 3 analyst dashboard. All inter-service traffic over TLS 1.3 within AKS. Network policies block egress from SOC namespace to OT VLAN.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin framework, Neo4j Go driver 5.x, confluent-kafka-go, Redis 7 client. Graph DB: Neo4j 5 AuraDB Enterprise on Azure Government. Stream: Kafka on AKS (3 brokers, 8 partitions per topic). Frontend: Vue 3 with TypeScript, D3.js v7 force-directed layout, Pinia state management. Infrastructure: AKS on Azure Government, Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault with workload identity, Azure Blob Storage, syslog-ng 4.x on isolated DMZ VM. Observability: Prometheus operator, Grafana 10.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Log Normalization: CEF parser for SCADA Modbus/DNP3 audit events and Windows Security Event Log XML to canonical LogEvent struct with source_system_domain tag. Correlation Rule Engine: Cypher-pattern-based rules with integer priority field evaluated in ascending order against live Neo4j graph; rules managed via API by threat_hunter role. Attack Graph Detection: 30-second deduplication window prevents duplicate attack_graph nodes from overlapping rule matches on identical seed events; confidence scoring based on rule severity and hop count. Analyst Workbench: D3.js force-directed graph rendering attack paths with node coloring by infrastructure domain; annotation creation; incident escalation with severity assignment. Audit Trail: append-only Neo4j audit_event nodes capturing every query, rule edit, and escalation.
target_output.system_components: Syslog-ng DMZ Relay (isolated VM, iptables ACCEPT UDP/514 from OT VLAN 10.20.0.0/16, DROP ALL outbound to OT VLAN, forwards to Kafka); Kafka Cluster (3 AKS pods, topics: scada-logs, it-logs, dead-letter); Log-Ingestor Service (Go, 4 replicas, Kafka consumer group, writes to Neo4j); Correlation-Engine Service (Go, 2 replicas, 15s poll cycle, evaluates Cypher rules, deduplicates attack_graph creation); API-Gateway Service (Go/Gin, 2 replicas, REST + SSE); Neo4j AuraDB Enterprise (Azure Government region, 2 read replicas); Redis 7 (AKS statefulset, correlation rule hot cache); Vue 3 Frontend (served via Nginx ingress); Azure Key Vault (workload identity access for all pods).
target_output.workflows: Log Ingestion and Normalization: SCADA syslog-ng relay receives UDP/514 from water treatment SCADA and traffic controller syslog feeds, normalizes to CEF, publishes to Kafka scada-logs topic. Go log-ingestor consumer creates ScadaDevice or ItHost infrastructure_node if not exists, creates log_event node with normalized_fields_json, creates GENERATED relationship from node to event. Unparseable events routed to dead-letter topic with original payload hash for analyst review. Correlation Rule Evaluation: Correlation-engine executes Cypher rules sorted ascending by priority integer every 15 seconds. Rule example: MATCH (a:ScadaDevice)-[:GENERATED]->(e1:LogEvent {event_type:'auth_failure'})-[:PRECEDES]->(e2:LogEvent {event_type:'privilege_escalation'})<-[:GENERATED]-(b:ItHost) WHERE e2.ingested_at - e1.ingested_at < 300 RETURN a,b,e1,e2. On match, check Redis deduplication key seed_event_id+rule_id with 30-second TTL before creating attack_graph node. If multiple rules match the same subgraph, highest severity rule wins; ties broken by lowest priority integer. Analyst Investigation: Analyst opens Vue 3 dashboard, views SSE feed of new attack_graph events, clicks subgraph to open D3.js force-directed visualization colored by domain (SCADA=red, IT=blue, network_device=gray). Adds annotation, escalates to incident_commander role with severity classification, triggering audit_event node creation.
target_output.data_model: infrastructure_node (id UUID, node_type ENUM scada_device/it_host/network_device, system_domain VARCHAR e.g. water_treatment/traffic_mgmt/city_it, ip CIDR, hostname, criticality_tier INT 1-3, last_seen TIMESTAMP). log_event (id UUID, source_node_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, raw_payload_hash SHA256, normalized_fields_json JSONB, ingested_at TIMESTAMP, vintage INT). correlation_rule (id UUID, name VARCHAR, cypher_pattern TEXT, priority INT ascending eval order, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, enabled BOOL, dedup_window_seconds INT default 30, created_by analyst_id, last_fired_at TIMESTAMP). attack_graph (id UUID, seed_event_id FK, matched_rule_id FK, node_ids UUID[], edge_ids UUID[], confidence_score FLOAT, severity ENUM inherited from rule, detected_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM open/investigating/closed). incident_report (id UUID, attack_graph_id FK, analyst_id FK, summary TEXT, severity ENUM critical/high/medium, escalated_to VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP). analyst_annotation (id UUID, target_node_id FK, content TEXT, analyst_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMP). audit_event (id UUID, user_id FK, action VARCHAR, target_type VARCHAR, target_id UUID, cypher_hash VARCHAR, ip VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: REST API /api/v1 via Go Gin with Azure AD JWT validation on all routes. GET /api/v1/attack-graphs?status=open&domain=scada (paginated, filtered by infrastructure domain). GET /api/v1/attack-graphs/:id/subgraph (returns node and edge lists for D3.js rendering). POST /api/v1/incidents (escalate attack_graph to incident, threat_hunter+ role). GET /api/v1/correlation-rules (list active rules). POST /api/v1/correlation-rules (create rule, threat_hunter role). PATCH /api/v1/correlation-rules/:id (update priority or cypher_pattern). GET /api/v1/log-events?source_node=:id&since=:ts (raw event lookup for analysts). GET /api/v1/alerts/stream (SSE endpoint streaming new attack_graph events to analyst dashboard). POST /api/v1/annotations (create analyst annotation on any node).
target_output.security_and_compliance: OT/IT Boundary: syslog-ng DMZ relay on isolated VM with iptables rules: -A INPUT -s 10.20.0.0/16 -p udp --dport 514 -j ACCEPT; -A OUTPUT -d 10.20.0.0/16 -j DROP; validated by city network team before prototype go-live. AKS network policy: deny all egress from SOC namespace to 10.20.0.0/16 CIDR. Authentication: Azure AD OIDC JWT with MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy; token expiry 8 hours matching analyst shift. RBAC: soc_analyst read-only graph and annotation create; threat_hunter adds rule management and ad-hoc Cypher; incident_commander adds escalation and cross-domain view; ciso_admin manages users and exports. Secrets: Azure Key Vault via workload identity, no static secrets in AKS manifests. Audit: append-only audit_event nodes in Neo4j; Neo4j transaction log backups to Azure Blob; CISA CSF DE.CM controls satisfied. Data encryption: AES-256 at rest via Neo4j AuraDB native encryption; TLS 1.3 for all Bolt connections and REST API traffic; Azure Storage encryption for Blob archive.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster on Azure Government eastus2 region: system node pool Standard_D4s_v5 x3, workload-spot pool Standard_D8s_v5 spot for log-ingestor and correlation-engine (toleration for spot eviction with stateless restart), memory pool Standard_E8s_v5 x2 for Redis statefulset. Syslog-ng relay on separate Standard_D2s_v5 VM in DMZ subnet, not in AKS cluster. Helm chart per service with image digest pinning from Azure Container Registry. GitHub Actions CI: golangci-lint, go test with dockerized Neo4j and Kafka, docker build, ACR push, helm upgrade --atomic. Environments: dev namespace (single replica), staging namespace (full topology), prototype-prod namespace (full topology, MFA enforced). Rollback: helm rollback to previous chart version; Kafka consumer offsets enable reprocessing from last committed position.
target_output.observability: Prometheus operator scrapes Go /metrics on port 9090 per service pod. Grafana dashboards: ingest_lag_seconds (Kafka consumer lag per topic), correlation_rule_eval_p99_ms (Cypher rule evaluation latency), neo4j_active_transactions (connection pool saturation), attack_graph_creation_rate (detections per minute per domain), dead_letter_rate (unparseable log fraction). Alerts: ingest_lag_seconds > 60 pages on-call SOC analyst; correlation_rule_eval_p99_ms > 5000 pages engineering; dead_letter_rate > 5% triggers log format investigation. Azure Monitor for AKS node CPU/memory and pod restart counts.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6K-8K/month on Azure Government for AKS workload + spot node savings (~30% reduction vs on-demand). Neo4j AuraDB Business tier adequate for prototype graph size. Spot node pool absorbs burst log ingestion at lower cost. Log-ingestor scales horizontally via Kafka partition assignment up to 8 replicas matching partition count. Correlation-engine scales to 4 replicas for large incident evaluation. Total prototype budget $80K covers 10 months of operation with room for Neo4j tier upgrade if graph grows beyond Business tier limits.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): IT network Windows Event Log ingestion via Kafka; log-ingestor normalization pipeline; 5 seed correlation rules for common lateral movement patterns (pass-the-hash, privilege escalation chain); basic Vue 3 analyst dashboard with SSE alert feed; Azure AD MFA authentication; AKS deployment on dev namespace. Phase 2 (4 weeks): SCADA CEF log ingestion from water treatment system; syslog-ng DMZ relay deployment and city network team ACL validation; correlation rule priority ordering and deduplication engine; D3.js attack subgraph visualization with domain coloring; analyst annotation tooling. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Traffic management controller log ingestion; incident escalation workflow; full RBAC role enforcement; Prometheus/Grafana observability dashboards; prototype-prod deployment with city CISO demo.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum pinned dependencies. golangci-lint with errcheck, staticcheck, gosec enabled. go test coverage >= 80% for correlation-engine rule evaluation logic. Neo4j integration tests use dockerized Neo4j 5 community in CI. Kafka consumer tests use embedded Kafka via testcontainers-go. Cypher rule changes require threat_hunter peer review and audit_event creation. No static secrets in any committed file; pre-commit hook scanning for credential patterns. ADR docs for Neo4j schema changes and correlation rule engine design decisions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Neo4j memory pressure during large coordinated attack graph expansion across all three infrastructure domains simultaneously. Mitigation: implement graph TTL on log_event nodes (90-day) and pagination on Cypher match results to prevent unbounded traversal. Risk: Spot node pool eviction during active incident investigation. Mitigation: log-ingestor restarts from Kafka committed offset; correlation-engine is stateless; analyst UI reconnects via SSE. Tradeoff: 15-second correlation evaluation polling vs streaming Cypher change data capture -- polling chosen for prototype simplicity with plan to evaluate CDC triggers in Phase 2. Tradeoff: Neo4j AuraDB over self-managed Neo4j cluster -- AuraDB reduces ops burden within prototype budget but limits raw query tuning access.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Neo4j AuraDB Business tier over Enterprise for prototype cost; upgrade path documented. Confirmed syslog-ng one-way relay over commercial data diode due to prototype budget; hardware data diode recommended for production. Resolved SCADA log format as CEF via Modbus audit logging feature; traffic controller logs as Windows Event XML. Deferred ML anomaly scoring to Phase 2 pending ground-truth annotation dataset accumulation.

metadata.notes: Round 3 municipal SOC prototype closing critical SCADA boundary ACL gap and correlation rule conflict resolution for graph-based threat detection.

profile.domain: Civic Infrastructure Reporting Mobile App

project_goal.value: Cross-platform mobile app enabling residents to report non-emergency public infrastructure issues such as potholes, broken streetlights, and graffiti to city maintenance departments with GPS-tagged photos and real-time status tracking
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual 311 call center intake with mobile-first self-service for infrastructure issue reporting

target_users.value: City residents reporting infrastructure issues; maintenance department staff reviewing and resolving tickets
target_users.rationale: Dual user base: public reporters and internal department staff

project_class.rationale: Primary interface is iOS and Android with camera, GPS, and push notification requirements

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Requires real-time status updates, department routing integrations, and analytics for service delivery metrics

complexity_level.rationale: Moderate complexity: multi-department routing logic, GPS clustering, photo upload pipeline, push notifications

risk_level.rationale: Non-sensitive public infrastructure data; no financial or health data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Resident profiles contain name and contact information for notification purposes

external_exposure.rationale: Public-facing app available on App Store and Google Play; no VPN requirement for residents

access_model.value: Optional resident account with Firebase Auth (email/Google OAuth); maintenance staff access via city SSO; anonymous report submission allowed
access_model.rationale: Lowering friction for residents increases report volume; staff need city identity binding

feature_scope.value: Issue report submission with GPS coordinates and photos; automatic routing to maintenance department by issue type and jurisdiction; real-time status tracking for residents; maintenance crew field status updates; public citywide service request dashboard; analytics for resolution time and volume by category
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for launch covering resident, crew, and management workflows

mvp_scope.value: Report submission with GPS and photo, auto-routing to 3 departments (roads, lighting, parks), push notification on status change, basic public status dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates routing accuracy and resident adoption before expanding to all departments

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic; Firebase Auth JWT validation; S3 photo uploads via pre-signed URLs; rate limiting on public report submission endpoint; PII fields encrypted at rest
security_baseline.rationale: Standard public mobile API security posture

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript; Expo SDK 51; React Query for data fetching
frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android; Expo accelerates camera and location API access

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify; TypeScript; Prisma ORM
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify throughput suits burst report submission; Prisma provides type-safe PostgreSQL access

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS; Redis 7 ElastiCache for push notification deduplication; S3 for photo storage
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits ticket status state machine; Redis deduplication prevents duplicate push notifications

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate; CloudFront for static assets and public dashboard
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container execution without server provisioning; CloudFront for global CDN on public dashboard

privacy_retention_policy.value: Resident PII retained until account deletion request; service_request records retained 5 years per city records policy; photos purged after 2 years; anonymous submissions retain no PII
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: City public records retention law and resident privacy expectations

future_scope.value: AI-assisted issue categorization from photo content; integration with city GIS for parcel-level ownership routing; iOS/Android widget for quick report submission
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap contingent on adoption metrics from launch

constraints.value: Budget under $2K/month infrastructure; must support offline report drafting with sync on reconnect; accessibility compliance WCAG 2.1 AA for mobile UI
constraints.rationale: City IT budget constraints and ADA mobile accessibility requirements

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for ECS and RDS metrics; custom CloudWatch alarms for report submission error rate and routing failure rate; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: AWS-native monitoring suitable for city IT team skill set

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; iOS and Android builds submitted to stores at end of each sprint for TestFlight and Play Console beta testing
execution_preference.rationale: Iterative beta testing with real residents accelerates UX validation

compliance_context.value: ADA Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility for mobile app; city open data policy requiring public service request data available via open API; GDPR-equivalent state privacy law for resident PII
compliance_context.rationale: Government mobile app must meet federal accessibility standards and local privacy law

project.goal: Mobile app for resident infrastructure issue reporting with auto-routing and real-time status tracking
project.target_users: City residents and maintenance department staff

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for local draft persistence

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT for residents; city SSO for staff

constraints.offline: SQLite draft persistence via Expo SQLite

open_questions.department_routing: GIS-based routing deferred to Phase 2; rule-based routing by issue category for MVP

confirmed_decisions.maps: React Native Maps with Google Maps SDK

reasoner_reviews.product: The automatic routing logic based on issue category and jurisdiction is the highest-value feature for reducing manual triage overhead in maintenance departments. The anonymous submission option is correct for maximizing resident engagement. The public citywide dashboard creates accountability and is a strong differentiator vs. phone-only 311 systems.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify with Prisma on ECS Fargate is well-suited for this load profile: burst submissions during commute hours and morning complaint windows, low sustained load. The offline draft persistence via Expo SQLite is the right call for residents in low-connectivity areas. Key gap from round 1: the routing assignment logic was underspecified -- the decision table mapping issue categories to department queues needs explicit design.
reasoner_reviews.security: Firebase Auth for resident-facing login is appropriate at this sensitivity level. Pre-signed S3 URLs for photo uploads prevent direct S3 exposure. Key concern from round 1 audit: rate limiting on the anonymous report submission endpoint was mentioned but not designed -- without it, the public endpoint is vulnerable to spam flooding that could overwhelm maintenance department queues.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $2K/month is achievable with Fargate spot capacity for the API tier and RDS db.t4g.medium for the PostgreSQL instance at expected submission volumes. CloudFront CDN for the public dashboard significantly reduces origin load at low cost.

backend.service_design: Single Fastify application with domain modules: reports (submission, routing assignment, status state machine), departments (routing rules management), notifications (push via Expo, email via SES), public-api (open data endpoints for citywide dashboard)
backend.api_patterns: REST API /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0; public endpoints unauthenticated for status dashboard; authenticated endpoints require Firebase JWT or city SSO JWT; rate limiting via fastify-rate-limit plugin 30 req/min per IP for report submission
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency key on POST /reports prevents duplicate submissions from offline sync reconnect; SQS dead-letter queue for failed push notifications; Prisma transaction for atomic report creation + routing_assignment record
backend.background_jobs: SQS-driven worker for push notification dispatch; nightly CloudWatch Scheduled Event triggering resolution-time analytics aggregation into summary table for dashboard
backend.scaling_notes: ECS Fargate auto-scaling on CPU 60% target; RDS read replica for public dashboard queries to avoid contention with submission writes

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth JWT verified via firebase-admin SDK on every authenticated endpoint; city staff authenticated via Azure AD SAML-to-JWT bridge; anonymous submission allowed without auth token
security.authorization_model: resident role: create reports, view own reports, subscribe to notifications; staff role: view and update reports in assigned department queue; supervisor role: reassign reports across departments; admin role: manage department routing rules
security.abuse_prevention: fastify-rate-limit 30 req/min per IP on POST /api/v1/reports; S3 pre-signed URL expiry 15 minutes for photo uploads; photo content-type validation (JPEG/PNG only, 10MB max) before pre-signed URL generation; CAPTCHA optional for anonymous submissions

data.entities: service_request (id UUID PK, category ENUM pothole/broken_streetlight/graffiti/debris/other, description TEXT, gps_lat DECIMAL, gps_lng DECIMAL, status ENUM submitted/routed/in_progress/resolved/closed, resident_id FK nullable, submitted_at, resolved_at), reported_location (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, address_geocoded, ward_id FK, jurisdiction_zone), photo_attachment (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, s3_key, content_type, file_size_bytes, uploaded_at), maintenance_department (id UUID PK, name, routing_categories ENUM[], jurisdiction_geometry POLYGON), routing_assignment (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, department_id FK, assigned_at, priority INT), status_update (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, staff_id FK, old_status, new_status, notes, updated_at), resident_profile (id UUID PK, firebase_uid, email_encrypted, display_name, notification_preferences_json, created_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS db.t4g.medium for transactional data; PostGIS extension for spatial queries on gps coordinates and jurisdiction geometry matching; S3 for photo attachments with presigned URL access; Redis for push notification deduplication within 5-minute window
data.analytics_events: daily_resolution_stats (department_id, category, avg_resolution_hours, report_count, date); weekly_volume_by_ward (ward_id, week, report_count, resolution_rate); report_heatmap data aggregated nightly for public dashboard

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: API service (2-6 tasks auto-scaling), notification worker (1-2 tasks on SQS queue depth). RDS PostgreSQL in Multi-AZ for HA. ElastiCache Redis single-node for prototype, Multi-AZ replica for production. CloudFront distribution for React Native web build and public dashboard static assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint + Prettier lint, Jest unit tests (70% coverage gate for routing logic), Prisma migrate deploy to staging, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Expo GitHub Action for OTA updates to TestFlight and Play Console beta channels.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Container Insights for ECS task CPU/memory. Custom CloudWatch metrics: report_submission_rate, routing_failure_count (no department matched), push_delivery_failure_rate. Alarms: routing_failure_count > 10/hour triggers engineering PagerDuty; API error rate > 2% triggers on-call alert. Firebase Crashlytics for React Native crash symbolication.

frontend.app_structure: Expo SDK 51 with React Native and TypeScript; Expo Router for file-based navigation; React Query for data fetching and cache management

previous_plan.title: City Infrastructure Reporter Mobile App - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native Expo app with Fastify/Node.js backend on ECS Fargate for resident infrastructure issue reporting, automatic department routing via PostGIS jurisdiction matching, and real-time push notification status updates.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React Native Expo app communicating over HTTPS to Fastify REST API on ECS Fargate; PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS for spatial routing; S3 for photo storage; SQS for async push notification dispatch.
previous_plan.workflows: Report submission stores GPS coordinates and photos, attempts PostGIS jurisdiction match to assign department -- overlap and fallback behavior not specified. Status updates trigger SQS message for push notification dispatch.

best_plan.title: City Infrastructure Reporter Mobile App - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: React Native Expo app with Fastify backend on ECS Fargate for resident infrastructure issue reporting with PostGIS routing and push notifications.
best_plan.architecture_overview: React Native communicating to Fastify API on Fargate; PostgreSQL PostGIS routing; S3 photos; SQS notifications.
