target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (routing overlap logic) and ISS-002 (rate limiting configuration). For ISS-001: define a three-tier routing decision: primary = PostGIS ST_Within intersection against department jurisdiction polygons; if multiple departments match, select by lowest routing_priority integer on the department record; if zero departments match, assign to city-wide fallback department with manual_triage flag set true. For ISS-002: configure fastify-rate-limit at 30 req/min per IP on POST /api/v1/reports, 5-minute block window, 429 with Retry-After header; additionally implement device-fingerprint-based limiting via Expo device token for authenticated users.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined three-tier routing decision algorithm in workflows and data_model: (1) PostGIS ST_Within check against department jurisdiction_geometry; (2) when multiple departments match, routing_priority INT on maintenance_department resolves tie by selecting lowest priority value; (3) when zero departments match, assign to designated fallback_department_id with manual_triage BOOL=true on routing_assignment record flagging for supervisor review queue
fix_report.expected_outcome: Maintenance departments receive correctly assigned tickets without overlap ambiguity; supervisor queue captures edge-case boundary submissions requiring manual triage

fix_report.action_taken: Specified fastify-rate-limit configuration: windowMs 60000ms, max 30 for anonymous POST /api/v1/reports, keyGenerator returns req.ip; on limit exceeded returns 429 with Retry-After: 300 header and JSON error body; authenticated resident submissions also rate-limited at 60 req/min by firebase_uid to prevent account-based spam
fix_report.expected_outcome: Anonymous public report endpoint protected against spam flooding without degrading legitimate resident submission experience

target_output.title: City Infrastructure Reporter Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native Expo mobile app enabling city residents to submit GPS-tagged photo reports of potholes, broken streetlights, graffiti, and other non-emergency infrastructure issues. The Fastify/Node.js backend auto-routes submissions to the correct maintenance department using a three-tier PostGIS spatial decision algorithm with jurisdiction overlap resolution and a manual triage fallback queue. Real-time status updates delivered via Expo push notifications. A public CloudFront-served dashboard consolidates all open service requests citywide with ward-level heatmaps.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native Expo app makes HTTPS calls to a Fastify REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. On report submission, the API executes a PostGIS spatial query against maintenance_department jurisdiction_geometry polygons to identify the correct routing assignment using three-tier logic (intersection match, priority tie-break, fallback triage). Photo uploads flow through pre-signed S3 URLs generated by the API. Status change events enqueue SQS messages processed by a Fargate notification worker that dispatches Expo push notifications and optionally email via SES. PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS handles spatial routing; Redis deduplicates push notifications within a 5-minute window. The public citywide dashboard is a static Next.js export served via CloudFront, polling the open-data API for live status counts.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Expo SDK 51, React Query, Zustand, Expo SQLite for offline drafts, React Native Maps with Google Maps SDK. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify with TypeScript, Prisma ORM, fastify-rate-limit, firebase-admin SDK. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS with PostGIS extension, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Storage: S3 with pre-signed URL access. Queuing: SQS for notification dispatch. Push: Expo Push Notification Service. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, CloudFront, GitHub Actions CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Report Submission: resident opens app, selects issue category, taps GPS pin or uses current location, uploads up to 3 photos via camera or gallery, adds optional description, submits with offline draft fallback. Routing Engine: three-tier PostGIS decision assigns department with jurisdiction overlap priority resolution and manual_triage fallback. Status Tracking: resident views live status (submitted/routed/in_progress/resolved) with timestamp history; push notification on each status transition. Crew Field Updates: maintenance staff app view shows department queue filtered by assigned ward; staff updates status from field with optional resolution notes. Public Dashboard: citywide map of open service_request pins clustered by category with ward-level resolution stats. Analytics: nightly aggregation of resolution time by department and category for management reporting.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (rate limiting and SQLi protection) routing to ECS Fargate API service (2-6 tasks); ECS Fargate notification worker (1-2 tasks, SQS-driven); PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with PostGIS extension; Redis 7 ElastiCache (push deduplication); S3 bucket (photo storage, CORS policy for pre-signed upload); SQS queue (notification dispatch with DLQ); CloudFront distribution (public dashboard static assets); Expo Push Notification Service (iOS/Android push delivery); GitHub Actions CI/CD.
target_output.workflows: Report Submission with Routing: resident taps 'Report Issue', selects category (pothole/broken_streetlight/graffiti/debris/other), pins GPS location (or uses device location), uploads photos via Expo ImagePicker (JPEG/PNG, 10MB max), submits. API validates idempotency key, generates S3 pre-signed URLs for photo uploads, runs PostGIS query: SELECT id, routing_priority FROM maintenance_departments WHERE ST_Within(ST_Point($lng, $lat), jurisdiction_geometry) AND $category = ANY(routing_categories) ORDER BY routing_priority ASC LIMIT 1. If one match: creates routing_assignment with department_id. If multiple match: selects lowest routing_priority. If zero match: assigns fallback_department_id with manual_triage=true on routing_assignment. Enqueues SQS message for resident push notification 'Report received, routed to [department]'. Field Status Update: maintenance crew opens staff app, views queue of service_requests assigned to their department, taps record to view GPS map and photos, updates status (in_progress/resolved) with notes. Prisma transaction updates service_request status and creates status_update record. SQS message dispatched for resident push notification. Redis deduplication key checked: if push sent for same request within 5 minutes, skip dispatch.
target_output.data_model: service_request (id UUID PK, category ENUM pothole/broken_streetlight/graffiti/debris/other, description TEXT, gps_lat DECIMAL(9,6), gps_lng DECIMAL(9,6), status ENUM submitted/routed/in_progress/resolved/closed, resident_id FK nullable, manual_triage BOOL default false, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, resolved_at TIMESTAMP nullable). reported_location (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, address_geocoded VARCHAR, ward_id FK, jurisdiction_zone VARCHAR). photo_attachment (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, s3_key VARCHAR, content_type VARCHAR, file_size_bytes INT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMP). maintenance_department (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, routing_categories ENUM[], jurisdiction_geometry GEOMETRY(POLYGON,4326), routing_priority INT ascending for overlap tie-break, fallback_department BOOL default false). routing_assignment (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, department_id FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, manual_triage BOOL). status_update (id UUID PK, service_request_id FK, staff_id FK, old_status ENUM, new_status ENUM, notes TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMP). resident_profile (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR UNIQUE, email_encrypted VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, notification_preferences_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0. POST /api/v1/reports (anonymous or authenticated, idempotency-key header required, rate-limited 30 req/min per IP / 60 req/min per firebase_uid). GET /api/v1/reports/:id (resident view with status history). PATCH /api/v1/reports/:id/status (staff role, updates status and creates status_update). GET /api/v1/reports/public?ward=:id&status=open (open data endpoint, unauthenticated, CloudFront cached 5 min). POST /api/v1/reports/:id/photos/presign (generates S3 pre-signed upload URL, validates content-type JPEG/PNG, 10MB max). GET /api/v1/departments (list departments with routing categories for client-side category picker). GET /api/v1/analytics/summary?department=:id&period=7d (management analytics, supervisor+ role).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Firebase Auth JWT validated via firebase-admin SDK; city staff via Azure AD SAML bridge issuing JWT. Rate Limiting: fastify-rate-limit on POST /api/v1/reports: windowMs=60000, max=30, keyGenerator=req.ip; authenticated submissions: max=60, keyGenerator=firebase_uid; 429 response with Retry-After: 300 header and JSON body {error:'rate_limit_exceeded', retry_after_seconds:300}. Photo security: content-type validation (JPEG/PNG only) before pre-signed URL generation; S3 bucket policy blocks public GetObject -- all access via pre-signed URLs. PII: resident email_encrypted with AES-256 at rest; RDS storage encryption enabled. WCAG 2.1 AA: React Native accessibility props on all interactive elements; screen reader tested via Expo Accessibility API. GDPR-equivalent: account deletion endpoint removes resident_profile and anonymizes service_request.resident_id to NULL.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster: API service 2-6 tasks (CPU 512, memory 1GB) auto-scaling on CPU 60%; notification worker 1-2 tasks scaling on SQS queue depth. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t4g.medium Multi-AZ (~$120/month). ElastiCache Redis cache.t4g.micro. S3 with Intelligent-Tiering for photos. CloudFront for public dashboard with 5-minute TTL on open service request data. GitHub Actions: ESLint/Prettier, Jest unit tests (70% coverage for routing module), Prisma migrate deploy, Docker build ECR push, ECS rolling update. Expo: EAS Build for iOS/Android production builds; OTA updates via Expo Updates for non-native JS changes. Rollback: ECS service rollback to previous task definition; Prisma migration rollback scripts in repo.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Container Insights for ECS task CPU/memory and request count. Custom CloudWatch metrics emitted by Fastify: report_submission_total (counter by category), routing_failure_total (zero-department match count), push_dispatch_latency_ms (SQS to Expo delivery). Alarms: routing_failure_total > 10 in 1 hour pages engineering (indicates jurisdiction data gap); API 5xx rate > 1% pages on-call; SQS queue depth > 500 pages notification worker. Firebase Crashlytics for React Native symbolicated crash reports by Expo release channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$400-600/month at 1K daily reports: RDS db.t4g.medium $120, ECS Fargate ~$150 (2 baseline tasks), ElastiCache $18, S3 + CloudFront ~$30, SQS $5. Well under $2K/month budget. At 5K daily reports: ECS scales to 4 tasks, RDS read replica added (~$300/month total increase). PostGIS spatial queries indexed on jurisdiction_geometry with GIST index; routing query p99 under 50ms at expected volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Report submission with GPS and 3 photos, PostGIS routing to 3 departments (roads, lighting, parks) with overlap tie-break and fallback queue, Firebase Auth for residents, push notifications on status change, basic public status dashboard, ECS/RDS/S3 infrastructure, GitHub Actions CI, TestFlight + Play Console beta. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Full department roster onboarding (all 8 city departments), staff mobile view for crew field updates, ward-level analytics dashboard, city SSO for staff auth, offline draft sync with idempotency key deduplication. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Public open-data API for citywide service requests, management analytics with resolution time reporting, SES email notifications as push fallback, WCAG 2.1 AA audit and remediation.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for React Native and Fastify. ESLint React Native community config + Prettier in CI. Jest unit tests for routing decision algorithm (three-tier logic, overlap tie-break, fallback) with 70% coverage gate. Prisma migrations committed and reviewed before staging deploy. React Native accessibility props enforced via eslint-plugin-react-native-a11y. Feature flags via Expo remote config for department-by-department rollout. ADR documents for PostGIS routing algorithm and offline sync strategy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostGIS jurisdiction polygon data quality -- inaccurate city GIS boundary data causes mis-routing to wrong department. Mitigation: import and validate jurisdiction polygons from city GIS team before launch; manual_triage queue captures edge cases. Risk: Expo OTA update blocked by Apple review for native module changes. Mitigation: separate JS-only updates (OTA eligible) from native dependency upgrades (full store build). Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for API -- Lambda cold starts would degrade submission latency during quiet periods; Fargate provides consistent p99 at slightly higher baseline cost. Tradeoff: Firebase Auth for residents over custom auth -- vendor dependency accepted in exchange for social login support and low implementation cost.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed PostGIS over Google Maps Routing API for jurisdiction matching to avoid per-query cost at scale. Selected SQS over direct Expo push API calls from API service for reliability and retry handling. Offline sync resolved as last-write-wins with server idempotency key deduplication. Anonymous submissions confirmed allowed without CAPTCHA for MVP -- device fingerprint rate limiting provides sufficient spam protection.

metadata.notes: Round 2 civic mobile app resolving PostGIS routing overlap logic and anonymous endpoint rate limiting for city infrastructure reporting.

profile.domain: State Government Emergency Preparedness Landing Page

project_goal.value: Public-facing landing page for state emergency preparedness campaign providing residents with disaster readiness checklists, evacuation route information, and sign-up forms for emergency alert SMS notifications
project_goal.rationale: Replaces static PDF handouts with an accessible, searchable, always-current web resource for statewide emergency preparedness

target_users.value: State residents seeking emergency preparedness guidance; includes elderly and low-literacy populations requiring plain-language content and accessible design
target_users.rationale: Broad public audience with accessibility and plain-language requirements under state communications standards

project_class.rationale: Purely informational static site with no server-side runtime; third-party API integration for alert sign-up only

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery, Google Analytics 4 for traffic measurement, third-party emergency alert SMS API integration

complexity_level.rationale: Statically generated pages, no authentication, no database, edge-cached delivery

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored on state infrastructure; SMS sign-up data handled entirely by third-party alert provider

data_sensitivity.rationale: No PII collected or stored by the state site; phone numbers submitted directly to third-party provider

external_exposure.rationale: Must be publicly accessible without authentication; target audience is all state residents

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication required; no user accounts
access_model.rationale: Emergency preparedness content must be maximally accessible without login friction

feature_scope.value: Disaster readiness checklists by hazard type (hurricane, wildfire, earthquake, flood, winter storm); county-specific evacuation route maps; downloadable printable checklist PDFs; embedded SMS alert sign-up form posting to state emergency alert provider API; county emergency contact directory; resource links to FEMA and Red Cross
feature_scope.rationale: Full content scope for statewide emergency preparedness reference site

mvp_scope.value: 5 hazard-type checklists, evacuation route static maps for 3 coastal counties, SMS alert sign-up form, county contact directory
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum content for launch before hurricane season; remaining counties added in waves

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront with HSTS header; no state-side data collection; Content Security Policy header blocking inline scripts; no cookies set except GA4 consent
security_baseline.rationale: Standard public government website security headers; no PII collection eliminates data breach risk

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript; static export (next export); Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js static export generates optimized HTML/CSS/JS with no server runtime; Tailwind enables accessible component styling without runtime CSS-in-JS overhead

backend_stack.value: None; fully static site with no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Landing page project class requires no server runtime; all dynamic behavior is client-side or delegated to third-party APIs

data_platform.value: None; no database required; content managed as Markdown files in repository
data_platform.rationale: Static content stored in repository as Markdown with Next.js MDX rendering; no persistent data storage on state infrastructure

hosting_target.value: CloudFront CDN with S3 origin; static assets deployed via GitHub Actions on merge to main
hosting_target.rationale: CloudFront provides global edge caching sustaining traffic spikes during emergency declarations; S3 is zero-server hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: No PII collected or retained by state infrastructure; GA4 configured with IP anonymization and no cross-site tracking; cookie consent banner for GA4
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State privacy policy requires minimal data collection for public informational sites

future_scope.value: Interactive evacuation route tool with address-based county lookup; multilingual content (Spanish, Vietnamese, Haitian Creole); live NOAA weather alert widget integration
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 enhancements contingent on resident engagement metrics from launch

constraints.value: Must achieve WCAG 2.1 AA; plain-language content at Grade 8 reading level; must load in under 3 seconds on 4G mobile; hosting cost under $50/month
constraints.rationale: State accessibility law and communications standards; low hosting budget for informational site

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 with custom events for checklist downloads and SMS sign-up form submissions; CloudFront access logs to S3 for traffic analysis during emergency events
observability_baseline.rationale: Traffic analytics to measure resident engagement; emergency declaration traffic spikes need retrospective analysis

execution_preference.value: Content-driven sprints with state communications team reviewing copy before each deployment; weekly deployments via GitHub Actions on main merge
execution_preference.rationale: Content accuracy is critical for emergency guidance; communications team approval gates each release

project.goal: Static emergency preparedness landing page with checklist content, evacuation maps, and SMS alert sign-up
project.target_users: State residents, elderly and low-literacy populations

frontend.content: Markdown/MDX files for checklist and route pages

security.headers: HTTPS HSTS, CSP blocking inline scripts
security.cookies: GA4 consent only, IP anonymization enabled

devops.deploy: Static export on merge to main

open_questions.sms_provider: Using existing state emergency alert API (Everbridge) for SMS sign-up form POST target

reasoner_reviews.product: The content-first approach is correct for this use case. Statically generating checklist and evacuation route pages ensures content is available even during CDN degradation scenarios. The decision to delegate SMS sign-up entirely to the Everbridge API with no state-side phone number storage is the right privacy-preserving approach. Plain-language Grade 8 reading level requirement is critical for the intended audience including elderly and low-literacy residents.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export to S3/CloudFront is the optimal architecture for a government informational landing page expected to receive unpredictable traffic spikes during active emergency declarations. The edge cache absorbs 100% of read traffic with zero backend scaling concerns. The key unresolved issue from previous rounds is the SMS form submission error handling -- if the Everbridge API is unavailable during a declaration event when sign-ups surge, residents receive no feedback and may assume success when they were not enrolled.
reasoner_reviews.security: No PII on state infrastructure is the correct posture for this project class. CSP header implementation was noted as incomplete in round 2 -- the specific policy directives need to be defined to block cross-site script injection while permitting Google Analytics and Everbridge form submission domains. HSTS preloading for the state domain should be included.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: CloudFront + S3 hosting cost is well under $50/month even at high traffic volumes. Next.js static export with image optimization generates WebP variants enabling sub-3-second load on 4G mobile. The constraint most at risk is Grade 8 plain-language review -- this requires a content workflow gate, not a technical one.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export with MDX content layer; pages: index (campaign hero, quick links), /checklist/[hazard] (5 hazard types), /routes/[county] (county evacuation maps), /alerts (SMS sign-up), /contacts (county directory). Dynamic routes pre-rendered at build time from MDX content files.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage: hero section with 5 hazard-type quick links, emergency alert sign-up CTA, county selector; Checklist pages: MDX-rendered checklist with printable PDF download link, section anchors for screen readers; Evacuation route pages: static SVG maps with alt-text descriptions of route logic; Alert sign-up: single-page form with phone number input, county selector, POSTs to Everbridge API, client-side success/error state with accessible status announcements.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA: semantic HTML5 landmarks (header, main, nav, footer); aria-label on all icon-only buttons; sufficient color contrast ratios (4.5:1 minimum for body text); keyboard navigation focus indicators; skip-to-content link; all SVG evacuation maps have longdesc alternative text descriptions; PDF checklist download links include file size in link text.
frontend.performance_notes: next/image with WebP generation for evacuation map images; Tailwind CSS purge eliminating unused classes; Next.js font optimization loading Inter via next/font with font-display:swap; CloudFront edge cache with Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400 for static assets; emergency declaration pages pre-warmed via CloudFront cache invalidation script in CI.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public access
security.secrets_management: Everbridge API endpoint URL and form token stored as GitHub Actions secrets and injected as Next.js NEXT_PUBLIC_ env vars at build time; no runtime secret access
security.abuse_prevention: CSP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.everbridge.net; img-src 'self' data: https:; frame-ancestors 'none'. Rate limiting not applicable -- no state-side API endpoints.
security.privacy_controls: GA4 configured with anonymize_ip: true; no GA4 remarketing audiences; cookie consent banner using client-side localStorage consent flag before GA4 script loads; Everbridge handles all phone number data under their BAA with the state.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (state-emergency-prep.state.gov) configured for static website hosting; CloudFront distribution with S3 origin, HTTPS-only redirect, HSTS max-age=31536000 includeSubDomains preload header; Route 53 alias record to CloudFront.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on push to main: install bun dependencies, next build with static export, run axe-core accessibility CI scan (zero violations gate), aws s3 sync dist/ to S3 bucket, CloudFront invalidation on /* path. Content PR workflow: communications team reviews MDX content changes via GitHub PR review before merge.
devops.observability_stack: GA4 with custom events: checklist_download (hazard_type, county), alert_signup_success, alert_signup_error (Everbridge API response status). CloudFront access logs shipped to S3 for post-event traffic analysis. CloudFront 5xx error rate alarm via CloudWatch (threshold: > 0.1% triggers ops notification).

previous_plan.title: State Emergency Preparedness Site - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static export deployed to CloudFront/S3 for state emergency preparedness content: hazard checklists, county evacuation maps, and Everbridge SMS alert sign-up. CSP header added in round 2.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Fully static Next.js 14 export; CloudFront with HSTS; S3 origin; GitHub Actions CI deploy; GA4 analytics with consent; Everbridge SMS API integration via form POST. No server runtime.
previous_plan.workflows: Residents browse checklists and evacuation maps from edge-cached static pages; SMS sign-up form POSTs to Everbridge API -- error handling not specified. PDF checklist downloads tracked via GA4 custom event.
previous_plan.system_components: S3 static hosting, CloudFront CDN, Route 53, GitHub Actions CI, GA4, Everbridge API (third-party). CSP header defined with GA4 and Everbridge domains whitelisted.

best_plan.title: State Emergency Preparedness Site - Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: Static Next.js export on CloudFront/S3 with emergency checklists, evacuation maps, Everbridge SMS sign-up, CSP headers. ISS-001 resolved; ISS-002 and ISS-003 remain.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Static export; CloudFront HSTS; S3; GitHub Actions; GA4 consent; Everbridge form POST. CSP directives specified.
best_plan.system_components: S3, CloudFront, Route 53, GitHub Actions, GA4, Everbridge. HSTS and CSP added.
best_plan.workflows: Checklist browse and download; SVG evacuation maps without accessible text alternatives; SMS sign-up with undesigned error path.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 closes ISS-002 (Everbridge form error handling) and ISS-003 (SVG map accessibility). For ISS-002: add client-side try/catch on Everbridge POST with three states -- success (confirmation message), API error (visible error alert with state emergency hotline 1-800-XXX-XXXX fallback), and timeout (same error state after 8-second fetch timeout). For ISS-003: each county SVG evacuation map gets a sibling details/summary element with plain-language textual route description satisfying WCAG SC 1.1.1; axe-core CI scan added as explicit zero-violations gate in GitHub Actions.

fix_report.action_taken: Added three-state form submission handler in AlertSignupForm component: (1) success state renders confirmation banner 'You will receive emergency alerts for [county]'; (2) error state (Everbridge 4xx/5xx or fetch exception) renders accessible error alert role='alert' with text 'Sign-up unavailable. Call 1-800-XXX-XXXX to enroll by phone or visit ready.state.gov/alerts'; (3) 8-second AbortController timeout triggers same error state; submit button shows loading spinner during pending state
fix_report.expected_outcome: Residents receive clear confirmation or actionable fallback guidance if Everbridge is unavailable during peak emergency declaration traffic, preventing silent enrollment failures

fix_report.action_taken: Added accessible text alternative to each county SVG evacuation map: a details/summary element beneath each SVG with summary='Evacuation route description for [County] County' and paragraph text describing primary evacuation corridors, shelter locations, and special-needs route notes in plain language at Grade 8 level; SVG elements receive aria-hidden=true with adjacent visually-hidden description linked via aria-describedby; axe-core GitHub Actions step added with failure on any WCAG 2.1 AA violation
fix_report.expected_outcome: Screen reader users receive equivalent route information satisfying WCAG 2.1 AA SC 1.1.1; axe-core CI gate prevents future regressions

target_output.title: State Emergency Preparedness Campaign Site
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Next.js 14 site deployed to CloudFront/S3 for statewide emergency preparedness, providing residents with hazard-specific readiness checklists, county evacuation route guides, downloadable printable PDFs, and a WCAG 2.1 AA compliant SMS alert enrollment form. No server-side runtime or state-managed PII. Phone number sign-up delegated entirely to the Everbridge state emergency alert API with a designed three-state form handler covering success, API failure, and timeout scenarios. Edge-cached at CloudFront for resilience during emergency declaration traffic surges.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Next.js 14 site with no server-side runtime. Content authored as MDX files in the repository, compiled to static HTML/CSS/JS via next build --output=export at CI time. S3 bucket hosts the static export; CloudFront distribution serves it globally with HTTPS-only redirect, HSTS max-age=31536000 includeSubDomains preload, and a defined CSP header whitelisting googletagmanager.com for GA4 and api.everbridge.net for SMS sign-up form submissions. Dynamic behavior is limited to client-side React: the alert sign-up form component, GA4 consent banner, and PDF download tracking events. On form submission, the client directly POSTs to the Everbridge API endpoint with an 8-second AbortController timeout and renders one of three accessible states: enrollment confirmation, API error with phone fallback, or timeout error. Zero data is retained on state infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, static export mode. Styling: Tailwind CSS with @tailwindcss/typography for MDX prose. Content: MDX with next-mdx-remote for hazard checklist and evacuation route pages. Fonts: next/font loading Inter with font-display swap. Images: next/image generating WebP variants for evacuation map photographs. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization and client-side consent gate. CI: GitHub Actions with axe-core accessibility scan gate. Hosting: S3 + CloudFront, Route 53. PDF generation: pre-generated printable PDFs committed to repository, not rendered at runtime.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hazard Checklists: 5 MDX pages (/checklist/hurricane, /wildfire, /earthquake, /flood, /winter-storm) with supply lists, action steps, and pet/livestock guidance; section anchors for screen readers; printable PDF download with file size in link text tracked via GA4 checklist_download event. Evacuation Routes: 3 coastal county pages (/routes/miami-dade, /routes/broward, /routes/palm-beach) each with static SVG map, aria-hidden=true on SVG, and adjacent details/summary accessible text description of primary corridors, shelter points, and special-needs routes in Grade 8 plain language. SMS Alert Sign-up: AlertSignupForm component with phone input, county selector, submit button; three-state handler: pending (spinner, button disabled), success (confirmation banner), error (accessible role=alert with state hotline 1-800-555-0100 fallback). County Contact Directory: MDX table of county emergency management office names, phone numbers, and 24/7 hotlines. Resource Links: curated external links to FEMA Ready.gov, Red Cross preparedness guides, NOAA weather radio.
target_output.system_components: S3 Bucket (state-emergency-prep.state.gov, static website hosting, versioning enabled for rollback); CloudFront Distribution (HTTPS-only, HSTS preload header, CSP response header via CloudFront response headers policy, 24-hour cache TTL for static assets, 5-minute TTL for index.html to allow content updates to propagate during active declarations); Route 53 Alias to CloudFront; GitHub Actions (build pipeline with axe-core scan gate, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation on merge to main); Google Analytics 4 (IP anonymized, consent-gated); Everbridge SMS API (third-party, no state-side data retention); Pre-generated PDF repository assets.
target_output.workflows: Resident Browses Checklist: resident arrives at homepage via search or state emergency alert link, selects hazard type from hero navigation, lands on /checklist/hurricane MDX page rendered as static HTML, reads supply list and action steps in plain language, clicks 'Download Printable Checklist (PDF, 280KB)', GA4 event checklist_download fires with hazard_type=hurricane. Resident Enrolls in SMS Alerts: resident navigates to /alerts, reads privacy notice that phone number is handled by state emergency alert system and not stored by this website, enters phone number and selects county, clicks 'Enroll in Alerts'. AlertSignupForm POSTs to Everbridge API endpoint with AbortController timeout 8000ms. On 200 response: renders success banner 'Enrolled for [County] County emergency alerts via text'. On 4xx/5xx or fetch exception: renders role=alert error message 'Unable to enroll online. Call 1-800-555-0100 to enroll by phone or visit an emergency shelter registration site.' On timeout: same error state. Emergency Declaration Traffic Surge: state activates emergency declaration, links to preparedness site from official communications. CloudFront edge cache serves all checklist and route pages without origin requests. CloudFront access logs record surge traffic to S3 for post-event analysis.
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on state infrastructure. Content stored as MDX files in repository under /content/checklists/[hazard].mdx and /content/routes/[county].mdx. Evacuation route accessible text stored inline in MDX as a details/summary block. County contacts stored as MDX table in /content/contacts/index.mdx. PDF checklist files stored in /public/pdfs/[hazard]-checklist.pdf. Everbridge manages all SMS enrollment records under their data processing agreement with the state. GA4 aggregated event data (checklist_download count by hazard_type, alert_signup_success count by county) accessible in GA4 dashboard; no raw PII in GA4 due to IP anonymization.
target_output.api_design: No state-managed API endpoints. Third-party integrations: Everbridge SMS enrollment API (POST https://api.everbridge.net/rest/contacts with phone, county fields, form token in Authorization header); Google Analytics 4 Measurement Protocol for server-side event forwarding is out of scope -- GA4 client-side tag only. CloudFront serves all content; no Lambda@Edge or CloudFront Functions for this project class. Future Phase 2 multilingual API for dynamic county selector is deferred.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS: CloudFront HTTPS-only with HSTS max-age=31536000 includeSubDomains preload, submitted to HSTS preload list. CSP response header policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.everbridge.net https://www.google-analytics.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; font-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'. Cookies: GA4 sets _ga cookie; consent banner uses localStorage flag; no cookie set before consent. PII: zero PII on state infrastructure; Everbridge phone data under state BAA. WCAG 2.1 AA: axe-core CI scan zero-violations gate; semantic HTML5 landmarks; SVG evacuation maps with aria-hidden + adjacent accessible text descriptions via details/summary. Section 508: applies as state agency site; covered by WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on push to main: bun install, next build (--output export), axe-core scan on built HTML files using axe-cli (zero violations on WCAG2AA ruleset, CI fails on any violation), aws s3 sync out/ s3://state-emergency-prep.state.gov --delete, aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DIST_ID --paths '/*'. Content PRs: MDX checklist and route file changes require state communications team GitHub review approval before merge. Emergency content updates (evacuation order changes): on-call ops engineer merges directly to main with communications team verbal approval for time-critical updates. Rollback: S3 bucket versioning enables restore of previous build within 5 minutes via s3 cp of prior version.
target_output.observability: GA4 custom events: checklist_download (hazard_type, county_context), alert_signup_success (county), alert_signup_error (error_type: api_error/timeout). GA4 real-time report monitored by communications team during active emergency declarations to track resident engagement. CloudFront access logs to S3 partition by day; queried via Athena for post-event traffic spike analysis (requests per minute, top pages, cache hit rate during declaration event). CloudWatch alarm: CloudFront 5xx error rate > 0.1% over 5 minutes sends email to ops team. CloudWatch alarm: S3 4xx error rate > 1% over 5 minutes (indicates broken asset reference in deployment).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$15-25/month baseline: S3 storage $2 for static export assets and PDFs, CloudFront $8-15 depending on traffic (1M requests/month free tier then $0.0075 per 10K). During emergency declaration traffic surge (10x-50x normal): CloudFront absorbs all read traffic with no origin scaling cost; estimated surge cost $30-60 for a 72-hour declaration period. Well under $50/month constraint. Route 53 hosted zone $0.50/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): 5 hazard-type MDX checklists, 3 coastal county evacuation route pages with accessible SVG maps and text descriptions, AlertSignupForm with three-state Everbridge integration and error handling, county contact directory, GA4 consent-gated analytics, HTTPS/HSTS/CSP headers, axe-core CI gate, S3/CloudFront deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Remaining 64 county evacuation route pages, printable PDF checklist generation, NOAA weather alert widget (client-side JavaScript embedding), Spanish and English bilingual toggle using next-i18next static export mode. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Interactive evacuation route tool with address-based county lookup using client-side PostalCode-to-county mapping JSON; Vietnamese and Haitian Creole translations; FEMA Integrated Public Alert and Warning System (IPAWS) alert widget embed.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. ESLint Next.js core-web-vitals config + Prettier. MDX content files must pass plain-language Grade 8 readability check (automated via textstat in CI) before merge. axe-core axe-cli scan on built HTML with zero WCAG2AA violations as hard CI gate. next/image required for all raster images -- no raw img tags. All SVG evacuation maps must have adjacent details/summary accessible text block; enforced via custom ESLint rule checking for aria-hidden SVG without sibling details element. PDF download links include file size in parentheses in link text.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Everbridge API unavailable during major emergency declaration exactly when sign-up demand is highest. Mitigation: three-state form handler with phone fallback number ensures residents have an alternative path. Risk: MDX content accuracy during fast-moving emergency (evacuation order changes). Mitigation: emergency merge process bypasses PR review for time-critical route updates; S3 versioning enables rapid rollback. Tradeoff: static MDX content over CMS -- CMS would enable non-technical staff editing but introduces server-side runtime dependency; static repo approach accepted for simpler hosting and no runtime failure modes during declarations. Tradeoff: pre-generated PDFs over server-side rendering -- PDFs are not dynamically personalized, so pre-generation eliminates Lambda/serverless cost and latency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Everbridge as existing state SMS provider -- no provider selection needed. Selected static MDX over CMS for zero server-side runtime dependency during emergency surges. Resolved evacuation route map format as SVG for scalability and accessibility compatibility over raster images. Grade 8 readability automated via textstat Python library in CI rather than manual review only.

metadata.notes: Round 3 state government static landing page closing Everbridge form error handling and SVG evacuation map accessibility gaps.

profile.domain: US Census Bureau Public Data API

project_goal.value: High-throughput public API service exposing aggregated US Census Bureau administrative datasets -- population estimates, housing unit counts, and demographic breakdowns -- at census tract and block-group level, consumed by research institutions, journalists, and civic tech applications
project_goal.rationale: Democratizes access to federal administrative statistics through a developer-friendly API eliminating the need to parse raw Census Bureau FTP files

target_users.value: University researchers, civic tech developers, data journalists, and government policy analysts querying census tract and block-group demographic data
target_users.rationale: Academic and civic use cases require both low-latency interactive queries and bulk data export capabilities

project_class.rationale: Pure API backend with no user-facing frontend; consumers integrate via HTTP clients and SDKs

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, analytics, batch_jobs, devops
capabilities.rationale: Requires API key management for rate limiting, batch ingestion jobs for annual Census releases, analytics for usage monitoring

complexity_level.rationale: Tens of millions of census tract records, millions of daily API requests from public consumers, multi-level caching required

risk_level.rationale: Public aggregated data; no PII; no financial data; open government data by policy

data_sensitivity.rationale: All exposed data is public Census Bureau aggregate statistics; Title 13 USC prohibits individual-level census records and only aggregated data is stored

external_exposure.rationale: Public API available to any registered developer; API key required for rate limit tier assignment only

access_model.value: API key required for authenticated rate limits (10K requests/day free, 100K/day academic, unlimited partner); unauthenticated requests allowed at 100 requests/day for exploration
access_model.rationale: Tiered access model enables casual exploration while providing higher limits for registered researchers and civic tech partners

feature_scope.value: Census tract and block-group demographic queries by FIPS code; population estimates 2010-2023 vintage years; housing unit counts; race and Hispanic origin breakdowns; API key registration and tier management; bulk NDJSON export endpoints; annual data vintage ingestion batch jobs
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope covering interactive queries and bulk export for all primary consumer use cases

mvp_scope.value: Census tract demographic queries by FIPS code for 2020 and 2022 vintage years; population, housing count, and race breakdown fields; API key registration; Redis caching for common FIPS queries
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates API design and ClickHouse query performance before adding block-group and bounding box features

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 on all endpoints; API key validation with rate limiting per tier; WAF blocking malformed FIPS inputs; no PII collection beyond API key contact email
security_baseline.rationale: Standard public API security posture for open government data service

frontend_stack.value: None; API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing frontend; API consumers access via HTTP clients; developer documentation site is a separate project

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; ClickHouse for OLAP queries; Redis 7 for multi-level cache
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides throughput-per-core efficiency for millions of daily requests; ClickHouse columnar storage delivers sub-second aggregation queries

data_platform.value: ClickHouse self-managed on GKE StatefulSet; Redis 7 for L1 query result cache; GCS for raw CSV ingestion staging and bulk export files; PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL for API key and job metadata
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse MergeTree partitioned by vintage year enables O(1) FIPS lookups; columnar compression reduces storage cost on 100M+ row tables

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP us-central1; Cloud Build for CI/CD; Cloud Armor WAF on GKE Ingress
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot eliminates node management overhead; Cloud Armor provides DDoS protection for public API endpoint

privacy_retention_policy.value: API key contact emails retained until key deletion; API request logs retained 90 days for abuse investigation; no census data row-level PII ever ingested (Title 13 USC aggregates only)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Open Government Data Act compliance and minimal PII retention posture

future_scope.value: ACS 5-year estimates integration; geographic bounding box queries using PostGIS; SDKs for Python and R; webhook notifications for new vintage year availability
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 scope contingent on adoption and API consumer feedback

constraints.value: ClickHouse query p99 under 200ms for single FIPS tract lookup; bulk export first-byte response within 5 seconds; infrastructure cost under $8K/month at 5M daily requests
constraints.rationale: Researcher and journalist use cases require low interactive latency; cost constraint ensures fiscal sustainability

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus metrics scraped from Go /metrics; Grafana dashboards for query latency by geography level and vintage year; Cloud Monitoring for GKE node and pod health
observability_baseline.rationale: Query performance monitoring is critical for ClickHouse tuning and cache hit rate optimization

execution_preference.value: Milestone-based delivery: API schema freeze before ClickHouse schema commit; vintages added incrementally; breaking changes versioned with 6-month deprecation window
execution_preference.rationale: Public API stability requires schema governance to avoid breaking downstream consumer integrations

project.goal: High-throughput public Census demographic data API with multi-level caching and annual vintage ingestion
project.target_users: Researchers, data journalists, civic tech developers

security.auth: API key validation middleware with per-tier rate limits
security.waf: Cloud Armor rules on GKE ingress

data.cache: Redis L1 cache for common FIPS queries
data.storage: GCS for raw CSV staging and NDJSON exports

devops.ci: Cloud Build with Go unit and integration tests

open_questions.clickhouse_hosting: Self-managed GKE StatefulSet selected over Cloud SQL for ClickHouse based on cost analysis

reasoner_reviews.product: The tiered API key model (free/academic/partner) is correct for balancing open access with cost control at scale. The unauthenticated 100 req/day tier removes sign-up friction for casual users evaluating the API. Bulk NDJSON export is essential for research institutions pulling complete census tract datasets for offline analysis.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse MergeTree partitioned by vintage_year with ORDER BY (fips_code, vintage_year) will deliver the required sub-200ms FIPS lookups via primary key index. The critical round 2 gap was missing ClickHouse CREATE TABLE DDL -- without explicit column type definitions and index specifications, the query performance claims cannot be validated. The async GKE Job export pattern is the correct design for 800K-row NDJSON exports meeting the 5-second first-byte constraint via streaming SELECT.
reasoner_reviews.security: No PII risk on census data given Title 13 USC aggregation requirement. Cloud Armor FIPS validation added in round 2. API key contact email is the only PII and retention policy is appropriately minimal. The remaining gap is specifying ClickHouse connection pool limits to prevent query starvation during bulk export execution.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ClickHouse self-managed on GKE is significantly cheaper than Cloud SQL for ClickHouse at this data volume (estimated $800 vs $3200/month for equivalent throughput). GCS bulk export with signed URLs eliminates GKE egress costs. The $8K/month budget is achievable.

backend.service_design: Single Go service with domain modules: query (FIPS and state-level demographic query handlers with Redis cache and ClickHouse fallthrough), export (async GKE Job launcher for NDJSON streaming exports to GCS), ingest (batch job orchestration for annual vintage loading), keys (API key CRUD and rate limit tier management via Redis token buckets)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful /api/v1; GET for tract and blockgroup demographic queries; POST /api/v1/exports for async bulk NDJSON export job returning job ID; GET /api/v1/exports/:id for status polling; rate limiting via Redis token bucket per api_key_id and tier
backend.background_jobs: Annual vintage ingest: Cloud Scheduler triggers GKE Job downloading Census FTP CSVs to GCS, running Go transform binary normalizing to ClickHouse INSERT batches (100K rows/batch); export jobs: GKE Jobs launched on-demand streaming ClickHouse SELECT to GCS via clickhouse-client pipe
backend.failure_handling: ClickHouse query timeout (5s) returns 504 with Retry-After; Redis cache miss falls through to ClickHouse with circuit breaker on connection pool; bulk export job failure sets export_jobs status=failed; ingest job validates row count against prior vintage before committing data_vintages status=complete
backend.scaling_notes: GKE Autopilot scales API pods on CPU 60% target (3-20 replicas); ClickHouse vertical scaling (larger node) before horizontal sharding for current data volume; Redis cluster mode when single-node memory exceeds 80%

security.auth_design: API key passed as X-Api-Key header; Go middleware computes SHA-256 hash, looks up in PostgreSQL (cached in Redis api_key_meta 5-min TTL), checks revoked_at, validates tier rate limit via Redis token bucket; unauthenticated requests use shared anonymous tier counter keyed by IP
security.authorization_model: Rate limit tiers: anonymous (100/day per IP), free (10K/day per key), academic (100K/day per key), partner (unlimited); tier stored on api_key PostgreSQL record; Redis INCR counter with EXPIREAT midnight UTC for daily limits
security.abuse_prevention: Cloud Armor rules: DENY requests where fips_code param matches regex [^0-9]; DENY fips_code length > 12 characters; DENY POST body > 2KB on query endpoints; rate limit 1000 req/min per IP at Cloud Armor WAF layer independent of API key tier; ClickHouse max_execution_time=5 per query to prevent runaway aggregations
security.secrets_management: PostgreSQL and ClickHouse credentials in GCP Secret Manager; GKE Workload Identity for pod access; no static secrets in container images or Kubernetes manifests

data.entities: census_tract_demographics (fips_tract_code FixedString(11), vintage_year UInt16, total_population UInt32, housing_units UInt32, occupied_units UInt32, white_alone UInt32, black_alone UInt32, hispanic_any_race UInt32, asian_alone UInt32, two_or_more_races UInt32, median_household_income Float32, poverty_rate Float32, ingested_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY vintage_year ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year); INDEX idx_state on substring(fips_tract_code,1,2) TYPE minmax GRANULARITY 4. census_blockgroup_demographics: same schema with fips_bg_code FixedString(12). geographic_boundaries (fips_code FixedString(12), geography_level Enum8, state_fips FixedString(2), county_fips FixedString(5), centroid_lat Float64, centroid_lng Float64) ENGINE=MergeTree() ORDER BY fips_code. api_keys (PostgreSQL): id UUID, key_hash CHAR(64) UNIQUE, tier ENUM free/academic/partner, contact_email VARCHAR, daily_limit INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ. export_jobs (PostgreSQL): id UUID, api_key_id FK, status ENUM pending/running/complete/failed, filter_params JSONB, gcs_object_key VARCHAR, signed_url VARCHAR, row_count BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. data_vintages (PostgreSQL): vintage_year SMALLINT, geography_level ENUM, row_count BIGINT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/complete/failed
data.storage_design: ClickHouse MergeTree for demographic fact tables partitioned by vintage_year; PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL for api_keys, export_jobs, data_vintages metadata; Redis L1 cache 1-hour TTL for single-FIPS tract query results; GCS buckets: census-raw-staging, census-exports, census-access-logs
data.analytics_events: API request logs archived to GCS with api_key_id, fips_queried, vintage_year, response_time_ms; nightly aggregation into api_usage_daily (api_key_id, date, request_count, distinct_fips_queried) for usage dashboards
data.migration_notes: New vintage year ingestion via INSERT batch jobs; ClickHouse ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new demographic fields; zero-downtime since MergeTree allows concurrent reads during bulk INSERT; ingest idempotency via unique constraint on data_vintages (vintage_year, geography_level)

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot us-central1: API Deployment (3-20 pods HPA), ClickHouse StatefulSet (n2-highmem-8 node, 500GB SSD PVC), Redis StatefulSet (standard PVC), PostgreSQL Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 Multi-AZ; Cloud Armor WAF on GKE Ingress with FIPS validation rules
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: go vet, go test (unit + dockerized ClickHouse and Redis integration tests), docker build, Artifact Registry push, kubectl rolling deploy. Vintage ingest GKE Job triggered by Cloud Scheduler; export GKE Jobs launched on-demand by API service
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Go /metrics; Grafana: request_latency_p99_ms by geography_level and vintage_year, cache_hit_rate (Redis L1 target >80%), clickhouse_query_duration_ms p50/p99, export_job_duration_seconds, api_request_rate by tier. Alerts: p99 > 200ms pages on-call; cache_hit_rate < 70% triggers ClickHouse tuning investigation; disk_usage > 80% triggers capacity alert
devops.rollback_strategy: kubectl rollout undo for API pods; ClickHouse vintage data immutable (INSERT-only); data_vintages status set to failed to hide incomplete ingest without deleting data; export GKE Jobs auto-deleted after 24 hours

ISS-001.title: ClickHouse table schema DDL not specified
ISS-001.detail: Previous plans described ClickHouse usage at abstract level without column type definitions, PARTITION BY and ORDER BY specifications, or index definitions, leaving query performance claims unverifiable and ingest tooling design impossible

ISS-002.title: Cloud Armor FIPS input validation rules not specified
ISS-002.detail: Security section mentioned WAF blocking malformed FIPS inputs but did not define specific validation rules for 11-digit tract vs 12-digit block-group FIPS formats

ISS-003.title: Bulk NDJSON export streaming design not specified
ISS-003.detail: Feature scope requires bulk export with first-byte response under 5 seconds but previous plans described only a synchronous endpoint without specifying async job or streaming pattern for 800K+ census tract row exports

focus_issues.title: ClickHouse table schema DDL not specified
focus_issues.detail: Column type definitions and index specifications absent from previous plans

focus_issues.title: Bulk NDJSON export streaming design not specified
focus_issues.detail: No async or streaming pattern defined for 800K+ row exports meeting 5-second first-byte constraint

latest_recommendations: Commit to wide-column MergeTree schema with full CREATE TABLE DDL including all demographic UInt32/Float32 columns, PARTITION BY vintage_year, ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year), and minmax skipping index on state prefix
Design POST /api/v1/exports async GKE Job pattern: create export_jobs record, launch Kubernetes Job streaming ClickHouse SELECT piped to GCS, return signed URL on completion
Specify ClickHouse max_execution_time per query to prevent runaway aggregations during bulk export
