issue_updates.detail: Endpoint still absent from API design

previous_plan.title: Census Demographic API - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go/Gin API on GKE Autopilot with ClickHouse for demographic queries and Redis L1 cache for common FIPS lookups. Cloud Armor WAF with FIPS validation rules added. API key tiering. Annual vintage ingest via Cloud Scheduler batch jobs. ISS-001 and ISS-003 unresolved.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go API pods on GKE Autopilot; ClickHouse on GKE StatefulSet; Redis L1 cache; GCS for export files; PostgreSQL for API key metadata. Cloud Armor WAF with FIPS regex on ingress.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: TLS 1.3; Cloud Armor FIPS regex validation (ISS-002 resolved in round 2); API key rate limiting by tier via Redis token bucket; minimal PII retention for contact email.
previous_plan.data_model: ClickHouse described abstractly without column definitions or DDL; census_tract_demographics and census_blockgroup_demographics mentioned without schema; data_vintages and api_keys in PostgreSQL. ISS-001 unresolved.

best_plan.title: Census Demographic API - Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: Go Gin API on GKE with ClickHouse MergeTree; Redis L1 cache; Cloud Armor WAF with FIPS validation. Schema DDL and bulk export design still unresolved.
best_plan.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot; ClickHouse StatefulSet; Redis; GCS; PostgreSQL for metadata. WAF on ingress with FIPS rules.
best_plan.data_model: Abstract ClickHouse description without column definitions, PARTITION BY, or ORDER BY specification. ISS-001 blocking.
best_plan.workflows: FIPS query served from Redis cache or ClickHouse fallthrough. Bulk export endpoint design absent. ISS-003 blocking.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 closes ISS-001 (ClickHouse schema DDL) and ISS-003 (bulk export async pattern). For ISS-001: provide full CREATE TABLE DDL for census_tract_demographics using wide-column MergeTree with explicit UInt32/Float32 typed columns, PARTITION BY vintage_year, ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year), and minmax skipping index on substring(fips_tract_code,1,2) for state-level range scans. For ISS-003: design POST /api/v1/exports async GKE Job pattern -- API creates export_jobs PostgreSQL record, launches Kubernetes Job that streams ClickHouse SELECT via clickhouse-client pipe to gzip NDJSON in GCS, updates job status to complete with signed URL; first GCS write byte within 2 seconds of ClickHouse query start satisfies the 5-second constraint.

fix_report.action_taken: Added full ClickHouse CREATE TABLE DDL for census_tract_demographics: wide-column MergeTree with all demographic fields as typed UInt32/Float32 columns, ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY vintage_year ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year), minmax skipping index on substring(fips_tract_code,1,2) for state-level geographic range queries; same schema pattern applied to census_blockgroup_demographics with FixedString(12) fips_bg_code
fix_report.expected_outcome: ClickHouse query optimizer satisfies FIPS point lookups via primary key index in O(log n) and state-level range scans via minmax skipping index; sub-200ms p99 for tract lookups validated by explicit schema design

fix_report.action_taken: Designed POST /api/v1/exports async GKE Job pattern: caller POSTs filter parameters (vintage_year, geography_level, fields[], optional state_fips), API creates export_jobs PostgreSQL record with status=pending, launches GKE Job manifest via Kubernetes Jobs API, Job container runs clickhouse-client streaming SELECT piped to gzip NDJSON written to GCS object, updates export_jobs status=complete with signed_url on finish; ClickHouse streaming SELECT begins writing first byte to GCS within 2 seconds of job start, well within 5-second constraint; caller polls GET /api/v1/exports/:id or registers webhook_url for completion callback
fix_report.expected_outcome: Full 800K census tract NDJSON export first GCS write within 5 seconds; signed URL returned to caller within 60-90 seconds for complete export; caller begins streaming download immediately on completion without blocking API pods

target_output.title: US Census Demographic Data API
target_output.executive_summary: A high-throughput Go/Gin API on GKE Autopilot serving aggregated US Census Bureau population estimates, housing counts, and demographic breakdowns at census tract and block-group level. ClickHouse MergeTree tables partitioned by vintage year with explicit wide-column DDL deliver sub-200ms FIPS tract lookups via primary key index; Redis L1 cache absorbs repeated common-geography queries. Annual Census Bureau FTP releases are ingested via GKE batch jobs transforming raw CSVs into typed ClickHouse INSERT batches. Bulk NDJSON export for research institutions uses an async GKE Job pattern streaming ClickHouse SELECT output to GCS signed URLs with first-byte delivery under 5 seconds. API key tiering (free/academic/partner) governs rate limits via Redis token buckets. Cloud Armor WAF validates FIPS code format.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 running Go API pods behind a Cloud Armor-protected GKE Ingress with TLS 1.3 termination. Each API request is validated for API key and rate limit via Redis token bucket, checked against Redis L1 query result cache (1-hour TTL for FIPS point lookups), and falls through to ClickHouse for cache misses. ClickHouse runs as a StatefulSet on a GKE n2-highmem-8 persistent SSD node; census_tract_demographics uses wide-column MergeTree PARTITION BY vintage_year ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year) enabling O(log n) primary key seeks and minmax skipping index on state FIPS prefix for geographic range scans. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL stores API key metadata and export job state. Bulk NDJSON export requests launch asynchronous GKE Jobs that stream ClickHouse SELECT output directly to GCS, decoupling export throughput from API pod memory. Annual Census Bureau vintage ingestion is driven by Cloud Scheduler triggering GKE ingest Jobs.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin framework, go-clickhouse v2 driver, go-redis v9, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL. Databases: ClickHouse self-managed GKE StatefulSet (n2-highmem-8, 500GB SSD PVC); PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL for API key and job metadata; Redis 7 GKE StatefulSet for L1 cache and rate limiting. Storage: GCS buckets for CSV staging, NDJSON exports, and access log archive. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud Armor WAF, Cloud Build, Cloud Scheduler, GCP Secret Manager, Prometheus + Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: FIPS Tract Query: GET /api/v1/tracts/:fips with vintage_year and fields params; Redis cache check then ClickHouse SELECT on census_tract_demographics primary key. Block-Group Query: GET /api/v1/blockgroups/:fips; same cache and ClickHouse pattern with census_blockgroup_demographics. State-Level Listing: GET /api/v1/tracts?state_fips=36 paginated scan using minmax skipping index. Bulk NDJSON Export: POST /api/v1/exports creates async GKE Job streaming ClickHouse to GCS; caller polls or uses webhook for signed URL. Vintage Availability: GET /api/v1/vintages lists available years with row counts from data_vintages. API Key Management: register, query usage, revoke. Annual Ingest Job: Cloud Scheduler triggers GKE Job downloading Census FTP CSVs, transforming to ClickHouse INSERT batches, updating data_vintages.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Armor WAF (FIPS regex validation: deny non-numeric fips_code params, deny length > 12, 1000 req/min per IP cap); GKE Ingress (TLS 1.3, routes to API service ClusterIP); Go API Service (GKE Autopilot 3-20 pods HPA on CPU 60%); ClickHouse StatefulSet (n2-highmem-8, 500GB SSD, census_tract and blockgroup tables); Redis StatefulSet (L1 query cache and rate limit token buckets); PostgreSQL Cloud SQL (api_keys, export_jobs, data_vintages); GCS buckets (census-raw-staging, census-exports, census-access-logs); GKE Export Jobs (on-demand, auto-deleted 24h); Cloud Scheduler + GKE Ingest Jobs (annual vintage trigger); Prometheus + Grafana (GKE Autopilot managed).
target_output.workflows: FIPS Tract Query: consumer sends GET /api/v1/tracts/36061019800?vintage_year=2022&fields=population,housing_units. Go handler extracts X-Api-Key header, computes SHA-256 hash, looks up api_key_meta in Redis (5-min TTL), increments daily INCR counter. Checks Redis L1 cache key census:tract:36061019800:2022:population,housing_units with 1-hour TTL. Cache hit returns JSON in under 5ms. Cache miss executes ClickHouse: SELECT fips_tract_code, vintage_year, total_population, housing_units FROM census_tract_demographics WHERE fips_tract_code='36061019800' AND vintage_year=2022; query uses primary key ORDER BY for O(log n) seek; result cached in Redis; response returned. Bulk Export Job: researcher POSTs to /api/v1/exports with body {vintage_year:2022, geography_level:'tract', state_fips:'36', fields:['population','housing_units','race_breakdown'], webhook_url:'https://example.edu/callback'}. API creates export_jobs record status=pending, launches GKE Job: clickhouse-client --query 'SELECT fips_tract_code,total_population,housing_units,white_alone,black_alone,hispanic_any_race FROM census_tract_demographics WHERE vintage_year=2022 AND substring(fips_tract_code,1,2)=36 FORMAT JSONEachRow' | gzip | gsutil cp - gs://census-exports/job-uuid.ndjson.gz. ClickHouse begins streaming first bytes to GCS within 2 seconds. Job updates export_jobs status=complete with GCS signed URL. Webhook POST sent with signed_url field. Annual Vintage Ingest: Cloud Scheduler fires POST /internal/ingest/trigger?vintage=2023. API launches GKE Job: downloads Census FTP CSV files to GCS census-raw-staging, validates header schema and row count against prior vintage, runs Go transform binary, executes ClickHouse INSERT batches (100K rows/batch) into census_tract_demographics, updates data_vintages status=complete. Full 800K tract row ingest completes in approximately 45 minutes.
target_output.data_model: census_tract_demographics: CREATE TABLE census_tract_demographics (fips_tract_code FixedString(11), vintage_year UInt16, total_population UInt32, housing_units UInt32, occupied_units UInt32, white_alone UInt32, black_alone UInt32, hispanic_any_race UInt32, asian_alone UInt32, two_or_more_races UInt32, median_household_income Float32, poverty_rate Float32, ingested_at DateTime DEFAULT now()) ENGINE=MergeTree() PARTITION BY vintage_year ORDER BY (fips_tract_code, vintage_year) SETTINGS index_granularity=8192; INDEX idx_state (substring(fips_tract_code,1,2)) TYPE minmax GRANULARITY 4. census_blockgroup_demographics: same schema with fips_bg_code FixedString(12) and 12-character FIPS prefix index. geographic_boundaries: (fips_code FixedString(12), geography_level Enum8('tract'=1,'blockgroup'=2), state_fips FixedString(2), county_fips FixedString(5), centroid_lat Float64, centroid_lng Float64) ENGINE=MergeTree() ORDER BY fips_code. api_keys (PostgreSQL): id UUID PK, key_hash CHAR(64) UNIQUE, tier ENUM free/academic/partner, contact_email VARCHAR, daily_limit INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. export_jobs (PostgreSQL): id UUID PK, api_key_id FK, status ENUM pending/running/complete/failed, filter_params JSONB, gcs_object_key VARCHAR, signed_url VARCHAR, row_count BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable. data_vintages (PostgreSQL): vintage_year SMALLINT, geography_level ENUM tract/blockgroup, row_count BIGINT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/complete/failed.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints under /api/v1. GET /api/v1/tracts/:fips_code?vintage_year=2022&fields=population,housing_units (X-Api-Key header, rate-limited per tier, Redis cache, ClickHouse fallthrough; returns JSON array with typed demographic fields). GET /api/v1/blockgroups/:fips_code?vintage_year=2022 (same pattern, 12-digit FIPS validated by Cloud Armor). GET /api/v1/tracts?state_fips=36&vintage_year=2022&limit=1000&offset=0 (paginated state-level listing using minmax skipping index). POST /api/v1/exports (body: vintage_year, geography_level, state_fips optional, fields[], webhook_url optional; returns 202 with export_job_id immediately). GET /api/v1/exports/:id (poll for status: pending/running/complete/failed; returns signed_url when complete). GET /api/v1/vintages (unauthenticated, lists available vintage years with row counts and geography levels from data_vintages). POST /api/v1/keys (register API key, contact_email required, returns key value once). DELETE /api/v1/keys/:id (revoke key). GET /api/v1/keys/:id/usage (daily request count for current day by tier). POST /internal/ingest/trigger (Cloud Scheduler only, restricted by Kubernetes NetworkPolicy to internal cluster traffic).
target_output.security_and_compliance: TLS 1.3 via GKE Ingress with Google-managed certificate. Cloud Armor WAF rules: DENY fips_code params matching regex [^0-9]; DENY fips_code length > 12; DENY POST body > 2KB on query endpoints; rate limit 1000 req/min per IP at WAF layer. API key validation: Go middleware computes SHA-256 of X-Api-Key header value, looks up in PostgreSQL (cached in Redis api_key_meta 5-min TTL), checks revoked_at, validates tier daily limit via Redis INCR counter with EXPIREAT midnight UTC. ClickHouse max_execution_time=5 seconds per query prevents runaway aggregations during bulk export. Open Government Data Act: all census aggregate data is public domain; no export controls. Title 13 USC compliance: only aggregated table-level data ingested; raw microdata never processed. PII: api_key contact_email only PII; retained until key deletion per privacy_retention_policy; GCS access logs contain api_key_id not email.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot us-central1: API Deployment (3 baseline replicas, HPA max 20 on CPU 60%); ClickHouse StatefulSet (1 pod, n2-highmem-8, 500GB SSD PVC; not auto-scaled; vertical resize for capacity increases); Redis StatefulSet (1 pod, standard PVC); PostgreSQL Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 Multi-AZ. Cloud Build pipeline: go vet, golangci-lint, go test (unit tests for rate limiting and FIPS validation; integration tests against dockerized ClickHouse and Redis), docker build, Artifact Registry push, kubectl rollout update API Deployment. Annual vintage ingest: Cloud Scheduler CRON triggers POST /internal/ingest/trigger; GKE Job with 16-goroutine worker pool and 100K-row ClickHouse INSERT batches; estimated 45-minute completion for 800K tract rows. Export GKE Jobs: launched on-demand by API service via Kubernetes Jobs API; auto-deleted by TTL after 24 hours; Job failure sets export_jobs status=failed. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo for API; ClickHouse vintage data immutable; incomplete ingest hidden via data_vintages status=failed flag without deletion.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scraping Go /metrics on port 9090 per pod. Grafana dashboards: request_latency_p99_ms by geography_level (tract/blockgroup) and vintage_year; cache_hit_rate (Redis L1 hits / total requests, target above 80%); clickhouse_query_duration_ms p50 and p99 from go-clickhouse driver instrumentation; export_job_duration_seconds from job start to GCS complete status; api_request_rate by tier (free/academic/partner). Alerts: request_latency_p99_ms for tract queries exceeding 200ms pages on-call for ClickHouse tuning investigation; cache_hit_rate below 70% triggers Redis memory and eviction policy review; ClickHouse disk_usage above 80% triggers capacity planning; export_job_failure_rate above 5% in 1 hour pages on-call.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $5.2K/month at 5M daily requests: ClickHouse n2-highmem-8 reserved approximately $800/month, GKE Autopilot API pods approximately $600/month at 3-5 baseline tasks, PostgreSQL Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 approximately $200/month, Redis GKE StatefulSet approximately $150/month, GCS storage and egress approximately $300/month, Cloud Armor approximately $100/month, Cloud Build approximately $50/month. Total well under $8K/month constraint. Scaling path: ClickHouse vertical resize to n2-highmem-16 (approximately $1.5K/month) if p99 degrades above 200ms at 20M+ daily requests; Redis cluster mode when single-node memory exceeds 80%; GCS export signed URL delivery eliminates GKE egress cost for bulk downloads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): FIPS tract queries for 2020 and 2022 vintages covering population, housing, and race breakdown fields; Redis L1 cache with 1-hour TTL; API key registration with free and academic tiers; Cloud Armor WAF with FIPS numeric validation; annual ingest pipeline for first two vintages; GKE Autopilot deployment with Cloud Build CI; Prometheus and Grafana dashboards for latency and cache hit rate. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Block-group geography level with census_blockgroup_demographics table; paginated state-level listing endpoint using minmax skipping index; POST /api/v1/exports async GKE Job pattern with GCS signed URLs and webhook callbacks; partner tier with unlimited rate limit; remaining 2015-2019 vintage years ingested. Phase 3 (6 weeks): ACS 5-year estimates data source integration; geographic bounding box query using PostGIS sidecar; developer documentation site; Python and R SDK packages published to PyPI and CRAN.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with pinned go.sum checksums. golangci-lint with errcheck, staticcheck, and gosec enabled in Cloud Build. Unit tests for Redis token bucket rate limiting logic and Cloud Armor FIPS regex; integration tests run against dockerized ClickHouse and Redis in Cloud Build pipeline. ClickHouse schema changes via versioned SQL migration files committed to repository; no schema modifications without migration file and PR review. API versioning policy: /api/v1 endpoints frozen after schema freeze milestone; breaking changes require /api/v2 with 6-month overlap deprecation period communicated via developer mailing list. Ingest job idempotency enforced via unique constraint on data_vintages (vintage_year, geography_level); duplicate ingest attempts detected and skipped before INSERT begins.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse single-node StatefulSet is a single point of failure; pod restart causes 30-60 second query unavailability. Mitigation: Redis L1 cache absorbs most traffic during brief restarts; ReplicatedMergeTree upgrade path documented for production SLA upgrade. Risk: Census Bureau FTP file format changes break ingest transform on annual vintage release. Mitigation: ingest job validates CSV header schema and row count against prior vintage before committing; schema validation failure sets data_vintages status=failed and alerts on-call without corrupting existing data. Tradeoff: self-managed ClickHouse over Cloud SQL for ClickHouse saves approximately $2.5K/month at this data volume; accepted operational burden for small engineering team. Tradeoff: Redis L1 query result cache over ClickHouse materialized views chosen for simpler invalidation model on annual vintage additions; materialized views deferred to Phase 2 if cache miss rate remains above 20% for complex multi-field queries.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed self-managed ClickHouse over Cloud SQL for ClickHouse based on cost analysis showing $800 vs $3200/month at equivalent throughput. Selected async GKE Job export pattern over synchronous Gin streaming to avoid 30-second request timeout on 800K-row queries. Confirmed NDJSON over CSV for bulk export to preserve type safety for downstream R and Python consumers. API key registration confirmed as self-service with email verification only; no manual approval required for free or academic tiers.

metadata.notes: Round 3 Census demographic API closing ClickHouse schema DDL gap and async bulk NDJSON export design for 800K tract row delivery.

profile.domain: County Assessor Property Tax Assessment System

project_goal.value: Internal web application for county assessor office managing property tax assessment workflows: recording field inspections, calculating assessed values, processing owner appeals, and generating annual tax notices
project_goal.rationale: Replaces legacy spreadsheet-based assessment workflow with an integrated system supporting the full assessment lifecycle from field inspection through notice generation

target_users.value: County field assessors, assessment supervisors, appeals hearing staff, and assessor office administrators
target_users.rationale: Internal-only users with distinct workflow roles requiring RBAC access control

project_class.rationale: Browser-based internal tool with rich data entry forms, assessment calculation engine, and reporting dashboards

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, batch_jobs, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Requires RBAC admin panel, batch jobs for annual notice generation, analytics for assessment ratio studies, and the full frontend/backend/data stack

complexity_level.rationale: Moderate complexity: statutory assessment calculation formulas, appeal workflow state machine, batch notice PDF generation, GIS parcel data integration

risk_level.rationale: Handles property financial data and owner PII; errors in assessed values or notice generation could trigger legal appeals and county revenue impacts

data_sensitivity.rationale: Property assessment records contain owner financial information including assessed values, exemptions, and tax liability calculations

external_exposure.rationale: Internal tool for assessor office staff only; not accessible from public internet; property owners use a separate public portal

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO with RBAC; roles: field_assessor, supervisor, appeals_staff, admin; MFA required for admin and supervisor roles
access_model.rationale: County IT standards require Azure AD integration for all internal applications; MFA for roles with financial data write access

feature_scope.value: Parcel record management; field inspection data entry with photo upload; automated assessed value calculation using statutory formulas and comparable sales; supervisor review and approval workflow; property owner appeal filing and hearing management; annual tax notice batch generation and PDF export; assessment ratio study analytics; exemption application processing
feature_scope.rationale: Full assessment lifecycle from inspection through notice generation

mvp_scope.value: Parcel record management, field inspection entry, assessed value calculation engine with statutory formulas, supervisor approval workflow, basic appeal filing, batch notice PDF generation for annual tax cycle
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP covers the critical annual assessment cycle before the county tax roll deadline

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with OIDC; TLS 1.3 for all traffic; Azure App Service with VNet integration; field-level audit logging for all assessed value changes; database encryption at rest via Azure SQL TDE
security_baseline.rationale: County security policy for internal financial data systems

frontend_stack.value: Angular 17 with TypeScript; Angular Material UI components
frontend_stack.rationale: County IT has existing Angular expertise; Angular Material provides accessible form components suitable for complex data entry workflows

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2; JPA with Hibernate for ORM
backend_stack.rationale: County IT Java expertise; Spring Boot 3.2 with virtual threads provides efficient concurrent request handling for assessment calculation workloads

data_platform.value: Azure SQL (PostgreSQL 16 compatible via Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server); Redis 7 for calculation result cache
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL on Azure with managed HA; Redis caches expensive statutory formula calculation results for frequently queried parcels

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service (Standard S2 tier) with VNet integration; Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos and generated notice PDFs
hosting_target.rationale: County Azure agreement; App Service VNet integration enforces internal-only access; managed TLS and auto-scaling without AKS complexity

privacy_retention_policy.value: Parcel assessment records retained indefinitely per county records law; field inspection photos retained 7 years; appeal records retained 10 years; audit logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State property assessment records retention statute

future_scope.value: GIS integration for parcel boundary visualization; automated comparable sales analysis using MLS data feed; property owner self-service appeal portal; mobile app for field assessors
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 enhancements after core assessment workflow is stable

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing county parcel data export (CSV from legacy GIS system); annual tax roll deadline is non-negotiable; budget under $3K/month infrastructure; App Service tier no larger than S2
constraints.rationale: County IT budget constraints and statutory tax roll filing deadline

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor Application Insights for Spring Boot; structured logging via SLF4J/Logback JSON appender; Azure Monitor alerts for assessment calculation error rate and batch job failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring consistent with county IT tooling

execution_preference.value: Milestone-based delivery aligned to annual assessment calendar; MVP must be live before field inspection season; batch notice generation must be tested before tax roll deadline
execution_preference.rationale: Assessment office workflows are calendar-driven by statutory deadlines

compliance_context.value: State property tax code compliance (uniform assessment ratio requirements, equalization procedures); county public records access requirements for parcel data; audit trail requirements for all assessed value changes under state administrative code
compliance_context.rationale: Property tax assessment is a regulated function with statutory requirements for uniform treatment and audit documentation

project.goal: Internal property tax assessment management covering inspections, valuations, appeals, and annual notice generation
project.target_users: County assessor office staff with distinct RBAC roles

security.auth: Azure AD OIDC via Spring Security OAuth2
security.audit: Field-level audit log for assessed value changes

data.db: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server
data.storage: Azure Blob for inspection photos and PDF notices

devops.hosting: Azure App Service Standard S2 with VNet integration

constraints.legacy_import: CSV parcel data from existing GIS export

open_questions.calculation_engine: Statutory formula implementation requires county assessor office sign-off on formula logic before coding

confirmed_decisions.pdf_generation: Apache PDFBox for tax notice PDF generation

reasoner_reviews.product: The annual assessment calendar creates hard milestones that must drive the delivery schedule. The MVP scope covering the full inspection-to-notice cycle is appropriate; appeal portal and GIS visualization can wait for year 2. The assessed value calculation engine is the highest-risk component -- statutory formula errors could invalidate the entire tax roll and require costly re-assessment, so the calculation logic must be reviewed and signed off by the county assessor before implementation.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot 3.2 on Azure App Service S2 with VNet integration is a solid choice for this load profile: dozens of concurrent internal users with periodic batch spikes for annual notice generation. The key gap from round 1 is the appeal workflow state machine -- the plan described appeal filing and hearing management but did not define the state transitions and guard conditions for the appeal_status lifecycle, which drives which RBAC roles can take which actions at each stage.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD SSO with OIDC is correct for county IT environment. Field-level audit logging for assessed value changes is required by state administrative code and was specified in the contract. The round 1 plan mentioned audit logging but did not specify the audit log schema or what fields are captured per change event -- this must be explicit to satisfy state compliance review.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure App Service S2 with VNet integration costs approximately $150/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2-vCore approximately $200/month; Redis Cache C1 approximately $55/month. Well within $3K/month budget with room for Azure Blob storage and Application Insights. Batch notice PDF generation can run as a background Spring @Scheduled task within the App Service without requiring separate compute.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3.2 application with domain modules: parcels (parcel record CRUD and GIS CSV import), assessments (inspection entry, calculated value engine, supervisor approval), appeals (filing, hearing scheduling, board decision recording, billing system notification), notices (batch PDF generation via Apache PDFBox), admin (user management, role assignment)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0; Spring Security OAuth2 JWT validation on all endpoints; role-based method security via @PreAuthorize annotations; OpenAPI spec generated at /v3/api-docs for Angular client code generation
backend.business_modules: AssessmentCalculationService: implements statutory uniform assessment ratio formulas; inputs property class code, land area, improvement value, comparable_sales_json; outputs calculated_assessed_value with formula_trace_json for audit. AppealWorkflowService: manages appeal_status state machine transitions with RBAC guards per state. BatchNoticeService: Spring @Scheduled task generating PDF tax notices for all active parcels in annual batch.
backend.failure_handling: Assessment calculation errors logged to assessment_calculation_errors table with full input snapshot; batch notice generation uses chunked processing (500 parcels/chunk) with per-chunk error recovery; appeal state transition violations return 422 with current state and allowed transitions in response body

frontend.app_structure: Angular 17 standalone components with lazy-loaded feature modules: parcel-management, inspection-entry, assessment-review, appeals-workflow, notice-management, admin. NgRx store for assessment workflow state across multi-step inspection and appeal forms.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Parcel Search: search by parcel ID or owner name, view parcel summary with assessment history. Inspection Entry: multi-step form for field assessor to record property condition, room counts, construction quality, and upload inspection photos. Assessment Dashboard: supervisor view of pending approvals with calculated value, prior year comparison, and formula trace. Appeal Filing: appeals_staff enters owner appeal details, copies, and comparable sales evidence. Hearing Calendar: schedule and record board hearing outcomes. Notice Batch: admin triggers annual batch with status progress and error report.
frontend.state_management: NgRx store: parcel feature state (selected parcel, assessment history), inspection form state (multi-step draft persistence), appeal workflow state (current status, available transitions by role), batch job state (progress tracking via SSE).
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA for all Angular Material form components; aria-describedby on validation error messages; keyboard navigation through multi-step inspection form; assessor office staff may use assistive technologies

security.auth_design: Azure AD OIDC via Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server; Angular MSAL library for token acquisition; tokens validated on every API request; 8-hour session lifetime with refresh token rotation
security.authorization_model: field_assessor: create and edit own inspection records, view own assigned parcel list; supervisor: review and approve all assessments in assigned district, view all parcels; appeals_staff: create appeals, manage hearing records, record board decisions; admin: manage users, roles, batch job triggers, import parcel CSV
security.audit_and_logging_controls: assessment_audit_log table capturing every assessed value change: parcel_id, changed_by_user_id, old_assessed_value, new_assessed_value, change_reason, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, formula_trace_json; immutable once written (INSERT only, no UPDATE or DELETE); exported to Azure Blob annually for long-term retention
security.security_risks: Appeal decision recording errors could affect owner tax liability; supervisor approval required before any assessed value is finalized; all value changes logged to immutable audit table

data.entities: parcel (id UUID PK, parcel_id VARCHAR UNIQUE county APN, owner_name, owner_address, property_class_code VARCHAR, land_area_sqft INT, zoning_code, situs_address, imported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). assessment_record (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, inspection_id FK nullable, land_value DECIMAL(12,2), improvement_value DECIMAL(12,2), calculated_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2), approved_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, formula_trace_json JSONB, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/finalized, assessed_by FK, approved_by FK nullable, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). field_inspection (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, assessor_id FK, inspection_date DATE, property_condition ENUM excellent/good/fair/poor, room_count INT, construction_quality_grade CHAR(1), notes TEXT, comparable_sales_json JSONB, photo_blob_keys TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). appeal_filing (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, appellant_name, appellant_contact, appeal_grounds TEXT, evidence_blob_keys TEXT[], filed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM filed/scheduled/heard/decided/withdrawn, decision ENUM sustained/partially_sustained/denied nullable, decision_notes TEXT, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). valuation_adjustment (id UUID PK, assessment_record_id FK, adjustment_type ENUM comparable_sale/exemption/correction, amount DECIMAL(12,2), reason TEXT, applied_by FK, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ). exemption_application (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, exemption_type ENUM homestead/veteran/senior/agricultural, approved BOOL nullable, approved_by FK, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). assessment_audit_log (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, changed_by_user_id FK, old_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2), new_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2), change_reason VARCHAR, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, formula_trace_json JSONB).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server for all transactional data; Redis 7 for caching expensive comparable_sales calculation results per parcel (1-hour TTL, invalidated on new inspection or sale); Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos and generated tax notice PDFs with container-level access policies
data.retention_and_deletion: Parcel and assessment records retained indefinitely; inspection photos retained 7 years via Azure Blob lifecycle policy; appeal records retained 10 years; assessment_audit_log retained 5 years then archived to Azure Blob Cold tier; no hard deletes on financial records -- soft delete via is_active flag

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service Standard S2 (2 vCore, 3.5GB RAM) in Azure region matching county data center; VNet Integration enabling private access to PostgreSQL Flexible Server and Redis Cache; Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (General Purpose 2-vCore, HA standby); Redis Cache C1 Standard (1GB); Azure Blob Storage account with private endpoint
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build with unit tests (90% coverage gate for AssessmentCalculationService), integration tests against containerized PostgreSQL and Redis, JAR artifact upload, Azure App Service deploy via Azure Web App task. Angular build: ng build --configuration production, artifacts deployed to App Service /wwwroot. Feature branches deploy to staging slot with slot swap to production after QA sign-off.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor Application Insights SDK in Spring Boot (micrometer-azure-monitor exporter); custom metrics: assessment_calculation_duration_ms, appeal_state_transition_count by status, batch_notice_generation_progress; structured JSON logging via Logback with correlation IDs; Application Insights alerts: calculation_error_rate > 1% pages ops team; batch job failure sends email to assessor admin.
devops.cost_controls: App Service S2 approximately $150/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2-vCore approximately $200/month; Redis Cache C1 approximately $55/month; Azure Blob approximately $20/month; Application Insights approximately $30/month. Total approximately $500/month, well under $3K/month constraint.

ISS-001.title: Appeal workflow state machine transitions not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described appeal filing and hearing management features but did not specify the appeal_status state machine: which transitions are valid, what RBAC role is required to execute each transition, and what guard conditions (e.g., hearing must be scheduled before status can move to heard) prevent invalid state changes

ISS-002.title: Assessment audit log schema not specified
ISS-002.detail: State administrative code requires an immutable audit trail for all assessed value changes. Round 1 plan mentioned audit logging as a requirement but did not specify the audit log table schema, what fields are captured per event, or how immutability is enforced

focus_issues.title: Appeal workflow state machine transitions not defined
focus_issues.detail: State transitions, RBAC guards, and guard conditions for appeal_status lifecycle absent from round 1 plan

focus_issues.title: Assessment audit log schema not specified
focus_issues.detail: Audit log table schema and immutability enforcement not specified despite state administrative code requirement

latest_recommendations: Define the appeal_status state machine with explicit transitions: filed -> scheduled (appeals_staff), scheduled -> heard (appeals_staff with hearing_date set), heard -> decided (appeals_staff with decision field populated), decided -> finalized (admin); guard conditions and RBAC requirements per transition

Specify assessment_audit_log table schema with all required fields: parcel_id, changed_by_user_id, old_assessed_value, new_assessed_value, change_reason, changed_at, formula_trace_json; enforce immutability via INSERT-only with database-level DENY on UPDATE and DELETE
Add formula_trace_json to assessment_record to document which comparable sales and statutory adjustments produced the calculated value
