previous_plan.title: County Assessor Property Tax System - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Angular 17 and Spring Boot 3.2 internal web application on Azure App Service with VNet integration for county property tax assessment management. Parcel records, field inspection entry, assessed value calculation with statutory formulas, supervisor approval, appeal management, and annual batch notice generation.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Angular 17 SPA served from App Service communicating to Spring Boot REST API; PostgreSQL 16 for data; Redis for calculation cache; Azure Blob for photos and PDFs. VNet integration for internal-only access. Azure AD SSO via MSAL and Spring Security OAuth2.
previous_plan.workflows: Field assessor enters inspection data and submits for calculation; supervisor reviews and approves assessed value; appeals_staff files owner appeals and manages hearing records. Appeal status transitions not specified. Audit logging mentioned but schema absent.
previous_plan.data_model: parcel, assessment_record with formula_trace_json, field_inspection with comparable_sales_json, appeal_filing, valuation_adjustment, exemption_application entities present. assessment_audit_log mentioned but schema not defined.

best_plan.title: County Assessor Property Tax System - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: Angular 17 and Spring Boot 3.2 on Azure App Service VNet for county assessment management. Assessment calculation with statutory formulas; supervisor approval; appeal management; batch notice generation.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Angular SPA + Spring Boot REST API on App Service VNet; PostgreSQL 16; Redis cache; Azure Blob; Azure AD SSO.
best_plan.workflows: Inspection entry to calculation to supervisor approval flow present. Appeal state machine transitions absent. Audit log schema absent.
best_plan.data_model: Core assessment entities present. assessment_audit_log schema unspecified. formula_trace_json present on assessment_record.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (appeal state machine) and ISS-002 (audit log schema). For ISS-001: define five appeal_status transitions -- filed->scheduled (appeals_staff, guard: hearing_date must be set), scheduled->heard (appeals_staff, guard: hearing_date in past), heard->decided (appeals_staff, guard: decision field populated), decided->withdrawn (appeals_staff), decided->finalized (admin only). For ISS-002: provide assessment_audit_log table schema with all required fields and enforce immutability via PostgreSQL row security policy denying UPDATE and DELETE for all roles except a dedicated audit_writer role. Plan quality is moderate -- state machine and audit schema addressed but phased implementation section is slightly thinner than ideal.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined appeal_status state machine in AppealWorkflowService with five transitions: filed->scheduled (appeals_staff role, guard: hearing_date must be non-null in request), scheduled->heard (appeals_staff role, guard: hearing_date is in the past), heard->decided (appeals_staff role, guard: decision ENUM value and decision_notes must be present in request body), decided->withdrawn (appeals_staff role, no guard), decided->finalized (admin role only, triggers billing system notification); invalid transition attempts return 422 with current_status and allowed_transitions[] in response body; all transitions create assessment_audit_log entry
fix_report.expected_outcome: Appeals staff and admin can only move appeal records through valid state transitions with role enforcement; invalid transitions are blocked at the service layer with informative error responses; all transitions are audited

fix_report.action_taken: Specified assessment_audit_log table schema: id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, changed_by_user_id FK, change_type ENUM assessed_value_change/exemption_change/appeal_decision, old_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, new_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, change_reason VARCHAR, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), formula_trace_json JSONB nullable; immutability enforced via PostgreSQL row security policy: GRANT INSERT ON assessment_audit_log TO app_user; DENY UPDATE, DELETE ON assessment_audit_log TO ALL; annual archive job exports audit_log rows older than 5 years to Azure Blob Cold tier
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every assessed value change and appeal decision is recorded in an immutable audit trail satisfying state administrative code requirements; county can produce complete audit history for any parcel on demand for assessment challenge proceedings

target_output.title: County Assessor Property Tax Assessment System
target_output.executive_summary: An internal Angular 17 and Spring Boot 3.2 web application deployed on Azure App Service with VNet integration for the county assessor office. Manages the complete property tax assessment lifecycle: field inspectors record parcel condition and comparable sales data; the AssessmentCalculationService applies statutory uniform assessment ratio formulas producing a formula_trace_json for audit; supervisors review and approve assessed values; appeals_staff manages owner appeal filings through a defined five-stage state machine; admin triggers annual batch PDF tax notice generation via Apache PDFBox. All assessed value changes and appeal decisions are recorded in an immutable assessment_audit_log table satisfying state administrative code requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Angular 17 SPA served directly from Azure App Service alongside the Spring Boot REST API backend; MSAL Angular library handles Azure AD token acquisition and silent renewal. Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server validates JWT tokens on all API requests; @PreAuthorize annotations enforce RBAC at the method level. Spring Boot connects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server via VNet private endpoint; Redis Cache accessed via VNet for calculation result caching. Azure Blob Storage holds inspection photos and generated notice PDFs, accessed via SAS tokens generated by the backend. Azure App Service VNet Integration ensures all traffic stays within the county Azure VNet -- no public internet exposure. AssessmentCalculationService implements statutory formula logic reviewed and signed off by county assessor; AppealWorkflowService enforces the five-stage appeal_status state machine with RBAC guards per transition.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Angular 17 with TypeScript, Angular Material UI, NgRx for assessment workflow state, MSAL Angular for Azure AD token acquisition. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2 with virtual threads, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, JPA with Hibernate 6, Apache PDFBox 3.x for notice PDF generation, Spring @Scheduled for batch jobs. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with TDE encryption at rest, Redis Cache C1 Standard for calculation result caching. Storage: Azure Blob Storage for inspection photos and notice PDFs. Infrastructure: Azure App Service Standard S2, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Application Insights with Micrometer exporter.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Parcel Management: search and view parcel records by APN or owner name; import from county GIS CSV export; view assessment history by tax year. Inspection Entry: field_assessor creates field_inspection record with property condition, room count, construction quality grade, comparable_sales_json, and photo uploads; submits for assessment calculation. Assessment Calculation: AssessmentCalculationService computes calculated_assessed_value using property_class_code statutory formula with comparable sales adjustments; stores formula_trace_json; creates assessment_record status=draft. Supervisor Approval: supervisor reviews draft assessment with formula trace and prior year comparison; approves setting status=approved or returns with correction notes. Appeal Workflow: five-stage state machine (filed/scheduled/heard/decided/finalized) with RBAC-gated transitions. Batch Notice Generation: admin triggers Spring @Scheduled batch generating PDF tax notices for all finalized assessments in current tax_year; progress visible via SSE stream. Audit Log: immutable assessment_audit_log capturing all assessed value changes and appeal decisions.
target_output.system_components: Azure App Service Standard S2 (Angular SPA + Spring Boot REST API, VNet integration); Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (General Purpose 2-vCore, HA standby, private VNet endpoint, TDE encryption); Redis Cache C1 Standard (VNet-integrated, calculation result cache); Azure Blob Storage (inspection photos, notice PDFs, audit log archive, private endpoint); Azure AD (SSO, OIDC token issuance, MFA policy for supervisor/admin roles); Application Insights (Spring Boot Micrometer exporter, custom metrics); Azure DevOps Pipelines (Maven build, Angular build, App Service deploy).
target_output.workflows: Field Inspection and Assessment Calculation: field_assessor selects assigned parcel from dashboard, opens multi-step inspection form, enters property_condition, room_count, construction_quality_grade, uploads inspection photos to Azure Blob, enters comparable_sales_json with 3-5 recent sales in same property class. On submit, Spring Boot AssessmentCalculationService applies statutory formula: calculated_assessed_value = (land_area_sqft * land_value_per_sqft * property_class_factor) + (improvement_value * condition_adjustment) + valuation_adjustments; formula_trace_json records all inputs and intermediate steps; assessment_record created status=draft. assessment_audit_log INSERT records the draft value. Supervisor Review and Approval: supervisor opens pending_approval queue filtered by district, reviews assessment_record with formula_trace_json, compares to prior year value and comparable sales. Clicks approve: assessment_record status set to approved, approved_by and approved_at populated, assessment_audit_log INSERT records old and new approved value with supervisor user_id. Appeal State Machine: owner contacts appeals_staff to file appeal. appeals_staff creates appeal_filing status=filed with grounds and evidence files. Transition to scheduled: appeals_staff sets hearing_date, system validates hearing_date is non-null, status set to scheduled. Transition to heard: appeals_staff records hearing completion after hearing_date has passed, guard validates hearing_date <= current date. Transition to decided: appeals_staff records board decision (sustained/partially_sustained/denied) with decision_notes; if sustained, triggers valuation_adjustment creation and assessment_record recalculation. Transition to finalized: admin only, triggers billing system notification via POST to county billing API. assessment_audit_log INSERT records each transition. Annual Batch Notice Generation: admin clicks Generate Notices for tax_year=2025 on Notice Management page. Angular SSE stream connects to GET /api/v1/notices/batch/progress. Spring @Scheduled task processes active parcels in 500-parcel chunks: for each parcel, fetches finalized assessment_record, applies exemption_application deductions, calculates net tax liability, renders Apache PDFBox notice template, saves to Azure Blob at notices/2025/{parcel_id}.pdf. Progress events sent via SSE. On completion, admin downloads batch manifest CSV with parcel_id and blob_key per notice.
target_output.data_model: parcel (id UUID PK, parcel_id VARCHAR UNIQUE APN, owner_name VARCHAR, owner_address VARCHAR, property_class_code VARCHAR e.g. R1/R2/C1/I1, land_area_sqft INT, zoning_code VARCHAR, situs_address VARCHAR, imported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOL default true). assessment_record (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, inspection_id FK nullable, land_value DECIMAL(12,2), improvement_value DECIMAL(12,2), calculated_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2), approved_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, formula_trace_json JSONB, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/finalized, assessed_by UUID FK, approved_by UUID FK nullable, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). field_inspection (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, assessor_id FK, inspection_date DATE, property_condition ENUM excellent/good/fair/poor, room_count INT, construction_quality_grade CHAR(1) A-F, notes TEXT, comparable_sales_json JSONB, photo_blob_keys TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). appeal_filing (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, appellant_name VARCHAR, appellant_contact VARCHAR, appeal_grounds TEXT, evidence_blob_keys TEXT[], filed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM filed/scheduled/heard/decided/withdrawn/finalized, hearing_date TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, decision ENUM sustained/partially_sustained/denied nullable, decision_notes TEXT, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). valuation_adjustment (id UUID PK, assessment_record_id FK, adjustment_type ENUM comparable_sale/exemption/correction, amount DECIMAL(12,2), reason TEXT, applied_by FK, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ). exemption_application (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year SMALLINT, exemption_type ENUM homestead/veteran/senior/agricultural, approved BOOL nullable, approved_by FK nullable, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). assessment_audit_log (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, changed_by_user_id FK, change_type ENUM assessed_value_change/exemption_change/appeal_decision/appeal_transition, old_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, new_assessed_value DECIMAL(12,2) nullable, change_reason VARCHAR, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), formula_trace_json JSONB nullable) -- INSERT-only enforced via PostgreSQL row security policy denying UPDATE and DELETE for app_user role. Property tax financial entities: assessment_accounts (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year INT, assessed_value_cents BIGINT, taxable_value_cents BIGINT, exemption_amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), assessment_method ENUM mass/individual), tax_invoices (id UUID PK, parcel_id FK, tax_year INT, invoice_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, gross_tax_cents BIGINT, penalties_cents BIGINT, total_due_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), due_date DATE, status ENUM issued/partial/paid/delinquent), payment_transactions (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, payment_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), method ENUM check/ach/card/wire, balance_after_cents BIGINT, posted_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0; Azure AD JWT validated by Spring Security on all endpoints. GET /api/v1/parcels?q=:search (search by APN or owner name, field_assessor+ role). GET /api/v1/parcels/:id/assessments?tax_year=2025 (assessment history, all roles). POST /api/v1/inspections (create field_inspection, field_assessor role). POST /api/v1/assessments (trigger calculation from inspection_id, field_assessor role). GET /api/v1/assessments/pending-approval (supervisor queue for current district). PATCH /api/v1/assessments/:id/approve (supervisor role, sets status=approved). POST /api/v1/appeals (create appeal_filing, appeals_staff role). PATCH /api/v1/appeals/:id/transition (body: {to_status, hearing_date?, decision?, decision_notes?}; AppealWorkflowService validates RBAC and guard conditions; returns 422 with current_status and allowed_transitions[] on invalid transition). GET /api/v1/notices/batch/progress (SSE stream, admin role, emits progress events during batch generation). POST /api/v1/notices/batch/trigger (admin role, triggers annual batch). GET /api/v1/audit-log?parcel_id=:id (query audit log for parcel, supervisor+ role). POST /api/v1/admin/parcels/import (admin role, accepts CSV parcel data upload from GIS export).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Azure AD OIDC via Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server; MSAL Angular for SPA token acquisition; 8-hour token expiry with refresh rotation; MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access policy for supervisor and admin roles. Authorization: RBAC via @PreAuthorize annotations using Spring Security authorities mapped from Azure AD group membership: field_assessor, supervisor, appeals_staff, admin. Appeal state machine RBAC guards enforced in AppealWorkflowService with invalid transition 422 response. Audit: assessment_audit_log INSERT-only via PostgreSQL row security policy (GRANT INSERT TO app_user; DENY UPDATE, DELETE); captures all assessed value changes, appeal transitions, and exemption changes; annual archive to Azure Blob Cold tier after 5 years. Data encryption: PostgreSQL TDE at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit; Azure Blob encrypted at rest; Redis in-transit TLS. Network: App Service VNet Integration; PostgreSQL and Redis on private VNet endpoints, no public internet exposure. Compliance: state property tax code audit trail requirement satisfied by immutable assessment_audit_log; uniform assessment ratio documentation preserved in formula_trace_json.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure App Service Standard S2 in county Azure subscription with VNet Integration; auto-scaling rule: add instance when CPU > 70% sustained 10 minutes, max 3 instances. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server General Purpose 2-vCore with HA standby in same Azure region; private endpoint in county VNet; TDE enabled. Redis Cache C1 Standard with VNet-injected instance. Azure Blob Storage account with private endpoint for inspection photos, notice PDFs, and audit archive. Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build (mvn verify with 90% coverage gate on AssessmentCalculationService unit tests), Angular build (ng build --configuration production), artifact publish, Azure Web App Deploy task for App Service. Feature branches deploy to App Service staging slot; QA approval triggers slot swap to production. Database migrations via Flyway on Spring Boot startup. Pre-production notice generation test: admin runs batch in staging against prior year data to validate PDF output before annual production run.
target_output.observability: Application Insights SDK via Spring Boot Micrometer Azure Monitor exporter. Custom metrics: assessment_calculation_duration_ms (histogram by property_class_code), appeal_transition_count (counter by from_status and to_status), batch_notice_progress_parcels_processed (gauge during batch), audit_log_insert_count. Log structured JSON via Logback with correlation IDs for cross-request tracing. Application Insights alerts: assessment_calculation_error_rate > 0.5% emails ops team; batch notice job failure sends alert to assessor admin email; appeal_transition 422 error rate > 5% in 1 hour flags potential RBAC misconfiguration. Angular performance: Application Insights JavaScript SDK tracking page load times for inspection form and assessment dashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $500-600/month: App Service S2 approximately $150/month, PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2-vCore approximately $200/month, Redis Cache C1 approximately $55/month, Azure Blob approximately $20/month for photos and PDFs, Application Insights approximately $30-50/month at county usage volume. Well under $3K/month constraint. Peak load during annual notice generation batch is handled within App Service S2 memory limits via 500-parcel chunked processing; auto-scaling to 3 instances covers concurrent user load during assessment approval season.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Parcel management with GIS CSV import, field inspection entry with Azure Blob photo upload, AssessmentCalculationService with statutory formula implementation and formula_trace_json, supervisor approval workflow, assessment_audit_log with INSERT-only immutability, basic appeal filing (status=filed), annual batch notice PDF generation with PDFBox, Azure AD SSO with RBAC, App Service VNet deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Full appeal state machine with all five transitions and RBAC guards, hearing calendar scheduling, appeal decision recording with billing system notification, exemption application processing, assessment ratio study analytics dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): GIS parcel boundary map integration, comparable sales automated lookup from county MLS feed, assessment history timeline visualization, notice PDF reprint workflow for lost notices.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2; Maven with enforced dependency versions. JUnit 5 + Mockito for AssessmentCalculationService unit tests; 90% line coverage required for calculation and appeal workflow service classes. Integration tests using Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis in Azure DevOps pipeline. Flyway for all database schema changes; migration files committed to repository and reviewed before merge. Angular strict mode TypeScript; eslint with @angular-eslint rules. Appeal state machine transition logic tested with explicit unit tests per transition including valid and invalid transition attempts. Audit log immutability validated in integration tests by verifying UPDATE and DELETE operations raise PostgreSQL permission errors. ADR documents required for statutory formula logic decisions reviewed by county assessor office.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: statutory assessment formula errors invalidate the tax roll requiring re-assessment of potentially thousands of parcels. Mitigation: AssessmentCalculationService formulas require written sign-off from county assessor before implementation; formula_trace_json enables post-hoc audit of every calculation; 90% unit test coverage gate for calculation service. Risk: annual batch notice generation fails mid-run near tax roll deadline. Mitigation: chunked processing (500 parcels/chunk) with per-chunk error recovery; staging pre-run with prior year data validates PDF generation before production run. Tradeoff: monolithic Spring Boot application over microservices -- accepted for moderate complexity at internal-user scale; domain module boundaries (parcels, assessments, appeals, notices) enable future extraction if assessment calculation service needs independent scaling. Tradeoff: Apache PDFBox over a PDF-as-a-service vendor -- no per-document API cost at annual batch scale; county data stays within VNet without third-party data processing.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Apache PDFBox over iText for PDF generation to avoid AGPL licensing cost for government use. Selected Flyway over Liquibase for database migrations based on team familiarity with SQL migration files. Resolved comparable sales input as JSON blob in inspection record for MVP; structured comparable_sales table deferred to Phase 2 MLS integration. Assessment formula reviewed and signed off by county assessor office before implementation begins.

metadata.notes: Round 2 county assessor system resolving appeal state machine and immutable audit log schema for statutory compliance; phased implementation slightly thinner than strong quality.

profile.domain: Municipal Government - Township Public Information Website

project_goal.value: Migrate township public information website off legacy CMS to a statically generated Hugo site publishing meeting agendas, ordinances, permit application forms, and department contact directories.
project_goal.rationale: Legacy CMS license expired; Hugo eliminates hosting complexity and reduces ongoing maintenance burden.

target_users.value: Township residents seeking permit forms, meeting schedules, and department contacts; township staff updating content via Git commits.
target_users.rationale: Primary audiences identified in stakeholder discovery sessions.

project_class.rationale: No server-side business logic required; all content is publicly readable and statically served.

capabilities.rationale: Static rendering with GA4 for visitor analytics; no auth, backend, or database required.

complexity_level.rationale: Content-only site with no interactive features beyond PDF downloads and external form links.

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected, no authentication, no financial transactions.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Site publishes only public government records with no personal or sensitive data stored.

external_exposure.rationale: Township website must be publicly accessible without authentication.

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication. Content authoring via Git commit access restricted to township staff.
access_model.rationale: Public information mandate; staff editing controlled via GitHub repo permissions.

feature_scope.value: Meeting agendas with archive, ordinance search by number and keyword, downloadable permit application PDFs, department contact pages, public notices board.
feature_scope.rationale: Covers all content categories currently on legacy CMS.

mvp_scope.value: Department contact pages, downloadable permit forms, meeting agenda archive for current year.
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable to decommission legacy CMS on deadline.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via CDN. Security headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy. No cookies set by the site itself.
security_baseline.rationale: Standard secure-by-default posture for a public-facing government site.

frontend_stack.value: Hugo with HTML5/CSS3 and vanilla JavaScript for agenda filtering and contact accordions.
frontend_stack.rationale: Hugo builds thousands of pages in under a second; zero runtime dependency; staff familiar with Markdown authoring.

backend_stack.value: None. Static file delivery only.
backend_stack.rationale: Static site requires no backend runtime.

data_platform.value: None. Content stored in Git repository as Markdown and PDF files.
data_platform.rationale: Git is the source of truth; no database needed for static content.

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean Spaces CDN with custom domain and SSL termination.
hosting_target.rationale: Low-cost object storage CDN sufficient for static asset delivery at township traffic volumes.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected or stored. GA4 anonymized analytics with IP anonymization enabled and 14-month data retention.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State open records law and privacy-by-design principle.

future_scope.value: Online permit application submission via embedded third-party form service; multilingual content via Hugo i18n.
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap pending budget approval.

constraints.value: Monthly hosting budget under $20. Content updates must be publishable by non-technical township staff within 10 minutes.
constraints.rationale: Small township operational constraints.

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for page views and document download events. GitHub Actions build status notifications to township IT email.
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static site with no server-side SLAs.

execution_preference.value: Single migration sprint with staff training session; then self-service Git authoring.
execution_preference.rationale: Township has no ongoing engineering staff; must be maintainable by non-developers after handoff.

project.goal: Migrate off legacy CMS to Hugo static site covering all public information categories
project.target_users: Township residents and municipal staff

frontend.templating: Go HTML templates with Markdown content
frontend.styling: Custom CSS with township branding; no CSS framework
frontend.interactivity: Vanilla JS for agenda date filtering and department contact accordion

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options via CDN response headers
security.transport: HTTPS enforced; HTTP redirects to HTTPS at CDN edge

data.content_store: Git repository (GitHub) as single source of truth
data.assets: PDF permit forms stored in /static/forms/, served directly by CDN

devops.ci: GitHub Actions workflow triggering Hugo build on push to main
devops.hosting: DigitalOcean Spaces with custom domain DNS
devops.environments: main branch deploys to production; PRs generate preview deployments

constraints.authoring: Staff edit Markdown in GitHub web interface

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is tightly bounded and appropriate for a township migration project. Meeting agendas, ordinances, and permit forms cover the full content inventory from the legacy CMS. The Git-based authoring workflow is the right tradeoff given the absence of ongoing engineering support.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Hugo is the correct choice here: sub-second build times handle the full content corpus, and the zero-runtime deployment eliminates server maintenance entirely. The only architectural risk is PDF asset management growing unwieldy without a naming convention; recommend establishing a directory structure and file naming standard in the content style guide.
reasoner_reviews.security: No server-side attack surface is the optimal security posture for a public information site. CSP headers and disabling third-party scripts except GA4 should be validated with a header scanner post-deploy. Section 508 compliance requires alt text on all images and accessible navigation landmarks.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DigitalOcean Spaces at ~$5/month bandwidth plus ~$12/year domain registration comfortably fits the $20/month budget. The 10-minute authoring target is achievable via GitHub web UI for Markdown edits.

frontend.app_structure: Hugo site with content types: agendas (list + single), ordinances (list + single), departments (list + single), permits (list page with PDF links), notices (list + single). Taxonomy: department tag shared across content types for cross-linking.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage with emergency notice banner slot, Departments index with contact cards, Permits page with filterable form list by category (residential/commercial/zoning), Agendas archive with year/month filter, Ordinances search by number and keyword, Public Notices board sorted by post date.
frontend.performance_notes: Hugo minifies HTML and CSS at build time. Images resized to responsive srcsets via Hugo image processing pipeline. Target Lighthouse performance score 95+ on all pages.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: semantic HTML5 landmark elements, skip-nav link, alt text required field in front matter schema, keyboard-navigable accordion for department contacts.

security.auth_design: No authentication layer. GitHub repository access controls enforce who can commit content updates.
security.abuse_prevention: CDN-level rate limiting on DigitalOcean Spaces edge. No server-side endpoints to abuse. GA4 script loaded from official CDN only.
security.privacy_controls: GA4 configured with anonymize_ip: true, ads_data_redaction: true. No cookies beyond GA4 measurement cookie.

data.entities: Content in Git: agendas (date, title, department, body Markdown, pdf_path), ordinances (number, title, adopted_date, pdf_path), departments (name, phone, email, hours, address), permit_forms (name, category, revision_date, pdf_path), public_notices (title, effective_date, expiry_date, body).
data.retention_and_deletion: Historical agendas and ordinances retained indefinitely in Git history per state open meetings law. Outdated permit form PDFs archived to /archive/ subdirectory and removed from active permits list page.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repo -> GitHub Actions (hugo build --minify) -> DigitalOcean Spaces bucket (s3cmd sync) -> CDN edge. Custom domain with DigitalOcean DNS CNAME to Spaces CDN endpoint.
devops.ci_cd_design: On push to main: checkout, install Hugo binary (pinned version), run hugo --minify, sync output/ to Spaces bucket with --delete flag, post build status to township IT email via SendGrid action.
devops.rollback_strategy: Revert commit and re-trigger pipeline restores prior version within 3 minutes. CDN cache purged automatically on deploy via Spaces API call in pipeline.
devops.cost_controls: DigitalOcean Spaces: $5/month for 250 GB storage/1 TB outbound. Domain: $12/year. GitHub Actions: free tier. Total: ~$6/month.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a CMS migration to Hugo static site with no server-side components. Key decisions: Hugo content types map directly to the legacy CMS content categories, Git is the authoring system to eliminate CMS licensing, and DigitalOcean Spaces CDN delivers everything under $20/month. Section 508 compliance and CSP headers are the primary non-trivial requirements.
target_output.title: Township Public Information Static Site - Hugo Migration Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Hugo-generated static site replacing the legacy CMS for a small township, delivering meeting agendas, ordinances, permit application PDFs, and department contacts via DigitalOcean Spaces CDN. Staff author content as Markdown files in a GitHub repository; GitHub Actions builds and deploys on every merge to main. No backend, no database, and no authentication layer required.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture with no server-side runtime. Content is authored as Markdown in a GitHub repository using Hugo front matter conventions. On every push to main, a GitHub Actions workflow invokes hugo --minify to build the full site into a static output directory, then syncs the output to a DigitalOcean Spaces bucket fronted by the CDN. The CDN terminates TLS, enforces HTTPS redirects, and delivers security response headers. GA4 is the sole third-party script, loaded asynchronously. The entire system has no server-side attack surface beyond the CDN edge itself.
target_output.technology_stack: Static Generator: Hugo (latest LTS, version pinned in CI). Templating: Go HTML templates with Markdown via Goldmark renderer. Styling: Hand-authored CSS3 with CSS custom properties for township brand colors; no CSS framework to minimize build dependencies. JavaScript: Vanilla ES6 for agenda year/month filter, ordinance lunr.js search, and department contact accordion. Hosting: DigitalOcean Spaces CDN. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization. DNS: DigitalOcean DNS.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Agendas Module: archive by year and month, PDF attachment link per agenda, department tag filter. Ordinances Module: client-side keyword and number search via lunr.js index built at Hugo build time. Permits Module: filterable list by category (residential, commercial, zoning, special use) linking to PDF downloads in /static/forms/. Departments Module: contact cards with phone, email, hours, and address in accessible accordion layout. Public Notices Board: sorted by effective date with automatic expiry hiding via Hugo build date comparison against expiry_date front matter field.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Repository (Markdown content and Hugo templates), Hugo Build Engine (static generation, image processing, lunr.js index generation at build time), GitHub Actions Runner (CI pipeline executing build and Spaces sync), DigitalOcean Spaces Bucket (static asset storage), Spaces CDN Edge (HTTPS termination, security headers, global delivery), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized visitor measurement). No databases, no application servers, no session layers.
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Township staff edits a Markdown file via GitHub web interface, commits to main, GitHub Actions detects push, runs hugo --minify in under 60 seconds, syncs changed files to Spaces bucket with --delete flag, CDN cache invalidation completes within 2 minutes and updated page goes live. Permit Form Replacement Workflow: Staff drops replacement PDF into /static/forms/residential/ using standardized filename (permit-type-YYYY-MM.pdf), updates corresponding Markdown front matter with new revision_date, commits to main, pipeline deploys; prior PDF version retained in Git history for records compliance but removed from active listing page.
target_output.data_model: Content stored as files in Git, not in a relational database. meeting_agendas/YYYY/MM/YYYY-MM-DD-title.md (front matter: date, title, department, pdf_path, body). ordinances/ORD-YYYY-NNN.md (front matter: number, title, adopted_date, pdf_path). departments/slug.md (front matter: name, phone, email, hours, address, head_name). permit_forms/slug.md (front matter: name, category ENUM residential/commercial/zoning/special_use, revision_date, pdf_path, description). public_notices/YYYY-slug.md (front matter: title, effective_date, expiry_date, body).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Client-side data interactions only: lunr.js search index loaded from /search-index.json generated at Hugo build time by iterating ordinances content (~50 KB gzipped for typical corpus of 300 ordinances). GA4 gtag.js pageview events and custom file_download events capturing permit form PDF filename. No form submission endpoints; permit inquiries link to township staff email addresses. Alert sign-up links out to county emergency notification system external URL.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at Spaces CDN edge with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect. Response headers: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'), X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. No session cookies set by the site. Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA target enforced via axe-core automated scan in GitHub Actions CI pipeline. State open meetings law posting deadline compliance enforced by staff process, not technically constrained.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline steps: (1) actions/checkout, (2) peaceiris/actions-hugo with pinned Hugo version v0.128.x, (3) hugo --minify --gc, (4) configure s3cmd with Spaces credentials from GitHub Secrets, (5) s3cmd sync public/ s3://township-site-prod/ --delete --acl-public --mime-type auto, (6) POST Spaces CDN cache purge API, (7) send build result notification to township IT email alias via SendGrid action. Single production environment from main branch. PRs generate Netlify Deploy Previews for staff review. Rollback: git revert offending commit, push to main, pipeline re-deploys in under 3 minutes.
target_output.observability: GA4 dashboard tracking weekly unique visitors by page, permit PDF download event counts by form name, agenda page views by meeting date. GitHub Actions email alert on build failure to township IT alias. UptimeRobot free-tier monitor pinging homepage every 5 minutes with email alert on downtime. DigitalOcean Spaces dashboard provides monthly bandwidth and request count summaries. No APM or error tracking required for a statically served site.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: DigitalOcean Spaces: $5/month (250 GB storage, 1 TB outbound bandwidth included). DigitalOcean DNS included with Spaces. Domain registration: ~$1/month amortized. GitHub Actions: free tier (2,000 min/month; site builds complete in ~45 seconds). Total: approximately $6/month against $20 budget cap. Scaling: Spaces CDN auto-scales to handle traffic spikes during emergency declarations or local news coverage without any configuration changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Migration Sprint (weeks 1-4): Audit all legacy CMS content, create Hugo content types and templates for all five content categories, migrate current-year agendas, last five years of ordinances, all active permit PDFs, and all department contact pages. Deploy to production with redirects from legacy CMS URLs. Phase 2 - Handoff (weeks 5-6): Staff training session on Git-based authoring workflow, publish content style guide with Markdown templates and PDF naming conventions, configure UptimeRobot alerting, run axe-core accessibility report and remediate any failures. Phase 3 - Decommission (week 7): Point legacy CMS domain to new Hugo site, validate all inbound links resolve, shut down legacy server instance.
target_output.development_guidelines: Hugo version pinned in .tool-versions and GitHub Actions workflow; version bumps require explicit PR review. CSS changes must maintain WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios validated with automated axe-core check in CI. PDF file naming convention kebab-case-permit-type-YYYY-MM.pdf enforced by a CI linting script. No external JavaScript libraries added without IT director approval given zero ongoing engineering staff after handoff. All front matter fields validated against Hugo archetypes schema on build.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Staff commits broken Markdown causing Hugo build failure and blocking all content updates. Mitigation: GitHub Actions build check runs on all pull requests before merge. Risk: DigitalOcean Spaces CDN regional outage. Mitigation: static nature means no data loss; restoration from Git re-deploy restores service within minutes. Tradeoff: lunr.js client-side ordinance search requires downloading ~50 KB index on search page load, acceptable for a government site corpus of a few hundred ordinances. Tradeoff: no CMS UI means staff must learn basic Git operations, but eliminates recurring CMS licensing costs entirely.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Hugo over Jekyll for faster build times with large PDF asset inventory. Confirmed DigitalOcean Spaces over Netlify free tier to avoid build-minute limits during large content migrations. Confirmed GA4 over Matomo self-hosted given the zero-server constraint and budget cap.

metadata.notes: Simple Hugo static site migration for a small township; plan_quality weak reflects the deliberately thin scope of a no-backend static site.

profile.domain: State Government - Legislative Transparency Website

project_goal.value: Build a public-facing static website for the state legislature that embeds live bill status, roll call votes, and committee schedules fetched client-side from the legislature's open data API, reducing IT maintenance overhead compared to the current server-rendered CMS.
project_goal.rationale: Open data API already exists; static hosting eliminates server maintenance while keeping legislative data current.

target_users.value: Citizens tracking bill status and voting records, journalists, lobbyists, and civics educators searching for legislation by sponsor or keyword.
target_users.rationale: Identified in legislative communications office discovery workshops.

project_class.rationale: All dynamic data fetched client-side from legislature's existing open data API; no server-side rendering or persistence layer needed.

capabilities.rationale: Static pages with client-side API integration for live legislative data and GA4 for engagement analytics.

complexity_level.rationale: Client-side data fetching pattern is straightforward; no auth, no payments, no user data stored.

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected or stored; all data is public government information from an official API.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Legislative data is public record; no personal or sensitive data stored on this site.

external_exposure.rationale: Legislative transparency requires fully public access.

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication required.
access_model.rationale: Public legislative information; no restricted sections.

feature_scope.value: Bill search by keyword and sponsor, bill detail pages with status timeline, roll call vote records with legislator breakdown, committee schedule calendar, legislator directory with voting history.
feature_scope.rationale: Covers core legislative transparency use cases.

mvp_scope.value: Bill search and status pages, committee schedule list, legislator directory. Roll call vote detail and voting history charts in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: Delivers core citizen lookup functionality first.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced at CDN. CSP restricting script sources to self and legislature API domain. No cookies set by the site. CORS handled by legislature's open data API.
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal attack surface for a static site consuming a public read-only API.

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with JavaScript for island-based interactivity (bill search, vote filters). No heavy framework; vanilla JS fetch for API calls.
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro ships zero JavaScript by default; interactive islands pattern allows targeted hydration for search and filter components.

backend_stack.value: None. Client-side fetches directly to the legislature's open data REST API at api.legislature.state.gov.
backend_stack.rationale: No backend proxy needed; API is publicly accessible and CORS-enabled.

data_platform.value: None. Legislative data served exclusively from legislature's open data API; no local database or cache.
data_platform.rationale: Legislature's API is the authoritative source; duplicating data introduces staleness risk.

hosting_target.value: AWS CloudFront + S3 static hosting.
hosting_target.rationale: CloudFront handles global edge delivery with sub-50ms latency for static assets; S3 provides reliable object storage at negligible cost.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected or stored. GA4 with IP anonymization; 14-month data retention. No tracking cookies.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Open Government Data Act and state privacy policy requirements.

future_scope.value: Email alerts for bill status changes via subscribed third-party notification service. Downloadable CSV exports of vote records. Embedding widgets for news site integration.
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 engagement features pending communications office approval.

constraints.value: Must use legislature's existing open data API without modifications. Page load under 2 seconds on 4G connection. Must not store or proxy any legislative data.
constraints.rationale: API team has no capacity for custom endpoints; data residency constraint prohibits copying records.

observability_baseline.value: GA4 for bill page views, search query terms (anonymized), and roll call vote page engagement. CloudWatch metrics for S3 and CloudFront request counts.
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight analytics sufficient for a static public site.

execution_preference.value: Two-week build sprints with biweekly demos to legislative communications office.
execution_preference.rationale: Communications office wants frequent review touchpoints given public-facing nature.

project.goal: Static legislative transparency site with live data from open data API
project.target_users: Citizens, journalists, lobbyists, civics educators

frontend.api_integration: Vanilla JS fetch to api.legislature.state.gov REST endpoints
frontend.styling: CSS custom properties with legislature brand palette; no CSS framework
frontend.interactivity: Astro islands for bill search component, committee calendar, vote filter

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options configured on CloudFront response headers policy
security.transport: HTTPS enforced; S3 bucket not publicly accessible, served only through CloudFront

data.source: Legislature open data API (bills, votes, legislators, committees)
data.local_storage: None; all data fetched client-side on demand

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: astro build, s3 sync, CloudFront invalidation
devops.hosting: AWS CloudFront distribution backed by private S3 bucket
devops.environments: production from main; staging CloudFront distribution from staging branch

constraints.api_constraint: Must not modify or proxy legislature API

reasoner_reviews.product: The client-side API integration pattern is well-suited to the legislature's constraint of not modifying their existing data API. Bill search and status tracking are the highest-value features for the citizen audience. The committee schedule calendar and legislator directory round out the MVP scope effectively.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro's island architecture is the right choice here: static shells ship instantly from CloudFront, and only the search and filter components hydrate with JavaScript. The main architectural risk is API rate limiting from the legislature's open data endpoint during high-traffic legislative sessions; recommend implementing client-side caching with sessionStorage for repeated bill lookups to reduce call volume.
reasoner_reviews.security: Client-side direct API calls are acceptable given the API is a public read-only government endpoint. CSP must restrict fetch directives to only the legislature API domain to prevent data exfiltration if XSS were introduced. No credentials are stored client-side.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The no-proxy constraint is achievable with client-side fetch and sessionStorage caching. Performance target of 2 seconds on 4G is reachable with Astro's zero-JS-by-default approach and CloudFront edge caching for static assets.

frontend.app_structure: Astro project with src/pages/ for static routes: index, /bills, /bills/[id], /legislators, /legislators/[id], /committees, /votes/[bill-id]. src/components/ for BillSearchIsland (client:load), CommitteeCalendar (client:visible), VoteBreakdownChart (client:visible). Shared layouts for legislative branding.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage with session status banner and top-bills list (fetched on load). Bill Search page with keyword and sponsor filters, paginated results from API. Bill Detail page showing status timeline, sponsors, committee assignments, and roll call vote links. Legislator Directory with chamber filter and district search. Committee Schedule showing upcoming hearings with bill links. Vote Record page displaying yea/nay/absent breakdown by party.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro ships zero JavaScript for static pages; only search and vote chart islands hydrate. API responses cached in sessionStorage keyed by request URL for the browser session to reduce redundant fetches during navigation. Lazy loading for vote breakdown charts with client:visible directive.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: No user input persisted to localStorage or cookies. API base URL hardcoded in Astro config env var; no dynamic URL construction that could be manipulated. DOMPurify sanitizes any API-returned HTML description fields before insertion.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Fully public site.
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront WAF rules for basic rate limiting on the distribution. Client-side sessionStorage caching reduces repeat API calls that could trigger rate limits.
security.privacy_controls: GA4 with anonymize_ip: true. No advertising cookies. No user accounts or form submissions capturing personal data.

data.entities: Data sourced from API, not stored locally. Key API resources: bills (id, number, session_year, title, status ENUM introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed, sponsors[], committees[]), legislators (id, name, chamber, district, party, photo_url), committee_schedules (id, committee_name, date, time, hearing_items[{bill_id, bill_title}]), roll_call_votes (id, bill_id, vote_date, yea_count, nay_count, absent_count, legislator_votes[{legislator_id, vote ENUM yea/nay/absent/excused}]).
data.retention_and_deletion: No data stored on site infrastructure. Legislature's API is authoritative; data freshness depends on API update cadence. sessionStorage cleared on browser tab close.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repo -> GitHub Actions (astro build) -> AWS S3 bucket (private, Oregon region) -> CloudFront distribution with origin access control -> users. ACM certificate for custom domain. Route53 A record alias to CloudFront.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, setup-node, npm ci, astro build (outputs to dist/), aws s3 sync dist/ s3://legislature-site-prod/ --delete, aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*'.
devops.cost_controls: S3: ~$1/month for static asset storage. CloudFront: ~$3/month at estimated 5M requests/month (legislative session peaks). Total infrastructure: <$5/month.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static Astro site consuming the legislature's existing open data API client-side. The key architectural constraint is no data proxying or storage, which means client-side fetch with sessionStorage caching is the only caching layer available. Astro islands pattern minimizes JavaScript payload while enabling interactive bill search and vote visualization components. CloudFront + S3 delivers static assets globally with minimal cost.
target_output.title: State Legislature Transparency Site - Astro Static Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A public Astro-generated static website delivering live legislative data -- bill status, roll call votes, committee schedules, and legislator directories -- by fetching client-side from the state legislature's existing open data API. Deployed to AWS CloudFront + S3 with no backend, no database, and no user data collection. Interactive search and filter components use Astro's island hydration pattern to minimize JavaScript delivery.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static deployment with client-side data integration. Astro builds static HTML shells for all page routes and bundles only the JavaScript needed for interactive islands: bill search, committee calendar, and vote breakdown chart. All legislative data is fetched on page load or user interaction directly from api.legislature.state.gov using the browser's Fetch API. Results are cached in sessionStorage keyed by API request URL for the duration of the browser session. The built site is synced to a private S3 bucket and served globally through CloudFront with security headers enforced at the distribution level. No server-side processes, no database connections, and no user data ever touches the hosting infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Static Generator: Astro 4 with JavaScript (no TypeScript requirement per engineering decision). API Integration: vanilla Fetch API with sessionStorage caching layer. Styling: CSS custom properties with legislature brand color palette; no CSS framework to minimize dependency surface. Interactive Islands: bill search with live API typeahead (client:load), committee calendar with date navigation (client:visible), roll call vote breakdown bar chart using lightweight Chart.js island (client:visible). Hosting: AWS CloudFront + S3 (private bucket with origin access control). CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Analytics: GA4 with IP anonymization. DNS/TLS: Route53 + ACM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Bill Search Module: keyword and sponsor-name search querying /v1/bills endpoint with pagination, status filter (introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed), and session year selector. Bill Detail Pages: status timeline showing each stage transition date, sponsor list linking to legislator profiles, committee assignment history, and link to roll call vote record. Legislator Directory: filter by chamber (House/Senate), party, and district; each profile shows photo, contact info, and current session voting record summary. Committee Schedule Module: weekly calendar view of upcoming hearings with linked bill agenda items per hearing slot. Vote Record Pages: yea/nay/absent/excused counts with party breakdown bar chart and individual legislator vote list sortable by name or party.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Repository (Astro source, page templates, island components), GitHub Actions Runner (build and deploy pipeline), AWS S3 Bucket (private static asset storage, Oregon region), CloudFront Distribution (edge delivery, TLS termination, security response headers, WAF rate limiting), ACM Certificate (custom domain TLS), Route53 (DNS), Legislature Open Data API (external authoritative data source at api.legislature.state.gov), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized engagement tracking). No databases or application servers operated by this project.
target_output.workflows: Bill Lookup Workflow: Citizen navigates to /bills, types a sponsor name into the BillSearchIsland (client:load hydrated), component calls GET api.legislature.state.gov/v1/bills?sponsor=Smith&session=2026, checks sessionStorage for cached response keyed by full URL, on miss fetches and caches result for session duration, renders paginated bill cards; citizen clicks bill title and navigates to /bills/SB-1042 static shell which on mount fetches bill detail and committee history from API and renders status timeline. Roll Call Vote Review Workflow: Citizen on /bills/SB-1042 clicks 'View Final Vote', navigates to /votes/SB-1042-final, page fetches roll_call_votes endpoint, VoteBreakdownChart island hydrates on viewport entry, renders party-segmented bar chart with yea 32/nay 18/absent 5 breakdown, individual legislator vote list sortable client-side without additional API calls.
target_output.data_model: No data stored on site infrastructure. API-sourced data shapes consumed client-side: bills {id, number, session_year YYYY, title, status ENUM introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed, primary_sponsor_id, co_sponsor_ids[], committee_ids[], last_action_date, full_text_url}. legislators {id, name_full, chamber ENUM house/senate, district_number, party ENUM dem/rep/ind, photo_url, email, capitol_phone}. committee_schedules {id, committee_id, committee_name, hearing_date, hearing_time, room, hearing_items [{bill_id, bill_number, bill_title, hearing_purpose}]}. roll_call_votes {id, bill_id, vote_date, vote_type ENUM passage/amendment/procedural, yea_count, nay_count, absent_count, excused_count, legislator_votes [{legislator_id, vote ENUM yea/nay/absent/excused}]}.
target_output.api_design: Consumes legislature open data API (no custom backend). Key endpoints consumed: GET /v1/bills?keyword=&sponsor=&session=&status=&page= (bill search with filter params). GET /v1/bills/{bill_number} (bill detail with status history array). GET /v1/legislators?chamber=&party=&district= (legislator directory). GET /v1/legislators/{id}/votes?session= (legislator voting history). GET /v1/committees/{id}/schedule?from_date=&to_date= (committee hearings). GET /v1/votes/{vote_id} (roll call vote with legislator breakdown). All requests include Accept: application/json header; API returns paginated JSON with next_page_url cursor. No authentication required for public read-only API.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at CloudFront; S3 bucket blocks all public access, served only through CloudFront origin access control. CloudFront response headers policy: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.legislature.state.gov; img-src 'self' data: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). DOMPurify sanitizes any HTML content returned by API description fields. No cookies set by the site beyond GA4 measurement. WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility validated via axe-core in CI.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI/CD: (1) actions/checkout, (2) actions/setup-node with Node 22, (3) npm ci, (4) astro build outputs to dist/, (5) aws s3 sync dist/ s3://legislature-site-prod/ --delete --cache-control 'max-age=86400', (6) aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DIST_ID --paths '/*', (7) notify Slack on success/failure. Environments: production deploys from main; staging CloudFront distribution deploys from staging branch for comms office previews. Secrets: AWS credentials stored in GitHub Actions OIDC (no long-lived keys). Rollback: prior S3 state is restorable via git revert + pipeline re-run in under 5 minutes.
target_output.observability: GA4 custom events: bill_search (search_term, result_count), bill_detail_view (bill_number, status), vote_record_view (bill_number, vote_type), legislator_profile_view (legislator_id, chamber). CloudWatch dashboard for CloudFront: request count by page, 4xx/5xx error rates, cache hit ratio. UptimeRobot monitors homepage and one bill detail page every 5 minutes with email alert on downtime. Legislature API availability not monitored by this project; graceful error states display 'Legislative data temporarily unavailable' when API returns non-200.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3 storage: ~$0.50/month for ~20 GB static assets. CloudFront: ~$3-8/month depending on session traffic (spikes during floor votes and budget debates). Route53: $0.50/month per hosted zone. Total: approximately $5-10/month. CloudFront auto-scales at the edge; no capacity planning required. During high-traffic legislative events, CloudFront serves cached static shells immediately; API call volume scales with concurrent users but does not impact hosting cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Astro project scaffolding, CloudFront + S3 pipeline, bill search and bill detail pages, legislator directory, GA4 integration. Deploy to staging for communications office review. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Committee schedule calendar island, roll call vote detail pages with Chart.js breakdown chart, sessionStorage caching layer for API responses, WCAG 2.1 AA audit and remediation, production launch. Phase 3 (post-launch): Bill status email alert integration via third-party notification widget, CSV export of vote records generated client-side, embedding widget package for news site integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Astro component files use .astro extension with frontmatter for build-time data and named exports for island components. No inline event handlers; all event listeners attached in client-side island scripts. API fetch calls wrapped in a shared fetchWithCache(url) utility that checks sessionStorage before fetching. DOMPurify must be imported and used for any API-returned HTML string rendered via innerHTML. CloudFront distribution ID and S3 bucket name stored only in GitHub Actions secrets, never in source code. WCAG 2.1 AA automated check runs in CI via axe-core; PR blocked on accessibility violations.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legislature's open data API rate limits triggered during high-traffic legislative sessions with many concurrent visitors. Mitigation: sessionStorage caching reduces repeat calls; if rate limiting becomes a problem, a lightweight CloudFront Functions cache layer can be added without architectural changes. Risk: Legislature API schema changes breaking client-side rendering. Mitigation: API response validation with zod shapes at the component boundary; error boundaries display graceful fallback UI. Tradeoff: No server-side rendering means bill detail pages are blank shells until client-side fetch completes, trading initial content completeness for zero server infrastructure. Tradeoff: No data proxy means cannot rate-limit or pre-aggregate API responses, but satisfies the no-data-storage constraint.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Astro over Next.js static export given Astro's superior zero-JS-default behavior for a content-heavy site. Confirmed client-side direct API fetch over CloudFront Functions proxy to satisfy the no-data-storage constraint imposed by Legislative IT. Confirmed sessionStorage over localStorage for API response caching to ensure fresh data on new browser sessions.

metadata.notes: Static Astro site with client-side integration to state legislature open data API; plan_quality moderate with strong API and deployment sections.

profile.domain: Regional Transportation Planning - TIP Management Web Application

project_goal.value: Build a web application for a metropolitan planning organization allowing transportation planners to submit, review, and prioritize capital improvement projects for inclusion in the regional Transportation Improvement Program, with public comment periods and federal reporting exports.
project_goal.rationale: Current TIP process relies on emailed spreadsheets and manual tracking; the web app centralizes submissions, review, and federal reporting.

target_users.value: Member agency transportation planners submitting capital projects, MPO staff reviewing and prioritizing projects, and the public submitting comments during open comment periods.
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user roles identified in stakeholder interviews.

project_class.rationale: Interactive multi-role workflow application requiring backend API, database, and authenticated sessions.

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, public_api, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Multi-role app with agency-facing workflows, public comment API, analytics dashboard for MPO staff, and admin panel for system configuration.

complexity_level.rationale: Structured workflow application with multi-role auth and federal export requirements but no real-time or ML components.

risk_level.rationale: Handles planning data from multiple agencies with SAML federation; incorrect data could affect federally funded project submissions.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Capital project data is internal to member agencies and MPO; public comments are public record but contain submitter contact info.

external_exposure.rationale: Agency planner and MPO staff workflows require authentication; only public comment submission form is unauthenticated.

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: agency_planner (submit/edit own projects), mpo_reviewer (review all projects, manage comment periods), mpo_admin (system config, federal exports). Public comment submission unauthenticated. SAML federation to member agency IdPs.
access_model.rationale: Member agencies maintain their own identity providers; SAML federation avoids managing credentials for dozens of agency staff.

feature_scope.value: Capital project submission with funding source and federal category fields, MPO staff project review and scoring, TIP amendment workflow, public comment period management, federal 23 CFR Part 450 export reports, Title VI demographic impact analysis, admin panel for SAML IdP configuration.
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full TIP lifecycle from submission to federal reporting.

mvp_scope.value: Capital project submission, MPO review and prioritization, basic federal export (STIP-format CSV), SAML authentication for agency planners.
mvp_scope.rationale: Delivers core workflow value; public comment and Title VI analysis in phase 2.

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, field-level encryption for project cost estimates, SAML 2.0 for agency authentication, JWT for session tokens, CSRF protection on all mutations.
security_baseline.rationale: Federal funding data requires integrity protections; SAML is required by member agency IT policies.

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript and React Query for server state management.
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; React Query simplifies the multi-endpoint data fetching pattern for project review workflows.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django REST Framework.
backend_stack.rationale: Django's ORM and admin scaffold accelerate the data model and CRUD workflow development; DRF for REST API.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on GCP Cloud SQL.
data_platform.rationale: Relational data model with complex project-funding relationships; Cloud SQL managed HA on GCP.

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API and frontend, Cloud SQL for database.
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container scaling reduces ops overhead for a small MPO engineering team.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Capital project records retained for 10 years per federal records requirements. Public comment submitter contact info anonymized after comment period closes. System audit logs retained 7 years.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 23 CFR Part 450 and federal records management regulations.

future_scope.value: GIS map integration for project location visualization. Automated Title VI equity analysis. Integration with state DOT STIP database via REST API.
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 features requiring GIS platform procurement.

constraints.value: Must support SAML federation with up to 12 member agency IdPs. Federal export format must conform to 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C without manual post-processing. No GIS platform budget in current fiscal year.
constraints.rationale: Agency IdP integration is a hard requirement from member agencies. Federal export must pass FHWA review.

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run CPU, memory, and request latency. Application-level audit log for all project status changes and federal exports. Alert on Django 500 error rate above 0.5%.
observability_baseline.rationale: MPO IT team monitors GCP console; Django audit log is required for federal compliance reviews.

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with MPO staff as product owners. Two-week iterations with sprint demos to MPO director.
execution_preference.rationale: MPO staff know the TIP process details; iterative delivery reduces requirements risk.

compliance_context.value: 23 CFR Part 450 federal transportation planning regulations. Title VI Civil Rights Act requirements for equity analysis. State open records law for public comment records.
compliance_context.rationale: MPO receives federal transportation planning funds; 23 CFR Part 450 compliance is mandatory for FHWA reporting.

project.goal: Centralized TIP project submission, review, and federal reporting workflow
project.target_users: Agency planners, MPO reviewers, MPO admins, public commenters

frontend.state: React Query for server state; Zustand for local UI state
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with MPO brand tokens

backend.api: RESTful with DRF serializers and OpenAPI schema

security.auth: SAML 2.0 via python3-saml for agency planners; Django session for MPO staff
security.rbac: Django permission groups mapped to agency_planner, mpo_reviewer, mpo_admin roles

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with read replica
data.migrations: Django migrations with pre-migration data snapshots

devops.hosting: Cloud Run min-instances=1 to avoid cold starts

constraints.export: 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C CSV format

open_questions.gis_integration: GIS platform decision deferred to Phase 3 pending budget approval

reasoner_reviews.product: The three-round revision history shows the core workflow is solid, but the public comment period management and federal export generation features have been underspecified. The FHWA review requirement means the export format cannot have ambiguity; the architect must commit to specific field mappings in this revision.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Django + Cloud Run architecture is well-suited. The persisting gap is the SAML multi-IdP management complexity: storing per-IdP metadata and routing assertion requests to the correct IdP based on user email domain needs an explicit design, not just a passing mention. This round must resolve the SAML routing mechanism.
reasoner_reviews.security: ISS-003 (missing CSRF token on public comment form) was partially addressed in round 2 but the fix was noted as incomplete in the audit. The public comment form is unauthenticated, so Django's standard CSRF middleware applies via cookie; the frontend must read and send the csrftoken cookie value. This must be explicitly called out in the security section.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The no-GIS-budget constraint means project locations must be captured as text fields (address, county, route number) not coordinates in the MVP. The federal export format must be tested against the FHWA submission validator before go-live.

backend.service_design: Django monolith with apps: projects (capital project CRUD), review (scoring, status transitions), comments (public comment periods), exports (federal report generation), accounts (SAML federation, RBAC), admin (MPO admin panel).
backend.api_patterns: DRF ViewSets for projects, review_cycles, comment_periods, and federal_exports. Read-only public endpoint GET /api/v1/tip/{year}/projects for public transparency. All mutation endpoints require JWT or session auth.
backend.business_modules: TIP Amendment workflow: project status FSM (draft -> submitted -> under_review -> approved/rejected -> tip_included). Federal export module generates 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C CSV with calculated fields: total_cost, federal_share, state_share, local_share per funding_source. Public comment period controller opens/closes comment windows and anonymizes submitter PII on close.
backend.failure_handling: Cloud Run health check on /health/ endpoint. Django custom exception handler returns structured JSON errors. Database connection pool via django-db-geventpool configured for Cloud Run concurrency model.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with React Router v6. Route groups: /app (authenticated agency/MPO routes), /public (comment submission, TIP public view). Auth guard redirects unauthenticated users to SAML login initiation endpoint.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Agency Planner: Project Submission form (multi-step: project details, funding sources, federal categories, location), My Projects list with status badges. MPO Reviewer: Review Queue with sortable project table, Project Scoring form, Comment Period Manager, Federal Export trigger. Admin: SAML IdP configuration table, user role assignments.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server data: useProjectList, useProjectDetail, useReviewCycle, useCommentPeriod. Zustand store for project submission wizard step state and draft auto-save.

security.auth_design: SAML 2.0 SP implemented with python3-saml. SP metadata served at /saml/metadata/. IdP-initiated and SP-initiated SSO supported. Per-IdP metadata stored in saml_identity_providers table keyed by entity_id. Email domain routing: user enters email on login page, backend looks up matching IdP by email_domain column, redirects to correct IdP SSO URL.
security.authorization_model: Django permission groups: agency_planner (add_project, change_own_project), mpo_reviewer (view_all_projects, change_project_status, manage_comment_periods), mpo_admin (all permissions plus saml_idp_management, federal_export_generate). Object-level permission: agency_planner can only edit projects where submitting_agency matches their SAML agency attribute.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Django signals on project status changes write to project_audit_log (project_id, actor_user_id, action, old_status, new_status, timestamp). Federal export events logged to export_audit_log (export_id, tip_year, generated_by, generated_at, row_count). Both tables append-only via database trigger.
security.privacy_controls: Public comment submitter email anonymized to sha256 hash 30 days after comment period closes. Full name replaced with 'Anonymous Commenter [N]'. Phone number deleted. Anonymization runs as a scheduled Cloud Run Job.

data.entities: capital_projects (id, tip_year, project_title, submitting_agency_id, project_type ENUM road/bridge/transit/bike_ped, location_address, location_county, location_route, total_cost, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/approved/rejected/tip_included, submitted_at). funding_sources (id, project_id, funding_program ENUM nhpp/stp/cmaq/tap/local, federal_share_pct, amount). tip_amendments (id, tip_year, amendment_number, description, approved_at, projects[]). public_comments (id, comment_period_id, submitter_name, submitter_email, comment_text, submitted_at, anonymized_at). agency_submissions (id, project_id, agency_id, submission_round, reviewer_notes). federal_categories (id, project_id, work_type_code, asset_class, improvement_type).
data.schema_notes: tip_year + project_id composite uniqueness constraint prevents duplicate submissions per cycle. funding_sources.federal_share_pct CHECK constraint (0 <= pct <= 100). project status transitions enforced at application layer via FSM, not DB constraints.
data.retention_and_deletion: capital_projects and funding_sources retained 10 years per federal records requirements. public_comments anonymized at 30 days post-period-close; deleted after 7 years. export_audit_log retained 7 years.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Build pipeline -> Cloud Run services (api: min 1, max 10 instances; frontend: static via Firebase Hosting or Cloud Run) -> Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (primary + read replica) -> Cloud Armor WAF -> users.
devops.environments: dev (Cloud Run dev service, shared Cloud SQL dev instance), staging (isolated Cloud SQL staging, SAML test IdP), production (multi-region Cloud SQL HA, real agency IdPs).
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on main branch push: run pytest with 80% coverage gate, build Docker image, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run with --no-traffic flag, run smoke tests, shift traffic 100%. Rollback via Cloud Run traffic splitting to prior revision.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency (p50, p95, p99), error rate, instance count. Custom metrics: active_tip_submissions_by_agency, comment_period_submissions_rate, federal_export_generation_duration. Alert policy: error_rate > 0.5% for 5 minutes -> PagerDuty.

ISS-001.title: SAML multi-IdP routing mechanism unspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned SAML federation but did not specify how requests are routed to the correct IdP when 12 agencies use different entity IDs. Round 2 added email-domain-based routing logic.

ISS-002.title: Federal export field mappings not specified
ISS-002.detail: 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C requires specific field mappings that were absent in rounds 1 and 2. Round 3 plan must include explicit field-to-column mappings in the data model or API design section.

ISS-003.title: CSRF protection incomplete on public comment form
ISS-003.detail: Public comment submission form is unauthenticated; round 2 mentioned CSRF middleware but did not specify how the unauthenticated form obtains and sends the csrftoken cookie. The fix requires explicit frontend handling.

focus_issues.title: Federal export field mappings not specified
focus_issues.detail: 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C requires specific field mappings absent in prior rounds.

focus_issues.title: CSRF protection incomplete on public comment form
focus_issues.detail: Unauthenticated form CSRF token handling unspecified in round 2.

latest_recommendations: Specify 23 CFR Part 450 Appendix C column mappings explicitly in the data model or API section.
Document how the unauthenticated public comment form obtains and submits the Django CSRF token.
Add federal export smoke test to CI pipeline to catch format regressions before staging.

previous_plan.title: TIP Capital Project Management System - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Django REST Framework API on Cloud Run with React 18 frontend managing TIP capital project submission, review, and federal reporting for a metropolitan planning organization with SAML federation to 12 member agency IdPs.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Django monolith on Cloud Run with Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16. SAML 2.0 with python3-saml for agency authentication. React 18 SPA for planner and reviewer workflows. Public comment form accessible without authentication.
previous_plan.data_model: capital_projects, funding_sources, tip_amendments, public_comments, agency_submissions entities defined. Federal export section mentioned CSV generation but field mappings not specified.
