best_plan.executive_summary: Flutter offline-first mobile app with Drift SQLite on-device storage and Spring Boot 3 backend on AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate. SACWIS XML transformation via sacwis_integration package. Keycloak MFA with CJIS session controls.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 must resolve ISS-002 (conflict detection and supervisor resolution flow) and ISS-003 (sync push case_id authorization). For ISS-002: use device_updated_at + server_updated_at comparison on safety_assessments; when server_updated_at > device_updated_at for matching case_id+assessment_date, create conflict_record and return it in push response; supervisor resolves via ConflictResolutionScreen. For ISS-003: on every sync push record, Spring service queries active_case_assignments WHERE worker_id = jwt.worker_id AND case_id = record.case_id before writing; unauthorized records returned with UNAUTHORIZED_CASE error code. Both fixes are targeted additions to existing sections without architectural restructuring.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified timestamp-based conflict detection in data_model (device_updated_at vs server_updated_at comparison on same case_id+assessment_date). Added conflict detection logic to api_design sync push description. Added Flutter ConflictResolutionScreen to frontend section with side-by-side diff and supervisor override action. Updated security_and_compliance to note conflict records as append-only audit trail.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent edits by worker and supervisor on the same assessment are detected at sync push time; supervisor resolves via in-app screen; no silent data loss.

fix_report.action_taken: Added case_id whitelist authorization check to api_design sync push endpoint description: Spring SyncService queries active_case_assignments WHERE worker_id = jwt.worker_id AND case_id IN (pushed_record.case_ids); unauthorized records rejected with 403 UNAUTHORIZED_CASE error code and returned in push response errors[]. Added corresponding CJIS test case to Spring integration test suite description in development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Workers cannot push assessment or visit records for cases outside their active assignment list; CJIS scope restriction enforced at API layer.

target_output.title: CPS Field Worker Mobile Application - Child Welfare Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first Flutter mobile application for state CPS field workers documenting home visit observations and safety assessments during visits in areas with intermittent connectivity, syncing to a Spring Boot 3 backend on AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate when network is available. Drift SQLite on-device encrypted storage handles structured assessment data for full 8-hour shifts offline. SACWIS integration via a JSON-to-XML transformation batch pipeline submits approved assessments to the legacy child welfare information system. Round 4 resolves concurrent edit conflict detection using device_updated_at comparison with supervisor resolution workflow, and enforces CJIS-required case_id authorization on sync push via active assignment whitelist check.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter mobile app with Riverpod state management and Drift SQLite (SQLCipher-encrypted) for offline case record storage. Field workers authenticate via Keycloak MFA on app launch; biometric re-auth within the 8-hour shift refresh token window avoids MFA disruption during field work. On connectivity restoration, SyncStateNotifier batches pending home_visits and safety_assessments from the device push queue and POSTs to the Spring Boot sync API. Server-side conflict detection compares device_updated_at in each pushed record against server_updated_at for matching case_id+assessment_date; conflicts are queued in conflict_records and returned to the device for supervisor resolution. Sync push authorization validates each pushed record's case_id against active_case_assignments for the JWT worker_id before writing, rejecting unauthorized records. Approved assessments enter the sacwis_pending_queue; a Spring @Scheduled job transforms records to SACWIS XML using SacwisXmlTransformer and submits to the state SACWIS batch endpoint every 30 minutes. All servers reside in AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1 within the state's FedRAMP Moderate boundary.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter (Dart), Riverpod for state management, Drift (SQLite) with SQLCipher encryption, flutter_secure_storage for Keycloak tokens and SQLCipher key, connectivity_plus for network state, local_auth for biometric unlock. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security with Keycloak adapter, Spring @Scheduled, JAXB for SACWIS XML serialization. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS GovCloud RDS Multi-AZ, SQLite via Drift on device. Auth: Keycloak on ECS Fargate (separate task), TOTP MFA. Infrastructure: AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate, RDS Multi-AZ, ALB (VPN-restricted), ECR, Secrets Manager, S3 GovCloud (inspection photos). CI/CD: Jenkins on GovCloud EC2, Fastlane for Flutter MDM distribution. Observability: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Safety Assessment Module: 12-domain structured form (caregiver functioning, child vulnerability, household hazards, domestic violence, substance abuse, mental health, prior history, child protective factors, family strengths, support network, agency services, overall risk); each domain captures rating ENUM safe/unsafe/not_applicable and narrative text; aggregate risk score computed locally; form auto-saves to Drift on each field exit. Home Visit Documentation: visit type (announced/unannounced/virtual), structured observations with checkboxes and free-form narrative, photo capture stored as encrypted Drift blob, GPS-stamped timestamp. Sync Engine: SyncStateNotifier tracks pending push queue, connectivity state, and conflict queue; push batches up to 50 records per request; pull delta downloads case record changes since last_synced_at. Conflict Resolution Screen: supervisor opens conflict queue, views side-by-side device payload vs server payload for each conflicted assessment, selects resolution source (device version / server version / manual merge), submits resolution which server writes to safety_assessments with resolution_source recorded. SACWIS Sync: nightly supervisor dashboard showing sacwis_submitted status per assessment and retry failures. Federal Timeliness: dashboard listing cases approaching 45 CFR Part 1355 deadline with days-remaining indicator.
target_output.system_components: Flutter mobile app (iOS 16+/Android 12+, MDM-provisioned), Drift SQLite on device (SQLCipher encrypted), Keycloak realm on ECS Fargate (MFA, refresh token issuance), Spring Boot 3 API on ECS Fargate (sync, case management, SACWIS batch), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (server case records), S3 GovCloud bucket (inspection photos, encrypted with SSE-KMS), SacwisXmlTransformer @Scheduled job (JSON to SACWIS XML, 30-minute cadence), SACWIS batch endpoint (state legacy system, external), Jamf Pro MDM (device provisioning and remote wipe), Jenkins GovCloud (CI/CD), Datadog APM (distributed tracing and alerts), ALB (VPN-restricted ingress).
target_output.workflows: Offline Assessment Workflow: Field worker opens Case Detail, navigates to New Safety Assessment; form fields write to Drift immediately on change; SyncStateNotifier marks assessment as pending_push; when worker regains connectivity (connectivity_plus detects), SyncStateNotifier triggers push batch to POST /api/v1/sync/push; Spring SyncService validates each record's case_id against active_case_assignments (worker_id from JWT); authorized records written to PostgreSQL; server compares device_updated_at against server_updated_at for same case_id+assessment_date: if server_updated_at > device_updated_at, server creates conflict_record and returns conflict_id in push response errors[]; Flutter SyncStateNotifier adds conflict to local conflict queue; supervisor opens ConflictResolutionScreen, reviews side-by-side diff, submits resolution_source (device/server/manual); server writes resolved version and marks conflict_record resolved. SACWIS Submission: supervisor approves submitted visit documentation, Spring @EventListener transitions assessment to approved, SacwisQueueService inserts record into sacwis_pending_queue; every 30 minutes @Scheduled job calls SacwisXmlTransformer.toXml(assessment) mapping internal fields to SACWIS XML schema elements (case_number -> <CaseId>, assessment_date -> <AssessmentDate>, overall_risk -> <RiskLevel>, safety_factors JSONB -> <SafetyFactors><Factor>... elements); XML POSTed to state SACWIS batch endpoint; success sets sacwis_submitted=true; failure increments retry_count with exponential backoff.
target_output.data_model: Server PostgreSQL: case_records (id UUID PK, case_number VARCHAR(20), assigned_worker_id FK users, supervisor_id FK users, case_status ENUM open/closed/transferred, version_vector JSONB). active_case_assignments (id UUID PK, case_id FK, worker_id FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, unassigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE; INDEX on worker_id + case_id for sync push authorization lookup). safety_assessments (id UUID PK, case_id FK, worker_id FK, assessment_date DATE, safety_factors JSONB {domain_key: {rating: 'safe|unsafe|not_applicable', narrative: '...'}, ...}, overall_risk ENUM safe/unsafe/conditionally_safe, device_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, server_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), sacwis_submitted BOOL DEFAULT false, version INT DEFAULT 1). conflict_records (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, device_payload JSONB, server_payload JSONB, device_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, server_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_source ENUM device/server/manual NULLABLE, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, resolved_by FK users). Conflict detection logic on sync push: SELECT server_updated_at FROM safety_assessments WHERE case_id = $case_id AND assessment_date = $date AND server_updated_at > $device_updated_at; if row exists, INSERT INTO conflict_records. sacwis_pending_queue (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, queued_at, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0, next_retry_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_error TEXT NULLABLE). home_visits (id UUID PK, case_id FK, worker_id FK, visit_date DATE, visit_type ENUM, observations_text BYTEA ENCRYPTED, photos_s3_keys TEXT[], submitted_at, sacwis_submitted BOOL). Device Drift tables mirror subset of server tables with pending_push_status field.
target_output.api_design: Spring Boot REST API (VPN-accessible only, ALB restricted to state VPN CIDR). POST /api/v1/auth/token (Keycloak token exchange; not Spring-implemented, proxied to Keycloak). GET /api/v1/sync/pull?worker_id=&since=&limit=100 returns: {cases: [...], assignments: [...], wipe_commands: [case_id, ...]}; filters to worker_id active assignments only. POST /api/v1/sync/push body: {records: [{record_type: 'assessment|visit', case_id, payload, device_updated_at}]}; for each record: (1) query active_case_assignments WHERE worker_id = jwt.worker_id AND case_id = record.case_id -- if not found, add to response errors[] with error_code UNAUTHORIZED_CASE; (2) if authorized and assessment: SELECT server_updated_at WHERE case_id + assessment_date match AND server_updated_at > device_updated_at -- if conflict, INSERT conflict_record, add conflict_id to response conflicts[]; (3) otherwise write record to DB. Response: {written_count, errors: [{case_id, error_code}], conflicts: [{conflict_id, assessment_id}]}. GET /api/v1/cases/{id}/conflicts (supervisor: list conflict_records for case). POST /api/v1/conflicts/{id}/resolve body: {resolution_source, merged_payload?} (supervisor only). GET /api/v1/timeliness (supervisor: cases approaching 45 CFR Part 1355 deadlines). GET /api/v1/sacwis/status?case_id= (sacwis_submitted status per assessment).
target_output.security_and_compliance: CJIS Security Policy v5.9 mobile controls: MDM-enrolled devices only (Jamf profile required before app launch check). Keycloak TOTP MFA on initial auth. Access token TTL 15 min; refresh token TTL 8 hours. Biometric re-auth (local_auth) exchanges stored refresh token for new access token without TOTP repeat. App enforces session lock on 15-min inactivity. On-device SQLCipher key derived from flutter_secure_storage hardware-backed keystore entry. Sync push case_id authorization: Spring SyncService executes SELECT COUNT(1) FROM active_case_assignments WHERE worker_id = :jwt_worker_id AND case_id = :record_case_id AND unassigned_at IS NULL; count=0 returns 403 UNAUTHORIZED_CASE in response errors[], record not written. Conflict records are append-only (no UPDATE or DELETE permitted on conflict_records table, enforced by DB trigger); resolution inserts new resolved version to safety_assessments. CAPTA data access log: all case record reads and writes emit to CloudWatch Logs with case_id (hashed for log visibility, not full case number) and worker_id. CJIS incident response: lost/stolen device triggers Jamf remote wipe + Keycloak refresh token revocation within 1 hour; CJIS incident report filed within 24 hours per policy v5.9 Section 5.13.1.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins GovCloud pipeline: (1) mvn test (JUnit + Testcontainers PostgreSQL), (2) mvn package, (3) docker build, (4) ECR push (GovCloud registry), (5) ECS task definition update, (6) ECS rolling deployment (min 50% healthy), (7) ALB health check validation. Flutter pipeline: (1) flutter test, (2) flutter build ipa --release + flutter build apk --release, (3) fastlane deliver to Jamf Pro Pro server for MDM distribution. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, test Keycloak), staging (multi-AZ RDS, test SACWIS endpoint mock), production (RDS Multi-AZ, real SACWIS endpoint, Jamf Pro production). Rollback: ECS deploy previous task definition revision. RDS point-in-time recovery tested quarterly.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with Java ddtrace auto-instrumentation on Spring Boot. Custom metrics: sync_push_records_per_request (histogram), sync_conflict_rate (conflicts per 1000 push records), sacwis_submission_success_rate (7-day rolling), sacwis_retry_queue_depth (gauge), timeliness_violations_open (count by case_type). Structured JSON log fields: worker_id, case_id_hash (SHA-256 of case_number), record_type, sync_action, sacwis_status. Alerts: sacwis_retry_queue_depth > 100 for 30 minutes (PagerDuty), sync push UNAUTHORIZED_CASE error rate > 2% for 10 minutes (security team notification), Keycloak login failure spike > 20/min.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate (API + Keycloak tasks): ~$120/month at 2 tasks min. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.r6g.large: ~$180/month. S3 GovCloud (inspection photos): ~$15/month. Datadog APM: ~$30/month per host equivalent. Jenkins EC2 (t3.medium): ~$30/month. Total: ~$400/month. Scaling: ECS API service scales on CPU > 60%; Keycloak task scales on connection count. Peak sync load occurs at shift end (5-6 PM) when field workers return to connectivity; ECS auto-scaling absorbs burst without pre-warming. RDS read replica can be added if reporting queries from supervisors impact sync write throughput.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (months 1-8): Flutter app with offline safety assessment forms and home visit documentation, Drift SQLite encrypted storage, Keycloak MFA, Spring Boot sync API with case_id authorization check, conflict detection and resolution workflow, SACWIS XML batch pipeline with SacwisXmlTransformer, ECS Fargate + RDS GovCloud deployment, Jenkins CI/CD, MDM distribution via Jamf Pro. Field pilot with 20 workers in one district. Phase 2 (months 9-16): Federal timeliness deadline tracking dashboard, supervisor review and approval workflow, court hearing calendar sync, photo capture with S3 GovCloud storage, expanded MDM rollout to all districts. Phase 3 (months 17-24): Real-time supervisor push notifications on high-risk assessments, court case management integration, risk scoring model (pending data governance approval).
target_output.development_guidelines: Spring SyncService case_id authorization check must be covered by a dedicated CJIS integration test: test_sync_push_rejects_unauthorized_case_id asserts 403 UNAUTHORIZED_CASE when worker pushes record for case_id not in active_case_assignments. Flutter form fields write to Drift on every FocusNode change; no batch-only saves that could lose data on crash. Conflict_records table is append-only: no DELETE or UPDATE in application code; enforced by DB trigger and reviewed in code review checklist. SacwisXmlTransformer JAXB mapping class annotated with SACWIS schema version reference comment for each field mapping. Keycloak realm configuration exported as JSON and versioned in Git; realm changes deployed via Keycloak admin import, not manual console edits.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Keycloak refresh token expiry during an extended-connectivity outage (>8 hours) logs out field worker mid-shift. Mitigation: 8-hour refresh TTL matches maximum shift length; supervisor can issue emergency token extension via Keycloak admin API for exceptional circumstances. Risk: SACWIS batch endpoint downtime causes assessment submission backlog. Mitigation: sacwis_pending_queue with exponential backoff retry; Datadog alert on queue depth; state SACWIS team SLA requires 99.5% uptime. Tradeoff: device_updated_at + server_updated_at conflict detection over vector clocks is simpler to implement and debug, at the cost of false conflicts when clocks are slightly out of sync; NTP enforcement via MDM policy mitigates clock drift. Tradeoff: Spring Boot monolith over microservices for a small state dev team reduces operational complexity and stays within GovCloud resource budget.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed timestamp-based conflict detection over vector clocks given NTP enforcement via MDM. Confirmed active_case_assignments query for sync push authorization rather than JWT-embedded case list (JWT would be too large for workers with 20+ assigned cases). Confirmed JAXB for SACWIS XML serialization over manual string building given SACWIS schema complexity. Risk scoring model deferred to Phase 3 pending state data governance board approval.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving concurrent edit conflict detection (ISS-002) and CJIS sync push case_id authorization (ISS-003) for a state CPS field worker mobile application.

profile.domain: Federal Agency Public Information Portal

project_goal.value: Serve millions of monthly visitors with policy guidance documents, press releases, program pages, and regulatory notices for a large U.S. federal agency
project_goal.rationale: Core mission of public-facing informational portal

target_users.value: General public, policy researchers, journalists, regulated entities seeking agency guidance
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in agency digital strategy

project_class.rationale: No authenticated sessions or server-side logic required; all content is static and publicly available

capabilities.rationale: Content delivery with traffic analytics; no backend services required

complexity_level.rationale: Large document corpus and CDN optimization add complexity beyond a simple brochure site

risk_level.rationale: No sensitive data collected; static delivery only

data_sensitivity.rationale: No PII or restricted data; all content is public government information

external_exposure.rationale: Federal public information portal accessible to all internet users

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication required
access_model.rationale: Agency information must be publicly accessible without barriers

feature_scope.value: Policy guidance pages, press release archive, program landing pages, regulatory notices, document search, contact directory, publication archives
feature_scope.rationale: Full content scope identified in agency web inventory

mvp_scope.value: Core program pages, press releases for current year, basic document library with search, and analytics integration
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP supports agency communications within first 90 days

security_baseline.value: HTTPS-only with HSTS, CSP headers, SRI for third-party scripts, no server-side code surface
security_baseline.rationale: Static delivery eliminates server-side attack vectors; header hardening addresses remaining exposure

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript (static export mode)
frontend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity and excellent static generation for large content libraries

backend_stack.value: None
backend_stack.rationale: Static website requires no server-side runtime

data_platform.value: None; content stored as Markdown files in Git repository
data_platform.rationale: Static content management via Git; no database required

hosting_target.value: CloudFront distribution with S3 origin in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Edge delivery for global performance; S3 for durable static asset storage

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; GA4 configured with IP anonymization and data retention set to 14 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII to retain; analytics configured for minimal data collection

future_scope.value: Spanish-language content parity, personalized content subscriptions via GovDelivery, integration with agency records search system
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap pending budget approval

constraints.value: Must use FedRAMP-authorized services only; Section 508 and Plain Writing Act compliance required; budget under $3,000/month for hosting
constraints.rationale: Federal procurement and accessibility mandates

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for page-level traffic, CloudFront access logs for origin hit analysis, GitHub Actions pipeline status in Slack
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard digital analytics stack for federal public sites

execution_preference.value: Content-first: migrate existing pages before adding new features; 2-week sprint cycles
execution_preference.rationale: Risk reduction by establishing stable base before extending functionality

project.goal: High-traffic static portal serving federal agency public content at edge scale
project.target_users: General public and regulated entities, millions of monthly unique visitors

frontend.search: Pagefind client-side search index built at CI time

security.headers: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, SRI on all external scripts
security.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA via automated axe scans in CI and manual testing

data.content: Markdown + MDX source files in monorepo, versioned in Git
data.cdn: CloudFront with 24-hour TTL for documents, 5-minute TTL for press releases

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: build Next.js static export, run axe accessibility scan, upload to S3, invalidate CloudFront
devops.environments: staging (branch previews on S3 prefix), production (root CloudFront distribution)

constraints.fedramp: All third-party services must have FedRAMP Moderate ATO

reasoner_reviews.product: The content scope covering policy guidance, press releases, and regulatory notices maps directly to the agency's communication mission. Incremental migration by content type reduces launch risk. The absence of user authentication simplifies delivery significantly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export on CloudFront is well-suited for tens of millions of monthly page views without server scaling concerns. Pagefind provides acceptable document search without a backend search service. Cache TTL differentiation between documents and press releases is a sound strategy.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static delivery eliminates the largest class of server-side vulnerabilities. CSP headers must be carefully scoped to allow USWDS CDN assets and GA4 without overly permissive directives. HSTS preloading should be evaluated for the agency domain.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: FedRAMP authorization requirement is the binding constraint on third-party tool selection. GA4 operates under Google's FedRAMP authorization. Budget headroom of $3,000/month is ample for S3 storage and CloudFront transfer at projected traffic volumes.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan lacks a clear content governance workflow — without defining who approves content merges and how emergency updates are handled, the editorial process will be ad hoc. The architecture is sound but the operational model for content publishing needs more definition.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 monorepo with static export; content in /content directory organized by type (guidance, press-releases, programs, notices); shared USWDS 3.x component library in /components/uswds
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home with alert banner for active emergencies, Program landing pages with child guidance documents, Press Release archive with year/month filters, Regulatory Notices listing with Federal Register links, Document Search powered by Pagefind, Contact Directory by division
frontend.performance_notes: next/image for all imagery with WebP conversion, Pagefind index built at CI time and served as static assets, CloudFront compression enabled for HTML/CSS/JS, preload hints on USWDS fonts
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA enforced via axe-core in CI pipeline; manual screen reader testing quarterly; USWDS components provide accessible patterns for navigation, accordions, and data tables; skip-to-main-content link on every page

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public access
security.secrets_management: No secrets required in static build; GA4 measurement ID stored as public environment variable in CI
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront WAF rate limiting to prevent scraping spikes from overwhelming origin; no user-submitted data paths
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudFront access logs written to S3 for 90-day retention; GitHub Actions audit log for deployment history

data.entities: program_pages (slug, title, summary, body_mdx, last_reviewed_date, owning_division), policy_documents (slug, title, document_type, publication_date, pdf_s3_key, federal_register_citation), press_releases (slug, title, body_mdx, published_at, contact_name, contact_email), regulatory_notices (slug, docket_id, title, comment_deadline, status ENUM proposed/final/withdrawn, federal_register_link), publication_archives (year, document_count, archive_pdf_s3_key)
data.storage_design: All content as MDX files in Git; PDF and binary documents stored in S3 public bucket with versioning enabled; Pagefind index artifacts stored in S3 alongside built HTML
data.retention_and_deletion: Published pages retained indefinitely per NARA records schedule; outdated press releases archived in place rather than deleted

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds Next.js static export, runs Pagefind indexer, syncs output to S3, issues CloudFront invalidation scoped to changed paths only
devops.environments: Production: CloudFront + S3 us-east-1 on agency domain; Staging: S3 website endpoint under staging.agency prefix for PR previews
devops.ci_cd_design: Push to main triggers production deploy; PRs trigger staging preview deploy; axe accessibility scan must pass before S3 sync; build time under 8 minutes for full 5,000-page corpus via Next.js ISR at build
devops.observability_stack: GA4 for user behavior and page performance; CloudFront real-time logs to S3 for access pattern analysis; GitHub Actions Slack notifications on deploy failure

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a large-scale federal static website. The key tension is balancing edge delivery performance at millions of monthly visits against the strict FedRAMP and Section 508 compliance requirements. Intentional flaw introduced: the deployment_and_operations section references a Node.js Express server for server-side rendering fallback, which directly contradicts the static_website contract and the backend_stack value of 'None'. This creates a detectable contract violation where the plan proposes server infrastructure that the frozen contract explicitly rules out.
target_output.title: Federal Agency Public Information Portal Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-traffic static website delivering federal policy guidance, press releases, regulatory notices, and program information to millions of monthly visitors via CloudFront edge delivery with an S3 origin. Built with Next.js 14 static export and USWDS 3.x components, the site requires no server-side runtime and meets Section 508, Plain Writing Act, and FedRAMP hosting constraints within a $3,000/month budget envelope.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static delivery architecture: Next.js 14 generates HTML at build time for all policy guidance pages, press releases, regulatory notices, and program landing pages. Built artifacts are uploaded to an S3 bucket with versioning, distributed globally via CloudFront with path-based TTL policies — 24-hour cache for stable documents, 5-minute TTL for press releases. Pagefind generates a client-side search index at build time, eliminating any backend search dependency. All pages are fully pre-rendered at build time with Next.js SSG; no server runtime is deployed alongside the CloudFront distribution. Document metadata queries are resolved client-side via the pre-built Pagefind search index, eliminating the need for any ECS Fargate or serverless backend component. All content governance flows through Git pull requests with required reviewer approval before CI deploys to production.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 TypeScript static export, USWDS 3.x component library, Pagefind 1.x for client-side full-text search. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization. Infrastructure: S3 with versioning for static asset storage, CloudFront with WAF, Route 53, ACM for TLS. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with axe-core accessibility scanning, S3 sync, and scoped CloudFront invalidation. Content: Markdown and MDX source files versioned in Git monorepo. Backend: Node.js 20 Express server on ECS Fargate for dynamic routing and metadata API.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Policy Guidance Library: hierarchical pages organized by program office with document versioning history and Federal Register citations. Press Release Archive: chronological listing with keyword search, filterable by year and program area. Regulatory Notices Hub: active and closed dockets with comment deadline countdowns and links to Regulations.gov. Program Landing Pages: each program office maintains a structured page with contacts, guidance, and related documents. Document Search: Pagefind-powered full-text search across all published HTML and indexed PDF content. Contact Directory: division-level directory with role and phone number, updated from Git-tracked structured data.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution with WAF (rate limiting, geo-restriction exempt for .gov domains), S3 static origin bucket with public read policy scoped to CloudFront OAC, GitHub Actions CI runner performing build-test-deploy in under 8 minutes, Pagefind indexer generating search artifacts embedded in the built site, GA4 tag loaded via USWDS-compatible script injection, Node.js 20 Express API server on ECS Fargate providing document metadata endpoints and fallback SSR routing for dynamic content pages.
target_output.workflows: Content Publishing: content editor creates a PR in GitHub with new or updated MDX files for a regulatory notice; automated CI runs accessibility scan and link checker; reviewer approves and merges; GitHub Actions builds the full site, syncs to S3, and issues a CloudFront invalidation for affected paths — total time from merge to live is under 6 minutes. Emergency Bulletin: communications staff push an alert banner configuration update to a JSON file in the repo; expedited PR review bypasses the standard 24-hour review window under the agency's documented emergency publishing policy; CloudFront serves the updated site globally within 5 minutes of S3 sync completing.
target_output.data_model: program_pages (slug, title_plain_language, summary_text, body_mdx, owning_division, last_reviewed_date, related_guidance_slugs[]). policy_documents (slug, title, document_type ENUM guidance/rule/policy/report, publication_date, pdf_s3_key, federal_register_citation, supersedes_slug). press_releases (slug, headline, body_mdx, published_at, contact_name, contact_phone, tags[]). regulatory_notices (slug, docket_id, title, comment_deadline, status ENUM proposed/interim_final/final/withdrawn, regulations_gov_link, federal_register_link). publication_archives (fiscal_year, document_count, archive_index_s3_key). contact_directories (division_slug, division_name, contacts[]). All entities stored as MDX/JSON files in Git; no database.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API endpoints. Client-side data access patterns: Pagefind search index fetched as static JSON shards from CloudFront (GET /pagefind/pagefind.js, GET /pagefind/*.pf_index). Document metadata served as static JSON files pre-generated at build time (GET /data/documents/{slug}.json). Press release RSS feed generated at build time (GET /press-releases/feed.xml). Future metadata API: GET /api/v1/documents?type=guidance&division=enforcement served from Node.js Express on ECS Fargate.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront with HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains). Content Security Policy scoped to allow USWDS CDN, GA4 (googletagmanager.com, google-analytics.com), and self; no inline scripts permitted except USWDS nonce-based exceptions. Subresource Integrity applied to all third-party script tags. X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking. CloudFront WAF with rate limiting at 2,000 requests per 5 minutes per IP. S3 bucket blocks all public access directly; CloudFront OAC enforces origin access. No cookies set except GA4 first-party analytics cookie with 14-month retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: (1) Install deps, run next build with static export, (2) Run axe-core on 20 sampled pages — fail build on any critical violation, (3) Run Pagefind indexer on built HTML output, (4) aws s3 sync with --delete flag to S3 bucket, (5) Issue CloudFront invalidation for changed paths via aws cloudfront create-invalidation, (6) Post Slack notification with deploy summary. PR branches deploy to S3 prefix staging/{branch-slug}/ accessible via staging CloudFront behavior for review. Full production deploy takes 7-8 minutes end-to-end. ECS Fargate cluster runs Node.js Express server with auto-scaling 1-3 tasks based on ALB request count, deployed independently via separate GitHub Actions workflow.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 tracks page views, document download events (PDF link clicks), search query terms and result counts, and session duration by content section. CloudFront real-time logs forwarded to S3 bucket, queryable via Athena for origin miss rate, cache hit ratio by path prefix, and geographic distribution analysis. GitHub Actions deployment history provides audit trail for all content changes. CloudWatch alarm on CloudFront 5xx error rate exceeding 0.5% triggers PagerDuty alert to on-call ops.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month at 10 million monthly page views: S3 storage ~$50/month for 50GB static assets, CloudFront transfer ~$600/month at $0.0085/GB for North American edge, CloudFront requests ~$100/month, Route 53 ~$10/month, GitHub Actions under free tier for public repo. ECS Fargate Express server adds ~$150/month for 1-3 tasks. CloudFront auto-scales to handle traffic spikes around major policy announcements without pre-provisioning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Migration (8 weeks): Migrate top-100 highest-traffic program pages and current-year press releases from legacy CMS to MDX, establish CI/CD pipeline with accessibility scanning, launch on CloudFront with full HTTPS and security headers. Phase 2 Content Parity (6 weeks): Complete migration of regulatory notices archive, policy document library, and contact directories; implement Pagefind search index covering all migrated content; decommission legacy CMS. Phase 3 Enhancements (4 weeks): Add GA4 event tracking for document downloads and search behavior; implement RSS feeds for press releases and regulatory notices; optimize CloudFront cache policies based on Phase 2 traffic data.
target_output.development_guidelines: All MDX content must pass automated axe-core scan and a Plain Writing Act readability check (Flesch-Kincaid grade level under 12) before merge. USWDS components must not be modified inline — extend via CSS custom properties only. PR descriptions must include a content review checklist confirming accuracy of Federal Register citations and contact information. New pages require a last_reviewed_date field set to publication date. GitHub branch protection requires 1 approval from the content governance team and passing CI checks.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Build time growth as content corpus expands past 10,000 pages. Mitigation: Next.js incremental static regeneration at build time and Pagefind's incremental indexing mode limit rebuild scope to changed content. Risk: ECS Fargate Express server introduces a server-side attack surface absent from a pure static architecture, contradicting the security rationale for static hosting. This component is architecturally inconsistent with the static_website contract and should be removed. Tradeoff: Pagefind client-side search vs. a hosted search service — Pagefind avoids third-party FedRAMP compliance review but limits search relevance tuning options for large corpora.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch to avoid FedRAMP compliance review for a third-party SaaS. Confirmed CloudFront OAC over legacy OAI for S3 origin security. Chose USWDS 3.x over a custom design system to inherit Section 508 compliance from a federally-maintained component library. Plain Writing Act compliance enforced via automated readability scoring in CI rather than manual review alone.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: plan introduces a Node.js Express server on ECS Fargate in architecture_overview, system_components, deployment_and_operations, and risks_and_tradeoffs, directly violating the frozen contract fields backend_stack=None and project_class=static_website.

profile.domain: Federal Procurement Open-Source Python SDK

project_goal.value: Open-source Python SDK abstracting SAM.gov API for entity registration lookups, exclusion checks under FAR Part 9.4, and contract opportunities searches for government procurement developers
project_goal.rationale: Reduces boilerplate for developers querying SAM.gov in procurement applications

target_users.value: Python developers building procurement compliance tools, debarment checkers, and contract opportunity monitors against the federal SAM.gov API
target_users.rationale: Identified through community demand on existing unofficial SAM.gov wrappers

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a PyPI package with no hosted platform or server infrastructure

capabilities.rationale: HTTP client integration with SAM.gov API, Pydantic response models, and pagination abstraction

complexity_level.rationale: Thin API wrapper with typed models and retry logic; no stateful processing

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored; API key is caller-owned and passed through

data_sensitivity.rationale: SDK does not store or persist any SAM.gov response data

external_exposure.rationale: Distributed publicly via PyPI and calls SAM.gov public API

access_model.value: API key passthrough — callers supply their own SAM.gov API key; SDK never stores credentials
access_model.rationale: SAM.gov API requires per-developer keys; SDK must not centralize credential storage

feature_scope.value: Entity registration lookup by UEI and CAGE code, exclusions check for debarred or suspended entities, contract opportunities search by NAICS code and date range, award notice retrieval, cursor-based pagination, retry with exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx
feature_scope.rationale: Core SAM.gov API endpoints most requested by procurement developers

mvp_scope.value: Entity lookup, exclusions check, and opportunities search with pagination and retry; Pydantic v2 typed models for all SAM.gov response types
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers three highest-priority SAM.gov endpoints for initial PyPI release

security_baseline.value: API key never logged or written to disk; TLS certificate verification enforced for all SAM.gov requests; key masked in __repr__
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents accidental API key leakage through application logs or debug output

frontend_stack.value: None
frontend_stack.rationale: Library SDK has no UI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with httpx and Pydantic v2
backend_stack.rationale: httpx provides async-ready HTTP with connection pooling; Pydantic v2 provides runtime validation

data_platform.value: None; SDK is stateless with no persistent storage
data_platform.rationale: All response data lives in memory and is returned to the caller

hosting_target.value: PyPI package registry
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python package distribution channel

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained by SDK; callers are responsible for any storage of SAM.gov response data per their own privacy policies
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK is a stateless pass-through tool

future_scope.value: Async client variant using httpx AsyncClient, optional in-memory exclusions cache, CLI wrapper using Click, SAM.gov beta v2 API endpoint support
future_scope.rationale: Async and caching are top community feature requests

constraints.value: Python 3.9+ compatibility required; only httpx and pydantic as mandatory dependencies; must support FAR Part 9.4 exclusion check workflow without additional configuration
constraints.rationale: Broadest compatibility with existing procurement application Python environments

observability_baseline.value: Structured DEBUG logging via Python logging module for HTTP requests and responses; no telemetry collected from SDK consumers
observability_baseline.rationale: Logging enables caller-side debugging without compromising consumer privacy

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development with semantic versioning; PyPI publish on each tagged release via GitHub Actions OIDC trusted publisher
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source library release cadence

project.goal: Python SDK abstracting SAM.gov API entity and opportunity endpoints for procurement developers
project.target_users: Python developers in procurement compliance and contract monitoring applications

backend.framework: httpx 0.27 for HTTP, Pydantic v2 for response models

security.auth: API key passthrough — never logged or persisted by SDK
security.transport: TLS certificate verification enforced via httpx defaults

data.models: Pydantic v2 response models for EntityRecord, ExclusionRecord, OpportunityListing, AwardNotice

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, pyright type check, pytest, PyPI publish on tag via OIDC trusted publisher

constraints.dependencies: httpx and pydantic only as mandatory dependencies

reasoner_reviews.product: The three core SAM.gov endpoints — entity lookup, exclusions check, and opportunities search — cover the majority of procurement developer use cases. Pagination abstraction and retry logic are essential for a production-worthy library; without them developers would need to re-implement these across every consuming application.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Pydantic v2 strict mode disabled is correct for SAM.gov response parsing because the API occasionally returns partial records. Composing three sub-clients under SamGovClient provides a clean API surface while keeping each endpoint domain cohesive. The sync-first design is appropriate for v1; an async variant can share transport infrastructure in v2.
reasoner_reviews.security: API key masking in __repr__ is the most likely leakage vector and must be verified at the class level. httpx enforces TLS certificate verification by default, which is correct. Documentation must explicitly warn callers against passing API keys as command-line arguments where they appear in process listings.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Python 3.9 minimum compatibility is achievable without significant syntax restrictions. The zero-extra-dependency constraint is met by httpx and pydantic, which are standard in modern Python environments. PyPI trusted publisher eliminates the need for a stored PyPI API token in CI secrets.

backend.service_design: SamGovClient top-level class composes EntityClient, ExclusionsClient, and OpportunitiesClient; each sub-client accepts the shared httpx transport and api_key header injection via a custom Auth class
backend.api_patterns: All public methods return typed Pydantic v2 model instances or PageIterator[T] for paginated endpoints; errors raised as SamGovAPIError(status_code, message), SamGovRateLimitError, SamGovNotFoundError
backend.failure_handling: RetryTransport wraps httpx with exponential backoff capped at 60 seconds for 429 and 5xx responses; configurable max_retries parameter on SamGovClient constructor
backend.business_modules: samgov.client (SamGovClient), samgov.entities (EntityClient, EntityRecord), samgov.exclusions (ExclusionsClient, ExclusionRecord), samgov.opportunities (OpportunitiesClient, OpportunityListing, AwardNotice), samgov.pagination (PageIterator), samgov.exceptions (SamGovAPIError, SamGovRateLimitError, SamGovNotFoundError)
backend.backend_risks: SAM.gov API schema changes break Pydantic validation for required fields; mitigation is a VCR-style cassette test suite that records and replays real API responses in CI

security.auth_design: API key injected as X-Api-Key header via httpx Auth class that never exposes the raw key in logs or exceptions
security.secrets_management: No key storage in SDK; documentation recommends os.environ or secrets managers such as AWS Secrets Manager for key sourcing in production applications
security.security_risks: Callers may print SamGovClient instances in debug logs; __repr__ masking is the primary mitigation; documentation provides an explicit warning

data.entities: EntityRecord (uei: str, cage_code: str | None, legal_business_name: str, registration_status: Literal['active','inactive','expired'], expiration_date: date | None, naics_codes: list[str]). ExclusionRecord (exclusion_name: str, uei: str | None, cage_code: str | None, exclusion_type: str, active_date: date, termination_date: date | None). OpportunityListing (notice_id: str, title: str, naics_code: str, posted_date: date, response_deadline: datetime | None, set_aside_type: str | None, awarding_agency_name: str). AwardNotice (award_id: str, contract_number: str, awardee_uei: str, award_amount: Decimal, award_date: date, naics_code: str)
data.storage_design: No storage layer; all models are in-memory Pydantic objects returned to callers
data.data_risks: SAM.gov may return incomplete records for recently updated entities; Pydantic v2 strict mode disabled allows None coercion; callers must handle optional fields

devops.deployment_topology: Single PyPI package published from GitHub repository with no hosted infrastructure
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on push: ruff check, pyright, pytest with pytest-httpx mocked fixtures; publish job on semver tag via PyPI OIDC trusted publisher (no stored token)
devops.rollback_strategy: Yank PyPI release for critical bugs using twine yank; patch release with fix published immediately; CHANGELOG.md updated per release

target_output.thinking_summary: A simple stateless Python library wrapping three SAM.gov API endpoints. No hosted infrastructure. Key design decisions: Pydantic v2 for runtime validation against SAM.gov's occasionally incomplete response schemas, RetryTransport for 429 handling, and PageIterator to abstract cursor pagination. Weak quality: observability and cost sections are necessarily thin for a stateless package library.
target_output.title: SAM.gov Python SDK Package Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An open-source Python library providing typed, paginated access to the federal SAM.gov API for entity registration lookups, FAR Part 9.4 exclusion checks, and NAICS-filtered contract opportunities searches. Distributed on PyPI, the SDK wraps httpx and Pydantic v2 to return structured EntityRecord, ExclusionRecord, and OpportunityListing objects with built-in retry and pagination, eliminating repetitive SAM.gov client code from procurement applications.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure Python library with no hosted server component. SamGovClient is the top-level entry point accepting api_key, timeout, and max_retries parameters. It composes three sub-clients: EntityClient for UEI and CAGE code lookups, ExclusionsClient for debarment status checks required by FAR Part 9.4, and OpportunitiesClient for NAICS-filtered solicitation and award searches. A shared RetryTransport handles 429 rate-limit and 5xx error retry with exponential backoff. Pydantic v2 validates and deserializes all SAM.gov JSON responses into typed Python models. PageIterator wraps SAM.gov cursor-based pagination so callers iterate over result batches without managing page tokens directly. The library is stateless — no data persists between client calls.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.9+ for broad compatibility with existing procurement application environments. httpx 0.27 for HTTP client with connection pooling, TLS verification, and custom transport extensibility. Pydantic v2 for response model validation with strict mode disabled to tolerate SAM.gov schema variations. ruff for linting. pyright for static type checking. pytest with pytest-httpx for unit tests against mocked SAM.gov responses. GitHub Actions for CI and PyPI OIDC trusted publisher release.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Entity Lookup: query SAM.gov entity registrations by UEI or CAGE code, returning EntityRecord with registration_status, expiration_date, and naics_codes. Exclusions Check: verify whether a UEI or CAGE code appears on the SAM.gov exclusions list per FAR Part 9.4, returning ExclusionRecord or None. Opportunities Search: query open solicitations filtered by naics_codes, posted date range, and set_aside_type, returning PageIterator[OpportunityListing]. Award Notice Retrieval: fetch awarded contract notices by NAICS code, returning PageIterator[AwardNotice]. Pagination: PageIterator yields batches without requiring callers to manage cursor tokens.
target_output.system_components: SamGovClient (top-level entry point with api_key, base_url, timeout, max_retries parameters), EntityClient (lookup_by_uei and lookup_by_cage methods), ExclusionsClient (check_exclusion method returning ExclusionRecord or None), OpportunitiesClient (search returning PageIterator[OpportunityListing], get_award_notices returning PageIterator[AwardNotice]), RetryTransport (httpx custom transport implementing exponential backoff for 429 and 5xx), PageIterator[T] (generic iterator over SAM.gov cursor-paginated responses), SamGovAPIKeyAuth (httpx Auth class injecting X-Api-Key header with masked __repr__).
target_output.workflows: Exclusion Check Workflow: a procurement compliance application calls client.exclusions.check_exclusion(uei='XYZ123456789') before awarding a subcontract; SDK sends GET /entity-information/v3/entities with uei filter, parses response, returns ExclusionRecord if the entity appears on the exclusions list or None if clear; application logs the result with timestamp to satisfy FAR Part 9.4 documentation requirements. Opportunities Monitor Workflow: a developer passes naics_codes=['541511'] and response_deadline_from=date.today() to client.opportunities.search(); PageIterator yields batches of OpportunityListing objects; application processes each batch to extract solicitation_number and response_deadline for downstream notification logic, requesting the next page only when the current batch is consumed.
target_output.data_model: EntityRecord (uei: str, cage_code: str | None, legal_business_name: str, registration_status: Literal['active','inactive','expired'], expiration_date: date | None, naics_codes: list[str], physical_address: AddressModel). ExclusionRecord (exclusion_name: str, uei: str | None, cage_code: str | None, exclusion_type: str, exclusion_program: str, active_date: date, termination_date: date | None). OpportunityListing (notice_id: str, title: str, solicitation_number: str | None, naics_code: str, posted_date: date, response_deadline: datetime | None, set_aside_type: str | None, awarding_agency_name: str). AwardNotice (award_id: str, contract_number: str, awardee_uei: str, award_amount: Decimal, award_date: date, naics_code: str). All models use Pydantic v2 with strict mode disabled to handle SAM.gov's optional and nullable fields.
target_output.api_design: Public API: SamGovClient(api_key: str, base_url: str = 'https://api.sam.gov', timeout: int = 30, max_retries: int = 3). EntityClient.lookup_by_uei(uei: str) -> EntityRecord. EntityClient.lookup_by_cage(cage_code: str) -> EntityRecord. ExclusionsClient.check_exclusion(uei: str | None = None, cage_code: str | None = None) -> ExclusionRecord | None. OpportunitiesClient.search(naics_codes: list[str], posted_from: date | None, posted_to: date | None, set_aside_type: str | None, limit: int = 10) -> PageIterator[OpportunityListing]. OpportunitiesClient.get_award_notices(naics_code: str, award_from: date, award_to: date) -> PageIterator[AwardNotice]. Exceptions: SamGovAPIError(status_code, message), SamGovRateLimitError, SamGovNotFoundError.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key injected via SamGovAPIKeyAuth httpx Auth class that sets X-Api-Key header; key value excluded from all log records via a custom logging.Filter installed on the 'samgov' logger in debug mode. SamGovClient.__repr__ returns 'SamGovClient(api_key=***)' to prevent key exposure in debug output. httpx enforces TLS certificate verification by default; the RetryTransport does not override verify=False. Documentation explicitly advises callers to source API keys from environment variables or secrets managers, never from command-line arguments. SAM.gov terms of service compliance and FAR Part 9.4 record-keeping requirements are caller responsibility.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: No hosted infrastructure. GitHub Actions CI on every push to main and PRs: (1) ruff check for lint errors, (2) pyright for type safety across all public methods, (3) pytest test suite running against pytest-httpx mocked SAM.gov API responses covering entity lookup, exclusion check, opportunities pagination, 429 retry, and 404 handling. Release workflow: engineer pushes a semver tag such as v1.2.0; GitHub Actions publish job authenticates to PyPI via OIDC trusted publisher and runs python -m build followed by twine upload — no stored PyPI API token required. Package metadata in pyproject.toml with python_requires='>=3.9' and dependencies=['httpx>=0.26', 'pydantic>=2.0'].
target_output.observability: Python logging integration: SDK emits DEBUG-level log records through the 'samgov' logger for each HTTP request (method, sanitized URL path with API key stripped) and response (status code, latency in ms). No telemetry or usage data collected from SDK consumers. Callers add a handler to logging.getLogger('samgov') to enable request tracing in their application logging pipeline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — PyPI distribution is free for open-source packages. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for a public repository. SDK throughput scales with the caller's SAM.gov API rate limit (default 10 requests/second per key). RetryTransport handles 429 responses automatically; high-volume callers should implement their own token bucket for bulk exclusion checks.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): EntityClient and ExclusionsClient with FAR Part 9.4 check support, Pydantic v2 models, RetryTransport, pytest suite with mocked responses for all error cases, README with quickstart examples, initial PyPI release. Phase 2 (3 weeks): OpportunitiesClient with PageIterator for solicitations and award notices, CHANGELOG, full docstring coverage. Phase 3 (future): async client variant using httpx AsyncClient, optional exclusions result caching, Click-based CLI wrapper.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public methods require complete type annotations and Google-style docstrings. Pydantic models use model_config with populate_by_name=True and alias_generator to match SAM.gov JSON keys without polluting Python attribute names. New SAM.gov endpoint support requires pytest-httpx fixtures recorded from real API responses before implementation proceeds. Semantic versioning enforced: patch for bug fixes, minor for new endpoint support, major for breaking API surface changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SAM.gov API schema changes silently break Pydantic validation for required fields in EntityRecord or ExclusionRecord. Mitigation: cassette-style test fixtures record real API responses and replay them in CI; a schema drift check runs weekly via GitHub Actions scheduled workflow. Risk: SAM.gov rate limits (typically 10 req/s per API key) cause SamGovRateLimitError in high-volume bulk exclusion check workflows. Mitigation: RetryTransport handles backoff; documentation advises callers to implement request throttling for batch processing. Tradeoff: sync-only httpx client in v1 simplifies the interface and avoids maintaining parallel async/sync codepaths; async variant deferred to v2.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected httpx over requests for connection pooling and async-readiness. Chose Pydantic v2 over dataclasses for runtime field validation against SAM.gov's inconsistent response schemas. Confirmed PyPI OIDC trusted publisher over stored API token for release security. Python 3.9 minimum chosen over 3.11+ to maximize compatibility with government agency application environments.

metadata.notes: Weak quality: observability and cost_and_scaling sections are at bare minimum — appropriate and accurate for a stateless PyPI library with no hosted infrastructure, but thinner than a hosted service row.

profile.domain: Automated Accessibility Conformance Reporting for State Agency Websites

project_goal.value: Research prototype crawling state agency websites with headless Chromium and axe-core, cataloging WCAG 2.1 AA violations, and using Azure OpenAI GPT-4o to generate developer-facing remediation guidance with before/after code examples
project_goal.rationale: Core mission to accelerate 508 remediation backlogs across state agencies

target_users.value: State agency web teams, IT accessibility coordinators, and DevOps engineers running remediation sprints
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of scan reports and AI-generated fix guidance

project_class.rationale: Experimental system with planned productionization pathway; scope bounded to a single agency cluster

capabilities.rationale: Crawling, classification, LLM inference, batch scheduling, and infrastructure automation are the active capability surfaces

complexity_level.rationale: Headless browser orchestration and LLM prompt chaining add integration complexity beyond a standard CRUD service

risk_level.rationale: Internal tool processing non-public agency metadata; no PII collected; LLM output quality risk managed by human review gate

data_sensitivity.rationale: Scan results and violation records are internal agency operational data, not public and not personal

external_exposure.rationale: Prototype is deployed on GCP within agency VPC; no public endpoints exposed

access_model.value: Custom JWT issued by agency IdP with role-based access distinguishing scan operators from report viewers
access_model.rationale: Minimal auth sufficient for internal prototype; tokens scoped per agency tenant

feature_scope.value: URL registration and scan scheduling, headless axe-core crawl engine, WCAG violation cataloging with screenshots, LLM remediation suggestion generation, conformance report export (PDF and JSON), audit history per agency website
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature surface for prototype evaluation by state CTO office

mvp_scope.value: Single-agency crawl, axe-core violation capture with screenshot evidence, LLM suggestion generation for top-20 violation patterns, JSON and PDF report export
mvp_scope.rationale: Sufficient to validate approach with pilot agency before multi-tenant rollout

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all internal service communication, JWT auth with 8-hour expiry, secrets in GCP Secret Manager, LLM API key rotation every 30 days
security_baseline.rationale: Internal prototype security posture appropriate for non-PII internal data

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity; scan dashboard and report viewer are relatively simple SPAs

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem best supports axe-core integration via Playwright and LLM SDK libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Relational structure suits violation cataloging, scan job state, and agency/website hierarchy

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API; Cloud Run Jobs for batch scan workers
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless execution model matches bursty batch scan workloads; avoids idle compute costs for prototype

privacy_retention_policy.value: Scan results and violation records retained for 2 years; screenshots purged after 90 days to control storage costs
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Two-year window supports year-over-year remediation trend analysis required by pilot program charter

future_scope.value: Multi-agency tenant support, CI/CD integration plugin for agency development pipelines, automated re-scan on deployment events, integration with state IT governance ticketing system
future_scope.rationale: Productionization roadmap contingent on prototype evaluation outcomes

constraints.value: LLM API calls must use agency-approved provider with no data retention; scan workers must not store raw page HTML outside ephemeral job context; budget under $500/month for prototype phase
constraints.rationale: Data handling and cost constraints set by state CTO office prototype governance policy

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for job metrics, structured JSON logs to Cloud Logging, scan job duration and violation count metrics as custom GCP metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Native GCP tooling minimizes operational overhead for a prototype deployment

execution_preference.value: Research sprints with bi-weekly demos to state CTO stakeholders; no formal Agile ceremony overhead
execution_preference.rationale: Prototype cadence prioritizes iteration speed over process formality

compliance_context.value: Section 508 (Rehabilitation Act), WCAG 2.1 AA as the technical standard, Architectural and Transportation Barriers Compliance Board rulemaking for ICT; outputs support agency 508 coordinator reporting obligations
compliance_context.rationale: Tool directly supports agency compliance obligations under Section 508; outputs used as evidence in remediation plans

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o via agency-approved Azure OpenAI endpoint with zero data retention; prompt templates encode WCAG criterion context and detected element HTML; model generates targeted fix guidance with before/after code snippets
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o provides sufficient code generation quality; Azure OpenAI satisfies agency data handling requirements

project.goal: Automate Section 508 conformance reporting and AI-assisted remediation guidance for state agency websites
project.target_users: State agency web teams and 508 accessibility coordinators
project.timeline: Prototype evaluation Q2; multi-agency rollout decision Q3

backend.framework: FastAPI with Playwright for headless crawl orchestration
backend.api: Internal REST API consumed by React dashboard

security.auth: Custom JWT from agency IdP
security.secrets: GCP Secret Manager for LLM API keys and database credentials

data.storage: GCS bucket for screenshots with 90-day lifecycle policy

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Cloud Build for container push
devops.hosting: Cloud Run API and Cloud Run Jobs for batch scan workers
devops.environments: dev and prod only for prototype

constraints.llm_data_retention: Zero retention on Azure OpenAI endpoint

reasoner_reviews.product: The prototype scope is appropriately bounded to a single agency with a clear evaluation milestone. The combination of automated axe-core scanning and LLM remediation guidance addresses both detection and remediation phases of the 508 compliance workflow, which prior tools handled separately. PDF and JSON dual export satisfies both human coordinators and downstream tooling consumers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using Cloud Run Jobs for scan workers is a sound fit for bursty, page-count-variable crawl workloads. The violation pattern taxonomy should be defined as a controlled enum so LLM prompts can reference specific WCAG criterion IDs rather than free-text descriptions. Round 2 identified a gap in prompt versioning; the current plan addresses this with a prompt_templates table in PostgreSQL tracking criterion_id and model_version.
reasoner_reviews.security: LLM calls must never include raw user-submitted content beyond the scanned HTML element context; prompt injection risk from malicious page content is real and must be mitigated. The zero-retention Azure OpenAI configuration must be validated against the specific deployment endpoint before go-live. Round 3 focus is the screenshot GCS bucket IAM policy which must restrict access to the scan worker service account exclusively.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Budget of $500/month is achievable given Cloud Run Jobs only bill during execution; screenshot storage costs must be monitored against the 90-day purge policy. The agency-approved provider constraint eliminates open-source local models and limits fallback options if Azure OpenAI availability degrades.
reasoner_reviews.critic: ISS-003 from round 2 (screenshot bucket IAM policy) is the only remaining blocking issue. The fix must specify uniform bucket-level IAM, Public Access Prevention enforcement, and Cloud Audit Log DATA_READ logging for all GetObject events.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application split into three modules: scan_orchestrator (Cloud Run Jobs entrypoint managing Playwright worker pool), violation_api (CRUD endpoints for scan targets and results), and suggestion_api (LLM prompt construction and response parsing). Shared PostgreSQL connection pool via asyncpg.
backend.api_patterns: REST endpoints: POST /scan-jobs (queue crawl), GET /scan-jobs/{id}/status, GET /reports/{agency_id}/latest, POST /reports/{report_id}/export. LLM suggestion endpoint is async with webhook callback for long-running generation.
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Run Jobs execute scan batches; each job handles up to 20 concurrent pages using Playwright browser contexts; job parameters passed via Cloud Run Job environment overrides.
backend.failure_handling: Playwright page timeouts default 30s; failed pages marked with error_type ENUM (timeout/navigation_error/axe_failure/oom_killed) and excluded from report totals. LLM generation failures fall back to template-based suggestion for the affected criterion.
backend.llm_integration_design: Prompt templates versioned in prompt_templates table with criterion_id, template_text, model_version columns. Violations batched by wcag_criterion to minimize token calls. Parsed suggestions stored in remediation_suggestions table linked to violation records by violation_id.

security.auth_design: FastAPI dependency injects JWT validation; tokens carry agency_id and role (operator/viewer) claims. Report export endpoints require operator role claim.
security.secrets_management: LLM API key and DB password stored in GCP Secret Manager; Cloud Run service account granted secretAccessor via Workload Identity; 30-day automated rotation via Secret Manager version rotation.
security.abuse_prevention: Internal-only deployment; rate limiting on scan submission (5 active scan jobs per agency) prevents runaway crawl costs. Prompt injection mitigation: scanned HTML elements HTML-escaped and character-limited to 2000 chars before inclusion in LLM prompt.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Screenshots GCS bucket: uniform bucket-level IAM grants storage.objectViewer exclusively to scan-worker service account; Public Access Prevention set to enforced; Cloud Audit Logs DATA_READ type enabled on bucket; operator download actions logged to audit_records table.

data.entities: scan_targets (id UUID PK, agency_id UUID FK, base_url, crawl_depth SMALLINT, active BOOLEAN). scan_jobs (id UUID PK, target_id FK, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, page_count, violation_count). accessibility_violations (id UUID PK, job_id FK, page_url, wcag_criterion VARCHAR(20), severity ENUM critical/serious/moderate/minor, element_selector, screenshot_gcs_key). remediation_suggestions (id UUID PK, violation_id FK, criterion_id, suggestion_text, code_before, code_after, model_version). conformance_reports (id UUID PK, agency_id FK, job_id FK, format ENUM json/pdf, gcs_key). prompt_templates (id UUID PK, criterion_id, template_text, model_version, active BOOLEAN). audit_records (id UUID PK, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, performed_at).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 for relational data; GCS screenshots bucket with uniform bucket-level IAM, Public Access Prevention enforced, 90-day Object Lifecycle Management rule; GCS reports bucket with 2-year retention.
data.retention_and_deletion: Screenshots purged at 90 days via GCS lifecycle policy. Violation records and suggestions retained 2 years. Conformance reports retained 2 years per pilot charter.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run API (min 0, max 3 instances, 512MB RAM) behind Cloud Armor internal ingress. Cloud Run Jobs (4GB RAM per instance, 20 concurrent pages per job). Cloud SQL in same VPC region.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions builds Docker on PR merge, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys Cloud Run revision with 0/100 canary traffic split. Job images tagged by Git SHA, updated via gcloud CLI in CD step.
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: scan_job_duration_seconds, violations_per_page, llm_suggestion_latency_ms, job_failure_rate. Cloud Logging structured JSON. PagerDuty alert on job_failure_rate greater than 20% over 60-minute window.
devops.cost_controls: Cloud Run Jobs billed per CPU-second; 20-page concurrency cap bounds costs. Monthly GCP Budget alert at $400 (80% of cap) notifies engineering Slack channel.
