fix_report.action_taken: Specified uniform bucket-level IAM binding on screenshots GCS bucket: storage.objectViewer granted exclusively to scan-worker@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com; Public Access Prevention set to enforced; Cloud Audit Log data access type DATA_READ enabled on bucket; all operator GetObject actions logged to audit_records table with user_id, resource_id, and performed_at.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Screenshots GCS bucket inaccessible to all principals except the scan worker service account; no public listing possible; all object access events auditable via Cloud Audit Logs and internal audit_records table.

target_output.title: Section 508 Automated Conformance Prototype - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: GCP-deployed research prototype orchestrating headless Chromium crawls of state agency websites using Playwright and axe-core, cataloging WCAG 2.1 AA violations with screenshot evidence, and generating developer-targeted remediation guidance via Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with zero data retention. Scan workers execute as Cloud Run Jobs with a 20-page concurrency cap; results persist in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL; conformance reports export as JSON and PDF. Designed for evaluation by a pilot agency before multi-tenant rollout to all state agencies.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier serverless architecture on GCP: React 18 SPA on Cloud Run communicates with a FastAPI backend (Cloud Run, min 0/max 3 instances) over internal HTTPS. Scan orchestration runs as Cloud Run Jobs spawning Playwright browser contexts against registered agency URLs, writing violation records to Cloud SQL and screenshots to GCS. LLM remediation suggestions generated asynchronously via Azure OpenAI GPT-4o using versioned prompt templates keyed by WCAG criterion ID. All services operate within a shared VPC with no public ingress beyond the Cloud Armor-protected API endpoint. Screenshot GCS bucket enforces uniform bucket-level IAM and Public Access Prevention.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Playwright 1.44, axe-core 4.9 via axe-playwright, asyncpg, Azure OpenAI Python SDK. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL. Storage: GCS screenshots bucket (90-day lifecycle, IAM-restricted) and GCS reports bucket (2-year retention). Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, TanStack Query. Infrastructure: Cloud Run, Cloud Run Jobs, Cloud SQL, GCS, Cloud Armor, GCP Secret Manager, Artifact Registry. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o zero-retention deployment endpoint.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scan Target Registry: agency admins register base URLs with crawl depth (1-5) and schedule frequency. Scan Job Execution: Cloud Run Job crawls URLs, runs axe-core on each page DOM, stores violations with element selector, WCAG criterion, severity, and screenshot GCS key. Violation Explorer: filterable table by criterion (e.g., 1.1.1 Non-text Content, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels, 4.1.3 Status Messages) and severity. LLM Suggestion Engine: async batch generation grouping by criterion, storing before/after code examples. Conformance Report Export: agency-branded PDF and machine-readable JSON with summary statistics and remediation inventory. Audit History: per-agency scan timeline with violation count trends across successive scans.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run API (FastAPI): scan job submission, violation CRUD, suggestion retrieval, report generation endpoints. Cloud Run Jobs (Playwright workers): browser-context pool with 20-page concurrency cap, 4GB memory per job instance. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: relational store for all application state including prompt_templates and audit_records. GCS screenshots bucket: uniform bucket-level IAM (scan worker service account only), Public Access Prevention enforced, DATA_READ audit logging enabled, 90-day Object Lifecycle. GCS reports bucket: PDF exports, 2-year retention. Azure OpenAI endpoint: GPT-4o inference, zero-retention configuration. GCP Secret Manager: LLM API key and DB credentials, 30-day rotation. Cloud Armor: internal-only security policy on Cloud Run ingress.
target_output.workflows: Scan Execution: operator submits POST /scan-jobs with target_id via dashboard; API creates scan_jobs record status=queued and enqueues Cloud Run Job; job instance launches Playwright, opens up to 20 concurrent browser contexts, navigates each page URL, injects axe-core, captures violations with element selector and severity, stores screenshots to IAM-restricted GCS bucket and violation records to PostgreSQL, marks job completed. LLM Suggestion Generation: on job completion a background task groups new accessibility_violations by wcag_criterion, constructs prompts from active prompt_templates row matching criterion_id and model_version, submits batch to Azure OpenAI GPT-4o zero-retention endpoint, parses structured JSON response with suggestion_text and code_before/code_after, stores to remediation_suggestions table linked by violation_id.
target_output.data_model: scan_targets (id UUID PK, agency_id UUID FK, base_url TEXT, crawl_depth SMALLINT 1-5, schedule_cron TEXT, active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_jobs (id UUID PK, target_id UUID FK, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, page_count INT, violation_count INT). accessibility_violations (id UUID PK, job_id UUID FK, page_url TEXT, wcag_criterion VARCHAR(20), severity ENUM critical/serious/moderate/minor, element_selector TEXT, screenshot_gcs_key TEXT, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). remediation_suggestions (id UUID PK, violation_id UUID FK, criterion_id VARCHAR(20), suggestion_text TEXT, code_before TEXT, code_after TEXT, model_version VARCHAR(20), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). conformance_reports (id UUID PK, agency_id UUID FK, job_id UUID FK, format ENUM json/pdf, gcs_key TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). prompt_templates (id UUID PK, criterion_id VARCHAR(20), template_text TEXT, model_version VARCHAR(20), active BOOLEAN). audit_records (id UUID PK, user_id UUID, action VARCHAR(50), resource_type VARCHAR(50), resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, performed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /scan-jobs {target_id, depth_override?} - enqueue new crawl job returning scan_job id. GET /scan-jobs/{id}/status - polling endpoint returning {status, page_count, violation_count, error_summary}. GET /agencies/{agency_id}/violations?criterion=1.1.1&severity=critical&job_id= - filtered violation list with pagination. GET /agencies/{agency_id}/reports/latest - most recent conformance report metadata and download URL. POST /reports/{report_id}/export {format: json|pdf} - async export trigger returning export_job_id. GET /export-jobs/{id} - export status and signed GCS download URL. All endpoints require Bearer JWT; export endpoints require operator role claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: custom JWT issued by agency IdP, validated via FastAPI dependency; tokens carry agency_id and role (operator/viewer) claims; 8-hour expiry with forced re-authentication for report export actions. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager stores Azure OpenAI API key and PostgreSQL password; Cloud Run service account granted secretAccessor role via Workload Identity; 30-day automated rotation. Screenshots GCS bucket hardening (ISS-003 resolution): uniform bucket-level IAM policy grants storage.objectViewer exclusively to scan-worker@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com; Public Access Prevention set to enforced at bucket level; Cloud Audit Logs DATA_READ log type enabled; all operator GetObject actions logged to audit_records table (user_id, action=screenshot_download, resource_id=screenshot_gcs_key, ip_address, performed_at). LLM prompt injection mitigation: HTML element content HTML-escaped and truncated to 2000 chars before inclusion in prompt context. Section 508 compliance context: tool outputs serve as evidence artifacts for agency 508 coordinator reporting per Architectural and Transportation Barriers Compliance Board requirements.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run API: containerized FastAPI image in Artifact Registry, min 0/max 3 instances, 512MB RAM, Cloud Armor security policy restricting ingress to agency VPC CIDR range. Cloud Run Jobs: scan worker image, 4GB RAM per instance, max 20 concurrent pages per job execution, target_id and concurrency_cap injected as environment overrides at job submission. Cloud SQL: PostgreSQL 16, db-n1-standard-2, daily automated backups with 7-day retention, us-central1 matching Cloud Run region. GCS buckets provisioned via Terraform with IAM bindings, lifecycle rules, and Public Access Prevention defined as infrastructure code; no manual gcloud iam commands permitted on buckets. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy strict check, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry on main merge. CD: gcloud run deploy with zero-traffic canary, automated smoke test hitting GET /scan-jobs/health, then 100% traffic promotion.
target_output.observability: Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: scan_job_duration_seconds histogram by target_id, violations_per_page gauge, llm_suggestion_latency_ms histogram, job_failure_rate counter per job type. Cloud Logging structured JSON with job_id and agency_id correlation fields on all log entries enabling cross-service trace correlation. Alerting: PagerDuty notification on job_failure_rate exceeding 20% over 60-minute window and on monthly GCP spend reaching $400 (80% of $500 prototype cap) via GCP Budget API.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run Jobs bill per CPU-second; 20-page concurrency cap prevents runaway scan costs for large agency websites. Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 approximately $60/month. GCS screenshots storage at $0.02/GB/month; 90-day purge keeps storage under 10GB. Azure OpenAI GPT-4o billed per token; batching suggestions by criterion reduces API calls significantly. Estimated total $200-350/month for a 10-agency pilot with weekly scans, well within $500 cap.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (complete): single-agency Playwright crawl, axe-core violation capture for 8 WCAG criteria, screenshot storage, PostgreSQL persistence, JSON export. Phase 2 (current round): LLM suggestion generation with prompt template versioning (ISS-002 resolved), 20-page concurrency cap (ISS-001 resolved), screenshot bucket IAM hardening (ISS-003 resolved), PDF report export, scan job monitoring dashboard. Phase 3 (productionization): multi-agency tenant isolation with agency_id row-level security, CI/CD pipeline integration plugin emitting scan results on deployment webhooks, automated ITSM ticket creation for critical violations.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linter, mypy strict, pytest with 85% coverage gate. Playwright integration tests run against an isolated Chromium fixture with a local test HTML page containing known WCAG violations. Prompt template changes require a new prompt_templates row with incremented model_version; existing active templates must not be edited in place. All GCS IAM and lifecycle policy changes managed via Terraform; manual gcloud iam commands on buckets are blocked by repository policy. Feature flags via environment variables for multi-tenant preview features.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Playwright OOM on JavaScript-heavy government web applications. Mitigation: 4GB job instance memory with automatic Cloud Run Job retry on OOM exit; pages exceeding memory limits flagged error_type=oom_killed. Risk: Azure OpenAI endpoint availability affecting suggestion generation. Mitigation: async suggestion generation with fallback to criterion-specific static templates; scan results stored independently of LLM step so reports remain available. Risk: axe-core false negatives on dynamic content rendered after JavaScript execution delays; mitigation is configurable page settle timeout defaulting to 2 seconds after DOMContentLoaded. Tradeoff: Cloud Run Jobs cold start latency (~10s) is acceptable for batch scans but would require architectural revisit if interactive single-page scan is added in Phase 3.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Cloud Run Jobs over GKE to avoid cluster management overhead at prototype scale. Chose axe-core over Pa11y for programmatic API integration with Playwright browser contexts. Confirmed Azure OpenAI over direct OpenAI API to satisfy agency zero-retention data handling requirement. Decided PDF export uses WeasyPrint server-side to ensure accessible document output structure.

metadata.notes: Round 3 surgical fix for ISS-003 screenshot GCS bucket IAM hardening; all three issues resolved by end of this round.

profile.domain: Election Administration / Government Data

project_goal.value: Ingest nightly voter registration extracts from 67 county election offices, standardize addresses via USPS CASS, deduplicate records deterministically, and load the authoritative state voter file into Snowflake for election administration reporting and NVRA list maintenance programs
project_goal.rationale: Core statutory obligation for the state election division to maintain an accurate statewide voter file

target_users.value: State election division staff, county election officials, authorized list-maintenance program administrators
target_users.rationale: Internal government users with role-differentiated access to voter data

project_class.rationale: Pure ETL workload; Airflow orchestrates batch DAGs with no end-user UI surface

capabilities.rationale: All capabilities are backend data processing; no frontend or auth UI required beyond IAM role enforcement

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-county schema harmonization, deterministic deduplication, CASS address standardization, and NVRA list maintenance integrations represent significant data engineering complexity

risk_level.rationale: Voter registration data errors could disenfranchise voters; a breach would expose voter PII and undermine public trust in election integrity

data_sensitivity.rationale: Voter records contain PII including name, DOB, SSN-4, driver license number, address, and registration status

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs entirely within AWS GovCloud VPC; county SFTP endpoints are network-peered, not internet-exposed

access_model.value: IAM roles with least-privilege per pipeline stage; Snowflake RBAC with analyst, county_viewer, and data_custodian roles; row access policies restrict county_viewer to own jurisdiction
access_model.rationale: NVRA and state statute require access controls limiting voter PII to authorized election officials only

feature_scope.value: County SFTP extract ingestion, schema validation, CASS address standardization with caching, deterministic voter deduplication, Snowflake SCD Type 2 load, monthly NCOA/death/DMV list maintenance runs, per-county data quality reports
feature_scope.rationale: Covers the full NVRA-mandated voter file maintenance lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Nightly county extract ingestion, schema validation, address standardization, deduplication, and Snowflake load for all 67 counties with quality report generation
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum required for election division to retire the legacy mainframe voter file system by statutory deadline

security_baseline.value: Encryption at rest via AWS KMS CMKs for S3 and Snowflake; TLS 1.3 for all data-in-transit; SFTP with public-key auth only; VPC-private endpoints; SSN-4 and DL tokenized before landing zone write; no PII in logs
security_baseline.rationale: GovCloud baseline and state voter data protection statute requirements

frontend_stack.value: None - pipeline has no UI; county submission status exposed via Snowflake reporting views queried by authorized tools
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class with no frontend specialist required

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA; custom Airflow operators for SFTP ingestion, CASS batch API, deduplication, and Snowflake COPY INTO loads
backend_stack.rationale: MWAA eliminates Airflow infrastructure management; Python ecosystem has mature libraries for address standardization and data quality

data_platform.value: Snowflake Business Critical tier on GovCloud as the central voter file warehouse; S3 for raw extract landing zone and processed file archival; no relational OLTP database
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake supports the multi-terabyte voter file with separation of storage and compute for cost-efficient batch workloads

hosting_target.value: AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1; MWAA for Airflow orchestration, S3 for landing and archive zones, Snowflake Business Critical for voter data warehouse
hosting_target.rationale: FedRAMP Moderate authorization requirement; voter data must remain in US-sovereign GovCloud regions

privacy_retention_policy.value: Active voter records retained indefinitely per state election code; cancelled/rejected records retained 7 years; NCOA and DMV match inputs purged after 30 days; audit logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State election code mandates retention periods for voter file history and list maintenance documentation

future_scope.value: Real-time county registration event streaming, online voter registration API integration, interstate crosscheck data exchange, voter history file integration
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements moving toward real-time voter file synchronization

constraints.value: Must complete nightly pipeline run within 4-hour window (02:00-06:00); county extract formats vary across 67 counties with no standardization mandate; cannot store voter SSN-4 or DL number in Snowflake in plaintext
constraints.rationale: 4-hour window preserves system availability for county daytime use; PII tokenization required by state statute

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards pulling Prometheus metrics from MWAA via CloudWatch exporter; Airflow task-level structured logs in CloudWatch; data quality metric time series in Snowflake; PagerDuty alerts on pipeline failures and quality threshold breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Election division requires visibility into per-county ingestion success rates and data quality trends

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI for pipeline code; Terraform for infrastructure; DAG changes require election division IT sign-off in GitLab MR before production deploy
execution_preference.rationale: Change management controls required for election-critical systems

compliance_context.value: NVRA Section 8 list maintenance requirements; HAVA Section 303 voter registration database standards; state election code voter data confidentiality provisions; state statutes governing SSN and DL number handling
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=personal trigger mandatory compliance context

project.goal: Nightly voter registration ETL from 67 county SFTP endpoints into Snowflake with CASS standardization, deduplication, and NVRA list maintenance
project.target_users: State election division staff and authorized county officials
project.timeline: Go-live before next primary election cycle

backend.operators: Custom SFTPIngest, CASSStandardize, Deduplication, and SnowflakeLoad operators

security.auth: IAM roles per pipeline stage; Snowflake RBAC with row access policies
security.pii_handling: SSN-4 and DL tokenized via AWS KMS before S3 write

data.landing: S3 with KMS CMK per-county prefix encryption
data.format: County-specific CSV schemas normalized to canonical voter schema via per-county YAML adapters

constraints.pii_restriction: No plaintext SSN-4 or DL in Snowflake

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope correctly covers the full NVRA list maintenance lifecycle. Nightly county extract ingestion with SCD Type 2 history preservation is essential for audit trails during contested election recounts. The 4-hour processing window is achievable with parallel county ingestion but requires careful capacity planning for peak election-cycle periods when extract volumes spike.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: MWAA with custom Airflow operators is the right orchestration layer for this workload. Parallelizing county ingestion via Airflow dynamic task mapping over all 67 counties is mandatory to fit the 4-hour window. CASS API rate limits must be accounted for - batch address submissions at 1000 addresses per call and cache CASS responses for recently standardized addresses. Snowflake COPY INTO with file format staging is preferable to row-by-row inserts for bulk load performance.
reasoner_reviews.security: KMS tokenization of SSN-4 and DL numbers before the S3 landing zone write is correctly identified as mandatory. Snowflake dynamic data masking must restrict raw token visibility to the data_custodian role only. SFTP key rotation needs a formalized schedule - quarterly rotation with 2-week overlap prevents county connectivity disruptions. CloudTrail must capture all S3 GetObject events on the voter file landing bucket.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour processing window with 67 counties requires averaging under 4 minutes per county for sequential runs, which is unachievable without parallelism. Dynamic task mapping running 15 concurrent county ingestions is essential. A dedicated Snowflake X-Large warehouse during the nightly load window avoids contention with analyst queries.

backend.service_design: Three Airflow DAGs: CountyIngestionDAG (nightly 02:00, dynamic task mapping over 67 county configs with 15 concurrent workers), ListMaintenanceDAG (monthly first Sunday, sequential NCOA/death/DMV match stages), DataQualityDAG (triggered by CountyIngestionDAG completion sensor). Custom operators: SFTPIngestOperator (paramiko SFTP, schema version detection, KMS tokenization before S3 write), CASSStandardizeOperator (1000-address batch submission, S3-backed 90-day response cache), DeduplicationOperator (deterministic match on name+DOB+ssn4_token with Jaro-Winkler fuzzy fallback), SnowflakeLoadOperator (COPY INTO staging then SCD Type 2 merge into CANONICAL schema).
backend.business_modules: voter_deduplication module: deterministic match on (first_name, last_name, dob, ssn4_token) with Jaro-Winkler fuzzy fallback for name variants, unresolved probabilistic matches queued for data custodian review. county_schema_adapter module: per-county YAML field mapping configs driving a schema normalization layer. list_maintenance module: NCOA file parser, state death registry matcher, DMV change feed processor, NVRA Section 8 maintenance notice generator.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly CountyIngestionDAG at 02:00 with 4-hour SLA; monthly ListMaintenanceDAG first Sunday of each month; post-load DataQualityDAG triggered by success sensor; weekly ArchivalJob moving S3 raw extracts older than 90 days to Glacier.
backend.failure_handling: Per-county task failures isolated via dynamic task mapping - 1 county failure does not block others. Failed counties logged to data_quality_reports table for morning review. MWAA task retries with exponential backoff (3 retries, 5/15/30 min intervals). CASS API failures fall back to cached results for addresses seen in last 90 days. Pipeline SLA breach triggers PagerDuty P1 with county-level failure breakdown.

security.auth_design: MWAA execution role scoped to S3 landing bucket and Secrets Manager paths per DAG; Snowflake uses key-pair auth with private keys in AWS Secrets Manager; no user credentials in DAG code or MWAA environment variables.
security.secrets_management: County SFTP credentials, CASS API keys, and Snowflake key-pairs in AWS Secrets Manager; 90-day automatic rotation enforced; DAGs retrieve secrets at runtime via Airflow SecretsManagerBackend integration.
security.privacy_controls: SSN-4 and DL numbers tokenized via KMS Encrypt in SFTPIngestOperator before any S3 write; Snowflake dynamic data masking on voter_ssn4_token and voter_dl_token restricting raw values to data_custodian role; Snowflake row access policies on voter_registrations restricting county_viewer role to rows matching session-context county_code.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail S3 data events on voter-file landing bucket; Airflow task logs in CloudWatch with county_code, operator name, record counts, no PII; Snowflake ACCESS_HISTORY queried nightly for anomalous access pattern detection; all list maintenance actions written to list_maintenance_actions table with federal NVRA documentation IDs.

data.entities: voter_registrations (vr_id UUID PK, county_code, state_voter_id, first_name, last_name, dob DATE, address_standardized, precinct_id, registration_status ENUM active/inactive/cancelled/pending, registration_date, party_affiliation, voter_ssn4_token, voter_dl_token, valid_from TIMESTAMP, valid_to TIMESTAMP, is_current BOOL for SCD2). county_submissions (submission_id UUID, county_code, extract_date, s3_uri, record_count, schema_version, validation_status, loaded_at). deduplication_records (dup_id UUID, master_vr_id FK, duplicate_vr_id FK, match_method ENUM deterministic/probabilistic, match_score FLOAT, resolved_by, resolved_at). list_maintenance_actions (action_id UUID, vr_id FK, action_type ENUM ncoa_update/death_cancel/dmv_address_change, source_program, federal_doc_id, county_notified_at). data_quality_reports (report_id UUID, county_code, run_date, total_records, failed_validation, dedup_flagged, cass_cache_hits, quality_score FLOAT).
data.storage_design: S3 structure: voter-file-landing/{county_code}/{extract_date}/ (KMS CMK, raw county extracts); voter-file-processed/{county_code}/{extract_date}/ (normalized files); voter-file-archive/ (Glacier lifecycle at 90 days). Snowflake schemas: RAW (COPY INTO staging tables), CANONICAL (SCD2 voter_registrations with data masking and row access policies), REPORTING (jurisdiction-scoped views for county and analyst access).
data.retention_and_deletion: Active voter records retained indefinitely per state election code. Cancelled/rejected records: 7-year retention. Raw S3 extracts: 90-day active then Glacier for 7 years. NCOA and DMV match input files: purged from S3 after 30-day processing window. list_maintenance_actions: 5-year retention per NVRA documentation requirements.

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA environment in VPC private subnets with no internet gateway; S3 VPC endpoint for landing bucket; Snowflake PrivateLink endpoint in GovCloud VPC; GitLab CI runner in same VPC; county SFTP endpoints connected via VPC peering or Direct Connect.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI stages: flake8 + mypy lint, pytest unit tests with mocked operators (85% coverage gate), integration tests against Snowflake dev environment with synthetic voter data, Terraform plan requiring IT sign-off, staging deploy + smoke test, production deploy requiring election division IT manager approval via GitLab MR.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana: per-county nightly run status heatmap, record volume trends over 90 days, deduplication rate, CASS cache hit ratio, list maintenance action backlog per county. Prometheus metrics via CloudWatch exporter: airflow_task_duration labeled by county_code and operator, task failure counts, snowflake_load_records_total. PagerDuty P1: SLA breach or Snowflake load failure. P2: county failure rate >10%, quality score <85% for any county, CASS quota utilization >80%.
devops.rollback_strategy: Snowflake time-travel enables voter_registrations rollback to any point within 90 days. Failed county loads produce no partial writes via Snowflake transaction atomicity. Airflow DAG version pinned by Git SHA; rollback by redeploying prior version and re-running affected date range. Terraform state stored in versioned S3 bucket.

ISS-001.title: CASS API rate limit not addressed in operator design
ISS-001.detail: Prior plan did not specify CASS API batch sizing or caching strategy; with 4M+ addresses across 67 counties, unconstrained per-address API calls would exhaust daily quotas and stall the pipeline.

ISS-002.title: SSN-4 and DL plaintext exposure in S3 landing zone
ISS-002.detail: Original design wrote raw county extracts including plaintext SSN-4 and driver license numbers to S3 before tokenization, violating state statute.

ISS-003.title: Sequential county ingestion violates 4-hour SLA
ISS-003.detail: Initial architecture processed 67 counties sequentially; estimated runtime was 9+ hours, exceeding the 02:00-06:00 processing window.

ISS-004.title: Missing Snowflake row access policy for county-scoped roles
ISS-004.detail: County election office roles could query voter registrations outside their jurisdiction; Snowflake row access policies were not specified until round 3.

focus_issues.title: CASS API rate limit not addressed in operator design
focus_issues.detail: CASSStandardizeOperator now uses 1000-address batch submission with S3-backed 90-day response cache; estimated 60% reduction in API calls for repeat addresses.

focus_issues.title: Missing Snowflake row access policy for county-scoped roles
focus_issues.detail: Snowflake row access policies added to voter_registrations and county_submissions restricting county_viewer role to rows matching session-context county_code.

latest_recommendations: Add CASS API batch submission (1000 addr/call) and S3-backed response caching
Specify Snowflake row access policies restricting county roles to their jurisdiction
Document PagerDuty escalation thresholds for data quality breaches

previous_plan.title: State Voter File ETL Pipeline Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Nightly Airflow pipeline on MWAA ingesting voter registration extracts from 67 county SFTP endpoints with parallel dynamic task mapping, KMS-tokenized PII, CASS address standardization, deterministic deduplication, and SCD Type 2 load into Snowflake on GovCloud.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Dynamic task mapping over 67 county SFTP endpoints with 15 parallel workers; SFTPIngestOperator tokenizes SSN-4 before S3 write; CASSStandardizeOperator submits addresses per-record without caching; DeduplicationOperator deterministic matching before SnowflakeLoadOperator COPY INTO with SCD2 merge.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: KMS CMK encryption on S3 landing bucket; SSN-4 and DL tokenized before landing zone write; Snowflake dynamic data masking on sensitive columns; NVRA and HAVA compliance controls documented; missing: Snowflake row access policies for county-scoped roles and CASS API rate limit controls.
previous_plan.data_model: voter_registrations (SCD2 with valid_from/valid_to), county_submissions, deduplication_records, list_maintenance_actions; no CASS response cache specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the two remaining open issues. ISS-001 addressed by redesigning CASSStandardizeOperator to submit addresses in 1000-record batches with an S3-backed 90-day response cache keyed on CASS-normalized input, reducing quota consumption by approximately 60% for repeat addresses from repeat county submissions. ISS-004 addressed by specifying Snowflake row access policies binding county_viewer role access to rows where county_code matches the session context variable set at login, eliminating cross-jurisdiction data exposure. All four original issues are now resolved; the plan reaches a production-ready security posture for FedRAMP-compliant NVRA voter file management.

fix_report.action_taken: CASSStandardizeOperator redesigned to submit addresses in 1000-record batches per CASS API call; S3-backed response cache with 90-day TTL added, keyed on CASS-normalized input address; daily quota utilization metric added to DataQualityDAG observability and PagerDuty P2 alert threshold set at 80% of daily quota
fix_report.expected_outcome: CASS API daily quota no longer at risk of exhaustion; operator throughput sufficient for 4M+ addresses within the nightly window with estimated 60% quota reduction from address cache hits

fix_report.action_taken: Snowflake row access policy added to voter_registrations and county_submissions tables; policy binds county_viewer role access to rows where county_code matches the CURRENT_SESSION county_code context variable set at Snowflake login; policy tested in staging against synthetic multi-county dataset confirming cross-jurisdiction queries return empty result set
fix_report.expected_outcome: County election staff can only query voter records within their own jurisdiction; unauthorized cross-county access attempts return empty results with no error disclosure

target_output.title: State Voter File ETL Pipeline Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A FedRAMP-compliant data pipeline on AWS GovCloud orchestrated by Apache Airflow 2.8 on MWAA, ingesting nightly voter registration extracts from all 67 county election offices via authenticated SFTP, standardizing addresses through USPS CASS with 1000-record batch submission and S3-backed response caching, deduplicating records deterministically against KMS-tokenized SSN-4 identifiers, and loading the authoritative voter file into Snowflake using SCD Type 2 history preservation. Monthly list maintenance DAGs reconcile the voter file against NCOA, state death registries, and DMV change feeds per NVRA Section 8 requirements. Snowflake dynamic data masking and row access policies enforce jurisdiction-scoped access for county election officials.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three Airflow DAGs on MWAA orchestrate the voter file lifecycle: CountyIngestionDAG runs nightly at 02:00 with dynamic task mapping over 67 county config objects, running 15 concurrent county ingestion chains within the 4-hour SLA window. Each county chain executes SFTPIngestOperator (SFTP fetch, schema detection, KMS tokenization of SSN-4 and DL before S3 write), CASSStandardizeOperator (S3 cache lookup then 1000-record batch CASS API submission for cache misses), DeduplicationOperator (deterministic match on name+DOB+ssn4_token with Jaro-Winkler probabilistic fallback), and SnowflakeLoadOperator (COPY INTO RAW staging then SCD Type 2 merge into CANONICAL voter_registrations). DataQualityDAG triggers on CountyIngestionDAG completion. ListMaintenanceDAG runs monthly processing NCOA, death registry, and DMV change feeds. All components operate within a GovCloud VPC private subnet with county SFTP endpoints connected via VPC peering and no internet egress.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA. Language: Python 3.12 with mypy type annotations. Warehouse: Snowflake Business Critical on GovCloud with PrivateLink and Tri-Secret Secure. Landing Zone: S3 with KMS CMK per-county prefix encryption and versioning. Address Standardization: USPS CASS API via SmartyStreets Python client with S3 response cache. Tokenization: AWS KMS Encrypt/Decrypt via boto3. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with Airflow SecretsManagerBackend. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Observability: Grafana plus Prometheus plus CloudWatch plus PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: County SFTP Ingestion: per-county YAML config, schema version detection, file completeness check, KMS tokenization, S3 landing write. Address Standardization: S3 cache lookup before API call, 1000-record batch CASS submission for misses, standardized address write to processed prefix and cache update. Voter Deduplication: deterministic match on name+DOB+ssn4_token, probabilistic fallback with match score, human review queue for unresolved duplicates. Snowflake Load: COPY INTO RAW staging, SCD2 merge into CANONICAL schema, county_submission record creation. List Maintenance: NCOA file parse, death registry match, DMV change feed processing, NVRA Section 8 notice generation, county SFTP dispatch. Data Quality: per-county quality score, threshold alerting, Grafana quality trend dashboard.
target_output.system_components: MWAA Environment (Airflow 2.8, private VPC subnets, SecretsManagerBackend, Large worker size for production). Custom Operators: SFTPIngestOperator (paramiko SFTP, KMS tokenization before S3 write), CASSStandardizeOperator (1000-record batch submission, S3 cache with 90-day TTL keyed on normalized address string), DeduplicationOperator (deterministic plus Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching), SnowflakeLoadOperator (COPY INTO plus SCD2 merge). S3 Buckets: voter-file-landing (KMS CMK, versioning enabled), voter-file-processed, voter-file-archive (Glacier lifecycle at 90 days). Snowflake: RAW schema for COPY INTO staging, CANONICAL schema with SCD2 voter_registrations with dynamic data masking and row access policies, REPORTING schema with jurisdiction-scoped views. GitLab CI runner on VPC-hosted EC2. Grafana plus Prometheus via CloudWatch exporter.
target_output.workflows: County Nightly Ingestion: at 02:00 CountyIngestionDAG triggers dynamic task map over 67 county config objects; each county task runs SFTPIngestOperator (connect to county SFTP endpoint, download dated extract file, detect schema version from header, tokenize SSN-4 and DL via KMS Encrypt, write tokenized file to s3://voter-file-landing/{county_code}/{extract_date}/), then CASSStandardizeOperator (check S3 cache per address, submit uncached addresses in 1000-record batches to CASS API, update cache with responses, write standardized output to voter-file-processed prefix), then DeduplicationOperator (query Snowflake CANONICAL for existing voters matching name+DOB+ssn4_token, flag exact matches to deduplication_records as deterministic, score probabilistic-only matches below threshold for data custodian queue), then SnowflakeLoadOperator (COPY INTO RAW.county_staging, execute SCD2 merge updating valid_to on changed records and inserting new versions into CANONICAL.voter_registrations). DataQualityDAG runs after all 67 county tasks report success or failure, computing per-county quality scores and triggering PagerDuty P2 if any county score falls below 85%. Monthly List Maintenance: ListMaintenanceDAG downloads NCOA weekly digest and state death registry extract on first Sunday of each month, runs deterministic match against active voter_registrations by name+DOB, generates list_maintenance_actions records with NVRA federal_doc_id references, produces county notification packages with required Section 8 documentation, and dispatches packages to county SFTP drop zones.
target_output.data_model: voter_registrations (vr_id UUID PK, county_code CHAR(3), state_voter_id VARCHAR unique per voter across county transfers for HAVA Section 303, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, dob DATE, address_standardized VARCHAR CASS-normalized, address_line2 VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, zip5 CHAR(5), zip4 CHAR(4), precinct_id VARCHAR FK, registration_status ENUM active/inactive/cancelled/pending, registration_date DATE, party_affiliation VARCHAR, voter_ssn4_token VARCHAR KMS-encrypted token, voter_dl_token VARCHAR KMS-encrypted token, valid_from TIMESTAMP, valid_to TIMESTAMP NULL for SCD2, is_current BOOLEAN, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). county_submissions (submission_id UUID PK, county_code CHAR(3), extract_date DATE, file_name VARCHAR, s3_uri VARCHAR, record_count INT, schema_version VARCHAR, validation_status ENUM passed/failed/partial, failed_record_count INT, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). deduplication_records (dup_id UUID PK, master_vr_id UUID FK, duplicate_vr_id UUID FK, match_method ENUM deterministic/probabilistic, match_score FLOAT, match_fields JSONB, resolved_by VARCHAR, resolved_at TIMESTAMP). list_maintenance_actions (action_id UUID PK, vr_id UUID FK, action_type ENUM ncoa_update/death_cancel/dmv_address_change, source_program VARCHAR, source_file VARCHAR, federal_doc_id VARCHAR NVRA Section 8, notice_generated BOOLEAN, county_notified_at TIMESTAMP). data_quality_reports (report_id UUID PK, county_code CHAR(3), run_date DATE, total_records INT, failed_validation INT, dedup_flagged INT, cass_cache_hits INT, cass_api_calls INT, quality_score FLOAT 0-100).
target_output.api_design: No external API surface; pipeline exposes data via Snowflake reporting views and MWAA Airflow REST API (internal VPC only). Airflow REST API for monitoring: GET /api/v1/dags/county_ingestion_dag/dagRuns/{run_id} (run status and duration), GET /api/v1/dags/county_ingestion_dag/dagRuns/{run_id}/taskInstances (per-county task status with county_code in task_id). Snowflake REPORTING schema views accessible to authorized roles: voter_file_county_summary (county_code, active_count, inactive_count, pending_count, last_updated_at - row access policy applied), submission_status_current (county_code, extract_date, validation_status, record_count, loaded_at - county_viewer scoped to own county), list_maintenance_open_actions (county_code, action_type, pending_count, oldest_pending_date), data_quality_trend (county_code, run_date, quality_score, cass_cache_hit_rate - 90-day history).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Encryption: S3 landing bucket encrypted with KMS CMK using per-county prefix key policies; Snowflake Business Critical with Tri-Secret Secure and customer-managed encryption key. PII tokenization: SFTPIngestOperator calls KMS Encrypt for SSN-4 and DL number before any S3 write - no plaintext sensitive identifiers in S3 or Snowflake. Snowflake dynamic data masking policies on voter_ssn4_token and voter_dl_token: data_custodian role sees raw token, county_viewer and analyst roles see masked value (***-****). Snowflake row access policies on voter_registrations and county_submissions: county_viewer role access restricted to rows where county_code = GET(CURRENT_SESSION(),'county_code'); cross-jurisdiction queries return empty results. SFTP authentication: public-key only, county private keys stored in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation schedule and 2-week overlap window. NVRA Section 8 compliance: all list maintenance actions written to list_maintenance_actions with federal_doc_id references; monthly county notification packages generated with required statutory documentation. HAVA Section 303: unique state_voter_id maintained per voter across county transfers in CANONICAL schema. Audit: CloudTrail S3 data events on landing bucket; MWAA task logs in CloudWatch with county_code and record counts but no PII; Snowflake ACCESS_HISTORY view queried nightly by DataQualityDAG to detect anomalous analyst access patterns.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure managed by Terraform: MWAA environment (Large for production, Medium for staging, Small for dev), VPC private subnets with no internet gateway, S3 buckets with KMS keys, Snowflake resource monitor capping nightly warehouse credits, GitLab CI runner EC2 instance in VPC. Environments: dev (3 synthetic county configs, Snowflake X-Small), staging (10 anonymized county configs, Snowflake Small, full DAG integration tests), production (67 real county SFTP connections, Snowflake X-Large auto-suspended between 06:00 and 01:30). GitLab CI pipeline: flake8 plus mypy lint, pytest with mocked AWS and Snowflake clients (85% coverage gate), integration tests against Snowflake dev schema with 500-record synthetic voter dataset, Terraform plan posted to MR for IT review, staging deploy with smoke test against anonymized data, production deploy requiring election division IT manager approval in GitLab MR. DAG deployment: DAG files synced from GitLab repo to MWAA S3 DAG bucket on production approval. Rollback: Snowflake time-travel to pre-load snapshot within 90 days; prior DAG version redeployment by Git SHA; Terraform state versioned in S3 with MFA delete protection.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards: per-county nightly run status heatmap (pass/fail/partial by county by date, 90-day history), record volume trend per county (detect anomalous volume drops indicating extract failures or county-side processing issues), deduplication rate trend over 12 months, CASS cache hit ratio trend, list maintenance action backlog per county with oldest pending action date. Prometheus metrics via CloudWatch exporter: airflow_task_duration_seconds labeled by county_code and operator_class, airflow_task_failure_total labeled by county_code, snowflake_load_records_total by county, cass_api_calls_total vs cass_cache_hits_total, list_maintenance_actions_pending by county. PagerDuty P1: pipeline SLA breach (any county task not complete by 06:00), Snowflake load failure, MWAA environment unavailable. PagerDuty P2: county failure rate exceeds 10% in single night, data quality score below 85% for any county, CASS API daily quota utilization above 80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA Large environment approximately $800/month. Snowflake X-Large nightly warehouse auto-suspended outside the 4-hour window approximately $600/month at GovCloud pricing. S3 storage for 67-county extracts and processed files approximately $150/month. KMS API calls for tokenization approximately $20/month. Total estimated $1,600/month for the production pipeline serving 67 counties. Scaling path for additional counties: MWAA worker count increase and dynamic task concurrency adjustment; Snowflake warehouse size scales independently of storage cost; S3 plus Glacier lifecycle keeps archive costs flat as voter file history grows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Core Pipeline (10 weeks): MWAA environment and VPC infrastructure via Terraform, SFTPIngestOperator with KMS tokenization, CASSStandardizeOperator with 1000-record batch submission and S3 response cache, DeduplicationOperator deterministic matching, SnowflakeLoadOperator SCD2 merge, DataQualityDAG, GitLab CI pipeline, onboarding of top 10 highest-volume counties. Phase 2 Full County Rollout (6 weeks): onboard remaining 57 counties using per-county YAML config generation tooling, Snowflake REPORTING schema views with row access policies deployed, Grafana dashboards live, PagerDuty alerts configured, county training on Snowflake query tool access. Phase 3 List Maintenance (6 weeks): ListMaintenanceDAG with NCOA, death registry, and DMV change feed integrations, NVRA Section 8 maintenance notice generation and county SFTP dispatch, compliance audit trail validation against federal documentation requirements.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict type annotations on all custom Airflow operator classes. pytest unit tests for each operator with mocked boto3, paramiko, and Snowflake Connector clients; 85% line coverage enforced in GitLab CI. Integration tests run against Snowflake dev environment with 500-record synthetic voter dataset containing no real PII. All per-county YAML adapter configs validated against JSON schema in CI. Airflow DAG integrity tests checking for import errors and task dependency cycles run on every commit. No PII, SFTP credentials, or Snowflake keys in test fixtures, log statements, or Airflow variable storage.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: County extract schema drift - counties change field names or formats without notice to state. Mitigation: schema version detection in SFTPIngestOperator alerts on unknown schema versions and falls back to last known good mapping; per-county YAML adapters decouple normalization logic from core pipeline. Risk: CASS API downtime during nightly window. Mitigation: 90-day S3 response cache provides fallback standardization for addresses seen in prior runs; CASS-failed records flagged for next-night retry without blocking Snowflake load. Risk: Snowflake maintenance window overlap with nightly pipeline. Mitigation: pipeline retries on transient Snowflake errors with exponential backoff; time-travel rollback if partial load is detected. Tradeoff: S3-backed CASS cache over Redis - S3 has higher per-lookup latency but eliminates Redis cluster operational overhead in GovCloud; acceptable for batch address processing since cache lookups are parallelized across 1000-record batches.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CASS API rate limits resolved via 1000-address batch submission and 90-day S3 response cache with estimated 60% API call reduction. Snowflake row access policies specified for county_viewer role restricting to own jurisdiction via session context variable. Duplicate voter resolution confirmed: auto-resolved on deterministic match, queued for election division data custodian review on probabilistic-only match below threshold. County schema normalization implemented via per-county YAML config approach rather than mandating county-side format standardization.

metadata.notes: Round 3 voter registration ETL pipeline on AWS GovCloud; resolves CASS API rate limiting and Snowflake county-scoped row access policy gaps; all 4 issues resolved.

profile.domain: State Unemployment Insurance Claims REST API

project_goal.value: Modernized REST API replacing a SOAP-based legacy interface for a state department of labor's unemployment insurance claims system, supporting claimant identity verification, weekly certification submissions, and payment status queries for partner UI applications
project_goal.rationale: Legacy SOAP service blocks adoption of modern identity federation and partner integrations

target_users.value: Partner-facing: state-contracted UI application developers, claimants via partner applications, employer reporting integrations, and state payment processing systems
target_users.rationale: API consumers identified in partner integration agreements

project_class.rationale: Backend REST API with no frontend; partner applications own the UI layer

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, integrations, batch_jobs, devops
capabilities.rationale: Full API lifecycle with batch reconciliation and partner-facing exposure

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system integration with legacy SACWIS, ACH payment rails, Login.gov OIDC, and eligibility rule engine

risk_level.rationale: Handles claimant PII and unemployment benefit payment authorization; fraud risk is significant

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores claimant SSN (tokenized), earnings records, weekly certification data, and benefit payment amounts

external_exposure.rationale: API accessed only by state-contracted partner applications with mTLS client certificates

access_model.value: mTLS for partner API access plus Login.gov OpenID Connect for claimant identity; RBAC controlling endpoint access by partner role
access_model.rationale: Partner mTLS prevents unauthorized API access; Login.gov provides REAL-ID-compliant identity assurance

feature_scope.value: Claimant identity verification via Login.gov, weekly certification submission with work search activity validation, benefit payment status query, employer wage record lookup for eligibility determination, adjudication decision recording, overpayment notice generation
feature_scope.rationale: Complete UI claims processing lifecycle API

mvp_scope.value: Weekly certification submission, payment status query, and identity verification; employer wage record integration via batch; SOAP migration for existing claimant dataset
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers active claimant weekly workflow for initial partner application migration

security_baseline.value: mTLS for all partner-facing endpoints, Login.gov OIDC for claimant identity, TLS 1.3 in transit, claimant SSN tokenized via state tokenization service, AES-256 encryption at rest for PII fields, CJIS-equivalent audit logging
security_baseline.rationale: State unemployment insurance code and federal UI program requirements mandate strong identity and data protection

frontend_stack.value: None
frontend_stack.rationale: API service only; partner applications own the frontend

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2
backend_stack.rationale: Existing team expertise; Spring Boot integrates well with Spring Security for mTLS and OIDC

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for idempotency key cache and session tokens
data_platform.rationale: Relational data with transactional integrity for certification submissions; Redis for sub-millisecond idempotency checks

hosting_target.value: AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate in us-gov-west-1
hosting_target.rationale: FedRAMP-authorized environment required for claimant PII; Fargate eliminates EC2 patching overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Weekly certification records retained 7 years per state unemployment insurance code; audit logs retained 3 years; PII purged upon claimant request after benefit year closes
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State UI records retention schedule and claimant rights under state privacy statute

future_scope.value: Real-time fraud detection scoring via ML model integration, self-service overpayment repayment portal, interstate claim exchange via ICON protocol
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap subject to legislative appropriation

constraints.value: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing SOAP consumers during 18-month migration window; all claimant data must remain in us-gov-west-1; no external SaaS processing of claimant SSNs
constraints.rationale: Partner migration timeline and state data residency requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing across certification submission flow, structured JSON logs with claim_id correlation, PagerDuty alerts for certification processing error rate exceeding 0.5%
observability_baseline.rationale: Benefits processing failures directly impact claimant payments; fast detection is critical

execution_preference.value: Strangler fig migration: new REST endpoints deployed alongside SOAP service; partner applications migrate endpoint by endpoint over 18 months before SOAP decommission
execution_preference.rationale: Eliminates hard cutover risk for a system processing weekly benefit payments

compliance_context.value: Social Security Act Title III UI requirements, IRS 940/941 reporting, state unemployment insurance code, REAL ID Act for claimant identity verification via Login.gov, FedRAMP Moderate for hosting
compliance_context.rationale: Federal UI program compliance and state regulatory requirements for claimant data handling

project.goal: Replace SOAP UI claims API with REST while maintaining partner application compatibility during 18-month migration
project.target_users: Partner UI application developers and state payment processing systems
project.timeline: REST MVP in 6 months, full SOAP decommission at 18 months

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.2 with Spring Security mTLS and OIDC

security.auth: mTLS partner certificates via AWS Certificate Manager, Login.gov OIDC for claimant identity
security.pii: SSN tokenized via state tokenization service; PII fields AES-256 encrypted at rest

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for idempotency keys and session tokens

devops.ci: Jenkins pipeline: build, unit test, integration test, security scan, ECS deploy

constraints.compatibility: Strangler fig — REST and SOAP operate in parallel for 18 months

reasoner_reviews.product: The strangler fig migration pattern is well-suited here — claimant weekly certifications cannot afford downtime, so running REST and SOAP in parallel during partner migration eliminates hard-cutover risk. Weekly certification and payment status are the highest-volume endpoints and must be the REST migration priority.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot with Spring Security handles mTLS and OIDC integration natively. Idempotency key checking in Redis before certification processing prevents duplicate benefit authorizations from retry storms — this is the most critical correctness requirement. The batch reconciliation job must run after ACH settlement window closes, typically 2am state time.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS is mandatory for partner-facing endpoints — certificate rotation procedures must be defined and tested before production launch. Login.gov OIDC tokens must be validated for ial2 assurance level to meet REAL ID requirements for claimant identity. SSN tokenization must happen at the API boundary before any data reaches the database layer.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 18-month SOAP migration window is achievable with the strangler fig approach if partner onboarding starts in month 3. FedRAMP GovCloud constraint limits some third-party tooling choices — confirm Datadog GovCloud availability.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot monolith with modular package structure: claims (weekly certification submission), identity (Login.gov OIDC token validation), employer (wage record lookup), payments (status query, ACH reconciliation), audit (immutable event recording); SOAP adapter layer exposing legacy WSDL endpoints backed by the same service layer
backend.api_patterns: REST OpenAPI 3.0; idempotency keys on POST /certifications via Idempotency-Key header; optimistic locking on claim records with ETag; event-sourced certification state machine (pending -> submitted -> validated -> approved/denied)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly ACH reconciliation job at 2am: fetches payment settlement files from state treasurer SFTP, matches against certification payment records, flags unmatched items, generates overpayment notice records for failed post-payment audits
backend.failure_handling: Transactional outbox pattern for certification submission events to decouple database write from downstream payment system notification; dead letter queue for failed notifications with alerting to on-call

security.auth_design: AWS Certificate Manager issues mTLS client certificates to partner applications; Spring Security mutual TLS filter validates client cert CN against approved partner registry; Login.gov OIDC integration validates id_token with ial2 claim for claimant identity assurance
security.authorization_model: RBAC: partner_ui_app (certification submit, payment query), employer_portal (wage record submit), admin (adjudication, overpayment), readonly_auditor (audit log query only)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only certification_audit_events table recording every state transition with actor, timestamp, IP, and claim_id; immutable log stream forwarded to S3 GovCloud for 3-year retention; Datadog SIEM integration for anomaly detection on unusual certification submission volumes
security.privacy_controls: SSN tokenized at API ingestion boundary via synchronous call to state tokenization service; token stored in claimant_profiles table; raw SSN never written to application database or logs

data.entities: ui_claims (id UUID, claimant_token FK, benefit_year_start, benefit_year_end, claim_status ENUM open/exhausted/cancelled, created_at). weekly_certifications (id UUID, claim_id FK, week_ending_date, work_search_activities JSONB, earnings_reported DECIMAL, certification_status ENUM pending/validated/approved/denied/duplicate, submitted_at, idempotency_key). claimant_profiles (id UUID, logingov_sub UNIQUE, ssn_token, name_encrypted, dob, address_encrypted, phone_encrypted). employer_wage_records (id UUID, employer_fein, claimant_token, base_period_quarter, wages DECIMAL, submitted_at). payment_transactions (id UUID, certification_id FK, amount DECIMAL, payment_method ENUM ach/debit_card, ach_trace_number, status ENUM pending/settled/returned, settled_at). adjudication_decisions (id UUID, claim_id FK, decision_type, decision_date, adjudicator_id, notes_encrypted)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ us-gov-west-1 with AES-256 encryption at rest via AWS KMS; weekly_certifications partitioned by week_ending_date for query efficiency; Redis 7 for 24-hour idempotency key TTL cache
data.migration_notes: SOAP legacy claim records migrated to PostgreSQL via ETL job in parallel with REST launch; claimant SSNs converted to tokens during migration using state tokenization service batch API; no backfill downtime required

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-gov-west-1 with 2-6 API tasks behind NLB for mTLS passthrough; separate ECS task for nightly batch reconciliation job; RDS Multi-AZ with automated failover; ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled for simplicity at current scale
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins pipeline: Maven build and unit tests, Spring Boot integration tests against Testcontainers PostgreSQL and Redis, OWASP Dependency-Check, Docker push to ECR GovCloud, ECS rolling deployment with health check gate; SOAP adapter deployed from same image with SOAP_ENABLED env flag
devops.backup_and_recovery: RDS automated daily snapshots retained 35 days; point-in-time recovery enabled for certification data integrity; certification_audit_events replicated to S3 GovCloud independently of RDS backups

ISS-001.title: Idempotency key TTL too short for ACH retry window
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan set Redis TTL for idempotency keys to 1 hour, insufficient for ACH retry scenarios where partner applications may retry certification submissions up to 24 hours after initial attempt due to network failures

history.note: TTL value not specified in round 1

ISS-002.title: mTLS certificate rotation procedure not defined
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan specified mTLS requirement but did not define partner certificate renewal workflow, revocation procedure, or operational runbook for certificate expiry events

history.note: Missing from round 1 security section

ISS-003.title: SSN tokenization failure handling undefined
ISS-003.detail: The state tokenization service is a synchronous dependency on the certification submission path; if it is unavailable, the API has no defined behavior — whether to queue, reject, or retry the request

history.note: Tokenization service failure mode not addressed in round 1

focus_issues.title: mTLS certificate rotation procedure not defined
focus_issues.detail: Partner certificate renewal and revocation workflow missing from security design; must be defined before partner onboarding begins

focus_issues.title: SSN tokenization failure handling undefined
focus_issues.detail: Tokenization service outage behavior on the certification submission critical path not addressed

latest_recommendations: Define mTLS certificate rotation runbook including 90-day renewal cadence, partner notification process, and emergency revocation procedure via CRL
Specify tokenization service circuit breaker behavior: reject certification submission with 503 and Retry-After header if tokenization unavailable, preventing SSN from touching database layer
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: UI Claims REST API Migration - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two high/medium issues unresolved; mTLS rotation gap is a partner onboarding blocker

previous_audits.summary: Solid foundation for the SOAP-to-REST migration with appropriate strangler fig approach and Login.gov OIDC integration. Two gaps block partner onboarding: undefined mTLS certificate lifecycle and undefined tokenization service failure handling on the critical certification submission path.
strengths: Strangler fig migration pattern correctly preserves SOAP endpoint compatibility during transition
Transactional outbox for certification events correctly decouples database writes from downstream payment notifications
ACH nightly reconciliation job with 2am timing is operationally sound
concerns: mTLS certificate rotation procedure absent — partner onboarding cannot begin without defined renewal and revocation workflow
SSN tokenization synchronous call is a single point of failure on certification submission with no defined fallback
blocking_issues: mTLS certificate lifecycle undefined
Tokenization service outage behavior not specified
recommendations: Add 90-day certificate renewal cadence, automated expiry alerting at T-30 days, CRL-based revocation endpoint, and emergency revocation runbook to security section
Define circuit breaker on tokenization service call: fail-fast with 503 Retry-After on outage rather than queuing raw SSN; add tokenization service availability to SLA dependencies

issue_updates.title: Idempotency key TTL too short
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan sets Redis idempotency key TTL to 24 hours to cover full ACH retry window

issue_updates.title: mTLS certificate rotation not defined
issue_updates.detail: No certificate lifecycle section in round 1 plan

issue_updates.title: SSN tokenization failure handling undefined
issue_updates.detail: No circuit breaker or fallback defined for tokenization service outage

best_plan.executive_summary: REST API replacing SOAP legacy interface for state unemployment insurance claims processing, supporting weekly certification submission, identity verification via Login.gov OIDC, and payment status queries with mTLS for partner applications on AWS GovCloud.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 of the UI claims SOAP-to-REST migration. ISS-001 (idempotency TTL) was resolved by extending Redis TTL to 24 hours. This round addresses ISS-002 (mTLS certificate rotation lifecycle) and ISS-003 (tokenization service circuit breaker). The mTLS rotation section needs a concrete 90-day renewal runbook and CRL revocation endpoint. The tokenization failure handling needs a circuit breaker with fail-fast 503 behavior rather than queuing raw SSNs, which would violate the security baseline.

fix_report.action_taken: Added mTLS certificate lifecycle section to security_and_compliance covering 90-day renewal cadence, automated T-30 expiry alerting via ACM Certificate Manager Events, CRL-based revocation endpoint published at .well-known/crl, and emergency revocation runbook with NLB security policy update procedure
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partner onboarding team has a complete operational reference for certificate lifecycle management; mTLS rotation no longer a blocker

fix_report.action_taken: Defined circuit breaker on synchronous tokenization service call using Resilience4j: open circuit after 5 consecutive failures within 10 seconds, reject certification submission with HTTP 503 and Retry-After: 30 header, preventing raw SSN from reaching database layer during tokenization outage
fix_report.expected_outcome: Certification submissions fail safely during tokenization outage with no SSN data residue; claimant retry via partner app succeeds when tokenization service recovers

target_output.title: UI Claims REST API Migration - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A modernized partner-facing REST API on AWS GovCloud replacing the state department of labor's SOAP-based unemployment insurance claims interface. Built with Spring Boot 3.2 on ECS Fargate, the service handles weekly certification submission with Login.gov OIDC identity assurance, mTLS partner authentication, nightly ACH reconciliation, and SOAP adapter co-existence during the 18-month migration window. This revision adds a complete mTLS certificate lifecycle operational runbook and a Resilience4j circuit breaker for the SSN tokenization dependency.
target_output.architecture_overview: Spring Boot 3.2 REST API deployed on ECS Fargate in AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1, fronted by a Network Load Balancer configured for mTLS passthrough so that client certificates reach the Spring Security layer for validation. The SOAP adapter module exposes the legacy WSDL interface backed by the same Spring service layer, enabling strangler fig migration. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all certification, payment, and claimant records with AES-256 encryption at rest via KMS. ElastiCache Redis 7 provides 24-hour idempotency key TTL storage for certification submission deduplication. Login.gov OIDC issues id_tokens validated at ial2 assurance level for claimant identity. All SSN values are tokenized at the API ingestion boundary via the state tokenization service protected by a Resilience4j circuit breaker before any data touches the database.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Security (mTLS + OIDC), Resilience4j for circuit breakers, Flyway for schema migrations. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ us-gov-west-1 with KMS encryption. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for idempotency keys and OIDC session tokens. Auth: AWS Certificate Manager for mTLS partner certs, Login.gov OIDC for claimant identity. CI/CD: Jenkins, Docker, ECR GovCloud, ECS Fargate. Observability: Datadog APM GovCloud, structured JSON logging with claim_id correlation. Batch: Spring Batch for nightly ACH reconciliation job.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Certification Submission: claimant authenticates via Login.gov ial2, partner app submits certification with work search activities and earnings; eligibility rules engine validates and approves or denies. Payment Status Query: partner app queries certification payment status by claim_id; returns payment_transaction record with ACH trace number when settled. Identity Verification: Login.gov OIDC token validation with ial2 claim enforcement; claimant profile creation on first certification. Employer Wage Record Integration: employer portal submits base-period wage records; wage data feeds eligibility calculation engine. Adjudication Recording: adjudicator records decision on disputed claim; claimant notification triggered. ACH Reconciliation: nightly batch matches payment settlement files against certification payment records; overpayment notices generated for failures.
target_output.system_components: NLB with mTLS passthrough, Spring Boot API service (ECS Fargate 2-6 tasks), SOAP Adapter module (same image, SOAP_ENABLED=true), Spring Security mTLS filter chain validating client cert CN against partner registry, Login.gov OIDC token validator (ial2 assurance enforcement), Resilience4j circuit breaker on state tokenization service HTTP client, Transactional outbox table for certification payment events, Spring Batch nightly reconciliation job (ECS scheduled task), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7 idempotency cache, ACM certificate lifecycle manager.
target_output.workflows: Weekly Certification Submission: partner app submits POST /v1/certifications with Bearer Login.gov id_token and Idempotency-Key header. Spring Security validates mTLS client cert CN. OIDC validator verifies id_token ial2 claim. Resilience4j circuit breaker calls state tokenization service to convert claimant SSN to token — if circuit is open (5 consecutive failures in 10s), API returns 503 with Retry-After: 30 and logs a circuit_open_event; no SSN touches the database. Idempotency key checked in Redis with 24-hour TTL; duplicate returns cached 200. Eligibility rules engine validates work search activities against week-ending-date requirements. weekly_certifications record inserted; certification_audit_events record appended. Payment notification published to transactional outbox. ACH Reconciliation: Spring Batch job at 2am retrieves SFTP settlement file from state treasurer. Each payment_transaction record matched by ACH trace number. Unmatched transactions flagged as potential overpayments; adjudication_decisions records created for manual review. Summary report emailed to UI program staff.
target_output.data_model: ui_claims (id UUID PK, claimant_token FK, benefit_year_start DATE, benefit_year_end DATE, claim_status ENUM open/exhausted/cancelled, base_period_wages DECIMAL, weekly_benefit_amount DECIMAL, created_at). weekly_certifications (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, week_ending_date DATE, work_search_activities JSONB, earnings_reported DECIMAL, certification_status ENUM pending/validated/approved/denied/duplicate, submitted_at, idempotency_key UNIQUE, processed_by_batch BOOLEAN). claimant_profiles (id UUID PK, logingov_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, ssn_token VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_encrypted BYTEA, dob DATE, address_encrypted BYTEA). employer_wage_records (id UUID PK, employer_fein VARCHAR, claimant_token FK, base_period_quarter VARCHAR, wages DECIMAL, submitted_at). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, certification_id FK, amount DECIMAL, payment_method ENUM ach/debit_card, ach_trace_number VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/settled/returned/voided, settled_at). outbox_events (id UUID PK, aggregate_id UUID, event_type, payload JSONB, published BOOLEAN, created_at).
target_output.api_design: REST v1 at /api/v1. POST /v1/certifications (submit weekly certification; requires Login.gov Bearer token + mTLS + Idempotency-Key). GET /v1/certifications/{id}/payment-status (payment transaction status by certification id). GET /v1/claims/{claim_id} (claim record and benefit year details). POST /v1/identity/verify (exchange Login.gov code for validated claimant profile). POST /v1/employer/wage-records (employer submits base period wages; requires employer mTLS cert). GET /v1/audit/certifications/{claim_id} (read-only audit trail for adjudicators). SOAP adapter: /ws/UIClaimsService (WSDL preserved at /ws/UIClaimsService?wsdl). All REST responses include correlation_id header matching Datadog trace ID.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS: AWS Certificate Manager issues 2-year client certificates to partner applications; Spring Security mutual TLS filter validates client cert CN against partner_registry table. Certificate lifecycle: 90-day renewal reminder emails sent via ACM Certificate Manager Events at T-30 and T-7 days before expiry; partner completes CSR renewal via documented partner portal runbook; CRL published at https://api.labor.state.gov/.well-known/crl updated within 1 hour of revocation; emergency revocation via ACM console triggers NLB security policy update within 15 minutes and partner notification within 30 minutes. Login.gov OIDC: id_token validated for ial2 acr claim — requests with ial1 tokens rejected with 403 for certification endpoints. SSN tokenization: Resilience4j circuit breaker wraps synchronous HTTP call to state tokenization service; circuit opens after 5 failures in 10 seconds; API returns 503 Retry-After: 30 during open state; raw SSN never written to application database or logs under any code path. Privacy: PII columns encrypted with AES-256 KMS; claimant_profiles purged on request after benefit year close; audit_events retained 3 years in S3 GovCloud. Compliance: Social Security Act Title III, IRS 940/941 reporting exports from PostgreSQL, REAL ID via Login.gov ial2, FedRAMP Moderate GovCloud hosting.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins CI pipeline: Maven build, unit tests (JUnit 5 + Testcontainers PostgreSQL + Redis), Spring integration tests against Testcontainers, OWASP Dependency-Check CVE scan (fail on critical), Trivy container image scan, Docker push to ECR GovCloud, ECS rolling deployment with health check gate requiring /actuator/health 200 from 2 tasks before draining old tasks. SOAP adapter deployed from same Docker image with SOAP_ENABLED=true environment variable. Environments: dev (single-AZ, Testcontainers in CI), staging (multi-AZ, mTLS test partner certs), production (multi-AZ us-gov-west-1a/1b, auto-scaling 2-6 Fargate tasks targeting 60% CPU, RDS Multi-AZ automatic failover). Batch job: Spring Batch ACH reconciliation runs as ECS scheduled task at 2am nightly via EventBridge Scheduler; separate task definition with enhanced IAM role for S3 treasury file access. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day KMS key rotation for database credentials and tokenization service API key.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM GovCloud configured via Java agent on ECS tasks. Distributed traces span certification submission from NLB through tokenization service call to database write. Structured JSON logs include claim_id, certification_id, partner_cn, and trace_id for correlation. Custom Datadog monitors: certification submission error rate > 0.5% triggers P1 PagerDuty page; tokenization circuit breaker open state triggers immediate P2 alert; idempotency collision rate > 1% indicates partner retry storm (P2); ACH reconciliation job failure triggers P1. Daily dashboard: certification submissions by status, payment settlement rate, partner API latency p50/p99, tokenization service availability.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month for steady-state at 15,000 weekly certifications/week: ECS Fargate 2 tasks at $300/month baseline scaling to 6 at peak Sunday evening certification rush; RDS Multi-AZ db.r6g.large at $1,200/month; ElastiCache Redis cache.r6g.large at $300/month; NLB at $150/month; ECR, CloudWatch, Secrets Manager at $200/month; Datadog GovCloud APM license at $650/month. Auto-scaling on ECS CPU 60% target handles Sunday evening peaks (3x normal volume) without pre-provisioning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 REST MVP (6 months): Deploy REST API alongside SOAP adapter with feature parity for weekly certification, payment status, and identity verification; onboard first partner application to REST; complete mTLS partner certificate issuance runbook. Phase 2 Partner Migration (months 7-15): Migrate remaining partner applications endpoint by endpoint; deploy employer wage record API; complete ACH reconciliation batch migration from SOAP legacy job. Phase 3 SOAP Decommission (months 16-18): Confirm zero SOAP traffic in Datadog; decommission SOAP adapter module and WSDL endpoint; remove SOAP_ENABLED flag from ECS task definitions.
target_output.development_guidelines: All certification state transitions must go through the CertificationStateMachine class — no direct status field mutations in repository layer. Resilience4j circuit breaker configuration in application.yml, not code, to allow ops tuning without redeployment. Flyway migrations required for every schema change; no DDL in application startup. Integration tests must use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis — no mocked repositories in integration test suite. PR requires 1 senior engineer approval and passing Jenkins pipeline including OWASP scan.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Tokenization service SLA degradation causes certification submission outages during peak Sunday evening periods when claimants file before midnight deadline. Mitigation: Resilience4j circuit breaker with 503 fail-fast protects database integrity; ops runbook defines escalation path to state tokenization service team for P1 outages. Risk: Partner certificate expiry causes mTLS rejection before renewal is completed. Mitigation: T-30 and T-7 ACM expiry alerts give partners ample lead time; emergency NLB security policy update procedure reduces revocation-to-blocking time to 15 minutes. Tradeoff: Synchronous tokenization call over async — async would complicate the certification submission transaction boundary and increase the risk of partial writes; synchronous with circuit breaker is safer for data integrity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Login.gov ial2 over in-house identity verification to meet REAL ID Act requirements. Confirmed strangler fig over big-bang REST migration to eliminate weekly-payment downtime risk. Chose Resilience4j circuit breaker over bulkhead pattern for tokenization service because fail-fast is safer than queuing requests with raw SSN data. 24-hour idempotency key TTL chosen to cover full ACH retry window identified in ISS-001.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing mTLS certificate lifecycle (ISS-002) and SSN tokenization circuit breaker (ISS-003) from audit round 1. Strong quality across all sections with domain-specific UI claims terminology throughout.

profile.domain: Automated Bulk Vehicle Registration Renewal Processing for State Motor Vehicle Agency

project_goal.value: Nightly batch automation tool that identifies vehicle registrations expiring within 30 days, calculates renewal fees by vehicle class and county surcharge, generates renewal notices with scannable payment barcodes, processes pre-authorized payment captures, and produces USPS-formatted mailing files
project_goal.rationale: Core operational requirement to replace a manual overnight batch process run by DMV staff

target_users.value: State DMV operations staff who monitor batch run health and exception queues; no end-user-facing UI required
