target_users.rationale: Fully automated process with operator monitoring only

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch automation without interactive UI; operator access through monitoring dashboards only

capabilities.rationale: Batch processing, Oracle DB integration, payment processor API, and USPS mailing file generation are the four active capability surfaces

complexity_level.rationale: Fee calculation logic with multi-variable surcharge tables and payment processor integration add meaningful complexity to the batch pipeline

risk_level.rationale: Processes vehicle owner PII and pre-authorized payment tokens; batch failures directly delay citizen renewal notices creating statutory compliance risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Vehicle owner name, address, and pre-authorized payment method on file constitute personal data under state privacy statutes

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on Azure Batch within agency network; payment processor calls go outbound over TLS but no inbound public endpoints

access_model.value: Service account with Azure Key Vault-managed credentials; no interactive user authentication required for batch execution; monitoring dashboard access via Azure AD for operations staff
access_model.rationale: Automated batch process requires service principal access only; human access limited to monitoring

feature_scope.value: Nightly registration expiry query, per-vehicle fee calculation with county surcharge tables, renewal notice generation with PDF417 barcode, pre-authorized payment capture via payment processor API, mailing file generation in USPS Intelligent Mail format, batch run logging with exception reports, retry logic for failed payment captures
feature_scope.rationale: Complete replacement of manual overnight batch with full feature parity

mvp_scope.value: Expiry query, fee calculation, renewal notice PDF generation, mailing file output; payment capture deferred to Phase 2 to de-risk PCI integration
mvp_scope.rationale: Phase 1 validates notice generation accuracy without payment processing risk

security_baseline.value: Credentials stored in Azure Key Vault; Oracle DB connection via encrypted JDBC with mutual TLS; payment processor credentials rotated quarterly; batch logs scrubbed of full PAN before storage
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS SAQ-D requirements for systems handling pre-authorized payment credentials

frontend_stack.value: None; no frontend; monitoring via Azure Monitor dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: Automation tool with no interactive UI; operations staff use Azure Monitor and Log Analytics

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Batch
backend_stack.rationale: Spring Batch provides first-class chunk-oriented processing, job repository, retry policies, and skip logic for the batch pipeline patterns required

data_platform.value: Oracle 19c (existing state DMV database); Azure Blob Storage for mailing files and batch run artifacts
data_platform.rationale: Oracle 19c is the authoritative vehicle registration database; no migration in scope

hosting_target.value: Azure Batch with pool auto-scaling; triggered nightly at 01:00 AM via Azure Logic Apps schedule
hosting_target.rationale: Azure Batch provides managed compute for batch jobs with per-job cost model; Logic Apps scheduler is the existing agency orchestration standard

privacy_retention_policy.value: Batch run logs retained 90 days; mailing files retained 30 days then purged; renewal notice PDFs retained 7 years per state motor vehicle records retention schedule
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State public records retention schedule for vehicle registration correspondence

future_scope.value: Phase 2: pre-authorized payment capture integration; Phase 3: real-time renewal processing for walk-in kiosk channel; Phase 4: multi-agency shared batch platform
future_scope.rationale: Phased expansion after Phase 1 notice generation validation

constraints.value: Must read from Oracle 19c without schema changes; batch must complete by 05:00 AM to allow staff review before business hours; USPS Intelligent Mail barcode generation must use licensed USPS barcode library
constraints.rationale: Oracle schema is frozen by enterprise change freeze; USPS IMb license is mandatory for bulk mail acceptance

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for batch job metrics, Log Analytics for structured log queries, Azure Alerts for batch failure and completion-time SLA breach
observability_baseline.rationale: Agency standard monitoring stack; no additional tooling budget for prototype phase

execution_preference.value: Waterfall delivery with formal UAT phase before production deployment; agency change management requires staged rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Agency change management policy requires formal sign-off before replacing production batch

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ-D for pre-authorized payment token storage and capture; state motor vehicle code Sections 301-305 governing renewal notice timelines; USPS Bulk Mail Acceptance requirements for IMb barcodes
compliance_context.rationale: PCI scope triggered by pre-authorized payment credentials on file; renewal notice timing is statutory; USPS postal acceptance requires licensed IMb barcode

project.goal: Replace manual overnight DMV renewal batch with automated Spring Batch pipeline processing 30-day expiry cohorts nightly
project.target_users: DMV operations staff monitoring batch health; no end-user UI
project.timeline: Phase 1 (notice generation) in 12 weeks; Phase 2 (payment capture) 8 weeks following

backend.integration: Oracle JDBC via OJDBC11, Azure Key Vault via Spring Cloud Azure

security.auth: Azure service principal for batch execution; Key Vault for credential storage; Oracle connection via mutual TLS
security.pci: PAN truncation in logs; payment credentials stored as Key Vault secrets

data.db: Oracle 19c (read-only queries on vehicle_registrations and vehicle_owners tables)
data.storage: Azure Blob Storage for mailing files, notice PDFs, batch run manifests

devops.scheduler: Azure Logic Apps nightly trigger at 01:00 AM

open_questions.payment_processor: Stripe or agency legacy processor for Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-defined for a replacement batch tool. Phase 1 deferring payment capture is the right risk reduction strategy; notice generation can be validated independently against existing manual output before introducing PCI complexity. The 05:00 AM completion SLA gives operations staff two hours before business hours to review exceptions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Batch is the correct framework choice for this workload. Using chunk-oriented processing with configurable chunk-size (recommend starting at 500 registrations per chunk) will balance Oracle read throughput with Azure Batch memory usage. The job repository should store batch metadata in Azure SQL rather than an in-memory H2 database to support restart-from-last-checkpoint on failure. Oracle read-only access is appropriate given the frozen schema constraint.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS SAQ-D scope is limited to the payment capture phase (Phase 2). For Phase 1, the key security controls are: Key Vault for Oracle credentials, mutual TLS on Oracle JDBC connection, PAN truncation in all log output, and service principal with least-privilege access to Oracle read-only role. Renewal notice PDFs must not include full payment account numbers.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Oracle read-only constraint means the batch cannot use Spring Batch's standard JobRepository on Oracle; use Azure SQL for the Spring Batch job repository schema instead. Completion by 05:00 AM for a 30-day cohort of approximately 10,000-50,000 registrations is achievable with parallelized step execution across Azure Batch nodes.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot application with Spring Batch configuration. Three main jobs: ExpiryQueryJob (reads Oracle, writes staging), NoticeGenerationJob (calculates fees, generates PDFs), MailingFileJob (generates USPS IMb file). Jobs chained via JobOperator with dependency ordering.
backend.business_modules: FeeCalculationService: applies vehicle_class base fee plus county_surcharge_table lookup; validates fee against statutory minimum and maximum for the registration year. BarcodeGenerationService: wraps licensed USPS IMb library to encode mailer ID, serial number, and delivery point barcode. NoticeRenderService: generates PDF renewal notice using JasperReports template with owner name, vehicle details, fee amount, and IMb barcode.
backend.background_jobs: ExpiryQueryJob: ItemReader with paging JDBC cursor reading vehicle_registrations WHERE expiry_date BETWEEN SYSDATE AND SYSDATE+30 in chunks of 500. NoticeGenerationJob: ItemProcessor applies FeeCalculationService and BarcodeGenerationService; ItemWriter stores notice records and uploads PDF to Azure Blob. MailingFileJob: reads notice records, formats USPS Intelligent Mail flat file, uploads to Azure Blob mailing-files container.
backend.failure_handling: Spring Batch skip policy: skip vehicle_registrations records with NULL address or invalid vehicle_class; write skipped records to exception_items table with skip_reason. Retry policy: 3 retries with exponential backoff for Oracle read timeouts. Job restart: Spring Batch job repository in Azure SQL enables restart-from-last-successful-chunk on batch failure.
backend.backend_risks: Oracle read throughput under concurrent Azure Batch node load may contend with daytime OLTP traffic if the batch window slips past 05:00 AM; mitigation is configurable throttle on JDBC fetch size and Azure Batch node count cap.

security.auth_design: Azure service principal for batch job execution; managed identity on Azure Batch node pool grants Key Vault secret access without credential files. Oracle database role DMV_BATCH_RO grants SELECT on vehicle_registrations, vehicle_owners, fee_schedules, county_surcharges only.
security.secrets_management: Oracle JDBC URL, username, and password stored as Azure Key Vault secrets; loaded via Spring Cloud Azure SecretClient at application startup. Payment processor API key stored in Key Vault; not loaded until Phase 2.
security.privacy_controls: Renewal notice PDFs contain owner name, address, and vehicle details only; no payment account numbers. Batch logs use a custom Log4j2 filter that masks patterns matching card number regex (16-digit sequences) before writing to Log Analytics.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Each batch run writes a batch_run_logs record to Azure SQL with job_name, run_date, records_processed, records_skipped, status, and completion_time. Skipped records written to exception_items with skip_reason for operations staff review.

data.entities: vehicle_registrations (Oracle, read-only: vin, registration_id, owner_id, expiry_date, vehicle_class, county_code, plate_number). vehicle_owners (Oracle, read-only: owner_id, name, mailing_address, city, state, zip, payment_auth_token_id). fee_schedules (Oracle, read-only: vehicle_class, base_fee, effective_date). county_surcharges (Oracle, read-only: county_code, surcharge_amount, effective_date). renewal_notices (Azure SQL write: notice_id, registration_id, fee_amount, barcode_data, pdf_blob_key, generated_at, mailing_status). batch_run_logs (Azure SQL: run_id, job_name, run_date, records_processed, records_skipped, status, start_time, completion_time). exception_items (Azure SQL: item_id, run_id, registration_id, skip_reason, created_at).
data.storage_design: Azure Blob Storage containers: renewal-notices-pdf (7-year retention via lifecycle policy), mailing-files (30-day retention), batch-manifests (90-day retention). All containers use server-side encryption with customer-managed keys in Key Vault.
data.migration_notes: No schema changes to Oracle; Azure SQL schema for Spring Batch job repository and agency-specific tables (renewal_notices, batch_run_logs, exception_items) deployed via Flyway migrations in CI pipeline.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Batch pool with Windows Server 2022 nodes running Java 21 JRE; pool auto-scales from 0 to 10 dedicated nodes based on pending task count. Spring Boot JAR deployed as Azure Batch application package uploaded via Azure DevOps Pipeline. Logic Apps workflow triggers batch job submission at 01:00 AM daily and monitors completion.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: Maven build with unit tests (JUnit 5), integration tests against an H2 in-memory Oracle-compatible test schema, JAR artifact upload to Azure Artifacts, Azure Batch application package publish. UAT environment gates require manual approval from DMV operations manager before production promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor batch job metrics: task execution count, task failure rate, pool node utilization. Log Analytics workspace for structured JSON logs from Spring Batch with MDC fields: run_id, job_name, chunk_number, records_processed. Azure Alerts: notify operations email on job failure, on completion_time exceeding 04:30 AM (30-minute SLA warning), and on exception_items count exceeding 500 per run.
devops.rollback_strategy: Spring Batch job repository preserves last successful step; failed jobs restart from last committed chunk. Application package rollback via Azure Batch application package version pinning; Logic Apps trigger points to explicit package version.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a Spring Batch automation tool replacing a manual DMV overnight renewal batch. Key design decisions: Spring Batch chunk-oriented processing with Azure SQL job repository (not Oracle, which is read-only) enables restart-from-checkpoint; Azure Batch auto-scaling pool handles variable nightly cohort sizes (10K-50K registrations); PCI scope deferred to Phase 2 payment capture so Phase 1 can be validated against existing manual output. Fee calculation with county surcharge lookup is the most complex business logic and needs its own service class with unit test coverage against the statutory fee tables.
target_output.title: DMV Nightly Registration Renewal Batch Automation
target_output.executive_summary: A Spring Batch automation tool running nightly on Azure Batch that processes vehicle registrations expiring within 30 days, calculates renewal fees against state fee schedules and county surcharge tables, generates renewal notices with USPS Intelligent Mail barcodes, and produces formatted mailing files for postal acceptance. Phase 1 covers notice generation; Phase 2 adds pre-authorized payment capture. All credentials managed via Azure Key Vault; Oracle 19c queried read-only without schema changes.
target_output.architecture_overview: Java 21 Spring Batch application packaged as a JAR and deployed as an Azure Batch application package. Three chained batch jobs execute nightly: ExpiryQueryJob reads from Oracle 19c via OJDBC11 in chunks of 500 registrations, NoticeGenerationJob calculates fees and renders PDF notices with IMb barcodes, and MailingFileJob formats the USPS Intelligent Mail flat file. Spring Batch job repository persists step metadata to Azure SQL, enabling restart-from-last-committed-chunk on failure. Azure Logic Apps triggers the pipeline at 01:00 AM with a 05:00 AM completion SLA. Azure Blob Storage holds notice PDFs (7-year retention), mailing files (30-day retention), and batch run manifests.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2 and Spring Batch 5.0. Database: Oracle 19c (read-only via OJDBC11 with mutual TLS) and Azure SQL (Spring Batch job repository, renewal_notices, batch_run_logs, exception_items). Storage: Azure Blob Storage with customer-managed key encryption. Scheduling: Azure Logic Apps nightly trigger. Compute: Azure Batch pool (Windows Server 2022, Java 21 JRE, auto-scale 0-10 nodes). Secrets: Azure Key Vault with managed identity. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor and Log Analytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Expiry Cohort Query: JDBC cursor reader selecting vehicle_registrations with expiry_date within 30 days, paged in chunks of 500. Fee Calculation Engine: FeeCalculationService applies vehicle_class base fee from fee_schedules joined with county_surcharges by county_code, validates against statutory fee floor and ceiling for the registration year. Notice Generation: JasperReports PDF template populated with owner name, address, vehicle make/model/year, plate number, fee amount, payment due date, and PDF417 IMb barcode. Mailing File Output: USPS Intelligent Mail flat file with mailer ID, serial number, and delivery point data per notice record. Exception Handling: skipped records (NULL address, invalid vehicle_class, Oracle read timeout) written to exception_items with skip_reason for operations review. Batch Run Reporting: batch_run_logs record per job execution with records_processed, records_skipped, and completion_time.
target_output.system_components: Azure Batch pool (auto-scale 0-10 dedicated nodes, Java 21 JRE, Spring Boot JAR as application package). Azure SQL (Spring Batch job repository schema, renewal_notices, batch_run_logs, exception_items tables). Oracle 19c (read-only; DMV_BATCH_RO role with SELECT on vehicle_registrations, vehicle_owners, fee_schedules, county_surcharges). Azure Blob Storage (renewal-notices-pdf, mailing-files, batch-manifests containers with encryption and lifecycle policies). Azure Key Vault (Oracle JDBC credentials, future payment processor API key). Azure Logic Apps (nightly 01:00 AM scheduler, completion monitor, SLA alert workflow). Azure Monitor and Log Analytics (structured batch metrics and alerting).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Batch Execution: Logic Apps trigger fires at 01:00 AM submitting an Azure Batch task for the Spring Boot JAR. ExpiryQueryJob reads Oracle vehicle_registrations in 500-record chunks, writes staging renewal_notices records to Azure SQL. NoticeGenerationJob reads staged records, FeeCalculationService computes fee_amount using vehicle_class and county_code lookups, BarcodeGenerationService generates IMb barcode via licensed USPS library, JasperReports renders PDF notice uploaded to Azure Blob renewal-notices-pdf container. MailingFileJob reads completed notice records, formats USPS Intelligent Mail flat file sorted by delivery point, uploads to Azure Blob mailing-files container for postal pickup. Exception Review: operations staff query exception_items table in Azure Monitor workbook each morning to action records skipped during the night run.
target_output.data_model: Oracle 19c (read-only): vehicle_registrations (vin VARCHAR2, registration_id NUMBER PK, owner_id NUMBER FK, expiry_date DATE, vehicle_class VARCHAR2, county_code VARCHAR2, plate_number VARCHAR2, registration_fee NUMBER). vehicle_owners (owner_id NUMBER PK, name VARCHAR2, mailing_address VARCHAR2, city VARCHAR2, state CHAR(2), zip VARCHAR2, payment_auth_token_id NUMBER). fee_schedules (vehicle_class VARCHAR2, base_fee NUMBER, effective_date DATE). county_surcharges (county_code VARCHAR2, surcharge_amount NUMBER, effective_date DATE). Azure SQL (managed by batch tool): renewal_notices (notice_id BIGINT PK IDENTITY, registration_id BIGINT, fee_amount DECIMAL(10,2), barcode_data VARCHAR(100), pdf_blob_key VARCHAR(500), generated_at DATETIME2, mailing_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT pending). batch_run_logs (run_id BIGINT PK IDENTITY, job_name VARCHAR(100), run_date DATE, records_processed INT, records_skipped INT, status VARCHAR(20), start_time DATETIME2, completion_time DATETIME2). exception_items (item_id BIGINT PK IDENTITY, run_id BIGINT FK, registration_id BIGINT, skip_reason VARCHAR(200), created_at DATETIME2).
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Spring Batch JobOperator for programmatic job launch and monitoring. Azure Batch REST API used by Logic Apps to submit tasks and poll job status. Azure Blob Storage SDK for notice PDF and mailing file upload. Oracle JDBC via OracleDataSource with mutual TLS. Azure SQL via Spring JDBC with HikariCP connection pool. All credentials loaded from Azure Key Vault via Spring Cloud Azure SecretClient at application context initialization.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Credential management: Oracle JDBC URL, username, and password stored as Key Vault secrets; loaded via managed identity on Azure Batch node pool using Spring Cloud Azure; no credential files on disk. Oracle connection via OJDBC11 mutual TLS with server certificate pinned in Azure Key Vault. PCI-DSS SAQ-D scope: payment auth tokens stored as Key Vault secret references in vehicle_owners only (Phase 2); Phase 1 does not load or process payment tokens. PAN masking: Log4j2 custom filter masks 16-digit sequences matching card number pattern in all log output before writing to Log Analytics. Renewal notice PDFs contain owner name, address, vehicle details, and fee amount only; no payment data included. State privacy statute compliance: vehicle owner PII (name, address) handled under state motor vehicle records confidentiality provisions; batch personnel with Azure AD access to Log Analytics must be DMV employees.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Batch pool: auto-scale formula scaling from 0 to 10 dedicated DS3_v2 nodes based on pending task count; pool uses Windows Server 2022 with Java 21 JRE pre-installed via startup task. Spring Boot JAR published as Azure Batch application package version-tagged by Git SHA from Azure DevOps Pipeline. Logic Apps workflow: nightly trigger at 01:00 AM, submits Batch task referencing current package version, polls Batch task status every 5 minutes, writes completion status to Log Analytics. Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: Maven build, JUnit tests (85% coverage gate), integration tests against H2 in-memory Oracle-compatible schema, artifact upload to Azure Artifacts, Batch application package publish. UAT environment requires manual DMV operations manager approval gate before production deployment. Azure SQL schema managed via Flyway migrations executed in CI pipeline.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor custom metrics: batch_records_processed (counter by job_name and run_date), batch_records_skipped (counter by skip_reason), batch_job_duration_minutes (gauge), batch_completion_hour (gauge for SLA tracking). Log Analytics workspace receives structured JSON logs with MDC fields: run_id, job_name, chunk_number, records_processed_this_chunk. Azure Alerts: notify operations team on any job failure; warn on completion_time projection exceeding 04:30 AM threshold (30-minute SLA buffer); alert when exception_items count per run exceeds 500 (indicating systematic data quality issue in Oracle source data).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Batch pool billed per node-hour; auto-scale to zero between nightly runs eliminates idle compute costs. DS3_v2 nodes at ~$0.14/hour; typical nightly run of 1-2 hours across 3-5 nodes costs under $2/night. Azure SQL Basic tier sufficient for job repository and reporting tables (~$5/month). Azure Blob Storage for notice PDFs at $0.02/GB/month; 7-year retention for approximately 1 million notices/year at 50KB average equals approximately 3.5TB after 7 years. Total estimated cost: $300-500/month including storage growth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (12 weeks): ExpiryQueryJob, FeeCalculationService with county surcharge lookup, NoticeGenerationJob with JasperReports PDF rendering and IMb barcode, MailingFileJob, exception handling with exception_items table, Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline, UAT validation against 3 months of historical renewal data. Phase 2 (8 weeks post-Phase 1): PreAuthorizedPaymentCaptureStep added to NoticeGenerationJob; Key Vault integration for payment processor API key; PCI-DSS control review; payment_transactions table; mailing_status update to paid/pending based on capture result. Phase 3 (future): real-time single-registration processing endpoint for kiosk channel.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2; Maven build with Checkstyle enforcing Google Java Style. JUnit 5 unit tests for FeeCalculationService covering all 12 vehicle classes and 67 county surcharge combinations; 85% line coverage gate in CI. Integration tests use Spring Batch test utilities with H2 in-memory database for job repository and a mock Oracle DataSource. All Azure resource provisioning (Batch pool, Blob containers, Key Vault access policies, Azure SQL schema) defined as Bicep templates in the repository; manual portal changes are prohibited. Flyway manages Azure SQL schema migrations; migration scripts reviewed in PR before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle 19c JDBC connection contention if batch window slips past morning OLTP traffic start. Mitigation: JDBC fetch size capped at 500 rows and Azure Batch node count capped at 10 to limit concurrent Oracle read connections; completion alert at 04:30 AM gives 30-minute buffer. Risk: JasperReports PDF rendering performance for large cohorts (50K+ registrations). Mitigation: NoticeGenerationJob parallelized with Spring Batch partitioned step across 4 Batch nodes using registration_id range partitioning. Risk: Licensed USPS IMb barcode library version dependency creating build fragility. Mitigation: library JAR vendored into the repository under a dedicated /lib directory with SHA-256 checksum verification in CI. Tradeoff: Azure SQL for Spring Batch job repository instead of an in-memory H2 database adds Azure SQL cost but enables restart-from-checkpoint which is non-negotiable for a statutory-deadline batch.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Azure SQL for Spring Batch job repository over Oracle to avoid DDL on the frozen Oracle schema. Chose JasperReports for PDF rendering over Apache PDFBox for the structured template support needed for agency-branded notices. Selected Azure Logic Apps for scheduling over a custom cron to align with existing agency orchestration standard. USPS IMb library vendored into repository pending formal license agreement with USPS.

metadata.notes: First-pass plan for Spring Batch DMV renewal automation; Phase 1 defers PCI complexity to Phase 2; Azure SQL job repository workaround for frozen Oracle schema is well-motivated.

profile.domain: Law Enforcement / Public Safety Technology

project_goal.value: Provide authorized law enforcement officers with a unified API gateway to query warrants, case records, and vehicle registration data from multiple fragmented records systems through a single authenticated endpoint
project_goal.rationale: Officers currently log into 4-6 separate records systems per query, creating safety delays during traffic stops and field investigations

target_users.value: Sworn law enforcement officers, detectives, and dispatch supervisors within the department
target_users.rationale: Access limited to credentialed department personnel; no public or civilian access

project_class.rationale: Backend API gateway with no UI; consuming applications (CAD, MDT terminals) provide the officer-facing interface

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, integrations, public_api, devops
capabilities.rationale: API gateway with upstream system integrations, RBAC auth, query audit logging, and deployment automation

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system fan-out queries, RBAC authorization by record type and officer role, tamper-evident audit logging, and upstream system adapter patterns

risk_level.rationale: CJIS-regulated criminal justice data; unauthorized access or data leakage could compromise investigations or enable targeting of individuals

data_sensitivity.rationale: Warrant and criminal case data classified as CJIS-protected; vehicle registration data is sensitive PII

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible only within the department network and authorized VPN endpoints; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Keycloak RBAC with officer, detective, supervisor, and dispatch roles; MFA required for all roles; per-role authorization policies governing which record types and query scopes are accessible
access_model.rationale: CJIS Security Policy mandates advanced authentication and role-based data access controls

feature_scope.value: Warrant record queries by subject name or ID, active warrant status checks, case record summaries by case number, vehicle registration lookups by plate or VIN, officer credential validation, query audit logging, upstream system health monitoring
feature_scope.rationale: Covers the primary records query workflows that currently require multi-system logins

mvp_scope.value: Warrant lookup, vehicle registration query, and case record summary endpoints with RBAC enforcement and tamper-evident audit logging for 3 upstream systems
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum set to eliminate the most common multi-system query scenarios during traffic stops

security_baseline.value: mTLS for upstream system connections; JWT with short expiry (15 min) for officer tokens; all queries logged to append-only audit table; no caching of warrant or case record responses; AES-256 encryption at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit
security_baseline.rationale: CJIS Security Policy minimum controls for criminal justice information systems

frontend_stack.value: None - consumed by existing CAD system and officer MDT terminal applications
frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class with no UI surface; frontend specialist not applicable

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; upstream adapters as interface implementations for each records system; Redis 7 for rate limiting and officer session metadata only
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency response times critical for officer safety during field queries; interface-based adapters enable adding upstream systems without core gateway changes

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for gateway-managed data: officer credentials cache, authorization policies, and append-only audit log; upstream records systems are the authoritative sources and are queried live
data_platform.rationale: Gateway does not own criminal records data; PostgreSQL stores only gateway operational data and the compliance audit trail

hosting_target.value: Azure Government AKS in department data center; no cloud egress for CJIS data
hosting_target.rationale: CJIS Security Policy restricts criminal justice data to authorized government cloud environments with no public internet routing

privacy_retention_policy.value: Query audit logs retained 7 years per CJIS requirements; officer credential cache purged within 1 hour of credential revocation; no warrant or case data persisted in gateway storage
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CJIS audit log retention and data minimization requirements

future_scope.value: Integration with state AFIS fingerprint database, federal NCIC terminal emulation, real-time alert subscriptions for warrant status changes, mobile officer app as a gateway consumer
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP integrations to broaden records coverage and support mobile officers

constraints.value: Upstream records systems use legacy SOAP and proprietary TCP protocols; gateway must translate to REST; P99 query latency must be under 2 seconds; CJIS data cannot leave the department network boundary
constraints.rationale: Legacy upstream systems cannot be modified; 2-second SLA critical for officer safety; network boundary constraint is regulatory

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for gateway service; per-upstream-system availability dashboards; PagerDuty alerts for upstream system degradation and query error rate spikes
observability_baseline.rationale: Department IT requires visibility into upstream system availability and gateway query performance

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; GitOps-style deployment to AKS with manifest review required for production changes
execution_preference.rationale: Department IT standard toolchain

compliance_context.value: CJIS Security Policy v5.9 (FBI); 28 CFR Part 23 criminal intelligence system operating policies; state police records confidentiality statutes; department data governance policy
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=confidential trigger mandatory compliance context

project.goal: Unified REST API gateway for warrant, case record, and vehicle registration queries from departmental records systems
project.target_users: Sworn officers, detectives, supervisors, dispatch
project.timeline: 6-month delivery for MVP with 3 upstream integrations

backend.adapters: Interface-based upstream adapters for SOAP warrant system, TCP case records system, REST vehicle registration system

security.auth: Keycloak JWT with 15-min expiry and MFA
security.upstream_transport: mTLS to all upstream system connections

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for gateway operational data and audit log
data.cache: Redis 7 for rate limiting counters and officer session metadata only

constraints.network: No CJIS data outside department network boundary

open_questions.upstream_soap_auth: Confirm SOAP warrant system auth method - basic or certificate
open_questions.rate_limit_policy: Per-officer or per-role rate limit thresholds TBD with operations

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is appropriately focused on the query gateway MVP. Warrant lookup and vehicle registration are the highest-frequency field query types; case record summary adds detective workflow value. The constraint that upstream systems cannot be modified makes the adapter pattern mandatory - interface-based adapters for SOAP and proprietary TCP protocols are the correct design response.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is a good fit for a latency-sensitive API gateway. The upstream adapter interface pattern is correct but needs more specificity - the SOAP warrant adapter will need a generated WSDL client and the TCP case records adapter will need a custom protocol framing layer. Fan-out query parallelism is important when an officer query touches multiple systems simultaneously; context-bounded goroutines with timeout propagation are appropriate here.
reasoner_reviews.security: CJIS Security Policy v5.9 requirements are demanding. mTLS to all upstream systems is mandatory, not optional. The 15-minute JWT expiry is correct for CJIS. The append-only audit log must capture officer badge number, query type, subject identifiers queried, upstream system hit, response status, and client IP - the current plan is too vague on audit field specifics. No warrant or case record data can be cached per CJIS data minimization requirements.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2-second P99 latency with fan-out to multiple legacy systems is aggressive. Upstream system response times for SOAP and proprietary TCP protocols may themselves approach 1-2 seconds. Circuit breakers on each upstream adapter are essential to prevent one slow system from timing out all gateway queries. The CJIS network boundary constraint needs explicit enforcement via AKS network policies.

backend.service_design: Single Go binary with Gin router; upstream adapters behind UpstreamAdapter interface (Query(ctx, QueryRequest) (QueryResult, error)); concrete adapters: SOAPWarrantAdapter (gowsdl-generated client for warrant management system), TCPCaseAdapter (custom framing over TCP for case records system), RESTVehicleAdapter (net/http client for DMV vehicle registration API). Fan-out engine parallelizes queries across applicable adapters using goroutines with context deadline propagation. Response merger normalizes heterogeneous adapter responses into canonical QueryResponse schema.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /queries/warrant (subject identifier, query reason), POST /queries/vehicle (plate or VIN), POST /queries/case (case number), GET /health/upstream (upstream system availability status for dispatch dashboards). All endpoints require Bearer JWT. Response schema normalizes all upstream data into canonical fields regardless of source system format.
backend.failure_handling: Per-upstream circuit breaker (gobreaker) with half-open probe after 30-second open window; upstream timeout of 1.5 seconds leaving headroom for gateway overhead under 2-second SLA; partial response policy returns available upstream results if 1 of 3 systems is degraded, with degraded_sources field in response; PagerDuty alert when any upstream circuit breaker opens.

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm with officer, detective, supervisor, and dispatch client roles; Gin middleware validates JWT signature and expiry on every request; officer badge number extracted from JWT sub claim; MFA enforced at Keycloak login; token refresh requires re-authentication after 15-minute expiry.
security.authorization_model: Per-endpoint authorization policies enforced in Gin middleware: officer role may query warrants and vehicle registrations, detective role adds case record queries, supervisor role adds cross-officer query history access, dispatch role restricted to active warrant status checks only. Policy violations logged to audit_log with AUTHORIZATION_DENIED event type.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: query_audit_log table in PostgreSQL: audit_id UUID, officer_id, badge_number, role, query_type ENUM warrant/vehicle/case, subject_identifier_hash (SHA-256 of queried name/plate/case number - not plaintext), upstream_systems_hit JSONB, response_status, client_ip, query_timestamp. Table has no UPDATE or DELETE grants; append-only enforced via PostgreSQL row security policy. Audit log exported to SIEM nightly.

data.entities: query_audit_log (audit_id UUID PK, officer_id VARCHAR, badge_number VARCHAR, role VARCHAR, query_type ENUM warrant/vehicle/case/health, subject_identifier_hash VARCHAR SHA-256, upstream_systems_hit JSONB, response_status ENUM success/partial/failure/denied, latency_ms INT, client_ip INET, query_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). officer_session_metadata (session_id VARCHAR PK in Redis, officer_id, badge_number, role, issued_at, expires_at - 15-min TTL). authorization_policies (policy_id UUID PK, role VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, action ENUM query/view_history, allowed BOOLEAN, updated_at). upstream_system_registry (system_id UUID PK, system_name VARCHAR, protocol ENUM soap/tcp/rest, base_endpoint VARCHAR encrypted, health_status ENUM healthy/degraded/unavailable, last_checked_at).
data.consistency_notes: Gateway is read-only against upstream systems; no write operations. PostgreSQL audit_log is append-only with row-level security preventing officer-role deletion or modification. Redis session cache has hard 15-minute TTL matching JWT expiry; no session extension without re-authentication.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster on Azure Government with gateway deployment in isolated namespace; Kubernetes NetworkPolicies restricting egress to upstream system endpoints only (no internet egress); PostgreSQL on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in same VNet; Redis on Azure Cache for Redis Basic tier in same VNet; Keycloak deployed in separate auth namespace on same cluster.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: go vet and staticcheck lint, unit tests with mocked upstream adapters (90% coverage gate), integration test against staging Keycloak and PostgreSQL, container image build and push to Azure Container Registry, AKS staging deploy with smoke tests against mock upstream systems, production deploy requiring GitOps MR approval from department IT security officer.
devops.devops_risks: SOAP warrant system may have undocumented auth requirements discovered only during integration testing. AKS network policy complexity for 3 upstream system endpoint CIDRs requires careful validation in staging. Keycloak certificate rotation during officer shifts could cause brief authentication outages if not handled with graceful rolling cert rollout.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a CJIS-compliant API gateway for law enforcement records queries. Key decisions: Go with Gin for sub-2-second P99 latency; interface-based upstream adapters to isolate SOAP/TCP/REST protocol differences behind a common contract; append-only PostgreSQL audit log with subject identifier hashing rather than plaintext storage; circuit breakers on each upstream adapter with partial response policy to maintain availability when legacy systems degrade. Plan quality is intentionally weak - several sections are thin on specifics given this is a first-pass weak-quality training row.
target_output.title: Law Enforcement Records Query Gateway API
target_output.executive_summary: A CJIS-compliant internal API service built in Go providing authorized law enforcement officers with a unified query gateway to warrant records, case summaries, and vehicle registration data from three legacy upstream records systems. Deployed on Azure Government AKS with Keycloak RBAC authentication, mTLS upstream connections, and an append-only query audit log meeting CJIS Security Policy requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go Gin API gateway deployed on Azure Government AKS, accepting officer JWT-authenticated queries and fanning out to upstream records systems via interface-based adapters. SOAPWarrantAdapter, TCPCaseAdapter, and RESTVehicleAdapter implement a common UpstreamAdapter interface; the fan-out engine parallelizes queries with goroutine-per-adapter and context deadline propagation. Responses are normalized to a canonical QueryResponse schema. All queries logged to an append-only PostgreSQL audit table. Redis handles rate limiting counters. No CJIS data cached or persisted in the gateway.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin. Auth: Keycloak with JWT (15-min expiry) and MFA. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL for audit log and gateway operational data. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis for rate limiting only. Upstream: gowsdl-generated SOAP client for warrant system, custom TCP framing for case records, net/http for vehicle registration. Container: AKS on Azure Government. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Warrant Query endpoint: officer submits subject name or ID, gateway queries warrant management system, returns active warrant list with severity and issuing court. Vehicle Registration Query: plate or VIN lookup against DMV registration API, returns registered owner and vehicle details. Case Record Summary: case number lookup against case records system, returns case status, assigned detective, and charge summary. Upstream Health endpoint for dispatch supervisors. Query audit log captures all queries with officer identity and subject identifier hash.
target_output.system_components: Go Gin gateway service (AKS deployment, 3 replicas). UpstreamAdapter interface with SOAPWarrantAdapter, TCPCaseAdapter, RESTVehicleAdapter implementations. Fan-out engine with per-adapter goroutines and context deadline. Keycloak auth server (separate AKS namespace). PostgreSQL 16 for audit log and authorization policies. Redis 7 for rate limiting counters. Azure Container Registry for images. AKS NetworkPolicies restricting egress to upstream system endpoints only.
target_output.workflows: Officer Field Query: officer's MDT terminal or CAD application sends POST /api/v1/queries/warrant with Bearer JWT; Gin middleware validates JWT signature and expiry, extracts badge_number and role, checks authorization policy; fan-out engine spawns goroutines for applicable upstream adapters with 1.5-second context deadline; SOAPWarrantAdapter issues SOAP request over mTLS to warrant management system; response normalized to canonical WarrantResult schema; audit record written to query_audit_log with subject_identifier_hash (SHA-256 of queried name); canonical response returned to caller. Upstream Degradation Handling: if SOAPWarrantAdapter circuit breaker is open, fan-out engine skips that adapter, includes degraded_sources: ['warrant_system'] in response, and emits PagerDuty alert; officer receives available results from healthy upstream systems without a full query failure.
target_output.data_model: query_audit_log (audit_id UUID PK, officer_id VARCHAR, badge_number VARCHAR, role VARCHAR, query_type ENUM warrant/vehicle/case, subject_identifier_hash VARCHAR, upstream_systems_hit JSONB, response_status ENUM success/partial/failure/denied, latency_ms INT, client_ip INET, query_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ - append-only via row security). authorization_policies (policy_id UUID PK, role VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, allowed BOOLEAN, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). upstream_system_registry (system_id UUID PK, system_name, protocol ENUM soap/tcp/rest, health_status ENUM healthy/degraded/unavailable, last_checked_at). officer_session_metadata stored in Redis with 15-min TTL.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints at /api/v1, Bearer JWT required. POST /queries/warrant body: {subject_name, subject_dob, query_reason}. POST /queries/vehicle body: {plate or vin, state_code, query_reason}. POST /queries/case body: {case_number, query_reason}. GET /health/upstream returns per-system status for dispatch supervisors (supervisor role required). Response envelope: {query_id, officer_id, results: [], degraded_sources: [], response_time_ms, timestamp}. Error responses follow RFC 7807 problem+json with no upstream error detail exposed to callers.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CJIS Security Policy v5.9: mTLS to all upstream system connections enforcing mutual certificate authentication; JWT 15-minute expiry with no refresh token (re-auth required); MFA at Keycloak login mandatory for all roles; no warrant or case record caching in gateway. Audit log: query_audit_log in PostgreSQL with append-only row security policy; subject identifiers stored as SHA-256 hashes not plaintext; log exported to SIEM nightly. AKS NetworkPolicies: no internet egress from gateway namespace; egress restricted to upstream system endpoint CIDRs and Keycloak namespace only. 28 CFR Part 23: criminal intelligence queries require documented query_reason captured in audit log.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster on Azure Government; gateway deployment in restricted namespace with resource limits and liveness probes. PostgreSQL and Redis on Azure managed services within the same VNet. Azure DevOps Pipeline: go vet, staticcheck, unit tests with mocked adapters (90% coverage gate), integration test in staging, container build to ACR, staging AKS deploy, production deploy with IT security officer GitOps approval. NetworkPolicy manifests reviewed before production apply.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing on all gateway requests with upstream adapter span instrumentation. Custom metrics: query_latency_p99 by query_type and upstream system, upstream_circuit_breaker_state by system_name, query_error_rate by role and query_type. Dashboard: upstream system availability status board for dispatch supervisors. PagerDuty alerts: any upstream circuit breaker open, gateway error rate above 5%, P99 latency above 2 seconds.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS cluster shared with other department services; gateway deployment 3 replicas at low CPU allocation. PostgreSQL Basic tier sufficient for audit log append workload. Redis Basic tier for rate limiting. Primary cost is Azure Government premium over standard pricing. Scaling: horizontal pod autoscaler on gateway deployment triggered by query throughput.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): SOAPWarrantAdapter, RESTVehicleAdapter, RBAC middleware, audit logging, AKS deployment, Keycloak realm setup, integration with CAD system. Phase 2 (6 weeks): TCPCaseAdapter for case records system, detective role query flows, upstream health dashboard, SIEM audit log export. Phase 3 (8 weeks): NCIC terminal emulation adapter, mobile officer app consumer onboarding, real-time warrant alert subscriptions.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum for dependency pinning. staticcheck and go vet in CI. Unit tests mock all upstream adapters via the UpstreamAdapter interface; 90% coverage on business logic. Integration tests use containerized Keycloak and PostgreSQL in Azure DevOps pipeline. No CJIS data in test fixtures; synthetic warrant and vehicle data only. All adapter implementations reviewed by department IT security before merging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SOAP warrant system has undocumented auth requirements not surfaced until integration testing. Mitigation: early spike sprint to validate SOAP auth before full adapter development. Risk: legacy TCP case records system has no documented protocol specification. Mitigation: protocol analysis sprint with department IT and system vendor. Risk: 2-second P99 with 3 upstream legacy systems is tight; SOAP round-trips alone may approach 1 second. Mitigation: circuit breakers and partial response policy prevent single slow system from breaching SLA for other query types.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Go with Gin selected over Java Spring for latency characteristics and goroutine-based fan-out simplicity. PostgreSQL selected for audit log over append-only log service to satisfy CJIS audit trail integrity requirements. Subject identifiers stored as SHA-256 hashes in audit log balancing CJIS audit requirements with data minimization principles. No response caching of warrant or case data per CJIS data minimization requirements.

metadata.notes: First-pass weak-quality law enforcement records gateway; CJIS compliance framing present but several sections thin on operational specifics.

profile.domain: Civic Tech Open-Source TypeScript SDK for Government Open Data

project_goal.value: TypeScript SDK for interacting with state open data portals implementing the Socrata SODA API, providing strongly-typed dataset discovery, SoQL query building, and streaming export utilities to reduce boilerplate for civic tech developers
project_goal.rationale: Reduces repeated HTTP and response-parsing code across civic tech applications consuming government open data

target_users.value: TypeScript developers building civic applications, data journalists, and open data researchers consuming government Socrata portals
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified from GitHub issue demand and Socrata developer community

project_class.rationale: Distributed as npm package; no hosted platform or server infrastructure

capabilities.rationale: HTTP client integration with Socrata SODA API, typed query builder, and streaming response handling

complexity_level.rationale: Query builder and HTTP client wrapper; no stateful processing or complex business logic

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored; connects only to public open data APIs

data_sensitivity.rationale: All Socrata data queried is publicly available open government data

external_exposure.rationale: Distributed publicly via npm; calls public Socrata portal APIs

access_model.value: Optional API app token passthrough for higher rate limits; anonymous access supported for basic queries
access_model.rationale: Socrata allows anonymous queries at lower rate limits; app tokens increase throughput

feature_scope.value: Dataset discovery by portal domain, SoQL query builder with $where/$select/$order/$limit/$offset, typed response parsing, cursor-based pagination via $offset, streaming download for large dataset exports, portal configuration management
feature_scope.rationale: Core Socrata SODA 2.1 features requested by civic tech developers

mvp_scope.value: SoQL query builder, typed dataset fetch with pagination, and streaming export; portal configuration with optional app token
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers most common open data consumption patterns for initial npm release

security_baseline.value: App token never logged; HTTPS enforced for all Socrata API calls; no credential storage in SDK
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents app token leakage in application logs

frontend_stack.value: None
frontend_stack.rationale: Library SDK with no UI component; compatible with both Node.js and browser environments

backend_stack.value: TypeScript with Bun runtime for development and testing
backend_stack.rationale: Bun provides fast TypeScript execution for development; output is ESM/CJS dual build compatible with Node.js and browsers

data_platform.value: None; SDK is stateless with no persistent storage
data_platform.rationale: All Socrata response data lives in memory and is returned to the caller

hosting_target.value: npm package registry
hosting_target.rationale: Standard JavaScript package distribution channel

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained by SDK; all Socrata data is publicly available and caller responsibility to handle downstream storage
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK is a stateless query tool over public open data

future_scope.value: Browser bundle for client-side use in static sites, optional response caching layer, CLI wrapper, GeoJSON geometry support for spatial datasets
future_scope.rationale: Browser and spatial support are top community requests

constraints.value: Zero mandatory dependencies beyond built-in fetch API; TypeScript strict mode; ESM and CJS dual build output for maximum ecosystem compatibility
constraints.rationale: Dependency-free design reduces bundle size for browser consumers; dual build covers Node.js CJS projects

observability_baseline.value: Optional debug callback for HTTP request/response logging; no telemetry collected from SDK consumers
observability_baseline.rationale: Debug callback lets callers wire into their own logging infrastructure without SDK imposing a logger dependency

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development; semantic versioning; npm publish on tagged release via GitHub Actions provenance attestation
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source library release cadence with npm provenance for supply-chain transparency

project.goal: TypeScript SDK for Socrata SODA API with typed query builder and streaming export
project.target_users: TypeScript developers in civic tech, data journalism, and open data research
project.timeline: npm release in 4 weeks from round 1

backend.runtime: TypeScript with Bun for dev; fetch API for HTTP

security.auth: Optional Socrata app token as X-App-Token header; never logged

data.models: TypeScript generics for typed dataset row shapes; SoqlQuery builder type

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: biome lint/format check, tsc type check, bun test, npm publish on tag via npm provenance

constraints.build: ESM + CJS dual output via bun build

reasoner_reviews.product: The SoQL query builder is the highest-value feature — developers currently construct raw URL query strings which are error-prone and untyped. Dataset discovery and streaming export are well-scoped for a library of this complexity. The zero-dependency constraint is a strong selling point for browser consumers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using native fetch as the HTTP layer is correct for dual browser/Node.js compatibility and zero dependencies. The AsyncGenerator pattern for streaming large datasets prevents memory exhaustion on datasets with hundreds of thousands of rows. TypeScript generics for row shapes provide IDE autocompletion without codegen.
reasoner_reviews.security: App token masking in debug output is the primary security concern for a library of this type. The token should be excluded from all debug callback payloads. HTTPS-only calls enforce transport security without additional configuration.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero-dependency constraint is achievable — fetch is available in Bun, modern Node.js (18+), and all target browsers. Dual ESM/CJS build via bun build covers the full TypeScript ecosystem without requiring esbuild or Rollup.

backend.service_design: SocrataClient top-level class accepting portal_domain and optional app_token; composes DatasetClient (discovery and fetch) and ExportClient (streaming download); SoqlQueryBuilder provides fluent interface for constructing type-safe SoQL query strings
backend.api_patterns: DatasetClient.query<T>(dataset_id, SoqlQuery) -> Promise<T[]>; DatasetClient.paginate<T>(dataset_id, SoqlQuery, page_size) -> AsyncGenerator<T[]>; ExportClient.stream<T>(dataset_id, SoqlQuery) -> AsyncGenerator<T>; typed SoqlQuery object prevents raw string injection
backend.business_modules: socrata/client.ts (SocrataClient), socrata/dataset.ts (DatasetClient, DatasetMetadata), socrata/export.ts (ExportClient), socrata/query.ts (SoqlQueryBuilder, SoqlQuery type), socrata/http.ts (internal fetch wrapper with token injection and debug callback), socrata/errors.ts (SocrataAPIError, SocrataRateLimitError)
backend.backend_risks: Socrata portal API response schemas vary by dataset; TypeScript generics provide type safety only when callers define accurate row types — documentation must set expectations about runtime vs compile-time safety

security.auth_design: App token injected as X-App-Token header in internal fetch wrapper; token value excluded from debug callback payload (replaced with '***' in logged request headers)
security.secrets_management: No token storage in SDK; documentation advises sourcing from environment variables in Node.js applications
security.security_risks: Browser consumers may expose app tokens in client-side JavaScript bundles; documentation must warn that app tokens carry rate limit quota, not personal data access, and are lower-risk than auth credentials

data.entities: DatasetMetadata (id: string, name: string, description: string, columns: ColumnSchema[], row_count: number, updated_at: string, portal_domain: string). ColumnSchema (field_name: string, data_type_name: string, description: string). SoqlQuery (select: string[], where: string | null, order: string | null, limit: number | null, offset: number). QueryResult<T> generic wrapping T[] response rows
data.storage_design: No storage; all responses are in-memory TypeScript objects or AsyncGenerator streams
data.data_risks: Socrata portal rate limits (default 1000 req/hr anonymous, 10000 req/hr with app token) constrain bulk dataset enumeration; streaming export respects rate limits but callers must implement backoff for multi-dataset workflows

devops.deployment_topology: Single npm package from GitHub repository; no hosted infrastructure
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on push: biome check for lint and format, tsc --noEmit for type checking, bun test for unit tests against mocked fetch responses; publish job on semver tag using npm publish with --provenance flag for supply-chain attestation
devops.rollback_strategy: npm deprecate for critical bugs while patch release is prepared; semantic versioning with patch releases for bug fixes; CHANGELOG.md per release

ISS-001.title: SoQL $where clause accepts raw strings enabling injection
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 SoqlQueryBuilder accepted a raw string for $where parameter; callers could construct queries like 'city = ' + userInput which is vulnerable to SoQL injection on portals with write access (rare but possible on authenticated portals)

history.note: Raw string accepted in round 1 query builder

ISS-002.title: AsyncGenerator streaming leaks memory on aborted iteration
ISS-002.detail: If a caller breaks out of a paginate() or stream() AsyncGenerator early without consuming all pages, the underlying fetch request for the next page may still be in flight, causing a memory leak and uncaught promise rejection in long-running Node.js processes

history.note: Generator cleanup on early return not implemented in round 1

focus_issues.title: AsyncGenerator streaming leaks memory on aborted iteration
focus_issues.detail: Generator return() method not implemented; early for-await-of break leaves in-flight fetch requests unresolved

latest_recommendations: Implement AsyncGenerator return() cleanup method that calls AbortController.abort() on any in-flight fetch request when iteration is terminated early, preventing memory leaks in long-running processes
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Socrata SODA TypeScript SDK - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Memory leak in streaming iteration is a correctness issue for long-running civic data pipeline consumers

previous_audits.summary: Well-structured TypeScript SDK with clean SoQL query builder and correct AsyncGenerator approach for streaming. ISS-001 resolved by switching $where to a typed predicate object. One remaining issue: AsyncGenerator does not implement return() cleanup, causing memory leaks on aborted iteration in Node.js data pipeline consumers.
strengths: Zero-dependency design with native fetch is correct for dual browser/Node.js compatibility
SoqlQueryBuilder fluent interface provides type-safe query construction without codegen
Dual ESM/CJS build strategy via bun build covers full npm ecosystem
concerns: AsyncGenerator return() not implemented — for-await-of break in a large dataset export leaves in-flight fetch unresolved
blocking_issues: AsyncGenerator memory leak on early iteration termination
recommendations: Add AbortController to each paginate() and stream() call; implement AsyncGenerator return() method that calls abort() on the active controller, allowing callers to use break in for-await-of without leaking fetch resources

issue_updates.title: SoQL $where raw string injection
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 SoqlQueryBuilder replaces raw string $where with typed SoqlPredicate object

issue_updates.title: AsyncGenerator memory leak on aborted iteration
issue_updates.detail: return() method not implemented in round 1 generator

previous_plan.title: Socrata SODA TypeScript SDK - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK for querying state Socrata open data portals with typed SoQL query builder, paginated dataset fetch, and AsyncGenerator streaming export. Zero dependencies, Bun-developed, dual ESM/CJS npm package.
previous_plan.api_design: SocrataClient(portal_domain, app_token?). DatasetClient.query<T>(dataset_id, SoqlQuery): Promise<T[]>. DatasetClient.paginate<T>(dataset_id, query, page_size): AsyncGenerator<T[]>. ExportClient.stream<T>(dataset_id, query): AsyncGenerator<T>. SoqlQueryBuilder.where(raw_string) — accepts raw string (ISS-001 vulnerability, resolved in round 2).
previous_plan.workflows: Dataset pagination: caller creates query with SoqlQueryBuilder, calls paginate(), iterates with for-await-of. If caller breaks early, in-flight fetch for next page is not aborted (ISS-002).

best_plan.title: Socrata SODA TypeScript SDK - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK for querying state Socrata open data portals with typed SoQL query builder, paginated dataset fetch, and AsyncGenerator streaming export. Zero dependencies, Bun-developed, dual ESM/CJS npm package.
best_plan.api_design: SocrataClient(portal_domain, app_token?). DatasetClient.query<T>(dataset_id, SoqlQuery): Promise<T[]>. DatasetClient.paginate<T>(dataset_id, query, page_size): AsyncGenerator<T[]>. ExportClient.stream<T>(dataset_id, query): AsyncGenerator<T>.
best_plan.workflows: Dataset pagination: caller creates query with SoqlQueryBuilder, calls paginate(), iterates with for-await-of. If caller breaks early, in-flight fetch for next page is not aborted (ISS-002).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 of the Socrata TypeScript SDK. ISS-001 (SoQL injection via raw $where string) was resolved in round 1 by switching to typed SoqlPredicate. This round addresses ISS-002: AsyncGenerator memory leak when iteration is terminated early. The fix is straightforward — add an AbortController to each paginate() and stream() generator, implement the return() method to call abort(), and update the internal fetch wrapper to respect AbortSignal. This is a correctness fix for long-running Node.js data pipeline consumers.

fix_report.action_taken: Added AbortController instantiation at the start of each paginate() and stream() AsyncGenerator call; implemented the generator's return() method to call controller.abort() before yielding return; updated internal fetch wrapper to pass signal: controller.signal so in-flight fetch is cancelled on early iteration termination; added bun test case verifying that breaking a for-await-of loop does not leave unresolved promises
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long-running Node.js civic data pipeline processes can safely break out of Socrata streaming loops without accumulating unresolved fetch promises or memory pressure from buffered response bodies

target_output.title: Socrata SODA TypeScript SDK - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency TypeScript SDK for querying state and local government Socrata SODA open data portals. Provides a typed SoQL query builder, paginated dataset fetch, and AbortController-backed AsyncGenerator streaming export for large dataset ingestion. Distributed as a dual ESM/CJS npm package built with Bun, targeting TypeScript developers in civic applications, data journalism, and government open data research. This revision fixes AsyncGenerator resource cleanup on early iteration termination.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure TypeScript library with no runtime dependencies beyond the native fetch API. SocrataClient is the entry point accepting a portal_domain such as 'data.cityofchicago.org' and an optional Socrata app token. It composes DatasetClient for typed query execution and paginated iteration, and ExportClient for row-level AsyncGenerator streaming over large datasets. SoqlQueryBuilder provides a fluent interface producing SoqlQuery objects — typed $select columns, $where via SoqlPredicate objects (no raw strings), $order, $limit, and $offset — which the internal fetch wrapper serializes to Socrata SODA URL parameters. All AsyncGenerator-based methods (paginate, stream) instantiate an AbortController and implement return() to abort in-flight fetch requests on early iteration termination. The library builds to dual ESM and CJS output via bun build, compatible with Bun, Node.js 18+, and modern browsers.
target_output.technology_stack: TypeScript 5.4 in strict mode. Bun 1.3 as the development runtime and build tool. Native fetch API for HTTP (zero dependencies). biome for lint and format. bun test for unit testing with mocked fetch via fetch-mock-bun. bun build for dual ESM/CJS output. GitHub Actions for CI with npm provenance attestation on release.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Dataset Query: call DatasetClient.query<ChicagoCrimeRow>(dataset_id, query) to fetch a typed array of row objects matching the SoQL filter. Dataset Pagination: call DatasetClient.paginate<T>(dataset_id, query, page_size) to iterate over batches via AsyncGenerator with AbortController cleanup on early break. Streaming Export: call ExportClient.stream<T>(dataset_id, query) to iterate over individual rows from large datasets without full response materialization. SoQL Query Building: compose $select, $where (typed predicates), $order, $limit, $offset via SoqlQueryBuilder fluent interface. Dataset Discovery: call DatasetClient.metadata(dataset_id) to retrieve DatasetMetadata including column schemas and row count. Portal Configuration: SocrataClient(portal_domain, {app_token, debug_callback}) configures the shared fetch wrapper.
target_output.system_components: SocrataClient (entry point with portal_domain, optional app_token, optional debug_callback). DatasetClient (query<T>, paginate<T>, metadata methods). ExportClient (stream<T> method). SoqlQueryBuilder (fluent interface: select(), where(SoqlPredicate), orderBy(), limit(), offset(), build()). SoqlPredicate type (field, operator ENUM eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte/like/is_null, value — prevents raw string $where injection). AbortableGenerator<T> internal utility wrapping AsyncGenerator with AbortController return() implementation. FetchWrapper (internal: injects X-App-Token header, calls debug_callback with sanitized headers, passes AbortSignal). SocrataAPIError, SocrataRateLimitError typed exceptions.
target_output.workflows: Paginated Crime Record Fetch: civic app instantiates SocrataClient('data.cityofchicago.org'). Builds query: SoqlQueryBuilder.select(['id','primary_type','date','block']).where(SoqlPredicate.eq('year','2024')).orderBy('date DESC').build(). Calls paginate<ChicagoCrimeRow>(dataset_id, query, 1000) which creates AbortController, enters fetch loop requesting rows via $offset increments. for-await-of processes each 1000-row batch. If caller breaks early (e.g., found target record), generator return() calls controller.abort(), cancelling the next in-flight fetch and preventing promise accumulation. Large Census Export: data journalist calls stream<TractDemographicRow>(dataset_id, SoqlQueryBuilder.build()) on a state demographics dataset with 500,000 rows. AsyncGenerator yields individual rows from successive $offset pages; caller pipes rows to a CSV transform stream without buffering the full response in memory. Early termination aborts cleanly via AbortController.
target_output.data_model: DatasetMetadata (id: string, name: string, description: string, columns: ColumnSchema[], row_count: number, updated_at: string, portal_domain: string). ColumnSchema (field_name: string, data_type_name: string, description: string). SoqlQuery (select: string[], where: SoqlPredicate | null, order: string | null, limit: number | null, offset: number). SoqlPredicate (field: string, operator: 'eq'|'neq'|'gt'|'gte'|'lt'|'lte'|'like'|'is_null', value: string | number | null). QueryResult<T> = T[]. All types exported from index.ts for caller use in defining row shapes.
target_output.api_design: Named exports from 'socrata-sdk'. SocrataClient constructor: new SocrataClient(portal_domain: string, options?: {app_token?: string, debug_callback?: (req: DebugRequest) => void}). DatasetClient.query<T>(dataset_id: string, query: SoqlQuery): Promise<T[]>. DatasetClient.paginate<T>(dataset_id: string, query: SoqlQuery, page_size?: number): AsyncGenerator<T[]>. DatasetClient.metadata(dataset_id: string): Promise<DatasetMetadata>. ExportClient.stream<T>(dataset_id: string, query: SoqlQuery): AsyncGenerator<T>. SoqlQueryBuilder.select(fields: string[]): SoqlQueryBuilder. SoqlQueryBuilder.where(predicate: SoqlPredicate): SoqlQueryBuilder. SoqlQueryBuilder.orderBy(expr: string): SoqlQueryBuilder. SoqlQueryBuilder.limit(n: number): SoqlQueryBuilder. SoqlQueryBuilder.offset(n: number): SoqlQueryBuilder. SoqlQueryBuilder.build(): SoqlQuery. Exceptions: SocrataAPIError(status, message), SocrataRateLimitError.
target_output.security_and_compliance: App token injected as X-App-Token header in FetchWrapper; token value replaced with '***' in debug_callback payload to prevent logging exposure. HTTPS enforced: FetchWrapper throws SocrataAPIError if portal_domain resolves to an http:// URL. SoqlPredicate type eliminates raw string $where construction; SoqlQueryBuilder.where() accepts only SoqlPredicate objects, resolving ISS-001. No credential storage in SDK. Browser consumers should treat app tokens as rate-limit quotas (not sensitive credentials), but documentation warns against bundling tokens in public client-side JavaScript. Socrata API terms of service and open data portal acceptable use policies are caller responsibility.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: No hosted infrastructure. Development: bun run dev executes TypeScript directly. CI: GitHub Actions on push — biome check for lint/format, tsc --noEmit --strict for type safety, bun test running suite with mocked fetch including AbortController cleanup test (verifies abort() called on generator return()). Release: push semver tag triggers publish job — bun build outputs esm/ and cjs/ directories, package.json exports map covers both, npm publish --provenance for supply-chain attestation; no stored npm token via npm provenance OIDC. Package.json: exports map with .mjs for ESM and .cjs for CJS; types: field pointing to .d.ts declarations.
target_output.observability: Optional debug_callback in SocrataClient constructor receives sanitized request objects (URL, method, response status, latency in ms — app token value replaced with '***'). Callers wire the callback to their own logging pipeline. No telemetry emitted from SDK. Console.warn emitted when approaching Socrata 429 rate limit response to alert callers in development.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — npm distribution is free for open-source packages. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for a public repository. SDK throughput scales with the caller's Socrata portal rate limit: 1,000 requests/hour anonymous, 10,000/hour with app token. Streaming export reduces caller memory cost for large datasets by processing rows incrementally rather than buffering full responses.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (round 1, complete): SoqlQueryBuilder with typed SoqlPredicate $where, DatasetClient.query and paginate, DatasetMetadata discovery, dual ESM/CJS npm release. Phase 2 (round 2, this revision): ExportClient.stream with AbortController cleanup (ISS-002 fix), bun test for generator cleanup, updated documentation with early termination examples. Phase 3 (future): Browser-optimized bundle with bundlesize check, GeoJSON geometry support for Socrata location columns, optional in-memory dataset response cache with TTL.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public functions require JSDoc with @example showing real Socrata dataset usage. SoqlPredicate must be the only accepted $where input — no string overload permitted. AsyncGenerator functions must instantiate AbortController before the first yield and implement return() calling abort(). bun test coverage must include happy-path, pagination boundary, and early-termination abort scenarios for each generator function. Semantic versioning: patch for bug fixes, minor for new query operators or methods, major for breaking API changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Socrata portal response schema varies per dataset and per portal operator — TypeScript generics provide compile-time safety only when callers define accurate row type interfaces; runtime field mismatches produce undefined values rather than errors. Mitigation: documentation provides a recipe for generating row types from DatasetMetadata.columns using a codegen helper. Risk: Socrata rate limits (10,000 req/hr with app token) constrain bulk multi-dataset civic data workflows. Mitigation: documentation provides a backoff recipe using stream() with a rate-limit-aware wrapper. Tradeoff: AbortController cleanup adds per-generator instantiation overhead but is negligible compared to network latency on Socrata API calls.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected native fetch over axios for zero-dependency constraint and browser compatibility. Chose SoqlPredicate typed object over raw string $where to resolve ISS-001 injection risk. Confirmed AbortController for streaming cleanup over WeakRef-based approach — AbortController is more explicit and widely understood. npm provenance attestation chosen over signed releases for simplicity.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing AsyncGenerator memory leak on aborted iteration (ISS-002) with AbortController cleanup. Strong quality with Socrata-specific terminology, typed SoQL query builder details, and civic tech domain context throughout.

profile.domain: Public Health / Government Digital Services

project_goal.value: Replace a decade-old CMS with a statically generated website for the county public health department, serving health program information, vaccination clinic schedules, WIC office locations, and public health data dashboards to the general public
project_goal.rationale: Legacy CMS is unmaintained, has security vulnerabilities, and is slow to update; static site reduces operational burden and improves page load performance

target_users.value: General public, county residents seeking health services, healthcare providers looking for program referral information
target_users.rationale: Public health department website serves all county residents without authentication

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime or database; all content statically generated and served from CDN

capabilities.rationale: Static site with client-side chart rendering from pre-aggregated CSV data and Google Analytics for traffic measurement

complexity_level.rationale: Static site generation with templated content and client-side data visualization; no backend services required

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected; no authentication; no server-side processing; primary risks are content accuracy and site availability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Only public health information published; no personal data collected or stored

external_exposure.rationale: Public health department website must be accessible to all county residents without authentication

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication required; staff content editing via Git repository and headless CMS with separate login
access_model.rationale: Public health information must be freely accessible; staff editing is a separate workflow via Git not exposed on the public site

feature_scope.value: Health program landing pages, vaccination clinic schedules with searchable location table, WIC office directory with addresses and hours, public health data dashboards (disease surveillance, vaccination rates), press releases and health alerts, staff directory, downloadable forms
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full content scope of the legacy CMS being replaced

mvp_scope.value: All core program pages, clinic schedule table, WIC office directory, and press release archive migrated from legacy CMS; new Section 508-compliant templates deployed to CDN
mvp_scope.rationale: Full content migration required before legacy CMS can be decommissioned

security_baseline.value: HTTPS-only via CDN; CSP headers disabling inline scripts; SRI for any third-party scripts; no cookies except analytics opt-in; no form submissions storing data on state infrastructure
security_baseline.rationale: Static site has minimal attack surface; CSP and SRI address the main client-side risks

frontend_stack.value: Eleventy 2.x with Nunjucks templating
frontend_stack.rationale: Eleventy is flexible for health department content hierarchy; Nunjucks provides macro reuse for program page templates; builds fast even for large content libraries

backend_stack.value: None - static site with no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: static_website project class; no backend specialist required

data_platform.value: None - content managed as structured data files in Git; public health dashboard CSV data pre-aggregated and stored in Cloud Storage as static assets
data_platform.rationale: No database required; dashboard data served as pre-aggregated static files to avoid backend API dependency

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Storage bucket with Cloud CDN fronting for global edge delivery
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud CDN provides edge caching and HTTPS termination; Cloud Storage is cost-effective for static asset hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; Google Analytics 4 in anonymized mode with IP anonymization; analytics data governed by GA4 terms; no server-side analytics data retention
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: data_sensitivity=none; no personal data retention obligations

future_scope.value: Spanish and Vietnamese language versions, appointment booking widget linking to third-party scheduling system, interactive vaccination clinic map, email alert subscription for health advisories
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration enhancements to improve community access and engagement
