constraints.value: Must meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility; clinic schedule updates must be live within 5 minutes of staff commit; no per-page rebuild of entire site; total build time under 3 minutes for 500+ page content library
constraints.rationale: Accessibility required by Section 508; 5-minute freshness requirement for clinic schedule changes during public health emergencies

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for traffic measurement; Cloud CDN cache hit rate monitoring in GCP Console; GitHub Actions build status notifications to team Slack channel
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static site with no backend

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions CI/CD with trunk-based development on main branch; preview deployments for PR review via Cloud Storage staging bucket
execution_preference.rationale: Standard Git workflow for content and code changes with automated deployment on merge

project.goal: Statically generated county public health website replacing legacy CMS
project.target_users: General public and county residents
project.timeline: Content migration and launch within 3 months

frontend.styling: Plain CSS with design tokens; no framework
frontend.charts: Chart.js for client-side dashboard rendering from CSV

constraints.freshness: Clinic schedule live within 5 minutes of commit

reasoner_reviews.product: Content scope is well defined and matches the legacy CMS content inventory. The vaccination clinic schedule freshness requirement of 5 minutes during emergencies is the most demanding operational constraint and drives the need for incremental build optimization. Pre-aggregated CSV files for public health dashboards is the correct approach to avoid backend API dependency on a static site.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Eleventy with Nunjucks is a strong choice for this content hierarchy. The 5-minute freshness requirement with 500+ pages requires Eleventy incremental builds rather than full rebuilds on every commit; round 1 plan did not address incremental build configuration. Dashboard CSV files must be pre-aggregated offline and committed alongside content, not fetched at build time from a live data source, to keep builds deterministic.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static site attack surface is low. CSP header configuration needs tightening - the round 1 plan allowed unsafe-inline which defeats the purpose of CSP. Subresource Integrity hashes are required for the Google Analytics script tag. Cloud Storage bucket must have public ACLs only on the output bucket, not the source or staging buckets.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Build time under 3 minutes for 500+ pages is achievable with Eleventy incremental builds but requires careful template partitioning to minimize cascade rebuilds. Cloud CDN cache invalidation should be scoped to changed pages only, not a full invalidation, to maintain edge cache efficiency during frequent clinic schedule updates.

frontend.app_structure: Eleventy 2.x project structure: _data/ (clinic_schedules.json, wic_offices.json, health_programs.json, staff_directory.json as structured data files), _includes/ (base layout, program page macro, clinic table partial, WIC office card partial), content/ (health-programs/, clinics/, wic/, press-releases/, dashboards/, forms/), public/ (pre-aggregated CSV files for Chart.js dashboard rendering).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Health Programs section (one page per program: immunization, maternal health, environmental health, communicable disease, WIC). Clinics section with searchable vaccination clinic schedule table filtered by date and location. WIC Office directory with address, hours, and contact per office. Public Health Dashboards with Chart.js rendering disease surveillance and vaccination rate CSVs. Press Releases archive with date-sorted index. Staff Directory. Downloadable Forms index.
frontend.performance_notes: Eleventy incremental builds enabled via --incremental flag; only changed template dependencies trigger rebuild of downstream pages. Image optimization via eleventy-img plugin generating WebP and AVIF formats at build time. Chart.js loaded only on dashboard pages via page-level script injection. Total build target: under 90 seconds for full build, under 15 seconds for single page change.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA target: skip navigation link on all pages, ARIA landmark regions (main, nav, aside, footer), alt text required for all images enforced in Nunjucks template macros, color contrast ratio above 4.5:1 verified via automated axe-core CI check on PR preview deployments, keyboard navigation tested on clinic schedule table sort controls.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public site. Staff content editing via GitHub repository with standard GitHub access controls; no CMS admin panel exposed on the public site.
security.abuse_prevention: Cloud Armor WAF on Cloud CDN for DDoS mitigation; rate limiting at CDN edge for repeat requesters; no forms or user-submitted input on the static site eliminates injection attack surface.
security.privacy_controls: Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization enabled and analytics_storage denied by default; cookie consent banner for analytics opt-in; no other cookies set by the site; HIPAA does not apply as no health information is collected from visitors.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repository -> GitHub Actions CI pipeline -> Eleventy build in Node container -> gsutil rsync to Cloud Storage production bucket -> Cloud CDN cache invalidation for changed paths. Staging deployment to separate Cloud Storage bucket triggered by PR; staging URL posted to PR as comment. Custom domain health-dept.county.gov with Cloud CDN SSL certificate via Google-managed certs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: install dependencies via npm ci, run axe-core accessibility check on rendered HTML sample, Eleventy incremental build, gsutil rsync with --delete flag for removed pages, Cloud CDN invalidation scoped to changed path patterns. Total pipeline target: under 5 minutes from commit to CDN edge update.
devops.observability_stack: Cloud CDN metrics dashboard in GCP Console: cache hit ratio, origin request rate, bandwidth. Google Analytics 4: pageviews, top program pages, search terms used in clinic schedule filter. GitHub Actions build status webhook to team Slack channel for build failures.

ISS-001.title: CSP header allows unsafe-inline defeating XSS protection
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 Content-Security-Policy header included unsafe-inline for script-src, negating XSS protection for any future inline script injection.

ISS-002.title: Full site rebuild on every commit violates 5-minute freshness SLA
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan triggered a full 500-page Eleventy rebuild on every commit; build time was estimated at 8-10 minutes, exceeding the 5-minute clinic schedule freshness requirement.

focus_issues.title: CSP header allows unsafe-inline defeating XSS protection
focus_issues.detail: CSP header updated to remove unsafe-inline; Chart.js loaded via nonce-based CSP to allow the one legitimate inline script use case.

focus_issues.title: Full site rebuild violates 5-minute freshness SLA
focus_issues.detail: Eleventy incremental builds enabled; build time reduced to under 90 seconds for full build and under 15 seconds for single-file changes.

latest_recommendations: Replace unsafe-inline in CSP with nonce-based script allowlisting
Enable Eleventy incremental builds to meet 5-minute clinic schedule freshness SLA
Add CDN cache invalidation scoped to changed paths rather than full invalidation
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: County Public Health Department Static Website v1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues from round 1 require architectural changes

previous_audits.summary: Solid static site architecture for the public health use case but two fixable issues prevent production deployment.
strengths: Eleventy with Nunjucks is the right SSG choice for the health department content hierarchy
Pre-aggregated CSV approach for dashboards correctly avoids backend API dependency
Cloud CDN hosting is appropriate for public traffic patterns
concerns: CSP header includes unsafe-inline negating XSS protection value
Full-site rebuild pipeline will not meet 5-minute clinic schedule freshness requirement
blocking_issues: Full rebuild build time (8-10 min) violates the 5-minute freshness SLA for clinic schedule updates
recommendations: Enable Eleventy incremental builds with --incremental flag
Replace unsafe-inline in CSP script-src with nonce-based allowlisting for Chart.js
Scope CDN cache invalidations to changed path patterns rather than full site invalidation

issue_updates.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline
issue_updates.detail: Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'unsafe-inline' found in round 1 security section

issue_updates.title: Full rebuild violates 5-minute SLA
issue_updates.detail: Pipeline runs full Eleventy build on every commit; 8-10 minute estimated build time exceeds freshness requirement

previous_plan.title: County Public Health Department Static Website v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Eleventy-based static site replacing the county public health department legacy CMS, served from GCP Cloud Storage via Cloud CDN with GitHub Actions CI/CD.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Eleventy full-site build triggered on every commit to main; output deployed to Cloud Storage production bucket via gsutil sync; Cloud CDN serving public traffic with full cache invalidation on each deployment.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS-only via Cloud CDN; CSP header with script-src including unsafe-inline for Chart.js compatibility; SRI on Google Analytics script tag.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: npm ci, full Eleventy build (estimated 8-10 min for 500+ pages), gsutil rsync, full CDN cache invalidation. No incremental build configuration.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the county public health static site resolves two issues from round 1. ISS-002 (full rebuild SLA violation) addressed by enabling Eleventy incremental builds with template dependency tracking, reducing single-file change builds to under 15 seconds and full builds to under 90 seconds, well within the 5-minute freshness SLA. ISS-001 (unsafe-inline CSP) addressed by replacing unsafe-inline with nonce-based CSP for the Chart.js dashboard pages, removing the XSS surface while preserving client-side chart rendering functionality. CDN cache invalidation scoped to changed path patterns rather than full invalidation to maintain edge cache efficiency.

fix_report.action_taken: Enabled Eleventy incremental builds via --incremental flag in eleventy.config.js; configured template dependency graph to minimize cascade rebuilds; single clinic_schedules.json data file change now triggers rebuild of only the 12 clinic-related pages rather than all 500+; GitHub Actions pipeline target reduced to under 5 minutes from commit to CDN edge update
fix_report.expected_outcome: Clinic schedule updates live within 2-3 minutes of staff commit; full site build under 90 seconds; 5-minute freshness SLA met with margin

fix_report.action_taken: Removed unsafe-inline from Content-Security-Policy script-src directive; Chart.js dashboard pages now use per-request nonce injected by Eleventy build and passed to script tags via data attribute; Google Analytics loaded via nonce as well; CSP now enforces strict script allowlist with no inline execution bypass
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP header now provides meaningful XSS protection; no inline script execution permitted without valid build-time nonce; no regressions in Chart.js dashboard functionality confirmed in staging

target_output.title: County Public Health Department Static Website
target_output.executive_summary: An Eleventy 2.x statically generated website replacing the county public health department's decade-old CMS, serving health program pages, vaccination clinic schedules, WIC office directories, and public health data dashboards from GCP Cloud Storage via Cloud CDN. Incremental builds ensure clinic schedule updates go live within 2-3 minutes of a staff commit. Chart.js renders pre-aggregated public health dataset CSVs client-side with no backend API dependency. Section 508 accessibility enforced via automated axe-core checks in CI.
target_output.architecture_overview: Eleventy 2.x static site generator processes structured content data files (clinic_schedules.json, wic_offices.json, health_programs.json) through Nunjucks templates into a fully static HTML/CSS/JS output directory. Incremental build mode tracks template dependency graph; a change to clinic_schedules.json triggers rebuild of the 12 clinic-related pages only, not the full 500-page library. GitHub Actions CI builds the site, runs axe-core accessibility checks on rendered HTML, and syncs changed files to a GCP Cloud Storage production bucket via gsutil rsync. Cloud CDN serves the site at the edge with cache invalidations scoped to changed path patterns. Public health dashboard pages load Chart.js from CDN with SRI hash and fetch pre-aggregated CSV files from the same Cloud Storage origin; no server-side API calls required.
target_output.technology_stack: SSG: Eleventy 2.x with Nunjucks templating, eleventy-img plugin for WebP/AVIF image generation. Charts: Chart.js 4.x loaded with SRI hash, rendering pre-aggregated CSV data fetched client-side. Styling: plain CSS with custom properties for health department brand tokens. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization and consent-required analytics_storage. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Hosting: GCP Cloud Storage production and staging buckets behind Cloud CDN with Google-managed SSL cert. Accessibility testing: axe-core CLI in GitHub Actions pipeline.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Health Programs section: one Nunjucks template macro generates each of the 8 program landing pages (immunization, maternal health, WIC, environmental health, communicable disease, oral health, chronic disease, behavioral health) from structured program data files. Clinic Schedules: searchable and filterable HTML table generated from clinic_schedules.json with JavaScript filter controls; updates trigger incremental rebuild of 12 clinic pages only. WIC Office Directory: county map with office card partials generated from wic_offices.json containing address, hours, phone, and services offered. Public Health Dashboards: disease surveillance and vaccination coverage pages with Chart.js bar and line charts rendering pre-aggregated CSV data from Cloud Storage. Press Releases archive: date-sorted index page with individual release pages. Downloadable Forms index: PDF links with file size and date annotations.
target_output.system_components: Eleventy 2.x build engine with incremental build mode enabled; eleventy-img image optimization pipeline. GitHub Actions runner (Node 20 container) executing build, axe-core check, and gsutil rsync steps. GCP Cloud Storage: production bucket (public read ACL on output directory only), staging bucket (private, PR preview access via signed URLs), CSV data bucket for pre-aggregated public health datasets. Cloud CDN with cache invalidation API called post-deploy scoped to changed paths. Google Analytics 4 tag loaded via nonce-based CSP with user consent check.
target_output.workflows: Staff Clinic Schedule Update: public health staff edits clinics/_data/clinic_schedules.json in GitHub (direct commit to main or via PR); GitHub Actions pipeline triggers on push to main; Eleventy incremental build detects clinic_schedules.json as changed dependency, rebuilds only the 12 affected clinic pages in approximately 15 seconds; gsutil rsync uploads only modified HTML files to Cloud Storage; Cloud CDN invalidation targets /clinics/* paths; clinic schedule changes are live at the CDN edge within 2-3 minutes of commit. Public Health Dashboard Update: epidemiology team replaces a pre-aggregated CSV file (e.g., vaccination_coverage_2026.csv) in the data bucket; no site rebuild is triggered since CSV files are fetched client-side at page load; updated data is visible to site visitors within CDN cache TTL (30 minutes for CSV files).
target_output.data_model: No database. Structured content data files in Git repository: clinic_schedules.json (array of clinic objects with name, address, dates, hours, vaccine_types, appointment_url), wic_offices.json (array with office_name, address, city, zip, phone, hours_json, services_list), health_programs.json (program_id, title, description, services, contacts, related_links). Pre-aggregated public health CSV files in Cloud Storage: disease_surveillance_{year}.csv (week, disease_name, case_count, rate_per_100k), vaccination_coverage_{year}.csv (vaccine, coverage_pct, target_pct, county, state_comparison). Staff directory and press releases as individual Markdown files with YAML front matter.
target_output.api_design: No backend API. Client-side JavaScript on dashboard pages fetches pre-aggregated CSV files from Cloud Storage using relative paths (/data/vaccination_coverage_2026.csv); parsed with PapaParse and rendered via Chart.js. Clinic schedule filter controls operate on the DOM of the statically rendered HTML table with no API calls. Google Analytics 4 measurement protocol for pageview tracking. No form submissions; contact forms link to county email addresses.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloud CDN; HTTP requests redirected to HTTPS. Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{BUILD_NONCE}' https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'none'; nonce generated per Eleventy build and embedded in script tags at build time. Subresource Integrity hashes on Chart.js and Google Analytics script tags. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Cloud Storage bucket ACLs: public read on /output/ prefix only; source and staging buckets are private. Google Analytics 4 configured with anonymize_ip: true and analytics_storage: denied by default; consent banner on first visit allows opt-in. WCAG 2.1 AA compliance enforced via automated axe-core scan in GitHub Actions pipeline on every PR and main branch push.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: checkout, setup Node 20, npm ci, run axe-core accessibility check on a sample of rendered HTML pages (fail build on any critical violations), Eleventy incremental build with --incremental flag, gsutil rsync --delete to Cloud Storage production bucket, Cloud CDN cache invalidation API call for changed path patterns extracted from gsutil rsync output. PR preview workflow: Eleventy full build, gsutil sync to staging bucket, signed URL comment posted to PR. Build time targets: incremental build for single data file change under 30 seconds; full build under 90 seconds. Total pipeline time from commit to CDN edge under 5 minutes.
target_output.observability: GCP Cloud CDN metrics dashboard: cache hit ratio (target above 90%), origin request rate, bandwidth consumption, error rate. Google Analytics 4 reports: top program pages by pageview, clinic schedule page engagement, PDF download events, search query terms used in clinic filter. GitHub Actions build status webhook to team Slack channel: build failure and accessibility violation notifications. Cloud Storage access logs for audit of direct bucket access.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Storage static hosting: approximately $5-15/month for 500-page site with PDF assets. Cloud CDN bandwidth: approximately $20-50/month for typical county health department traffic volumes. GitHub Actions: within free tier for public repository or standard minutes for private. Total estimated $30-70/month. Scales to traffic surges during public health emergencies via Cloud CDN edge capacity without origin scaling; no server-side infrastructure to scale.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Content Migration (6 weeks): Eleventy project scaffolding, Nunjucks base layout and program page macro, migration of all legacy CMS pages to Markdown and JSON data files, incremental build configuration, GitHub Actions CI with axe-core checks, staging deployment for content review and accessibility audit. Phase 2 Launch (2 weeks): DNS cutover from legacy CMS to Cloud CDN, full CDN cache warm, legacy CMS decommission, staff training on Git-based content workflow. Phase 3 Enhancements (ongoing): Spanish and Vietnamese language versions via Eleventy i18n plugin, interactive clinic map with Leaflet.js, email alert subscription widget linking to third-party list management service.
target_output.development_guidelines: Eleventy configuration in eleventy.config.js with explicit incremental build passthrough configuration. All clinic, WIC, and program content stored as JSON data files in _data/; no content hardcoded in Nunjucks templates. Nunjucks macros for reusable components (program_card, clinic_row, office_card) with required alt text parameter enforcement. Automated axe-core accessibility check runs on every commit; critical violations block merge. New pages must pass WCAG 2.1 AA before PR merge. CSV data files in Cloud Storage follow naming convention {dataset}_{year}.csv with a manifest.json tracking available vintages.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Incremental build cache invalidation if Eleventy template dependency graph is misconfigured; a template change not tracked as a dependency would produce stale rendered output. Mitigation: full build run weekly in CI to detect incremental/full build output divergence. Risk: Pre-aggregated CSV files for dashboards become stale if epidemiology team does not update them on the expected schedule. Mitigation: CSV file freshness check in CI comparing last-modified timestamp against expected update frequency; Slack alert if data is overdue. Tradeoff: Git-based content workflow over headless CMS provides version history and PR review for content changes but requires staff familiarity with Git for clinic schedule updates.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Eleventy incremental builds selected over full rebuilds to meet 5-minute clinic schedule freshness SLA. Nonce-based CSP selected over hash-based for Chart.js compatibility since chart configuration varies per dashboard page. Pre-aggregated CSV files for dashboards confirmed over live API calls to maintain static site architecture and offline build determinism. Cloud CDN cache invalidation scoped to changed paths per gsutil rsync output to preserve cache efficiency for unchanged pages during frequent clinic updates.

metadata.notes: Round 2 county public health static site resolving CSP unsafe-inline and full-rebuild SLA issues; Eleventy incremental builds and nonce-based CSP now specified.

profile.domain: Modernized Budget Actuals Data Pipeline for City Budget Office

project_goal.value: Modernized data pipeline ingesting departmental budget actuals from the city ERP via daily API extraction, applying dbt transformations to produce department-level budget-vs-actual summaries in BigQuery, and feeding automated Looker dashboards used by city council for fiscal oversight
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragile spreadsheet-based reporting with automated, auditable pipeline to support city council fiscal oversight

target_users.value: City budget analysts, department finance directors, and city council members consuming Looker dashboards; data engineering team operating the pipeline
target_users.rationale: Dual user base: technical operators managing the pipeline and non-technical consumers viewing dashboards

project_class.rationale: Pure ELT pipeline with no interactive user-facing application; value delivered through BigQuery tables and Looker dashboards

capabilities.rationale: ERP integration, batch extraction, dbt transformation, BigQuery analytics layer, and pipeline DevOps are the active capability surfaces

complexity_level.rationale: ERP API integration and multi-layer dbt model hierarchy add meaningful complexity beyond a simple extract-and-load

risk_level.rationale: City financial data is sensitive; pipeline failures directly impact city council oversight dashboards creating political and governance risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Budget actuals and expenditure records are financial data subject to GAAP fund accounting requirements and public records disclosure laws

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline and BigQuery datasets accessed only within city GCP project; Looker is internal-only deployment

access_model.value: GCP service accounts with Workload Identity for pipeline execution; BigQuery dataset-level IAM granting analyst role to budget team; Looker embedded authentication for city council dashboard access
access_model.rationale: Principle of least privilege across GCP service accounts; Looker handles council member auth separately from pipeline service accounts

feature_scope.value: Daily ERP actuals extraction via REST API, incremental dbt models for department expenditure and revenue, budget-vs-actual variance calculations, fund accounting rollups per GASB standards, Looker dashboard feeds, pipeline health monitoring with data freshness checks
feature_scope.rationale: Complete replacement of spreadsheet reporting with automated pipeline

mvp_scope.value: Daily extraction of expenditure actuals, staging layer, two core dbt models (department_budget_summary, fund_rollup), basic Looker dashboard for budget analysts
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimal scope to replace highest-priority manual reporting workflow before end-of-fiscal-year

security_baseline.value: GCP service accounts with Workload Identity; BigQuery column-level security on salary line items; all pipeline credentials in GCP Secret Manager; ERP API key rotated quarterly
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires column-level access control for salary details; service account credentials must not be stored as files

frontend_stack.value: None; analytics served via Looker (existing agency license)
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend; Looker is the agency-standard BI platform

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with dbt Core 1.8 and Apache Airflow 2.9 on Cloud Composer
backend_stack.rationale: dbt for transformation layer versioning and lineage; Airflow for DAG orchestration with dependency management and retry logic

data_platform.value: BigQuery on GCP with dataset partitioning by fiscal_period; ERP source is PeopleSoft Financials REST API
data_platform.rationale: BigQuery is the city analytics platform standard; PeopleSoft is the existing ERP

hosting_target.value: Cloud Composer 2 (managed Airflow) on GCP; dbt jobs run as Composer DAG tasks
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Composer provides managed Airflow with GCP-native authentication and BigQuery operator integration

privacy_retention_policy.value: BigQuery raw tables retained indefinitely for audit purposes; mart tables refreshed daily with no deletion; pipeline logs in Cloud Logging retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Government financial records are subject to indefinite retention under state public records law; pipeline logs are operational and retained under standard IT policy

future_scope.value: Revenue actuals pipeline, capital project expenditure tracking, multi-year budget trend analysis, automated anomaly detection for budget overruns, public-facing fiscal transparency dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after Phase 1 expenditure pipeline is stable

constraints.value: PeopleSoft REST API has a 1000-record-per-page limit and a daily rate limit of 100K records; dbt models must follow city naming convention (stg_, int_, mart_); BigQuery slot reservation capped at 500 slots by IT policy
constraints.rationale: PeopleSoft API constraints require pagination handling; naming convention enables lineage tracking in the city data catalog; BigQuery slot cap controls cost

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics from Cloud Composer; pipeline data freshness alerts via dbt Cloud (or custom check); PagerDuty for critical DAG failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Grafana/Prometheus is city IT monitoring standard; PagerDuty ensures on-call engineer is alerted before city council morning briefings

execution_preference.value: Iterative sprints with weekly demos to budget office stakeholders; pipeline changes gated by dbt CI tests
execution_preference.rationale: Budget office needs frequent visibility into pipeline progress to plan dashboard development in parallel

compliance_context.value: GAAP fund accounting requirements, Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) standards 54 and 84 for fund categorization, state public records disclosure requirements for budget data, city financial management policy requiring audit trail for all expenditure data transformations
compliance_context.rationale: City financial data is subject to GAAP and GASB standards; dbt model lineage provides the transformation audit trail required by city financial management policy

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet-based budget reporting with automated dbt+Airflow pipeline feeding BigQuery and Looker
project.target_users: Budget analysts, finance directors, city council members
project.timeline: MVP (expenditure pipeline) by end of fiscal quarter

backend.integration: PeopleSoft Financials REST API with pagination and rate limiting

security.auth: GCP service accounts with Workload Identity
security.column_security: BigQuery column-level security on salary line items
security.secrets: GCP Secret Manager for ERP API key

data.db: BigQuery with dataset partitioning by fiscal_period
data.naming: stg_ staging, int_ intermediate, mart_ mart layer per city convention

devops.ci: Cloud Build with dbt CI test suite

open_questions.erp_auth: PeopleSoft REST API uses OAuth2 or API key for daily extractions

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope is appropriately bounded to the expenditure actuals workflow for MVP. Budget analysts have confirmed that department_budget_summary and fund_rollup are the two dbt models needed to replace the current Friday afternoon spreadsheet process. The Looker integration should be treated as a first-class output alongside BigQuery tables, not an afterthought.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The ISS-001 gap from round 1 was the absence of PeopleSoft API pagination handling in the extract DAG. The current plan must specify how the Airflow DAG handles paginated extraction including rate limit backoff. dbt model layering (stg -> int -> mart) following city naming conventions is correct. Consider BigQuery partitioning on fiscal_period_id rather than ingestion time for query cost optimization on year-over-year comparison queries.
reasoner_reviews.security: BigQuery column-level security for salary line items is a non-negotiable control given the financial data sensitivity. The GCP service account for Cloud Composer must not have BigQuery Data Editor on the entire dataset; it should have dataset-level write access only to the staging dataset and read access to source tables.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PeopleSoft 100K daily record limit requires the extract DAG to calculate the delta since last successful run and request only changed records using the last_modified_date filter parameter. For the first full load (historical backfill), an incremental bootstrap DAG with date-range chunking is needed to stay within the rate limit across multiple days.

data.entities: BigQuery raw layer: raw_expenditure_actuals (journal_id, dept_code, account_code, fund_code, amount, fiscal_period, transaction_date, erp_source_id, extracted_at). BigQuery staging: stg_expenditures (journal_id, department_id, account_code, fund_code, amount_usd, fiscal_year, fiscal_period, transaction_date). BigQuery intermediate: int_dept_appropriations (department_id, fiscal_year, appropriation_amount, amendment_count, last_updated). BigQuery mart: mart_dept_budget_summary (department_id, dept_name, fiscal_year, fiscal_period, appropriated_amount, expended_amount, encumbered_amount, variance_amount, variance_pct). BigQuery mart: mart_fund_rollup (fund_code, fund_name, fund_type ENUM general/special/capital/enterprise, fiscal_year, total_revenue, total_expenditure, fund_balance).
data.storage_design: BigQuery dataset raw_budget: raw tables partitioned by extracted_at DATE, clustered by dept_code. Dataset staging_budget: stg_ models partitioned by fiscal_period. Dataset analytics_budget: mart_ models partitioned by fiscal_year, clustered by department_id. All datasets use CMEK encryption with city KMS key.
data.analytics_events: dbt source freshness checks run after each extract DAG completion; data_freshness_hours metric exported to Prometheus. mart_dept_budget_summary used as Looker Explore source with LookML dimensions for dept_name, fund_type, and fiscal_period drill-down.
data.consistency_notes: dbt uses incremental models with unique_key on journal_id for expenditure tables; full refresh triggered on schema changes detected in CI. mart_fund_rollup is a full-refresh model run after all incremental models complete to ensure fund balance consistency.
data.migration_notes: Historical backfill from PeopleSoft using date-chunked bootstrap DAG extracting 90-day windows across the previous 3 fiscal years; bootstrapped data loaded to raw_expenditure_actuals before incremental DAG takes over.

backend.service_design: Cloud Composer 2 environment running two primary DAGs: erp_extract_dag (daily at 06:00 AM) and dbt_transform_dag (daily at 07:00 AM, triggered on extract success). Python operator tasks use PeopleSoft REST client with OAuth2 token refresh and exponential backoff on 429 rate limit responses.
backend.api_patterns: PeopleSoft REST API: GET /financials/v1/journals?last_modified_after={checkpoint}&page_size=1000&page={n} with OAuth2 Bearer token. Pagination loop in Python operator continues until empty page returned. Checkpoint (last successful extraction timestamp) stored in Airflow Variables.
backend.background_jobs: erp_extract_dag: authenticate to PeopleSoft, paginate through journal records since last checkpoint, write to BigQuery raw_expenditure_actuals via BigQuery Storage Write API for high-throughput load. dbt_transform_dag: run dbt source freshness, run stg_ models, run int_ models, run mart_ models in dependency order, run dbt test suite, export freshness metric to Prometheus.
backend.failure_handling: erp_extract_dag: 3 retries with 5-minute exponential backoff on API errors; 429 responses trigger 15-minute sleep before retry; partial extractions checkpoint to Airflow Variables so retry resumes from last successful page. dbt_transform_dag: fails fast on any model error; PagerDuty alert fires on any DAG failure to ensure council dashboards are not silently stale.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Composer 2 environment in city GCP project us-central1; private IP configuration with VPC peering to city on-premise network for PeopleSoft API access. BigQuery datasets in same GCP project. Looker connected to BigQuery via service account with BigQuery Data Viewer on analytics_budget dataset.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on main branch merge: dbt compile (syntax check), dbt test on dev BigQuery dataset with sample data, Python unit tests for PeopleSoft client pagination logic. DAG files deployed to Cloud Storage bucket that Composer syncs automatically. dbt project deployed via Composer environment update with new requirements.
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: erp_records_extracted (counter by dept_code), dbt_model_execution_time_seconds (histogram by model_name), pipeline_freshness_lag_hours (gauge). Grafana dashboards on Prometheus scrape of Cloud Monitoring. PagerDuty alert on any DAG failure, on freshness lag exceeding 4 hours before 08:00 AM morning briefing.
devops.backup_and_recovery: BigQuery raw tables are append-only with time travel enabled (7-day retention); failed dbt runs can be re-triggered with full_refresh flag against time-travel snapshot. Airflow Variables backup to Cloud Storage daily for checkpoint recovery.

security.auth_design: Cloud Composer service account: BigQuery Data Editor on raw_budget and staging_budget datasets only; BigQuery Data Viewer on PeopleSoft source tables. mart_budget service account: BigQuery Data Editor on analytics_budget dataset. Looker service account: BigQuery Data Viewer on analytics_budget only.
security.secrets_management: PeopleSoft OAuth2 client credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager; loaded via Composer environment secret binding, not in Airflow Variables or plaintext DAG files.
security.privacy_controls: BigQuery column-level security policy tags applied to salary-related columns in stg_expenditures (account_code IN personal_services_codes); policy tag grants column access to compensation_authorized group only; budget analysts and council members excluded from this tag.

previous_plan.title: City Budget Actuals Data Pipeline - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: dbt and Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer 2 extracting PeopleSoft Financials actuals daily into BigQuery, running stg/int/mart dbt models, and feeding Looker dashboards for city council fiscal oversight.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Cloud Composer 2 orchestrates erp_extract_dag and dbt_transform_dag; PeopleSoft REST API extraction to BigQuery raw tables; dbt stg/int/mart model hierarchy; Looker on analytics_budget dataset.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: GCP service accounts with Workload Identity; CMEK encryption on BigQuery datasets; PeopleSoft credentials in Secret Manager. Column-level security for salary data not specified.
previous_plan.data_model: raw_expenditure_actuals, stg_expenditures, int_dept_appropriations, mart_dept_budget_summary, mart_fund_rollup tables defined with partition and cluster keys.

best_plan.title: City Budget Actuals Data Pipeline - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: dbt and Airflow pipeline extracting PeopleSoft Financials actuals into BigQuery daily with stg/int/mart model hierarchy feeding Looker council dashboards.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Cloud Composer 2 with erp_extract_dag and dbt_transform_dag; BigQuery raw and analytics datasets; Looker BI layer.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: GCP service accounts, Workload Identity, CMEK on BigQuery. Column-level security for salary data absent.
best_plan.data_model: Raw, staging, intermediate, and mart BigQuery tables with fiscal_period partitioning and dept_code clustering.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (PeopleSoft pagination) and focuses on ISS-002 (BigQuery column-level security for salary data). The fix requires defining BigQuery policy tags for salary account codes in stg_expenditures, granting the compensation_authorized IAM group access to those tags, and explicitly excluding the Looker service account from that group. The pagination fix adds the checkpoint mechanism and paginated extraction loop to the erp_extract_dag description.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified PeopleSoft REST API pagination loop in erp_extract_dag: Python operator fetches pages of 1000 records using last_modified_after checkpoint stored in Airflow Variables; continues until empty page; 429 rate limit responses trigger 15-minute sleep before retry; checkpoint updated only on full successful extraction completion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Daily extractions correctly retrieve all changed records without hitting the 100K daily rate limit; partial failures resume from last successful checkpoint page.

fix_report.action_taken: Added BigQuery column-level security policy tags to salary-related columns (gross_salary, benefits_amount, overtime_amount) in stg_expenditures; policy tag compensation_sensitive created in BigQuery Policy Tags taxonomy; access granted to compensation_authorized IAM group (HR and compensation analysts only); Looker service account explicitly excluded from compensation_authorized group; mart_dept_budget_summary aggregates salary columns as total_personal_services without exposing individual salary columns.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Budget analysts and city council members cannot query individual salary-level columns directly; only compensation_authorized group members can access granular salary data; Looker dashboards display only aggregated personal services totals.

target_output.title: City Budget Actuals Data Pipeline - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Cloud Composer 2-orchestrated ELT pipeline on GCP that extracts daily expenditure actuals from PeopleSoft Financials REST API with paginated checkpoint-based extraction, loads to BigQuery raw tables, applies dbt stg/int/mart transformations following city naming conventions, and feeds Looker dashboards for city council fiscal oversight. Financial data controls include BigQuery column-level policy tags restricting salary column access to the compensation_authorized group, CMEK encryption, and GCP Workload Identity for all service accounts.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Airflow DAGs orchestrated by Cloud Composer 2: erp_extract_dag (06:00 AM daily) paginating through PeopleSoft Financials REST API with OAuth2 authentication and checkpoint-based incremental extraction, loading to BigQuery raw_budget dataset via Storage Write API. dbt_transform_dag (07:00 AM, triggered on extract success) runs stg_ -> int_ -> mart_ model chain with source freshness checks and dbt test assertions. BigQuery analytics_budget dataset serves as the Looker data source with dataset-level IAM and column-level policy tags on salary fields. All GCP service accounts use Workload Identity with least-privilege dataset permissions; CMEK encryption on all BigQuery datasets.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on Cloud Composer 2 (Python 3.12 operators). Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 with BigQuery adapter. Warehouse: BigQuery with dataset partitioning, clustering, and CMEK encryption via city KMS key. ERP source: PeopleSoft Financials REST API with OAuth2. BI layer: Looker (existing city license). Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with Workload Identity binding. CI/CD: Cloud Build with dbt compile and dbt test gates. Monitoring: Cloud Monitoring custom metrics, Grafana, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Daily ERP Extraction: paginated PeopleSoft REST API fetch of all journal records modified since last checkpoint; OAuth2 token refresh; 429 rate limit backoff. dbt Transformation Layer: stg_expenditures (cleaned, typed staging), int_dept_appropriations (appropriation and amendment rollup), mart_dept_budget_summary (department-level budget vs. actual with variance), mart_fund_rollup (fund-level balance per GASB 54/84 fund type classification). Column-Level Security: BigQuery policy tags on salary columns in stg_expenditures granting access to compensation_authorized group only. Data Quality: dbt source freshness checks, not_null and unique tests on journal_id and department_id. Pipeline Health Monitoring: freshness lag metric and DAG failure alerting via PagerDuty.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment (us-central1, private IP, VPC peering to city on-premise for PeopleSoft access). BigQuery raw_budget dataset (raw_expenditure_actuals, partitioned by extracted_at). BigQuery staging_budget dataset (stg_expenditures with policy-tagged salary columns). BigQuery analytics_budget dataset (int and mart models; Looker service account has Data Viewer here only). PeopleSoft Financials REST API (city ERP, OAuth2, 1000-record pages, 100K daily rate limit). GCP Secret Manager (PeopleSoft OAuth2 client credentials, KMS key references). BigQuery Policy Tags taxonomy (compensation_sensitive tag; access restricted to compensation_authorized group). Grafana and Prometheus (pipeline health dashboards via Cloud Monitoring scrape). PagerDuty (DAG failure and data freshness alerting).
target_output.workflows: Daily Extraction: erp_extract_dag fires at 06:00 AM; Python operator loads last_checkpoint from Airflow Variables; paginates GET /financials/v1/journals?last_modified_after={checkpoint}&page_size=1000&page={n} with OAuth2 Bearer token; accumulates records across pages; on empty page response, writes batch to BigQuery raw_expenditure_actuals via Storage Write API; updates checkpoint in Airflow Variables to current run start time; 3 retries with exponential backoff on 5xx; 429 triggers 15-minute sleep. Daily Transformation: dbt_transform_dag fires at 07:00 AM triggered by extract DAG success sensor; runs dbt source freshness check against raw_expenditure_actuals; executes stg_expenditures incremental model (unique_key=journal_id); executes int_dept_appropriations; executes mart_dept_budget_summary and mart_fund_rollup; runs dbt test suite; exports freshness_lag_hours metric to Cloud Monitoring; Looker PDT cache invalidated automatically on mart model completion.
target_output.data_model: BigQuery raw_budget.raw_expenditure_actuals: journal_id STRING, dept_code STRING, account_code STRING, fund_code STRING, amount NUMERIC, fiscal_period STRING (YYYY-Pnn), transaction_date DATE, erp_source_id STRING, extracted_at DATE PARTITION KEY. BigQuery staging_budget.stg_expenditures: journal_id STRING, department_id STRING, account_code STRING, fund_code STRING, amount_usd NUMERIC, fiscal_year INT, fiscal_period STRING, transaction_date DATE, gross_salary NUMERIC [POLICY TAG: compensation_sensitive], benefits_amount NUMERIC [POLICY TAG: compensation_sensitive], overtime_amount NUMERIC [POLICY TAG: compensation_sensitive]. BigQuery analytics_budget.mart_dept_budget_summary: department_id STRING, dept_name STRING, fiscal_year INT PARTITION KEY, fiscal_period STRING, appropriated_amount NUMERIC, expended_amount NUMERIC, encumbered_amount NUMERIC, total_personal_services NUMERIC (aggregated salary, no individual salary columns), variance_amount NUMERIC, variance_pct NUMERIC. BigQuery analytics_budget.mart_fund_rollup: fund_code STRING, fund_name STRING, fund_type STRING (general/special_revenue/capital_projects/enterprise per GASB 54), fiscal_year INT, total_revenue NUMERIC, total_expenditure NUMERIC, fund_balance NUMERIC.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API exposed. PeopleSoft API consumed by Airflow operators: GET /financials/v1/journals (paginated extraction), POST /oauth/token (OAuth2 client credentials grant for token refresh). BigQuery Storage Write API used for high-throughput raw data load. Airflow REST API (internal) used by Cloud Composer for DAG trigger monitoring. All credentials loaded from GCP Secret Manager via Workload Identity at operator runtime.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Workload Identity: Cloud Composer service account grants BigQuery Data Editor on raw_budget and staging_budget only; Data Viewer on source reference tables. mart_budget service account: Data Editor on analytics_budget. Looker service account: Data Viewer on analytics_budget; explicitly NOT member of compensation_authorized group. BigQuery column-level security (ISS-002 resolution): policy tag taxonomy 'compensation_sensitive' created in BigQuery; applied to gross_salary, benefits_amount, overtime_amount columns in stg_expenditures; Fine-Grained Reader role granted to compensation_authorized IAM group (HR and compensation analysts only); all other identities including Looker service account and budget_analysts IAM group denied access to tagged columns. CMEK encryption on all three BigQuery datasets using city KMS key. PeopleSoft OAuth2 client credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager; not in Airflow Variables or DAG files. GAAP and GASB compliance: mart_fund_rollup follows GASB 54 fund type classification (general, special_revenue, capital_projects, enterprise); dbt model lineage in dbt docs constitutes the transformation audit trail required by city financial management policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 environment: private IP, VPC peering to city on-premise network where PeopleSoft is hosted; environment size small-2 with 2 schedulers. DAG files deployed via Cloud Storage bucket sync (Composer automatically picks up changes within 60 seconds). dbt project deployed as Composer PyPI package dependency; version pinned in requirements.txt. Cloud Build CI pipeline: dbt compile against dev BigQuery dataset, dbt test with seed data, Python unit tests for PeopleSoft pagination client, coverage gate 80%. BigQuery Policy Tags taxonomy managed via Terraform; IAM group membership (compensation_authorized) managed via Google Workspace Admin SDK in CI pipeline. Historical backfill: bootstrap_extract_dag triggered manually with date-chunked 90-day windows across 3 prior fiscal years, staying within PeopleSoft 100K daily rate limit.
target_output.observability: Cloud Monitoring custom metrics exported from dbt Python operator post-run: erp_records_extracted_total (counter by dept_code), dbt_model_rows_affected (gauge by model_name), pipeline_freshness_lag_hours (gauge; target below 2 hours). Grafana dashboards on Prometheus scrape of Cloud Monitoring metrics for data engineering team. PagerDuty integration: DAG failure alert fires within 5 minutes of any task failure; freshness_lag_hours exceeding 4.0 before 08:00 AM morning briefing fires warning-level alert to data engineering on-call.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Composer 2 small-2 environment approximately $300/month. BigQuery compute at 500-slot reservation for transformation jobs; slot-based pricing approximately $400/month at full utilization. BigQuery storage for 3 years of raw actuals at $0.02/GB/month; estimated under $50/month. Total pipeline cost approximately $750-800/month, justified against manual analyst time savings of 15-20 hours per week.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (complete): PeopleSoft OAuth2 extraction, raw_expenditure_actuals load, stg_expenditures, mart_dept_budget_summary, basic Looker dashboard. Phase 2 (current): pagination checkpoint fix (ISS-001 resolved), BigQuery column-level security policy tags (ISS-002 resolved), mart_fund_rollup per GASB 54, int_dept_appropriations for amendment tracking, data freshness monitoring. Phase 3: revenue actuals pipeline, capital project expenditure tracking, automated budget overrun anomaly detection, public fiscal transparency dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: dbt models must follow city naming convention: stg_ for staging, int_ for intermediate, mart_ for mart layer; no exceptions. All dbt models include not_null and unique tests on primary keys; freshness checks on source tables. PeopleSoft API client has unit tests for pagination loop, rate limit backoff, and checkpoint update logic with 80% line coverage. BigQuery Policy Tags taxonomy changes require a Terraform plan review by a data governance representative before apply. Airflow Variables used only for checkpoints; credentials must be in Secret Manager.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PeopleSoft daily 100K record rate limit hit during high-activity fiscal year-end periods when journal volumes spike. Mitigation: erp_extract_dag monitors record count mid-extraction and pauses with a next-day continuation flag if approaching 90K records, splitting the load across two nights. Risk: dbt model failures silently leaving Looker dashboards stale without city council being aware. Mitigation: PagerDuty alert on any dbt test failure fires before 08:00 AM council briefing; freshness metric displayed prominently in Looker dashboard header. Tradeoff: incremental dbt models require handling late-arriving PeopleSoft journal corrections (backdated amendments); mitigation is a weekly full-refresh run of stg_expenditures on Sunday nights to reconcile prior-period corrections.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed PeopleSoft REST API uses OAuth2 client credentials grant for service-to-service extraction. Selected BigQuery Storage Write API over streaming inserts for bulk raw load to avoid 10MB per-row streaming limit. Chose dbt incremental models over full refresh daily to limit BigQuery slot consumption within the 500-slot cap. Confirmed Looker is internal-only deployment; no public dashboard exposure in Phase 1.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 pagination and ISS-002 BigQuery column-level security; mart_fund_rollup added with GASB 54 fund type classification.

profile.domain: Federal Regulatory Agency Compliance-Hardened Public Website

project_goal.value: Compliance-hardened static public website for a federal regulatory agency publishing proposed and final rules, public comment dockets, and enforcement actions, meeting strict Section 508, Plain Writing Act, FedRAMP-authorized hosting, and Administrative Procedure Act publication requirements
project_goal.rationale: Replaces aging CMS with a maintainable static site that demonstrably meets federal accessibility and plain-language mandates

target_users.value: General public, regulated industries, attorneys, and journalists seeking agency regulatory actions, proposed rules, and enforcement records
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in agency website traffic analysis and Freedom of Information Act request patterns

project_class.rationale: All content is publicly available regulatory records; no server-side logic or authenticated sessions required

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with Dynatrace analytics and Regulations.gov third-party integration for public comments

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-content-type regulatory corpus, automated accessibility and plain-language CI checks, and Dynatrace RUM add complexity beyond a simple static site

risk_level.rationale: Federal agency public presence; defacement or availability failures carry reputational and legal notice risks under APA publication requirements

data_sensitivity.rationale: All content is public regulatory record; no PII collected or stored

external_exposure.rationale: Federal regulatory publications must be publicly accessible without authentication barriers

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication; all regulatory content freely accessible
access_model.rationale: APA and agency transparency mandates require unrestricted public access to regulatory actions

feature_scope.value: Proposed rule pages with active comment period countdowns and Regulations.gov links, final rule archive with Federal Register citations, public comment docket listing, enforcement action records with case status, agency guidance documents, Federal Register citation cross-references, press release archive
feature_scope.rationale: Complete regulatory communications scope per agency digital strategy

mvp_scope.value: Proposed rules, final rules, and enforcement actions migrated from legacy CMS; automated Section 508 CI check; Dynatrace RUM integration; CloudFront + S3 GovCloud hosting
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP establishes FedRAMP-compliant hosting baseline and Section 508 enforcement before full content migration

security_baseline.value: HTTPS-only with HSTS preloading, CSP with nonce-based exception for Dynatrace RUM script, SRI on all third-party tags, X-Frame-Options DENY, CloudFront WAF managed rule group for common exploits
security_baseline.rationale: Static site security hardening appropriate for a federal agency public presence

frontend_stack.value: Gatsby 5 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Agency content team familiar with Gatsby; excellent static generation for large structured regulatory document corpora

backend_stack.value: None
backend_stack.rationale: Static website; no server-side runtime required

data_platform.value: None; all regulatory content stored as structured Markdown and JSON files in Git repository
data_platform.rationale: Git-versioned content provides audit trail for regulatory publication history without a CMS database

hosting_target.value: AWS GovCloud CloudFront distribution with S3 origin in us-gov-west-1
hosting_target.rationale: FedRAMP Moderate authorized hosting required for federal agency; GovCloud ensures US data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; Dynatrace RUM configured with IP anonymization; session recording disabled; analytics data retained 13 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Agency privacy impact assessment requires no PII in analytics

future_scope.value: Spanish-language rule summaries, full-text search via Pagefind, RSS feeds for each regulatory category, automated Federal Register cross-reference linking
future_scope.rationale: Language access and search improvements on Year 2 digital strategy roadmap

constraints.value: FedRAMP-authorized hosting required; Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA must pass in CI before each deployment; Plain Writing Act Flesch-Kincaid grade level under 12 for all rule summaries; no third-party SaaS with data-processing terms outside FedRAMP boundary
constraints.rationale: Federal procurement, accessibility, and plain-language mandates

observability_baseline.value: Dynatrace Real User Monitoring for page performance and visitor behavior; CloudFront access logs to S3 for 90-day retention; GitHub Actions deployment audit trail; Dynatrace synthetic monitor pinging homepage every 5 minutes
observability_baseline.rationale: APA publication availability requirements necessitate uptime monitoring with alerting

execution_preference.value: Content-first migration: regulatory content types migrated one-by-one; 2-week sprint cycles; no feature additions until full legacy CMS parity
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces risk of incomplete regulatory record availability during transition

project.goal: FedRAMP-hosted static site for federal regulatory publications meeting Section 508 and Plain Writing Act requirements
project.target_users: General public, regulated industries, attorneys, journalists
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, full migration at 6 months

frontend.content: Markdown + JSON source files in Git

security.headers: HSTS preloading, CSP with nonce for Dynatrace RUM, SRI, X-Frame-Options DENY
security.waf: CloudFront WAF managed rule group for common exploits
security.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA via automated axe-core scans in CI

data.content: Structured Markdown and JSON files versioned in Git monorepo
data.cdn: CloudFront with 1-hour TTL for regulatory documents, 10-minute TTL for docket status pages

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: Gatsby build, axe accessibility scan, Flesch-Kincaid readability check, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation

constraints.plainwriting: Flesch-Kincaid grade level under 12 for rule summaries

reasoner_reviews.product: By round 3, the full regulatory corpus — proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, and dockets — should be migrated with Section 508 and Plain Writing Act checks integrated in CI. The primary remaining concerns are the Regulations.gov comment period integration reliability and the automated plain-language scoring coverage across all content types.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Gatsby 5's incremental builds have been tuned over two rounds to handle the full regulatory document corpus without build time regressions. The CloudFront TTL differentiation between stable rule documents and active comment period docket pages is well-calibrated. The nonce-based CSP exception for Dynatrace RUM is the correct approach given the FedRAMP constraint on third-party scripts.
reasoner_reviews.security: HSTS preloading requires confirmed deployment to HSTS preload list — this is an irreversible action for the agency domain and must be explicitly approved by the agency CIO. CSP nonce generation in Gatsby SSG context is handled via gatsby-plugin-csp with per-build nonce injection. SRI hash rotation must be automated on Dynatrace RUM script version updates.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: FedRAMP authorization covers AWS GovCloud S3 and CloudFront. Dynatrace GovCloud instance is FedRAMP authorized. Regulations.gov API is a federal government service not subject to FedRAMP review. Plain-language CI check using Flesch-Kincaid scoring requires defining the exact content fields subject to grading — rule titles are excluded, only summary paragraphs are graded.

frontend.app_structure: Gatsby 5 monorepo with gatsby-source-filesystem pulling Markdown content from /content/{proposed-rules,final-rules,enforcement-actions,dockets,guidance,press-releases}; USWDS 3.x components in /src/components/uswds; shared regulatory templates in /src/templates
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage with active proposed rule alert banner, Proposed Rules index with comment deadline countdowns, Proposed Rule detail page with Regulations.gov comment link and Federal Register citation, Final Rules archive with year/subject-matter filters, Enforcement Actions listing with case status badges, Public Docket detail page, Agency Guidance library, Press Release archive
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA enforced via axe-core CI scan on 30 sampled pages per build; USWDS components provide accessible patterns for regulation table navigation, accordion rule summaries, and breadcrumb navigation; all PDFs linked from pages must have an HTML summary page per Section 508
frontend.performance_notes: Gatsby incremental builds scoped to changed content nodes; CloudFront compression on HTML/CSS; WebP images via gatsby-plugin-image; proposed rule pages with active comment periods excluded from long-TTL cache

security.auth_design: No authentication; all content publicly accessible
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront WAF AWS managed rule group (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet) blocking common exploits; rate limiting at 5,000 requests per 5 minutes per IP for bot mitigation
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudFront access logs to S3 GovCloud bucket retained 90 days; GitHub Actions workflow audit log for all content deployments; Dynatrace synthetic monitoring log for availability
security.security_risks: HSTS preload submission is irreversible for agency domain; must be approved by agency CIO and tested on staging for minimum 6 months before submission

data.entities: proposed_rules (slug, docket_id, title, summary_plainlanguage, comment_deadline, status ENUM open/closed/withdrawn, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, published_at). final_rules (slug, docket_id, title, effective_date, cfr_citation, federal_register_citation, pdf_s3_key, related_proposed_rule_slug). enforcement_actions (slug, case_number, respondent_name, violation_type, case_status ENUM open/settled/dismissed, settlement_amount, order_date). public_dockets (docket_id, title, comment_count, comment_period_open BOOLEAN, last_updated). agency_guidance (slug, title, guidance_type, issued_date, pdf_s3_key). press_releases (slug, headline, published_at, pdf_s3_key)
data.storage_design: All entities stored as Markdown with YAML frontmatter in Git; PDFs stored in S3 GovCloud public bucket with versioning; Gatsby GraphQL layer queries content at build time
data.retention_and_deletion: Regulatory records retained indefinitely per NARA records schedule; superseded proposed rules archived in place with status=withdrawn rather than deleted

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds Gatsby static output, runs axe-core scan and Flesch-Kincaid check, syncs to S3 GovCloud, issues CloudFront invalidation for changed paths only
devops.environments: Production: CloudFront + S3 us-gov-west-1 on agency domain; Staging: S3 website endpoint for PR branch previews
devops.ci_cd_design: Push to main triggers production deploy; PRs trigger staging preview; axe scan must pass with zero critical violations; Flesch-Kincaid check on rule summary fields must score under grade 12; build time target under 10 minutes via Gatsby incremental builds
devops.observability_stack: Dynatrace RUM for real user performance monitoring; Dynatrace synthetic monitor pinging /proposed-rules every 5 minutes; CloudFront access logs to S3 for traffic analysis

ISS-001.title: CSP nonce not regenerated per build — Dynatrace RUM blocked in staging
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 CSP implementation used a static nonce value hard-coded in gatsby-config.ts; Dynatrace RUM script was blocked in staging because the nonce in the CSP header did not match the nonce in the script tag after the next deployment

history.note: Static nonce causes CSP mismatch
gatsby-plugin-csp configured to regenerate nonce per build via build-time env variable injection

ISS-002.title: Plain Writing Act check not applied to enforcement action summary fields
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 Flesch-Kincaid CI check was scoped only to proposed_rules.summary_plainlanguage and final_rules frontmatter; enforcement_actions.violation_description field was not included despite containing dense legal prose exceeding grade 12

history.note: Enforcement action summaries excluded from readability check
CI check extended to cover enforcement_actions.violation_description and agency_guidance.abstract fields

ISS-003.title: Regulations.gov comment link shown after comment period closes
ISS-003.detail: Proposed rule detail pages continue displaying the 'Submit a Comment on Regulations.gov' button after proposed_rules.comment_deadline passes; the Regulations.gov portal rejects submissions after the deadline but the agency site does not update the call-to-action, causing public confusion and potential complaints

history.note: Comment link not conditioned on comment_deadline in template

focus_issues.title: Regulations.gov comment link shown after comment period closes
focus_issues.detail: Proposed rule detail pages show 'Submit a Comment' button after comment_deadline passes; fix requires template conditional and a nightly build trigger to update closed-period pages

latest_recommendations: Add a comment_deadline conditional in the ProposedRuleDetail Gatsby template that replaces the Regulations.gov comment button with a 'Comment period closed' notice after the deadline date; configure a GitHub Actions scheduled workflow to trigger a nightly incremental build on days when comment periods close, ensuring the production site reflects closed status before the business day begins
