target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 of the federal regulatory agency static website. ISS-001 (static CSP nonce) and ISS-002 (incomplete Plain Writing Act check) were resolved in rounds 1 and 2 respectively. This round addresses ISS-003: the Regulations.gov comment submission link remaining visible on proposed rule pages after the comment_deadline passes. The fix requires two changes: a template conditional in ProposedRuleDetail and a GitHub Actions scheduled nightly build to update pages on comment-period-closing days without waiting for a content commit.

fix_report.action_taken: Added comment_deadline conditional in the ProposedRuleDetail Gatsby template: if today's date > frontmatter.comment_deadline, render a 'Comment period closed on {date}' notice with a link to the comment summary document instead of the Regulations.gov submission button. Added a GitHub Actions workflow scheduled at 6am ET daily using a cron trigger that runs gatsby build --incremental only on days when any proposed_rule has a comment_deadline matching today's date, ensuring closed-period pages are updated before the business day. Flesch-Kincaid check extended to also scan the comment_summary_description field added to proposed_rules frontmatter
fix_report.expected_outcome: Proposed rule pages accurately reflect comment period status within hours of deadline passing; public users see 'Comment period closed' rather than a link that Regulations.gov will reject; APA public participation accuracy requirement satisfied

target_output.title: Federal Regulatory Agency Public Website - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: A compliance-hardened static website for a federal regulatory agency on AWS GovCloud CloudFront plus S3, publishing proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, and public comment dockets under strict Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA, Plain Writing Act, and Administrative Procedure Act publication requirements. Built with Gatsby 5 and USWDS 3.x components, the site enforces accessibility and readability in CI and uses a nightly scheduled build to keep comment period status accurate for proposed rule pages. This revision resolves the final open issue: the Regulations.gov comment submission link incorrectly shown after comment deadlines pass.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static delivery architecture using Gatsby 5 generating HTML at build time for proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, docket listings, guidance documents, and press releases. Source content lives as structured Markdown with YAML frontmatter and JSON files in a Git monorepo. Gatsby's GraphQL data layer ingests all content nodes at build time and applies ProposedRuleDetail, FinalRuleDetail, EnforcementActionDetail, and DocketListing templates with USWDS 3.x components. The ProposedRuleDetail template conditionally renders a Regulations.gov comment submission link or a 'Comment period closed' notice based on the comment_deadline frontmatter field evaluated against build time. Gatsby static output is deployed to S3 GovCloud and distributed via CloudFront with path-based TTL policies. A GitHub Actions nightly scheduled workflow runs an incremental Gatsby build on days when comment deadlines are reached, updating closure notices without requiring a content commit. No server-side runtime exists — all content is statically rendered.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Gatsby 5 with TypeScript, USWDS 3.x component library, gatsby-plugin-csp for per-build CSP nonce injection for Dynatrace RUM. Analytics: Dynatrace Real User Monitoring (GovCloud instance) with IP anonymization, session recording disabled. Infrastructure: AWS GovCloud CloudFront with WAF (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet), S3 us-gov-west-1 with versioning and OAC, Route 53, ACM GovCloud for TLS. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with axe-core scan, Flesch-Kincaid readability scorer, S3 sync via aws s3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter and JSON in Git monorepo.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Proposed Rules: detail pages with comment_deadline countdown while open; 'Comment period closed on {date}' notice with comment summary link when deadline passed. Final Rules: archive with CFR citation, Federal Register link, PDF download, and related proposed rule cross-reference. Enforcement Actions: case listings with respondent name, violation type, case_status badge (open/settled/dismissed), and settlement amount. Public Dockets: per-docket page with comment count and open/closed status. Agency Guidance: document library with guidance_type filter and PDF download. Press Releases: chronological archive with date and headline. All pages: USWDS breadcrumb navigation, skip-to-content link, Section 508 compliant table structures for regulatory listings.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution (WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, rate limiting 5,000 req/5min per IP, OAC for S3 origin), S3 GovCloud public-read-via-OAC bucket with versioning, GitHub Actions CI runner (Gatsby build, axe-core sampler, Flesch-Kincaid CI script, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation), GitHub Actions scheduled nightly workflow (comment deadline checker — scans proposed_rules frontmatter for comment_deadline == today, triggers incremental Gatsby build if any match), gatsby-plugin-csp (per-build nonce injection for Dynatrace RUM), Dynatrace RUM tag loaded via USWDS-compatible nonce-tagged script injection, ProposedRuleDetail template (comment_deadline conditional: submission link vs. closure notice).
target_output.workflows: Proposed Rule Comment Period Close: GitHub Actions cron runs at 6am ET daily. Script scans all proposed_rules Markdown frontmatter for comment_deadline matching today's date (America/New_York). If any match, triggers gatsby build --incremental. ProposedRuleDetail template evaluates comment_deadline at build time: if build_date > comment_deadline, renders 'Comment period closed on {formatted_date}. View comment summary.' with link to comment_summary_document_url instead of the Regulations.gov submission button and external link disclaimer. Incremental build completes in under 3 minutes for a single changed content node. S3 sync and CloudFront invalidation scoped to affected proposed-rule paths complete within 5 minutes. Public users see accurate closure status before 7am ET on the day comment periods close. New Enforcement Action Published: agency staff creates enforcement_actions/2026-ea-042.md in the Git repository with frontmatter fields. PR reviewed by communications officer confirms Flesch-Kincaid grade level of violation_description is under 12. PR merged to main triggers GitHub Actions production build. axe-core scans the rendered enforcement action page — passes required before S3 sync. Updated enforcement actions listing and detail page live within 8 minutes of merge.
target_output.data_model: proposed_rules frontmatter (slug, docket_id, title, summary_plainlanguage, comment_deadline DATE, comment_summary_document_url: str | null, status ENUM open/closed/withdrawn, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, agency_contact, published_at). final_rules frontmatter (slug, docket_id, title, effective_date, cfr_citation, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, pdf_s3_key, related_proposed_rule_slug: str | null, published_at). enforcement_actions frontmatter (slug, case_number, respondent_name, violation_type, violation_description, case_status ENUM open/settled/dismissed, settlement_amount: number | null, order_date, published_at). public_dockets frontmatter (docket_id, title, comment_count: number, comment_period_open BOOLEAN, last_updated). agency_guidance frontmatter (slug, title, guidance_type, abstract, issued_date, pdf_s3_key). press_releases frontmatter (slug, headline, summary, published_at, pdf_s3_key). All content stored as Markdown + JSON in Git; PDFs in S3 GovCloud with versioning.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API endpoints. Static data access patterns: Gatsby GraphQL queries content nodes at build time from gatsby-source-filesystem; no runtime API calls from server. Client-side: Dynatrace RUM script loaded via nonce-tagged script tag. Regulations.gov integration: external link to https://www.regulations.gov/docket/{docket_id}/comment only rendered when comment_deadline >= build_date in ProposedRuleDetail template. Federal Register cross-references: static links to federalregister.gov using federal_register_citation frontmatter field. No fetch calls in browser JavaScript except Dynatrace RUM telemetry to Dynatrace GovCloud endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS-only enforced via CloudFront with HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload) — preload list submission pending agency CIO sign-off after 6-month staging validation. CSP per-build: gatsby-plugin-csp injects a unique nonce per build into both the Dynatrace RUM script tag and the Content-Security-Policy header; nonce value sourced from BUILD_NONCE environment variable set by GitHub Actions. SRI applied to all third-party script and stylesheet tags. X-Frame-Options: DENY. CloudFront WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet blocks known exploit patterns; rate limiting at 5,000 req/5min per IP. S3 OAC ensures direct S3 URL access is blocked — all traffic must traverse CloudFront. Dynatrace RUM configured with IP anonymization enabled and session recording disabled per agency privacy impact assessment. No cookies set by the site itself; Dynatrace may set a first-party analytics cookie with 13-month retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions production workflow on push to main: (1) gatsby build with BUILD_NONCE env variable set to uuid, GATSBY_BUILDING_FOR_PROD=true; (2) axe-core scan on 30 sampled pages — fail CI on any critical or serious violation; (3) Flesch-Kincaid script scoring proposed_rules.summary_plainlanguage, final_rules frontmatter summary, enforcement_actions.violation_description, agency_guidance.abstract — fail CI if any field scores above grade 12; (4) aws s3 sync --delete to GovCloud bucket; (5) aws cloudfront create-invalidation scoped to changed paths. PR workflow deploys to staging S3 prefix for reviewer preview. Nightly scheduled workflow at 6am ET: Python script enumerates proposed_rules frontmatter for comment_deadline == today (America/New_York); if any match, runs gatsby build --incremental followed by targeted S3 sync and invalidation. Full deploy time: under 10 minutes. Nightly incremental: under 4 minutes. Secrets: BUILD_NONCE injected per workflow run; no persistent secrets stored in GitHub Actions beyond AWS OIDC role ARN.
target_output.observability: Dynatrace RUM (GovCloud instance) tracks page load performance (Largest Contentful Paint, First Input Delay, Cumulative Layout Shift) per page type — proposed rule pages, enforcement action listings, and docket detail pages segmented separately. Dynatrace synthetic monitor pings /proposed-rules and /enforcement-actions every 5 minutes from US-East and US-West synthetic nodes; alert on HTTP non-200 or LCP > 3 seconds triggers email to agency digital team. CloudFront access logs to S3 GovCloud bucket with 90-day retention; AWS Athena queries analyze traffic by content type and identify high-demand regulatory documents. GitHub Actions deployment log provides audit trail for each content change including commit author, changed content files, and build status.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $600/month at 500,000 monthly page views: S3 GovCloud storage $30/month for 20GB regulatory content and PDFs, CloudFront transfer $400/month at $0.085/GB GovCloud pricing, CloudFront requests $80/month, Route 53 $10/month, ACM $0, GitHub Actions under free tier for public repo. Dynatrace GovCloud RUM licensing separate from infrastructure budget. CloudFront auto-scales to handle traffic surges around major regulatory announcements (final rule publications, enforcement actions against prominent firms) without pre-provisioning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Proposed rules, final rules, and enforcement actions migrated from legacy CMS; automated Section 508 axe-core CI check; Flesch-Kincaid check on proposed rule summaries; Dynatrace RUM with IP anonymization; CloudFront + S3 GovCloud deployment with HSTS and CSP. Phase 2 Content Parity (8 weeks): Public dockets, agency guidance, press release archive migrated; Flesch-Kincaid check extended to enforcement action and guidance summaries (ISS-002 resolved in round 2); comment period closure handling (ISS-003 resolved in round 3); legacy CMS decommission. Phase 3 Enhancements (future): Pagefind full-text search index, RSS feeds per regulatory category, Federal Register citation auto-linking, Spanish-language rule summaries.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Markdown content frontmatter must include a last_reviewed_date field set at publication. Proposed rule pages must include comment_summary_document_url when the rule has a closed comment period — CI warns if comment_deadline is in the past and comment_summary_document_url is null. USWDS components must not be modified inline — override via CSS custom properties only. PDF links must have an HTML summary page for Section 508 compliance — CI checks for the presence of a matching HTML summary slug for each pdf_s3_key. PR descriptions must confirm Plain Writing Act Flesch-Kincaid score of any new summary text is under grade 12.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Nightly comment deadline build does not trigger if GitHub Actions is down at 6am ET; a proposed rule page could show an open comment button for up to 24 hours after deadline on a GitHub outage day. Mitigation: Dynatrace synthetic monitor checks /proposed-rules homepage for stale open-comment notices as a secondary detection signal; documented manual deploy runbook for GitHub outages. Risk: HSTS preloading is irreversible for the agency domain — if a future migration requires temporary HTTP access it will be impossible. Mitigation: 6-month staging validation period and agency CIO sign-off required before preload list submission. Tradeoff: Gatsby incremental builds over full builds for nightly deadline updates — incremental reduces comment-close latency from 10 minutes to 3 minutes but requires careful gatsby-cache management to prevent stale node artifacts.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Dynatrace GovCloud instance (FedRAMP authorized) over Google Analytics for APA privacy compliance. Selected gatsby-plugin-csp over manual CSP injection for per-build nonce reliability (resolving ISS-001). Confirmed nightly scheduled GitHub Actions build over Regulations.gov webhook integration for comment period close detection — webhook integration would require server-side infrastructure absent from the static website contract. Flesch-Kincaid grade threshold of 12 confirmed with agency Plain Language coordinator as consistent with Plain Writing Act implementation guidance.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving ISS-003: Regulations.gov comment link shown after comment period closes. Fix uses a Gatsby template conditional plus a nightly scheduled GitHub Actions incremental build. Strong quality with federal regulatory domain terminology, APA/Section 508/Plain Writing Act specifics, and three rounds of internally consistent issue history.

profile.domain: City Traffic Management - Real-Time Adaptive Signal Control

project_goal.value: Research prototype for adaptive signal timing using live sensor data to minimize intersection delay across city corridors
project_goal.rationale: Core research objective for automated phase optimization

target_users.value: Traffic engineers and operations staff at the city traffic management center
target_users.rationale: Primary operators during prototype evaluation

project_class.rationale: Experimental system not yet production-certified for live traffic control

capabilities.value: backend, data, analytics, realtime, integrations, devops
capabilities.rationale: Real-time ingest, time-series storage, NTCIP integration, and ops tooling required

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-second timing decisions, NTCIP protocol integration, concurrent multi-intersection optimization

risk_level.rationale: Prototype operates alongside existing controllers with manual override capability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sensor counts and phase timing data contain no personal information

access_model.rationale: Differentiate write access to timing overrides from monitoring access

feature_scope.rationale: Full research prototype feature set for corridor evaluation

mvp_scope.value: 8-intersection pilot corridor with live sensor ingest, Webster rule-based optimizer, and read-only monitoring dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Validate sensor-to-controller loop before expanding to full city network

security_baseline.value: JWT auth, TLS 1.3 for internal connections, SNMPv3 authPriv for controller communication, network segmentation from public internet
security_baseline.rationale: Prototype on isolated TMC VLAN; signal controller access restricted to known pod CIDR

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team proficiency; WebSocket support for live dashboard updates

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum
backend_stack.rationale: Sub-millisecond message handling latency required for real-time phase computation

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for sensor time-series and phase history; Redis 7 for latest phase state
data_platform.rationale: Hypertable partitioning and continuous aggregates for corridor analytics

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes in TMC server room
hosting_target.rationale: Low-latency access to traffic controller network requires on-premise hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor count data retained 90 days hot, 2 years cold archive; no personal data collected
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Research data retention for post-study analysis

future_scope.value: Expand to 120 city intersections, integrate with incident management system, replace Webster optimizer with ML model
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 research roadmap contingent on pilot results

constraints.rationale: Safety constraint: human override always supersedes algorithm

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for per-intersection metrics, AlertManager on sensor dropout, structured JSON logs via Loki
observability_baseline.rationale: Prototype requires close monitoring during live corridor trials

execution_preference.value: Research sprint cycles with bi-weekly engineer checkpoint reviews
execution_preference.rationale: Academic research pace with milestone checkpoints

project.goal: Adaptive signal timing reducing average intersection delay 15% on pilot corridor
project.target_users: Traffic engineers and TMC operators

backend.framework: Tokio async runtime, custom NTCIP SNMP client

security.auth: Custom JWT RS256
security.transport: TLS 1.3 internal, SNMPv3 authPriv for controllers

data.cache: Redis 7 for phase state with Lua CAS override locking

devops.hosting: Bare-metal Kubernetes in TMC server room

open_questions.ml_optimizer: Deferred to phase 2 after 6 months of live data collection

reasoner_reviews.product: The 8-intersection pilot corridor provides a realistic test of the sensor-to-controller loop while limiting blast radius. The 15% delay reduction target gives a measurable research success criterion for the evaluation period.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust Axum with Tokio is the right choice for sub-second phase computation. TimescaleDB continuous aggregates on sensor_readings will feed the optimizer without requiring a separate OLAP layer. Round 2 must specify SNMPv3 credentials management and Redis override atomicity, both flagged as blocking issues in round 1.
reasoner_reviews.security: The controller_push service writing SNMPv3 SET commands to live traffic signals is the highest-risk component. Credentials must be stored in Kubernetes Secrets with quarterly rotation, and the pod must have NetworkPolicy egress restricted to the controller VLAN CIDR. Override lock must be atomic to prevent optimizer from overwriting engineer commands.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bare-metal Kubernetes provides the necessary L2 adjacency to the controller VLAN. Redis Lua CAS for override locking keeps the critical path under 5ms which satisfies the 200ms override delivery constraint with significant margin.

backend.service_design: Three Axum services: sensor_ingest (NTCIP SNMP poller per intersection), phase_optimizer (per-corridor Webster computation worker), controller_push (SNMPv3 SET client with override lock check)
backend.api_patterns: REST for dashboard queries, WebSocket per corridor for live phase streaming, Redis pub/sub between services
backend.failure_handling: Sensor dropout detection via missed-poll counter; SNMPv3 failure triggers fallback to baseline fixed plan and pages on-call via AlertManager webhook

data.entities: intersection_controllers, sensor_readings (hypertable), phase_plans, timing_adjustments, performance_metrics (hypertable), incident_events, corridor_configurations
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertables on sensor_readings (7-day chunks) and performance_metrics (30-day chunks); continuous aggregate for 5-minute demand averages; Redis sorted set for latest phase snapshots
data.retention_and_deletion: sensor_readings 90 days hot then S3 cold archive; performance_metrics 2 years for research analysis

devops.deployment_topology: 3-node bare-metal Kubernetes, sensor_ingest DaemonSet, phase_optimizer 2-replica active-standby, controller_push single replica with Redis leader election
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo clippy + test on PR; Docker multi-stage build on merge; kubectl rolling update with health gate
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Axum /metrics every 15s; Grafana corridor heatmap and SNMP health dashboards; AlertManager on sensor dropout >30s or SNMP failure rate >0.1/min; Loki for structured log aggregation

security.auth_design: JWT RS256 tokens with 8-hour TTL; roles: engineer, operator, analyst
security.authorization_model: Axum middleware gates POST /override to engineer role; internal service calls use separate service tokens
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All phase plan writes and overrides logged to timing_adjustments with user_id, before/after plan diff, and timestamp

ISS-001.title: NTCIP controller push authentication underspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify how controller_push authenticates to signal controllers. NTCIP 1202 v3 supports SNMP community strings and optional TLS; plan must specify approach consistent with installed hardware.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer in round 1 audit

ISS-002.title: Redis phase state concurrent write race condition
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan described Redis for latest phase state but did not address concurrent writes from the optimizer and manual override paths. Engineer override could be silently overwritten by the optimizer within the same 15-second cycle.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner in round 1 review

focus_issues.title: NTCIP controller push authentication underspecified
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify SNMPv3 or alternative auth for controller_push service.

focus_issues.title: Redis phase state concurrent write race condition
focus_issues.detail: Optimizer can silently overwrite engineer manual overrides; no atomic locking in round 1 design.

latest_recommendations: Specify SNMPv3 authPriv with AES-128 for controller_push and store credentials in Kubernetes Secrets with quarterly rotation
Implement Redis Lua CAS override_locked_until key so optimizer skips intersection while engineer override is active

previous_plan.title: Adaptive Signal Timing Research Prototype - Corridor Pilot Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: On-premise Rust and TimescaleDB prototype for real-time adaptive signal timing on an 8-intersection pilot corridor, pushing optimized phase plans to NTCIP-compatible controllers every cycle.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three Axum services on bare-metal Kubernetes: sensor_ingest polls NTCIP loop detectors, phase_optimizer runs Webster computations, controller_push delivers timing plans to signal controllers.
previous_plan.data_model: intersection_controllers, sensor_readings hypertable, phase_plans, performance_metrics hypertable; Redis for latest phase snapshot per intersection.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: JWT for dashboard; TLS for internal connections; controller authentication not specified in round 1.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by specifying SNMPv3 authPriv with SHA-256/AES-128 matching the installed Econolite ASC/3 controller capabilities, storing credentials in Kubernetes Secrets with quarterly rotation. ISS-002 is resolved by a Redis Lua CAS script setting override_locked_until equal to one full cycle length, making the optimizer skip any intersection with an active override lock. Both fixes integrate cleanly into the existing three-service Rust architecture without structural changes.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified SNMPv3 authPriv (SHA-256 auth, AES-128 encryption) for all controller_push SNMP SET operations; credentials stored per intersection in Kubernetes Secrets, rotated quarterly via Ansible playbook; on 3 consecutive SNMPv3 failures controller_push reverts intersection to baseline fixed plan and fires AlertManager critical alert.
fix_report.expected_outcome: controller_push authenticates with encrypted SNMP sessions; no plaintext controller credentials in config or logs; automatic safe-state fallback on auth failure

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced direct Redis SET with Lua CAS script: override write atomically sets phase_state and override_locked_until = CURRENT_UNIX + cycle_length_sec; optimizer checks override_locked_until before writing and skips if locked; engineer dashboard shows lock countdown per intersection.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Manual engineer overrides cannot be overwritten by the adaptive optimizer within the same phase cycle; override lock self-expires after one cycle duration without engineer action

target_output.title: Adaptive Signal Timing Research Prototype - Corridor Pilot v2
target_output.executive_summary: On-premise Rust and TimescaleDB prototype ingesting NTCIP loop detector counts from an 8-intersection pilot corridor and pushing SNMPv3 authPriv-authenticated phase plan adjustments to Econolite ASC/3 signal controllers every 15-second cycle. Round 2 resolves controller authentication and Redis override atomicity, enabling safe live corridor evaluation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three Axum microservices on bare-metal Kubernetes in the TMC server room: sensor_ingest polls each intersection via NTCIP 1202 v3 SNMP GET every 15 seconds, writing vehicle_count and occupancy to TimescaleDB hypertables; phase_optimizer runs a per-corridor Tokio task computing Webster green-split adjustments from a 5-minute rolling demand aggregate; controller_push issues SNMPv3 SET commands (authPriv: SHA-256 / AES-128) to update active timing plans after checking Redis override_locked_until. React 18 dashboard streams live phase state and performance metrics over WebSocket from the controller_push broadcast channel. All three services are deployed on a 3-node cluster with direct Layer-2 access to the traffic controller VLAN.
target_output.technology_stack: Rust 1.77 with Axum 0.7 and Tokio async runtime; snmp-parser crate for NTCIP decoding; sqlx for async TimescaleDB queries; deadpool-redis for pooled Redis connections. TimescaleDB 2.14 on PostgreSQL 16 with hypertables and continuous aggregates. Redis 7 with Lua CAS scripts committed to scripts/ directory. React 18 TypeScript with Recharts and native WebSocket. Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29, Harbor local registry, GitHub Actions CI, Grafana + Prometheus + AlertManager + Loki.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sensor Ingest: NTCIP 1202 v3 SNMP GET polling every 15 seconds per intersection storing vehicle_count, occupancy_pct, speed_mph per detector lane. Adaptive Optimizer: per-corridor Tokio task applying Webster formula to 5-minute demand aggregate, writing phase_plan rows. Controller Push: SNMPv3 authPriv SET to controller MIB phase split objects, checking override_locked_until before each write, recording timing_adjustment rows. Manual Override: engineer POST sets override_locked_until via Lua CAS and delivers plan to controller within 200ms. Corridor Dashboard: WebSocket-fed React interface with phase state indicators, queue length estimates, and delay trend sparklines. Incident Logging: operator-tagged incident_event records correlated with performance metric anomalies.
target_output.system_components: sensor_ingest (Axum + Tokio DaemonSet, one SNMP polling task per intersection, batch INSERT to sensor_readings via COPY). phase_optimizer (2-replica active-standby Deployment, per-corridor Tokio task reads 5-min continuous aggregate, writes phase_plans). controller_push (single replica with Redis leader election, reads phase_plans queue, checks override_locked_until, issues SNMPv3 SET, writes timing_adjustments). Redis 7 (phase state sorted set per intersection, Lua CAS for override_locked_until). TimescaleDB (sensor_readings 7-day chunks, performance_metrics 30-day chunks, 5-minute continuous aggregate for demand computation). React dashboard (WebSocket subscription, Recharts corridor heatmap). Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager + Loki (15s scrape interval, corridor and SNMP health dashboards).
target_output.workflows: Sensor-to-Controller Cycle: sensor_ingest polls NTCIP agent on 15-second tick, decodes detector occupancy objects, batch-inserts to sensor_readings; continuous aggregate refreshes 5-minute demand window; phase_optimizer reads aggregate, runs Webster formula, writes phase_plan row with delivery_status=pending; controller_push dequeues plan, calls Redis GET override_locked_until for intersection -- if expired or absent, issues SNMPv3 authPriv SET to controller MIB green_split_seconds objects, marks delivery_status=delivered, writes timing_adjustment with predicted delta_delay_sec. Manual Override: traffic engineer POSTs to /intersections/:id/override with desired phase splits; Axum middleware validates engineer JWT role; Lua CAS atomically sets override_locked_until = now + cycle_length_sec and writes override phase_plan to Redis; controller_push delivers override to controller within 200ms SLA; React dashboard shows per-intersection override lock countdown; optimizer skips locked intersections until TTL expires.
target_output.data_model: intersection_controllers (id UUID PK, ntcip_ip INET, snmpv3_username VARCHAR, corridor_id UUID FK, controller_model VARCHAR, firmware_version VARCHAR, baseline_plan_id UUID FK). sensor_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, intersection_id UUID, detector_lane SMALLINT, vehicle_count SMALLINT, occupancy_pct NUMERIC(5,2), speed_mph NUMERIC(5,1)) -- hypertable 7-day chunks. phase_plans (id UUID PK, intersection_id UUID FK, plan_type ENUM adaptive/fixed/override, green_splits_json JSONB, cycle_length_sec SMALLINT, computed_by ENUM optimizer/engineer, effective_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delivery_status ENUM pending/delivered/failed). timing_adjustments (id UUID PK, intersection_id UUID FK, prior_plan_id UUID FK, new_plan_id UUID FK, trigger ENUM sensor/manual/incident, predicted_delta_delay_sec NUMERIC, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, engineer_id UUID). performance_metrics (time TIMESTAMPTZ, intersection_id UUID, avg_delay_sec NUMERIC, throughput_vph SMALLINT, queue_length_veh SMALLINT) -- hypertable 30-day chunks. incident_events (id UUID PK, corridor_id UUID FK, incident_type ENUM accident/special_event/sensor_failure, start_at TIMESTAMPTZ, end_at TIMESTAMPTZ, notes TEXT). corridor_configurations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, intersection_order UUID[], optimizer_params_json JSONB, active BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: GET /corridors/:id/phase-state -- current phase snapshot for all corridor intersections (operator+). GET /intersections/:id/sensor-history?window=5m -- recent demand aggregate (analyst+). POST /intersections/:id/override -- manual override with engineer role required, Lua CAS write. PATCH /intersections/:id/override/cancel -- cancel active lock, engineer role. GET /intersections/:id/timing-adjustments?limit=50 -- recent adjustment history. WebSocket /ws/corridors/:id/live -- streams phase_state and performance_metrics every 15s. GET /incidents -- list incident_events. POST /incidents -- log new incident (operator+). Internal: POST /phase-plans used by phase_optimizer with service token.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 tokens with 8-hour TTL; roles: engineer/operator/analyst enforced in Axum middleware. SNMPv3 authPriv (SHA-256 / AES-128) for all controller_push SNMP SET operations; per-intersection credentials in Kubernetes Secrets, quarterly rotation via Ansible. controller_push NetworkPolicy: egress restricted to controller VLAN CIDR /24 only. On 3 consecutive SNMPv3 failures: revert intersection to baseline fixed plan via safe-state SET, fire AlertManager critical alert, log failure chain to Loki. TLS 1.3 on all internal service connections. Lua CAS override lock prevents optimizer from overwriting engineer commands. MUTCD compliance: all pushed timing plans within legal minimum green and all-red clearance intervals validated before SET.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 on 3 Intel Xeon nodes (64GB RAM each) in TMC server room. Namespaces: adaptive-signal-prod, monitoring. sensor_ingest DaemonSet (1 pod per node), phase_optimizer Deployment (2 replicas active-standby via Redis lock), controller_push Deployment (1 replica with Redis leader election to prevent duplicate SNMP SETs). Harbor at harbor.tmc.local. GitHub Actions: cargo clippy --deny warnings + cargo test on PR; Docker multi-stage build (rust:1.77-slim) on merge to main; kubectl rollout update with readiness probe gate. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo; controller_push on startup reverts all intersections to baseline fixed plan if phase_plans queue is empty. ConfigMaps for corridor topology; Kubernetes Secrets for SNMPv3 credentials.
target_output.observability: Prometheus 15-second scrape from all Axum /metrics endpoints. Key metrics: sensor_ingest_poll_latency_ms histogram, sensor_dropout_consecutive_count gauge per intersection, optimizer_compute_latency_ms histogram, controller_push_delivery_latency_ms histogram, controller_push_snmp_failures_total counter, override_locked gauge per intersection. Grafana dashboards: Corridor Phase Heatmap (green/amber/red per intersection per cycle), Sensor Health (dropout indicators per detector lane), SNMP Delivery Health (per-controller failure rate trend). AlertManager: CRITICAL if sensor_dropout_consecutive_count > 2 for any intersection; WARNING if snmp_failures rate > 0.1/min. Loki for structured JSON logs with intersection_id and plan_id correlation fields.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Self-hosted bare-metal; 3 Kubernetes nodes already owned by TMC. TimescaleDB grows ~50MB/day for 8-intersection pilot; 90-day retention = ~4.5GB. S3 cold archive estimated $2/month. Chunk compression (7:1 ratio) keeps storage flat when expanding to 40 intersections. Optimizer CPU scales linearly with corridor count; current cluster supports ~40 concurrent corridors before hardware expansion.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Sensor Baseline (weeks 1-4): Deploy sensor_ingest for 8 intersections, validate NTCIP poll reliability, collect 4 weeks of baseline demand data, configure Prometheus dropout alerts. Phase 2 - Shadow Optimizer (weeks 5-10): Deploy phase_optimizer in shadow mode computing plans without pushing to controllers; compare Webster plan outputs to live fixed timing to validate demand model accuracy. Phase 3 - Live Corridor Trial (weeks 11-24): Enable controller_push with SNMPv3 during off-peak hours initially, expand to all-day after 2-week off-peak validation, collect performance_metrics for before/after delay analysis against 15% reduction target.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy --deny warnings in CI; unsafe blocks require inline justification comment. TimescaleDB: all sensor_readings writes use sqlx COPY protocol for batch throughput; single-row INSERT forbidden in hot path. Redis: Lua scripts committed to scripts/ directory, loaded at startup via EVALSHA; no inline EVAL strings in application code. NTCIP: controller_push must check override_locked_until and record timing_adjustment atomically in same operation. All phase splits validated against MUTCD minimum green intervals before SNMP SET.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SNMPv3 credential compromise grants write access to live traffic signals. Mitigation: quarterly rotation, Kubernetes Secrets encryption at rest, NetworkPolicy restricts SNMP egress to controller CIDR only. Risk: controller_push crash leaves intersections on last pushed adaptive plan. Mitigation: on restart, controller_push reverts all intersections to baseline fixed plan before rejoining leader election. Tradeoff: Webster formula over ML model -- Webster is interpretable and safe for prototype evaluation; ML deferred to phase 2 after 6 months of live sensor data. Tradeoff: single controller_push replica over HA -- prevents duplicate SNMP SETs which some Econolite firmware versions handle poorly.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose SNMPv3 authPriv over per-controller TLS because installed Econolite ASC/3 controllers support SNMP v3 natively without firmware upgrade. Confirmed Redis Lua CAS for override atomicity over PostgreSQL advisory locks to keep override delivery path under 5ms. Deferred ML optimizer to phase 2 pending 6 months of live sensor data.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolving NTCIP auth and Redis override race for adaptive signal timing research prototype.

profile.domain: Civic Data Engineering CLI for Bulk Procurement Data Download and Normalization

project_goal.value: CLI tool for civic data engineers to bulk download, validate, and normalize public contracting data from multiple state and local procurement portals, consolidating vendor, award, and spending records into a unified schema for transparency research and publication to open data platforms
project_goal.rationale: Reduces manual download and spreadsheet cleanup work for civic tech researchers working across dozens of incompatible procurement portal formats

target_users.value: Civic data engineers, government transparency researchers, and journalism data desks consuming procurement data for analysis
target_users.rationale: Technical users comfortable with CLI tools and local Python environments

project_class.rationale: Locally-executed command-line tool with no hosted infrastructure; value is in the command interface and data processing library

capabilities.rationale: CLI command parsing, portal API integration, batch download, local data transformation, and export are the four active capability surfaces

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-portal API adapters with heterogeneous schema normalization and fuzzy vendor deduplication add meaningful complexity

risk_level.rationale: Processes publicly available procurement data only; no PII, no credentials beyond optional portal API keys; runs locally

data_sensitivity.rationale: All data sources are publicly accessible government procurement portals; no personal or sensitive data in scope

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on researcher local workstation or CI runner; makes outbound API calls to public portals but exposes no inbound endpoints

access_model.value: No auth required for public portal access; optional per-portal API key stored in local config file or environment variable; no user accounts or sessions
access_model.rationale: Most procurement portals are publicly accessible without authentication; API keys are optional rate limit bypasses

feature_scope.value: Multi-portal bulk download with date range filtering, schema validation against unified procurement schema, vendor name and EIN fuzzy deduplication, data quality report generation, CSV and Parquet export, optional DuckDB local persistence, portal adapter configuration via YAML
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature surface for civic researchers processing multi-portal procurement data

mvp_scope.value: Three portal adapters (California eProcure, Illinois Comptroller, USASpending.gov), unified schema validation, CSV export, basic data quality report
mvp_scope.rationale: Three portals cover majority of initial civic research use cases; CSV export covers most downstream analysis tools

security_baseline.value: API keys stored as environment variables or in .env file outside the repository; no credential logging; HTTPS for all portal API calls
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal security posture appropriate for a local CLI tool processing public data

frontend_stack.value: None; CLI output via Click with Rich library for formatted terminal output and progress bars
frontend_stack.rationale: CLI tool; terminal is the interface; Rich provides readable output without a browser UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click
backend_stack.rationale: Click is the standard Python CLI framework; Python ecosystem has best support for data processing, DuckDB, and HTTP client libraries needed for portal adapters

data_platform.value: DuckDB for local persistence and query; Parquet and CSV for export; portal adapters emit Python dataclasses conforming to unified procurement schema
data_platform.rationale: DuckDB provides SQL query capability over local Parquet files without a server; Parquet is the standard interchange format for civic data pipelines

hosting_target.value: Local workstation or CI runner; distributed as a PyPI package; no hosted infrastructure
hosting_target.rationale: CLI tool installed by researchers via pip or pipx; no cloud deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: No retention policy required; data stored locally on researcher workstations at their discretion; tool does not persist data beyond explicit export commands
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: All data is public; retention is the researcher's responsibility, not the tool's

future_scope.value: Additional portal adapters (state-specific portals), natural language query interface over DuckDB, streaming download for very large portal exports, integration with OCDS (Open Contracting Data Standard) schema
future_scope.rationale: Planned expansion after MVP adapters are validated with civic research community

constraints.value: Must run on Python 3.10+ with no compiled C extensions to ensure portability across researcher environments; DuckDB must be optional dependency (not required for CSV-only workflows); total package size under 20MB
constraints.rationale: Researchers use varied Python environments; compiled dependencies create installation friction; package size limit ensures fast pip install in CI runners

observability_baseline.value: Structured log output to stderr with --verbose flag; data quality report written to output directory after each run; exit code 1 on validation failures for CI integration
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI tools should be scriptable; structured logs and non-zero exit codes enable CI pipeline integration for automated procurement monitoring

execution_preference.value: Open-source development with community contributions; GitHub Issues for feature requests; semantic versioning with changelog
execution_preference.rationale: Civic tech open-source norms; community feedback drives portal adapter prioritization

project.goal: CLI tool for multi-portal procurement data download, normalization, and deduplication
project.target_users: Civic data engineers and transparency researchers
project.timeline: MVP (3 adapters, CSV export) in 8 weeks; community release on PyPI

backend.framework: Click for CLI, httpx for async HTTP, Pydantic for schema validation
backend.data_processing: DuckDB for local queries, pandas for transformation, rapidfuzz for vendor name fuzzy matching

security.api_keys: Environment variables or .env file; never logged or included in output files
security.transport: HTTPS for all portal API calls; certificate verification enabled

data.schema: Unified procurement schema as Pydantic model with adapter-specific field mapping

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with pytest and mypy

open_questions.deduplication_threshold: Fuzzy match threshold for vendor name deduplication: 85% or 90%

reasoner_reviews.product: The three-adapter MVP covers the most common civic research use cases. The data quality report is an important output that should be standardized as a JSON file rather than only printed to terminal so researchers can include it in automated pipelines. The OCDS future scope item is ambitious and should be noted as a schema design constraint now so the unified schema can map to OCDS fields without a breaking change.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 1 left the portal adapter plugin architecture underspecified; using a registry-based adapter pattern with a PortalAdapter abstract base class allows community contributors to add new portal adapters without modifying core CLI code. The fuzzy deduplication threshold should be configurable via CLI flag with a sensible default; hardcoding 85% or 90% will frustrate researchers with different data quality expectations.
reasoner_reviews.security: For a local CLI processing public data the security posture is appropriate. The one concern is API key leakage through verbose logging or exception tracebacks that print request headers; the HTTP client must suppress Authorization headers in debug output.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DuckDB as optional dependency is the right approach given the compiled extension concern; the extras_require pattern in pyproject.toml (install with pip install procurement-cli[duckdb]) is clean. Parquet export via pyarrow similarly should be an optional extra to keep base install lean.

backend.service_design: Click command group 'procurement' with four subcommands: download (fetch from portals), validate (check schema), deduplicate (vendor fuzzy matching), export (CSV/Parquet output). PortalAdapter abstract base class with required methods: fetch_awards(date_from, date_to) -> Iterator[RawAwardRecord] and get_metadata() -> PortalMetadata. Adapter registry in adapters/__init__.py mapping portal slug to adapter class.
backend.api_patterns: Each portal adapter implements httpx AsyncClient with portal-specific pagination; California eProcure uses cursor-based pagination, Illinois Comptroller uses offset pagination, USASpending.gov uses after_cursor. Adapters normalize response JSON to RawAwardRecord Pydantic model before yielding.
backend.background_jobs: No background jobs; all processing is synchronous within CLI command execution. Download command uses asyncio.run() wrapping httpx AsyncClient for concurrent multi-portal fetching when multiple --portal flags specified.
backend.failure_handling: HTTP errors: httpx raises HTTPStatusError; adapter catches 429 with configurable --retry-after (default 60s) and retries up to --max-retries (default 3) times with exponential backoff. Schema validation failures: written to validation_errors.jsonl in output directory with record identifier and field error details; run continues with --skip-invalid flag.

data.entities: Unified procurement schema (Pydantic model): ContractAward (award_id STRING, portal_slug STRING, vendor_name STRING, vendor_ein STRING NULLABLE, award_amount DECIMAL, award_date DATE, contract_description STRING, agency_name STRING, agency_code STRING NULLABLE, fiscal_year INT, solicitation_type STRING NULLABLE, normalized_at DATETIME). DeduplicationResult (canonical_vendor_name STRING, matched_names LIST[STRING], ein_resolved STRING NULLABLE, confidence_score FLOAT). DataQualityReport (portal_slug STRING, records_fetched INT, records_valid INT, records_invalid INT, validation_errors_by_field DICT, deduplication_clusters INT).
data.storage_design: Parquet files written to output/{portal_slug}/{fiscal_year}/awards.parquet; optional DuckDB file at output/procurement.duckdb with view definitions over Parquet files. CSV export to output/combined_awards.csv after deduplication.
data.data_risks: Vendor EIN field is frequently missing or misformatted across portals; fuzzy name matching without EIN confirmation may over-merge distinct vendors with similar names; threshold configurability mitigates this.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package distributed as a wheel; no server deployment. GitHub Actions matrix build on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 across ubuntu-latest and macos-latest.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with 80% coverage gate, mypy strict, ruff linter, Hatch build to verify wheel size under 20MB, optional-dependency matrix (with and without duckdb extra). Release workflow on git tag: Hatch build, publish to PyPI via trusted publisher.
devops.observability_stack: Structured logging to stderr via Python logging with --verbose flag enabling DEBUG level. Data quality report written to data_quality_{timestamp}.json in output directory. Exit code 1 on any validation errors when --fail-on-errors flag provided.

security.secrets_management: API keys loaded from environment variables (PROCUREMENT_CLI_{PORTAL}_API_KEY) or .env file via python-dotenv; never included in log output. httpx transport configured with event_hook to redact Authorization headers before logging in verbose mode.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting handled per-adapter respecting Retry-After headers; configurable max concurrent portal requests (default 3) to avoid overwhelming small portal servers.

ISS-001.title: Portal adapter architecture not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described downloading from portals but did not define an adapter interface or registry, making community-contributed adapters impossible without modifying core CLI code

history.note: No PortalAdapter abstract base class or adapter registry defined

ISS-002.title: Fuzzy deduplication threshold hardcoded
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned 85% fuzzy match threshold as a fixed value; researchers need to tune this per-dataset and it should be a configurable --dedupe-threshold CLI flag

history.note: Threshold hardcoded at 85% without CLI flag

focus_issues.title: Fuzzy deduplication threshold hardcoded
focus_issues.detail: Deduplication threshold should be a configurable CLI flag; hardcoded value frustrates researchers with varying data quality expectations

latest_recommendations: Add --dedupe-threshold flag to deduplicate subcommand with default 0.85 and validation between 0.5 and 1.0
Document threshold tuning guidance in CLI help text and README
Consider per-portal threshold configuration in YAML adapter config

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 deduplicate threshold configurability not yet addressed

previous_audits.summary: Round 1 established a reasonable CLI command structure and data model but the portal adapter architecture was undefined and the fuzzy deduplication threshold was hardcoded, limiting extensibility and researcher control.
strengths: DuckDB as optional dependency with extras_require is a clean approach
Pydantic v2 for schema validation with per-field error reporting is appropriate
asyncio.run() wrapping httpx AsyncClient for concurrent multi-portal download is sound
concerns: No PortalAdapter abstract base class defined; community contribution path unclear
Fuzzy match threshold hardcoded; researchers need to tune per dataset
Data quality report format not specified (JSON vs. terminal output)

recommendations: Add --dedupe-threshold FLOAT flag to deduplicate subcommand (default 0.85, range 0.5-1.0)
Write data quality report as JSON file in output directory in addition to terminal summary
Document threshold guidance in CLI --help text

issue_updates.title: Fuzzy deduplication threshold hardcoded
issue_updates.detail: Deduplicate subcommand uses fixed 0.85 threshold; no CLI flag defined

previous_plan.architecture_overview: Click command group with download, validate, deduplicate, export subcommands; httpx async HTTP client per portal; Pydantic ContractAward unified schema; DuckDB optional persistence; Parquet output.
