previous_plan.data_model: network_segments, privileged_accounts, access_policies, endpoint_agents, security_incidents, patch_records stored in PostgreSQL 16.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Conditional Access with MFA; PAM dual-approval for privileged accounts; Sentinel SIEM for event correlation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by enumerating the explicit Azure Firewall permit rules for each critical inter-zone flow before enabling default-deny policy. ISS-002 is resolved by adding an Azure DevOps pipeline schedule condition that blocks the terraform apply stage outside 2am-5am weekday windows, with a separate break-glass pipeline requiring CAB issue number in the run variable to override. Both changes are implemented in Terraform and pipeline YAML without structural changes to the zone architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Enumerated explicit Azure Firewall Network and Application rules for all legitimate inter-zone flows: 911-dispatch spoke permitted outbound to Hub DNS (UDP 53), Hub logging endpoint (TCP 514/6514), and AD domain controllers (TCP 389/636/3268); Finance spoke permitted to Hub AD (TCP 389/636), Finance-internal SQL (TCP 1433 intra-spoke only), and internet via explicit FQDN Application rules only; IT-Admin spoke permitted to all spokes on management ports (TCP 22/3389/5986) with source restricted to PAM gateway IP only. All other cross-zone traffic denied by Azure Firewall default-deny rule.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Default-deny policy can be safely enabled without blocking critical county operational flows; all permitted rules version-controlled in Terraform network_segmentation module

fix_report.action_taken: Added Azure DevOps pipeline schedule condition on the terraform-apply stage: condition evaluates current UTC hour (22-5) and day (Mon-Fri); apply stage skips with informational message outside window. Created break-glass-apply pipeline with required variable CAB_TICKET_NUMBER; CAB ticket number logged to audit_pipeline_runs PostgreSQL table. Both pipelines require PR approval from NetworkSegmentationContributor role before merge.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Production Terraform applies automatically restricted to 2am-5am window; emergency applies require documented CAB ticket number logged for audit trail

target_output.title: County Zero-Trust Infrastructure Hardening v2
target_output.executive_summary: Hub-and-spoke Azure Government VNet with Azure Firewall Premium microsegmentation across 911-dispatch, finance, and IT-admin zones, with enumerated inter-zone permit rules and CAB-gated Terraform apply pipelines. Round 2 resolves default-deny permit gaps and change window enforcement, enabling safe production deployment for cyber insurance compliance deadline.
target_output.architecture_overview: Azure Government Hub VNet hosts Azure Firewall Premium, ExpressRoute gateway, DNS resolver, and centralized logging. Three spoke VNets connected via VNet peering: 911-dispatch-spoke (CAD systems, radio gateway VMs), finance-spoke (ERP and tax system VMs), it-admin-spoke (management jump servers, PAM gateway). On-premise Hyper-V cluster connected via ExpressRoute carries court case management and legacy department VMs. Azure Firewall Premium enforces default-deny with explicit Network and Application permit rules per zone. CyberArk Privileged Access Manager deployed in it-admin-spoke with session recording to restricted Azure Blob. Microsoft Defender for Endpoint deployed via Intune to all county managed endpoints. Terraform 1.8 manages all Azure resources; Ansible 2.17 manages on-premise Hyper-V SDN and EDR agent deployment. Microsoft Sentinel aggregates firewall, AD, PAM, and EDR telemetry.
target_output.technology_stack: Terraform 1.8 with AzureRM provider 3.99; Ansible 2.17 with azure.azcollection and community.windows galaxy collections. Azure Government services: Azure Firewall Premium, VNet/Peering, ExpressRoute, Azure AD with Conditional Access, Microsoft Intune, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Microsoft Sentinel, Azure Monitor, Azure Blob Storage. CyberArk PAM 14.x on-premise VMs in it-admin-spoke. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL for PAM audit and infrastructure inventory. Azure DevOps Pipelines. PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Network Microsegmentation: Azure Firewall Premium policies enforcing default-deny with enumerated permit rules per zone; NSG flow logs to Sentinel. Privileged Access Management: CyberArk vault for domain admin, Azure subscription owner, and network admin accounts; dual-approval workflow; session recording to restricted Azure Blob. EDR Deployment: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint pushed via Intune policy to all Windows endpoints; Linux servers via Ansible. Identity Hardening: Azure AD Conditional Access requiring MFA + Intune-compliant device; legacy NTLMv1 disabled via Group Policy. Vulnerability Management: Defender for Cloud vulnerability assessment weekly scans with Sentinel integration. Change Pipeline: Terraform plan/apply via Azure DevOps with schedule gate and break-glass override.
target_output.system_components: Azure Hub VNet (Azure Firewall Premium, ExpressRoute GW, Azure DNS Private Resolver, Log Analytics Workspace). 911-dispatch spoke VNet (CAD application VMs, radio gateway, NSG with restricted inbound). Finance spoke VNet (ERP VMs, tax system VMs, Azure SQL for financial data). IT-admin spoke VNet (PAM gateway VM, CyberArk vault VM pair, Ansible control node, management jump servers). On-premise Hyper-V SDN (court case management VLAN, legacy department VLANs, Hyper-V virtual switch with SDN policies). PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PAM audit_sessions and network_segments inventory. Azure Blob (restricted container for PAM session recordings, RBAC: PAMAuditor role separate from PAMAdministrator). Microsoft Sentinel workspace. Defender for Endpoint via Intune.
target_output.workflows: Firewall Rule Change: engineer opens PR modifying Terraform network_segmentation module; tflint + terraform validate run in CI; PR comment shows terraform plan diff; NetworkSegmentationContributor approves; merge triggers terraform apply stage with schedule condition check (UTC 22-5, Mon-Fri); if outside window, apply stage skips with CAB-required message; emergency applies via break-glass pipeline requiring CAB_TICKET_NUMBER variable logged to audit_pipeline_runs. Privileged Access Request: admin requests domain controller access in CyberArk portal; request routes to dual-approver queue (two IT-admin role approvers required); on approval, CyberArk checks out rotating credential and establishes recorded RDP session to target via PAM gateway; session recording uploaded to restricted Azure Blob on session end; audit_sessions row written with admin UPN, target system, start/end time, and blob key. EDR Incident Response: Defender for Endpoint detects suspicious process on endpoint; alert forwarded to Sentinel via Defender connector; KQL detection rule fires P1 Sentinel incident; PagerDuty page to SOC on-call; SOC analyst reviews Defender timeline and Azure AD sign-in correlation in Sentinel workbook; if confirmed, isolates endpoint via Defender API and opens CyberArk session to affected server for forensic review.
target_output.data_model: network_segments (id UUID PK, segment_name VARCHAR, vnet_cidr CIDR, zone ENUM dispatch/finance/it_admin/on_premise, department VARCHAR, criticality ENUM critical/high/medium/low, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). privileged_accounts (id UUID PK, upn VARCHAR, account_type ENUM domain_admin/azure_owner/network_admin, vault_id VARCHAR, last_rotated TIMESTAMPTZ, active BOOLEAN). access_policies (id UUID PK, source_segment_id UUID FK, dest_segment_id UUID FK, protocol VARCHAR, port_range VARCHAR, action ENUM permit/deny, firewall_rule_name VARCHAR, terraform_resource_id VARCHAR). endpoint_agents (id UUID PK, hostname VARCHAR, os_type ENUM windows/linux/macos, department VARCHAR, edr_status ENUM enrolled/pending/exempt, last_checkin TIMESTAMPTZ). security_incidents (id UUID PK, sentinel_incident_id VARCHAR, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, source_system VARCHAR, status ENUM open/investigating/resolved, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). vulnerability_findings (id UUID PK, resource_id VARCHAR, cve_id VARCHAR, cvss_score NUMERIC(4,2), status ENUM open/patched/accepted, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). patch_records (id UUID PK, endpoint_id UUID FK, patch_kb VARCHAR, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, applied_by VARCHAR). audit_pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, pipeline_name VARCHAR, triggered_by VARCHAR, cab_ticket VARCHAR, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, terraform_resources_changed JSONB).
target_output.api_design: No public API. Operator interfaces: Azure DevOps Pipelines web UI for Terraform plan/apply review and merge. CyberArk PAM web portal (HTTPS on it-admin-spoke, VPN required) for privileged access requests and session management. Microsoft Sentinel portal for SOC investigation. Ansible ad-hoc commands via Ansible control node in it-admin-spoke for emergency on-premise configuration. CLI: terraform plan/apply for sandbox and break-glass scenarios; az CLI for Azure resource queries. All management access requires Azure AD MFA + Intune-compliant device per Conditional Access policy.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure Firewall Premium default-deny with explicitly enumerated permit rules version-controlled in Terraform. CyberArk dual-approval for all privileged accounts; session recordings in restricted Azure Blob (PAMAuditor RBAC separate from PAMAdministrator). Azure AD Conditional Access: MFA + Intune-compliant device for all users; Named Locations policy restricting admin portal access to county IP ranges and VPN. Break-glass accounts with emergency access policy: no Conditional Access exclusion; Sentinel alert on break-glass sign-in. CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model Level 2 controls: identity verification on every request, device health validated via Intune compliance, network access determined by Firewall policy not VLAN membership. CIS Controls v8: Control 4 (Secure Config), Control 5 (Account Management), Control 6 (Access Control), Control 13 (Network Monitoring). Cyber insurance compliance: session recording, MFA, EDR, and vulnerability scanning documented in quarterly posture report.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR pipeline (tflint, terraform validate, terraform plan as PR comment, Ansible lint + --check), merge pipeline (terraform apply with schedule gate 2am-5am UTC Mon-Fri). Break-glass pipeline (separate pipeline definition requiring CAB_TICKET_NUMBER variable; no schedule gate; approver from IT Director group required; audit_pipeline_runs row written on trigger). Environments: sandbox (Terraform workspace, no production resource impact), staging-nonprod (full topology clone in non-production subscription), production (Azure Government production subscription, ExpressRoute connected). Terraform state in Azure Blob backend with state locking via Azure Blob leases. Ansible inventory from Azure Resource Manager dynamic inventory plugin. Change management: all production PRs require CAB ticket number in PR description field; CI validates ticket format against CAB system API.
target_output.observability: Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace receiving: Azure Firewall IDPS and network rule logs, NSG flow logs, Azure AD sign-in and audit logs, CyberArk syslog (CEF format), Defender for Endpoint alerts, Defender for Cloud vulnerability findings. KQL workbooks: Zero-Trust Posture Dashboard (permit/deny ratio per zone, privileged session count, EDR coverage percentage), Privileged Access Anomaly (unusual access hours, new target systems), Firewall Policy Drift (rules added outside change window). PagerDuty: P1 on Sentinel high-severity incident, P2 on EDR offline >4 hours for critical-zone endpoint, P3 on vulnerability CVSS >9.0 unpatched >7 days. Azure Monitor metric alerts on ExpressRoute circuit utilization >80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Firewall Premium ~$3,500/month (fixed + data processing). CyberArk PAM licensing $800/month (200 privileged accounts). Defender for Endpoint via M365 E5 license already in county contract. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server ~$150/month. Sentinel Log Analytics ingestion ~$400/month at estimated 5GB/day. Total: ~$4,850/month. Scales with additional department spoke VNets at ~$200/month per spoke for Firewall processing.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Critical Zone Segmentation (weeks 1-6): Deploy Hub VNet with Azure Firewall, 911-dispatch and finance spokes, enumerate all permit rules, enable default-deny; deploy CyberArk PAM for domain admin accounts; configure break-glass pipeline and change window gate. Phase 2 - Full EDR and Identity Hardening (weeks 7-12): Roll out Defender for Endpoint to all endpoints via Intune; disable NTLMv1 across all domains; implement Conditional Access device compliance check; onboard remaining departments to Sentinel. Phase 3 - On-Premise SDN and Remaining Zones (weeks 13-20): Deploy Hyper-V SDN for on-premise court and legacy VLANs; extend PAM to remaining privileged accounts; complete vulnerability management integration; generate cyber insurance compliance posture report.
target_output.development_guidelines: Terraform: all modules require tflint --strict pass in CI; no hardcoded resource IDs or subscription IDs (use data sources and variables only); Firewall rules must include a comment field with the business justification and approving CAB ticket. Ansible: all playbooks idempotent; --check dry-run required before any production playbook execution. Change management: PR description must contain CAB ticket number in format CAB-YYYY-NNN; CI validation rejects PRs without valid ticket format. Break-glass: every break-glass pipeline execution creates audit_pipeline_runs record; CISO notified via email on any break-glass trigger.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Firewall rule misconfiguration blocks 911-to-CAD communication. Mitigation: permit rules enumerated and tested in staging-nonprod topology clone before production apply; 911 dispatch operations team participates in staging validation. Risk: CyberArk PAM outage prevents admin access to critical systems. Mitigation: break-glass local admin accounts with hardware-backed credentials in county safe; Sentinel alert on PAM health check failure. Tradeoff: Azure Firewall Premium over Network Security Groups only -- Premium provides IDPS and FQDN application rules needed for CISA Zero Trust Level 2; NSGs alone insufficient. Tradeoff: CyberArk over Delinea -- county has existing CyberArk proof-of-concept; switching to Delinea adds vendor transition cost.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed CyberArk over BeyondTrust based on existing POC and county IT familiarity. Confirmed Azure Firewall Premium over Network Virtual Appliance for IDPS capability without managing VM patching. Confirmed change window gate in Azure DevOps over external approval tool to keep pipeline self-contained.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolving Azure Firewall permit rules and change window gate for county zero-trust hardening project.

profile.domain: State Workforce Development - Apprenticeship Program Registration and OJT Tracking

project_goal.value: Manage apprenticeship program registrations, track on-the-job training hours, connect employers with registered sponsors, and generate federal Department of Labor RAPIDS reporting
project_goal.rationale: Replaces spreadsheet-based tracking and manual RAPIDS exports for state apprenticeship office

target_users.value: Apprentices, employers, program sponsors, and state agency administrators
target_users.rationale: All four roles interact with the system for registration, hour logging, review, and reporting

project_class.rationale: Requires React SPA, Python API, and PostgreSQL backend with batch reporting jobs

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, batch_jobs, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Multi-role UI, FastAPI backend, OJT batch reporting, admin dashboard for agency staff

complexity_level.rationale: Moderate data model complexity; no real-time requirements; batch reporting is the main technical challenge

risk_level.rationale: Handles personal data for apprentices and employers; federal reporting errors could trigger compliance audit

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores apprentice PII (name, SSN for RAPIDS, DOB, wage rates) and employer EIN

external_exposure.rationale: All portals require Auth0 login; no public unauthenticated access

access_model.value: RBAC with four roles: apprentice (view own records), employer (manage own apprentices and OJT logs), sponsor (review sponsor program), admin (agency staff with full access)
access_model.rationale: Each role has distinct data access scope matching 29 CFR Part 29 program participant responsibilities

feature_scope.value: Apprentice registration and profile management, employer onboarding, OJT hours log submission and approval, occupation standard compliance checks, apprenticeship agreement management, completion certificate generation, federal RAPIDS reporting export, admin dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full lifecycle from registration through completion and federal reporting

mvp_scope.value: Apprentice registration, OJT hours log submission with sponsor approval, basic compliance check against occupation standard, and RAPIDS CSV export
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed to replace manual spreadsheets and satisfy federal reporting obligations

security_baseline.value: Auth0 RBAC, TLS 1.3, SSN encrypted at rest with Cloud KMS, OWASP Top 10 mitigations, 90-day access log retention
security_baseline.rationale: PII data requires encryption at rest; OWASP baseline for public-facing authenticated portal

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; reuse of existing agency component library

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async endpoints and Pydantic validation of OJT submission payloads

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL with automated backups and point-in-time recovery on GCP

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API, Cloud Storage + Firebase Hosting for SPA
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers for cost reduction; no fixed monthly server costs for low-volume state agency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Apprentice records retained 5 years after program completion per 29 CFR Part 29; SSN masked in all logs and exports except RAPIDS submission
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal records retention requirement for registered apprenticeship programs

future_scope.value: Mobile app for apprentice OJT logging, integration with community college transcript systems for related technical instruction hours, interstate portability for multi-state sponsors
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 features after initial agency adoption

constraints.value: RAPIDS export must produce CSV in exact DOL-specified format (Form ETA-671 equivalent); system must support time-based and competency-based occupation standards
constraints.rationale: DOL RAPIDS system accepts only the prescribed CSV schema; both standard types required by 29 CFR Part 29

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring with custom metrics, Cloud Logging structured logs, uptime checks on API health endpoint
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native monitoring within existing agency GCP account

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with agency program staff demos at end of each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Agency stakeholder involvement needed to validate OJT workflow accuracy

compliance_context.value: 29 CFR Part 29 and 30 National Apprenticeship Act regulations, EEO reporting requirements, state apprenticeship standards
compliance_context.rationale: Federal apprenticeship regulations mandate specific data collection and reporting

project.goal: Digitize apprenticeship registration, OJT tracking, and federal RAPIDS reporting for state workforce agency
project.target_users: Apprentices, employers, program sponsors, state agency admins
project.timeline: Q4 delivery for next apprenticeship cohort

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for multi-step form UI state

security.auth: Auth0 with RBAC
security.encryption: SSN encrypted via Cloud KMS, TLS 1.3

data.cache: None; Cloud SQL connection pool via Cloud Run

devops.hosting: Cloud Run for API, Firebase Hosting for React SPA

constraints.standard_types: Time-based and competency-based occupation standards supported

open_questions.ssn_storage: Evaluate Cloud KMS envelope encryption vs application-level AES

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope covering registration, OJT hour submission, and RAPIDS export replaces the most painful manual workflows for both employers and agency staff. The occupation standard compliance check is critical to include in MVP because employers frequently submit hours against incorrect standard types, causing RAPIDS rejection.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI on Cloud Run is well-suited for this workload -- the API handles form submissions and report generation, not real-time traffic. PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL provides the relational integrity needed for apprenticeship_agreements and ojt_hours_logs foreign key chains. The RAPIDS CSV export should be a batch job rather than an on-demand endpoint to handle large cohorts without timeout risk. SSN must be encrypted at rest; Cloud KMS envelope encryption at the application layer is cleaner than relying solely on Cloud SQL disk encryption for HIPAA-analog PII protection.
reasoner_reviews.security: SSN and EIN fields must be encrypted at the column level using Cloud KMS-backed envelope encryption, not just disk encryption. RBAC must prevent employers from viewing apprentice SSNs directly in the UI -- SSNs surface only in the RAPIDS export batch job output, which is restricted to admin role. Auth0 RBAC must be configured with Fine-Grained Authorizations to scope employers to their own apprentices only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The RAPIDS CSV format is strictly prescribed; the export batch job must implement the ETA-671 field mapping exactly including coded values for occupation classification, wage type, and ethnicity. A test export against DOL validation rules should be part of CI.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with domain routers: apprentices, employers, sponsors, ojt_logs, agreements, reporting. Separate Celery worker (or Cloud Run Job) for batch RAPIDS CSV generation
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0; Pydantic v2 models for OJT submission validation against occupation_standard constraints
backend.failure_handling: Pydantic validation rejects OJT submissions exceeding occupation standard max hours; RAPIDS export job writes to GCS and sends notification on completion rather than streaming response

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with role-based route guards; separate view trees for apprentice portal, employer portal, sponsor portal, admin dashboard
frontend.pages_and_flows: Apprentice: Dashboard, My OJT Hours, My Agreement, Certificates. Employer: Apprentice Roster, Submit OJT Hours, Program Status. Sponsor: Program Review, Approve OJT Logs, Compliance Dashboard. Admin: Agency Dashboard, RAPIDS Export, User Management, Occupation Standards Library.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server state with 5-minute cache; Zustand for multi-step apprentice registration and OJT submission wizard state

data.entities: apprentices, employers, program_sponsors, ojt_hours_logs, occupation_standards, apprenticeship_agreements, completion_certificates, federal_reports
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16; SSN and EIN encrypted with Cloud KMS AEAD envelope encryption; RAPIDS export artifacts stored in GCS bucket with uniform access control
data.retention_and_deletion: Apprentice records archived after 5 years post-completion; SSN column set to NULL after archive; GCS RAPIDS export files lifecycle to Coldline after 1 year

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run for FastAPI (min 1, max 10 instances); Firebase Hosting for React SPA; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in private VPC with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy in Cloud Run. Cloud Run Jobs for RAPIDS batch export.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff + mypy + pytest --cov=80% on PR; Docker build and Artifact Registry push on merge; Cloud Run deploy via gcloud run deploy; Cloud Run Job container update. Integration tests against Cloud SQL local proxy in CI.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring: custom metrics for ojt_submission_rate, rapids_export_job_duration, apprentice_completion_rate; uptime checks on /health; Cloud Logging structured JSON with request_id correlation

security.auth_design: Auth0 M2M token for API service account; user tokens with custom claims: role (apprentice/employer/sponsor/admin), employer_id, apprentice_id for scoping
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency injection checks Auth0 JWT claims; employer endpoints filter queryset by employer_id claim; admin endpoints check role=admin; SSN read restricted to admin + RAPIDS export service account only
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All OJT log approvals and RAPIDS exports logged to audit_events table with user_id, action, resource_id, and timestamp

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a state apprenticeship management system. Key decisions: FastAPI on Cloud Run for cost-effective serverless deployment aligned with state agency budget constraints; Cloud KMS envelope encryption for SSN at application layer providing defense-in-depth beyond Cloud SQL disk encryption; RAPIDS batch export as Cloud Run Job rather than streaming API endpoint to handle large cohort exports without timeout risk; Auth0 Fine-Grained Authorization for employer-scoped data access preventing cross-employer apprentice data leakage.
target_output.title: State Apprenticeship Program Management System
target_output.executive_summary: Fullstack application on GCP for a state workforce development agency managing apprentice registrations, on-the-job training hour tracking, occupation standard compliance validation, and federal RAPIDS reporting. Built with React 18, FastAPI on Cloud Run, and PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL, with Auth0 RBAC controlling access for apprentice, employer, sponsor, and admin roles.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via Firebase Hosting communicates over HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on Cloud Run, authenticated through Auth0 JWT tokens with role-based claims. Backend connects to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy in the same Cloud Run service network. Apprentice SSNs and employer EINs are encrypted using Cloud KMS AEAD envelope encryption at the application layer before storage. A Cloud Run Job handles nightly RAPIDS CSV batch generation for large cohorts, writing export files to a GCS bucket. Auth0 Fine-Grained Authorization scopes employer API access to their own apprentice records only. GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline deploys to Cloud Run and Firebase Hosting on merge to main.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, React Hook Form. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0, google-cloud-kms for envelope encryption, Cloud SQL Auth Proxy. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with automated backups and point-in-time recovery. Auth: Auth0 with RBAC and Fine-Grained Authorization. Infra: Cloud Run (API), Cloud Run Jobs (RAPIDS export), Firebase Hosting (SPA), GCS (export artifacts), Artifact Registry. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Google Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Apprentice Registration: multi-step wizard capturing personal info, SSN (encrypted), occupation, wage, start date, linked employer and sponsor. OJT Hours Submission: employer submits monthly ojt_hours_log against occupation_standard constraints; Pydantic validates hours do not exceed standard maximum per period; sponsor reviews and approves or requests correction. Apprenticeship Agreement Management: digital agreement linking apprentice, employer, and sponsor with occupation_standard, wage progression schedule, and related technical instruction hours. Occupation Standard Compliance Check: real-time validation of cumulative OJT hours against time-based or competency-based standard thresholds at submission. Completion Certificate Generation: triggered when apprentice meets all standard requirements; PDF generated and stored in GCS. RAPIDS Export: Cloud Run Job compiling ETA-671 format CSV from finalized records; restricted to admin role. Admin Dashboard: agency staff view for program-wide metrics, pending approvals, and compliance flags.
target_output.system_components: Firebase Hosting (React SPA, global CDN, HTTPS enforced). Cloud Run API service (FastAPI, min 1 instance, max 10, Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar, 1 vCPU / 512MB). Cloud Run Jobs (RAPIDS export job, triggered on schedule or admin request, writes to GCS). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP, automated daily backups, 7-day PITR). Cloud KMS key ring for SSN/EIN envelope encryption. GCS bucket (RAPIDS exports, completion certificate PDFs; uniform access control, lifecycle to Coldline after 1 year). Auth0 tenant (RBAC, Fine-Grained Authorization, M2M token for export service). Google Cloud Monitoring + Cloud Logging.
target_output.workflows: OJT Hours Submission: employer logs in and navigates to Submit OJT Hours; selects apprentice from their roster; enters hours per week for the month with task category; React Hook Form validates non-negative values; FastAPI Pydantic model checks cumulative hours against occupation_standard.max_hours_per_period; if valid, ojt_hours_log row created with status=pending; sponsor receives in-app notification. Sponsor Review: sponsor reviews pending ojt_hours_logs; views apprentice cumulative progress against occupation standard; approves or adds correction request; on approval status updated to approved; audit_events row written with sponsor_id and timestamp; apprentice receives confirmation notification. RAPIDS Export: agency admin triggers export from admin dashboard; Cloud Run Job queries all apprentices with finalized records in the reporting period; maps each record to ETA-671 CSV fields including occupation_code, program_type, ethnic_code, wage_type, total_ojt_hours; encrypts SSN field with admin-role KMS key for export; writes CSV to GCS with presigned URL emailed to admin; Cloud Monitoring metric recorded.
target_output.data_model: apprentices (id UUID PK, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, dob DATE, ssn_encrypted BYTEA, email VARCHAR, phone VARCHAR, employer_id UUID FK, sponsor_id UUID FK, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). employers (id UUID PK, org_name VARCHAR, ein_encrypted BYTEA, contact_name VARCHAR, contact_email VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN). program_sponsors (id UUID PK, sponsor_name VARCHAR, sponsor_type ENUM joint/unilateral/employer, contact_email VARCHAR, state_approval_number VARCHAR). ojt_hours_logs (id UUID PK, apprentice_id UUID FK, employer_id UUID FK, log_month DATE, hours_worked NUMERIC(6,2), task_category VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/approved/correction_requested, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reviewed_by UUID FK). occupation_standards (id UUID PK, onet_code VARCHAR, title VARCHAR, standard_type ENUM time_based/competency_based, total_hours_required INTEGER, max_hours_per_period INTEGER, related_instruction_hours INTEGER). apprenticeship_agreements (id UUID PK, apprentice_id UUID FK, employer_id UUID FK, sponsor_id UUID FK, standard_id UUID FK, start_date DATE, projected_end_date DATE, wage_progression_json JSONB, status ENUM active/suspended/cancelled/completed). completion_certificates (id UUID PK, apprentice_id UUID FK, agreement_id UUID FK, issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ, gcs_pdf_key VARCHAR). federal_reports (id UUID PK, report_type ENUM rapids_eta671, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, generated_by UUID FK, gcs_csv_key VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, user_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /apprentices (register; admin/employer). GET /apprentices/:id (profile; own apprentice or admin). POST /ojt-logs (submit; employer). GET /ojt-logs?apprentice_id=&status= (list; scoped by role). PATCH /ojt-logs/:id/review (approve/request correction; sponsor). GET /agreements/:id (view agreement; parties + admin). POST /reports/rapids-export (trigger Cloud Run Job; admin only). GET /reports/:id/download (presigned GCS URL; admin only). GET /occupation-standards (list standards). GET /admin/dashboard-metrics (program stats; admin only). Auth: Auth0 JWT validated in FastAPI dependency. Rate limiting: 60 req/min per user via Cloud Run concurrency settings.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RBAC with four roles; Fine-Grained Authorization scopes employer access to own apprentices. Cloud KMS AEAD envelope encryption for ssn_encrypted and ein_encrypted columns; encryption key accessible only to API service account and RAPIDS export service account; never logged or returned in API responses. TLS 1.3 enforced on Cloud Run and Firebase Hosting. SSN masked in all Cloud Logging output (log sanitizer middleware strips last 7 digits). OWASP Top 10: SQL injection prevented by SQLAlchemy parameterized queries; XSS prevented by React escaping; CSRF not applicable (JWT stateless). 29 CFR Part 29 compliance: occupation standard type (time-based/competency-based) validation enforced at OJT submission. EEO data collected per 29 CFR Part 30 fields captured at apprentice registration. Audit events table for all approval and export actions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff + mypy (strict) + pytest --cov=80% on PR; Docker multi-stage build (python:3.12-slim) on merge; Artifact Registry push; gcloud run deploy --image with traffic split 80/20 for canary validation; promote to 100% after health check. Cloud Run Job container updated on merge. Firebase Hosting deploy via firebase-tools in CI. Environments: dev (Cloud Run --no-traffic deployment), staging (full deployment with test data), production (Cloud SQL private IP, KMS encryption live). Secrets: GCP Secret Manager for Auth0 client secret and DB connection string. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy v2 in Cloud Run service container for IAM-authenticated DB connections. Rollback: Cloud Run previous revision via gcloud run services update-traffic.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: apprentice_registrations_total (counter), ojt_log_submissions_total (counter), rapids_export_job_duration_seconds (histogram), apprentice_completion_rate (gauge). Uptime checks on GET /health every 60 seconds with PagerDuty alert on failure. Cloud Logging structured JSON with request_id, user_id (hashed), and employer_id on every API request. Log-based alerts: Cloud Run error rate >2% over 5 minutes; RAPIDS export job failure. Dashboard: registration funnel, OJT approval queue depth per sponsor, cumulative hours vs standard completion percentage.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run ~$30/month at 500 daily requests (serverless, min 1 instance). Cloud SQL db.g1-small ~$50/month. Firebase Hosting $5/month. GCS + KMS ~$10/month. Total: ~$95/month for MVP scale. Scales to $200/month at 5,000 daily requests before needing Cloud Run instance count increase. RAPIDS export job runs once weekly; max runtime 5 minutes for 2,000 apprentice cohort.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (10 weeks): Apprentice registration with SSN encryption, employer OJT hour submission, sponsor approval workflow, occupation standard compliance check, RAPIDS CSV export for admin, Auth0 RBAC, CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Apprenticeship agreement digital management, wage progression tracking, completion certificate PDF generation, admin dashboard metrics. Phase 3 (4 weeks): EEO reporting fields, interstate sponsor portability, bulk OJT import from employer CSV, email notifications via SendGrid.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff + mypy strict in CI; all FastAPI endpoints use Pydantic v2 models with field-level validators for OJT hour range checks. SSN handling: ssn_encrypted column never returned in any API response; Cloud KMS encrypt/decrypt called only in ApprenticeService.create() and RAPIDS export job; unit tests mock KMS client. RAPIDS export: ETA-671 field mapping defined in a dataclass with typed fields; unit tests assert output CSV matches DOL sample records. React: no SSN values in Redux or Zustand state; employer portal filtered by employer_id claim from Auth0 JWT at every query.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: RAPIDS CSV format change by DOL breaks export. Mitigation: ETA-671 field mapping isolated in rapids_formatter.py module; DOL format version tracked in config; integration test against DOL sample records in CI. Risk: Cloud Run cold start latency on low-traffic periods. Mitigation: min-instances=1 configured for production to maintain warm instance. Tradeoff: Cloud Run Jobs over Celery for RAPIDS export -- Cloud Run Jobs eliminate Redis broker dependency and cost for a weekly batch job; acceptable for this workload frequency. Tradeoff: Auth0 over custom JWT -- Auth0 Fine-Grained Authorization simplifies employer-scoped access without custom middleware logic.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloud KMS envelope encryption over application-level AES for SSN because KMS provides key rotation and IAM-based access control without managing key material in application code. Confirmed Cloud Run Jobs over Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Functions for RAPIDS batch because Jobs provide better timeout handling and container reuse. Selected competency-based standard support in MVP because 30% of state-registered occupations use competency-based standards.

metadata.notes: First-pass fullstack apprenticeship management system with Cloud KMS SSN encryption and RAPIDS DOL reporting.

profile.domain: State Arts and Culture Agency - Grants Management and Panel Review

project_goal.value: Enable applicants to submit grant proposals, reviewers to score applications through structured panel review, and agency staff to manage awards, disbursements, and compliance reporting
project_goal.rationale: Replace legacy Access database grant tracking with web application supporting full lifecycle from application to closeout

target_users.value: Applicant organizations, peer reviewers, agency program officers, and agency finance staff
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user populations with separate workflows and data access scopes

project_class.rationale: Next.js SPA, Go API, PostgreSQL backend with batch disbursement and reporting jobs

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, batch_jobs, admin_panel, devops
capabilities.rationale: Multi-role UI, Go API, panel review scoring, disbursement batch processing, financial reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Complex panel review workflow, disbursement integration with state accounting system, 2 CFR 200 compliance tracking

risk_level.rationale: Handles award financial data, budget documents, and disbursement requests against state accounting system

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores grant award amounts, grantee budget breakdowns, disbursement transactions, and federal pass-through grant financial data

external_exposure.rationale: All portals require Azure AD B2C login; no public unauthenticated access beyond login page

access_model.value: RBAC with five roles: applicant, reviewer, program_officer, finance_officer, admin; reviewers blinded to applicant identity during scoring
access_model.rationale: 2 CFR 200 requires conflict-of-interest controls; reviewer blinding is a standard panel review fairness requirement

feature_scope.value: Grant application submission with document upload, conflict-of-interest disclosure, blinded panel scoring with rubric, award decision workflow, disbursement request generation, state accounting integration, progress report submission, budget amendment workflow, closeout reporting, admin dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full grants lifecycle from solicitation through closeout required for 2 CFR 200 compliance

mvp_scope.value: Application submission, blinded panel scoring, award decision, and first disbursement request with state accounting interface
mvp_scope.rationale: Core lifecycle for first grant cycle; progress reporting and closeout in subsequent sprints

security_baseline.value: Azure AD B2C RBAC, TLS 1.3, financial data encrypted at rest with Azure Key Vault, OWASP Top 10 mitigations, audit log for all financial transactions
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires encryption at rest; audit log required for 2 CFR 200 compliance

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js server components for document-heavy pages; team proficiency

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin
backend_stack.rationale: Go for API performance and type safety; Gin for routing and middleware

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL with zone-redundant HA on Azure Government-adjacent region

hosting_target.value: AKS on Azure
hosting_target.rationale: AKS provides the container orchestration needed for multiple services with autoscaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Grant records retained 7 years post-closeout per 2 CFR 200.334; financial transaction logs retained 7 years; reviewer scoring data retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal Uniform Guidance records retention requirements for pass-through grants

future_scope.value: Public grants portal for applicant self-service status tracking, integration with federal grants.gov for subaward reporting, machine-learning-assisted application completeness check
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 features after initial grant cycle validation

constraints.value: Disbursement requests must integrate with state FMIS (Financial Management Information System) via REST API; reviewer scoring must be blinded to applicant org name and contact information during active review period
constraints.rationale: FMIS integration is non-negotiable for payment processing; reviewer blinding required for conflict-of-interest compliance

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with custom metrics, Application Insights for API tracing, Log Analytics workspace, PagerDuty alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Azure Monitor investment in agency Azure tenant

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with Azure DevOps Pipelines; formal UAT with agency program staff before each production release
execution_preference.rationale: Agency stakeholder UAT required for financial workflows before go-live

compliance_context.value: 2 CFR Part 200 Uniform Guidance for federal pass-through grants, state grant administration manual, state financial management requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Federal pass-through grant requirements mandate conflict-of-interest controls, financial reporting, and records retention

project.goal: Grants management system covering application through closeout with panel review and FMIS disbursement integration
project.target_users: Applicants, reviewers, program officers, finance staff, admins

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Context for reviewer scoring session

security.auth: Azure AD B2C RBAC
security.encryption: Azure Key Vault for financial data encryption keys, TLS 1.3

data.cache: Redis 7 for reviewer session state during scoring

constraints.fmis: REST API integration with state FMIS for disbursement requests
constraints.reviewer_blinding: Applicant identity hidden from reviewers during active review period

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope is appropriately bounded at application submission through first disbursement. Progress reporting and closeout are clearly Phase 2. The blinded panel scoring workflow is the most complex UX element and needs detailed specification to avoid reviewer frustration during live grant cycles.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go Gin is appropriate for the API surface area. The round 3 plan resolved reviewer blinding via a database view but left the FMIS disbursement integration underspecified -- particularly around idempotency handling for duplicate disbursement requests, which are a real risk with state FMIS systems that may deliver webhook callbacks multiple times. Round 4 must address idempotency key management for disbursement_requests.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 3 plan added Azure Key Vault for financial field encryption but did not address the reviewer conflict-of-interest disclosure workflow -- COI disclosures must be stored and accessible to program officers even after the review panel closes, and a reviewer who discloses a conflict must be excluded from scoring that application automatically. This was flagged in round 3 but not resolved.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The FMIS REST integration requires mTLS per state IT security policy. Round 3 mentioned mTLS but did not specify certificate rotation procedures or fallback behavior when the FMIS endpoint is unavailable.

backend.service_design: Go Gin API with domain packages: applications, panels, awards, disbursements, reports. Separate batch worker for disbursement request generation and FMIS submission with idempotency.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful OpenAPI 3.0; idempotency keys on POST /disbursements; mTLS client cert for FMIS outbound calls
backend.failure_handling: FMIS submission failures logged to disbursement_attempts table with retry backoff; PagerDuty alert after 3 consecutive FMIS failures; never double-submit a disbursement request

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with role-based layouts; blinded review mode strips applicant PII from all reviewer-visible components
frontend.pages_and_flows: Applicant: My Applications, Submit Proposal, Upload Documents, Check Status. Reviewer: Assigned Applications (blinded), Score Application, COI Disclosure. Program Officer: Review Panel Management, Award Decisions, Progress Report Review. Finance: Disbursement Queue, FMIS Submission Status. Admin: Program Config, User Management, Reporting Dashboard.
frontend.state_management: React Query for grant data; reviewer scoring session in React Context to prevent partial score loss on navigation

data.entities: grant_applications, funding_programs, review_panels, scoring_rubrics, award_decisions, disbursement_schedules, progress_reports, budget_amendments
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16; award_amount and disbursement_amount encrypted via Azure Key Vault AEAD; blinded_application_view database view strips applicant_org_name and contact fields for reviewer queries
data.retention_and_deletion: Grant records archived 7 years post-closeout; reviewer scoring retained 3 years; financial transaction logs retained 7 years in append-only audit_ledger table

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster: api-deployment (3 replicas), batch-worker Deployment (1 replica), ingress via NGINX Ingress Controller. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server zone-redundant. Redis on Azure Cache for Redis. Azure Blob Storage for document uploads.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: go vet + golangci-lint + go test ./... -coverprofile on PR; Docker build and ACR push on merge; kubectl rollout update with Helm chart. UAT gate on staging before production promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Application Insights SDK in Go API for distributed traces; custom metrics: application_submissions_total, disbursement_requests_total, fmis_submission_latency_ms; Azure Monitor alerts on FMIS failure rate; Log Analytics workspace.

ISS-001.title: FMIS disbursement idempotency not addressed
ISS-001.detail: Round 3 plan added FMIS mTLS mention but did not specify idempotency key management for disbursement requests. State FMIS systems commonly re-deliver webhook callbacks; without idempotency the system could submit duplicate payment requests to the state treasury.

history.note: First raised in round 2 when FMIS integration was added
Round 3 added mTLS but did not address idempotency

ISS-002.title: Reviewer COI disclosure workflow incomplete
ISS-002.detail: Reviewer conflict-of-interest disclosures must automatically exclude the reviewer from scoring affected applications and must be retained post-panel-close. Round 3 plan captured COI fields but did not implement the automatic exclusion logic or retention policy.

history.note: Raised by compliance reviewer
COI fields added to schema but exclusion logic not specified

ISS-003.title: mTLS certificate rotation not specified
ISS-003.detail: Round 3 specified mTLS for FMIS but did not describe certificate issuance, rotation cadence, or fallback when the FMIS endpoint is unavailable.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer in round 3 audit

focus_issues.title: FMIS disbursement idempotency not addressed
focus_issues.detail: Duplicate disbursement submission risk to state treasury without idempotency key management.

focus_issues.title: Reviewer COI disclosure workflow incomplete
focus_issues.detail: Automatic reviewer exclusion from conflicted applications not implemented.

latest_recommendations: Implement idempotency key as UUID generated at disbursement_request creation, stored in disbursement_requests.idempotency_key with UNIQUE constraint; FMIS POST includes key in X-Idempotency-Key header; re-submissions return cached response

Implement coi_disclosures table with application_id + reviewer_id; on COI disclosure creation, set panel_assignments.excluded=true for that reviewer-application pair; COI records retained 3 years on cascade-protected delete

Specify mTLS client certificate issued by Azure Private CA, 1-year TTL, renewed 30 days before expiry via cert-manager in AKS; FMIS fallback: queue disbursement_attempt in PostgreSQL and retry with exponential backoff for up to 48 hours
