previous_plan.title: State Arts Grant Management System v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js and Go Gin grants management system on AKS with blinded panel review, Azure Key Vault financial field encryption, and mTLS-secured FMIS disbursement integration.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: AKS-hosted Go Gin API with PostgreSQL on Azure Database; blinded_application_view strips applicant PII for reviewers; disbursements submitted to state FMIS via mTLS REST.
previous_plan.data_model: grant_applications, funding_programs, review_panels, scoring_rubrics, award_decisions, disbursement_schedules, progress_reports, budget_amendments; coi_disclosures present but exclusion logic absent.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C RBAC; Azure Key Vault for financial encryption; mTLS for FMIS; COI fields captured but exclusion not automated.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 resolves both high-severity issues. ISS-001: disbursement idempotency implemented via a UUID idempotency_key on disbursement_requests with a UNIQUE constraint; the FMIS HTTP client sends X-Idempotency-Key header; on a 409 response from FMIS, the system updates the local record from the cached response body rather than retrying. ISS-002: a coi_disclosures table is added; on INSERT a PostgreSQL trigger sets panel_assignments.excluded=true for the reviewer-application pair and emails the program officer; excluded reviewers see a blinded conflict notice instead of the scoring form. ISS-003 resolved by cert-manager in AKS issuing client certificates from Azure Private CA with 1-year TTL and 30-day auto-renewal. All three fixes are internally consistent and do not require structural changes to the existing API or schema.

fix_report.action_taken: Added idempotency_key UUID column to disbursement_requests with UNIQUE constraint; FMIS HTTP client sets X-Idempotency-Key header on every POST /payments call; on FMIS 409 Conflict response, batch worker reads cached payment_reference from response body and updates local disbursement_request without re-submitting; disbursement_attempts table logs each attempt with fmis_response_code and idempotency_key for audit trail.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Duplicate disbursement submissions to state treasury eliminated; any FMIS retry or webhook re-delivery results in 409 with cached response rather than duplicate payment request

fix_report.action_taken: Added coi_disclosures table (reviewer_id, application_id, disclosed_at, relationship_description); PostgreSQL trigger on coi_disclosures INSERT sets panel_assignments.excluded=true for matching reviewer-application row and queues program_officer notification; excluded reviewer sees blinded COI notice on assigned application instead of scoring form; coi_disclosures records protected from cascade delete with 3-year retention enforced by retention policy job.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Reviewers with disclosed conflicts are automatically excluded from scoring affected applications; program officers are notified and can reassign the panel slot; COI records retained for 3 years for audit

target_output.title: State Arts Grant Management System v4
target_output.executive_summary: Next.js 14 and Go 1.22 Gin grants management system on AKS for a state arts agency, covering application submission through disbursement with blinded panel review, Azure Key Vault financial encryption, and idempotency-safe mTLS FMIS disbursement integration. Round 4 resolves FMIS duplicate payment risk via idempotency keys and implements automatic reviewer COI exclusion for 2 CFR 200 compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: AKS-hosted Go Gin API (3 replicas) serves four role-based user populations through a Next.js 14 front end on Azure Static Web Apps. Applications are submitted with document uploads to Azure Blob. Blinded review is enforced by a PostgreSQL view stripping applicant PII from all reviewer-role queries. Panel scoring sessions use Redis for draft score preservation. Award decisions trigger disbursement_request records; a batch Go worker submits requests to the state FMIS via mTLS REST with idempotency key headers, storing each attempt in disbursement_attempts for audit. Financial fields (award_amount, disbursement_amount, budget line items) are encrypted using Azure Key Vault AEAD. coi_disclosures trigger automatic panel_assignment exclusion. Azure AD B2C provides RBAC with five roles. Azure Application Insights instruments distributed traces across API and batch worker.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, React Context for scorer session. Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin, GORM for PostgreSQL ORM, azure-sdk-for-go for Key Vault and Blob Storage, net/http for FMIS mTLS client with x509 cert loaded from cert-manager Secret. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database Flexible Server, zone-redundant HA. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache. Container registry: Azure Container Registry. AKS 1.29 with cert-manager, NGINX Ingress. Azure AD B2C. Azure Key Vault. Azure Blob Storage. Azure DevOps Pipelines. Azure Monitor + Application Insights + Log Analytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Grant Application Submission: multi-part form with project narrative, budget upload to Azure Blob, supporting documents, applicant org profile. Conflict-of-Interest Disclosure: reviewer submits COI on assigned application; trigger excludes reviewer and notifies program officer. Blinded Panel Scoring: reviewer sees application stripped of org name and contact info; scores each rubric criterion; draft scores saved to Redis per session; final submission written to scoring_rubric_responses. Award Decision: program officer reviews aggregate panel scores, records award_decision with award_amount (Key Vault encrypted); applicants notified. Disbursement Workflow: finance officer initiates disbursement_request; batch worker submits to FMIS via mTLS POST with X-Idempotency-Key; payment_reference recorded on success. Progress Reporting: grantee submits narrative and financial reports against award milestones. Budget Amendment: grantee requests reallocation between budget line items; program officer approves within 2 CFR 200 limits.
target_output.system_components: AKS api-deployment (Go Gin, 3 replicas, HPA on CPU 60%). AKS batch-worker deployment (disbursement FMIS submitter, 1 replica). AKS cert-manager (issues mTLS client cert from Azure Private CA ClusterIssuer, 1-year TTL, auto-renew 30 days before expiry). Azure Database PostgreSQL Flexible Server (zone-redundant, db.Standard_D4s_v3). Azure Cache for Redis (C2 Standard). Azure Blob Storage (grant documents and budget uploads, private access, SAS tokens for download). Azure Static Web Apps (Next.js 14 hosting). Azure Key Vault (AEAD keys for award_amount, disbursement_amount, budget_line_item_amounts). Azure AD B2C (custom policies for applicant self-registration, reviewer invite flow). Azure Application Insights. Log Analytics workspace. PostgreSQL views: blinded_applications_view (strips applicant_org_name, contact_email, contact_name for reviewer role queries). PostgreSQL trigger: coi_disclosures_exclusion_trigger (sets panel_assignments.excluded=true on INSERT).
target_output.workflows: COI Disclosure and Exclusion: reviewer opens assigned application in scoring portal; clicks 'Disclose Conflict of Interest'; submits relationship_description; INSERT to coi_disclosures fires PostgreSQL trigger setting panel_assignments.excluded=true for reviewer_id + application_id; application disappears from reviewer queue; SendGrid email queued to program officer with reviewer name and disclosed relationship; program officer reassigns panel slot from reviewer pool. Disbursement Submission: finance officer creates disbursement_request for approved award milestone; batch worker polls disbursement_requests WHERE status=pending; generates idempotency_key UUID if absent; submits POST to FMIS /api/v2/payments with mTLS client cert, X-Idempotency-Key header, and payment payload; on 200 response writes payment_reference and sets status=submitted; on 409 response reads cached payment_reference from FMIS response body and sets status=submitted without re-submission; on 5xx retries with exponential backoff (1m, 2m, 4m, 8m) up to 48 hours; after 48 hours sets status=failed and pages PagerDuty; each attempt written to disbursement_attempts with fmis_http_status and idempotency_key. Blinded Panel Review: reviewer opens scoring portal; Go API enforces reviewer role JWT claim; all application queries routed through blinded_applications_view; rubric criteria displayed; draft scores written to Redis key scorer:reviewer_id:application_id with 8-hour TTL; final submit writes scoring_rubric_responses rows and deletes Redis draft; panel scores aggregated into panel_score_summaries on panel close.
target_output.data_model: grant_applications (id UUID PK, funding_program_id UUID FK, applicant_org_id UUID FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/awarded/declined, narrative_blob_key VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). funding_programs (id UUID PK, program_name VARCHAR, cycle_year SMALLINT, award_ceiling_cents BIGINT, review_opens DATE, review_closes DATE). review_panels (id UUID PK, application_id UUID FK, status ENUM assembling/active/closed). panel_assignments (id UUID PK, panel_id UUID FK, reviewer_id UUID FK, excluded BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scoring_rubrics (id UUID PK, program_id UUID FK, criterion VARCHAR, max_points SMALLINT). scoring_rubric_responses (id UUID PK, assignment_id UUID FK, rubric_id UUID FK, score SMALLINT, justification TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). coi_disclosures (id UUID PK, reviewer_id UUID FK, application_id UUID FK, relationship_description TEXT, disclosed_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- protected from cascade delete. award_decisions (id UUID PK, application_id UUID FK, decided_by UUID FK, award_amount_encrypted BYTEA, decision ENUM awarded/declined, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ). disbursement_schedules (id UUID PK, award_id UUID FK, milestone_description VARCHAR, due_date DATE, amount_encrypted BYTEA). disbursement_requests (id UUID PK, schedule_id UUID FK, idempotency_key UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL, status ENUM pending/submitted/failed, payment_reference VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). disbursement_attempts (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, idempotency_key UUID, fmis_http_status SMALLINT, response_body TEXT, attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). progress_reports (id UUID PK, award_id UUID FK, report_period VARCHAR, narrative TEXT, financial_report_blob_key VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). budget_amendments (id UUID PK, award_id UUID FK, requested_by UUID FK, line_item_changes_json JSONB, status ENUM pending/approved/denied, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_ledger (id UUID PK, actor_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, amount_cents BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE grants.
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /applications (submit; applicant). GET /applications/:id (view; parties + admin; reviewer queries use blinded view). POST /applications/:id/coi (disclose COI; reviewer; triggers exclusion). GET /panels/:id/applications (reviewer queue; blinded). POST /panels/:id/applications/:app_id/scores (submit scores; reviewer). POST /awards (record award decision; program_officer). GET /awards/:id (view award; grantee + program_officer + finance). POST /disbursement-requests (initiate; finance_officer). GET /disbursement-requests/:id/status (check FMIS status; finance_officer). POST /progress-reports (submit; grantee). POST /budget-amendments (request; grantee). GET /admin/dashboard-metrics (program stats; admin). All endpoints validate Azure AD B2C JWT with role claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C five-role RBAC; reviewer endpoints enforce blinded_applications_view at PostgreSQL query layer. Azure Key Vault AEAD encryption for award_amount_encrypted, disbursement schedule amounts, budget line item amounts; Key Vault access policy restricts to api-deployment managed identity and finance-officer role only. mTLS for FMIS: cert-manager ClusterIssuer backed by Azure Private CA; client cert rotated 30 days before 1-year expiry; FMIS fallback on cert rotation gap: queue disbursement_requests and retry after cert renewal. Disbursement idempotency: UNIQUE constraint on disbursement_requests.idempotency_key; batch worker checks idempotency before submission; FMIS 409 handled as success with cached reference. COI exclusion: PostgreSQL trigger enforces exclusion atomically; cannot be bypassed by application layer. Audit ledger table append-only (GRANT INSERT only to api role; no UPDATE/DELETE). 2 CFR 200 controls: COI policy enforced, financial records retained 7 years, progress report milestones tracked.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS 1.29 on Azure. Namespaces: grants-prod, monitoring, cert-manager. api-deployment (3 replicas, HPA CPU 60% target). batch-worker Deployment (1 replica). cert-manager with Azure Private CA ClusterIssuer. NGINX Ingress with TLS termination (Let's Encrypt for public; Azure Private CA for internal). Azure DevOps Pipelines: go vet + golangci-lint + go test -cover=80% on PR; Docker build and ACR push on merge; Helm chart upgrade with --atomic rollback on failure; UAT gate: staging deployment gated on manual approval from program officer UAT signoff. Environments: dev, staging (with test FMIS sandbox), production. Azure Blob for document storage with private endpoints. Rollback: Helm rollback to previous chart revision. Secrets: Azure Key Vault CSI driver mounting secrets into pods; no secrets in environment variables.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights SDK in Go API and batch worker: distributed trace per disbursement submission chain from finance officer action through FMIS response. Custom Azure Monitor metrics: application_submissions_total, panel_scores_submitted_total, disbursement_fmis_success_rate (gauge), disbursement_attempt_latency_ms (histogram), coi_disclosures_total (counter). PagerDuty: CRITICAL on disbursement_request status=failed after 48 hours; HIGH on FMIS HTTP error rate >10% over 30 minutes; HIGH on cert-manager certificate expiry <7 days. Log Analytics workspace for all AKS pod logs with disbursement_request_id and idempotency_key correlation fields. Azure Monitor workbook: grant cycle funnel (applications submitted -> under review -> awarded -> first disbursement).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS node pool 3x Standard_D4s_v3 ~$900/month. Azure Database PostgreSQL Flexible D4s ~$300/month. Redis C2 ~$80/month. Azure Blob ~$20/month. Key Vault + App Insights ~$50/month. Total ~$1,350/month at one active grant cycle. Second grant cycle adds batch worker load but no scaling tier change needed. HPA scales API to 6 replicas during peak application submission window.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP Grant Cycle (12 weeks): Application submission with document upload, Azure AD B2C RBAC, blinded panel scoring with COI exclusion, award decision, first disbursement request via FMIS with idempotency, audit ledger. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Progress report submission, budget amendment workflow, grantee dashboard with award status and disbursement history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Admin reporting dashboard with grant cycle analytics, batch closeout report generation, retention enforcement batch job for 7-year rule.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck enabled in CI; all GORM queries against reviewer-role requests use blinded_applications_view exclusively -- enforced by ReviewerRepository type that wraps the view. Disbursements: batch worker must check idempotency_key UNIQUE before submission; FMIS client MUST NOT retry on 409 -- treat as success. COI: coi_disclosures INSERT must go through COIService.Disclose() which wraps trigger invocation in a transaction with the notification queue insertion; never INSERT directly to table from handler. Key Vault: encrypt/decrypt calls only in EncryptedMoneyType GORM custom type; never store plaintext award amounts in application memory beyond the request scope.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: FMIS system unavailability during disbursement deadline. Mitigation: 48-hour retry window covers typical weekend outages; PagerDuty escalation to finance officer and FMIS vendor contact on failure. Risk: PostgreSQL trigger for COI exclusion bypassed by direct DB access. Mitigation: api role has no INSERT privilege on panel_assignments directly -- only through stored procedure; audit_ledger captures all assignment changes. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL trigger for COI exclusion over application-layer logic -- trigger is atomic and cannot be bypassed by application bugs; tradeoff is tighter coupling to PostgreSQL. Tradeoff: cert-manager over manual mTLS cert management -- cert-manager automates rotation reducing human error risk at cost of AKS add-on dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed idempotency key as client-generated UUID (not FMIS-assigned) because FMIS does not issue idempotency tokens on the state system. Confirmed PostgreSQL trigger for COI exclusion over application logic to ensure atomicity. Confirmed cert-manager with Azure Private CA over self-signed certs to satisfy state IT security policy for mTLS certificate chain validation.

metadata.notes: Round 4 resolving FMIS idempotency and reviewer COI exclusion for state arts grants management system.

profile.domain: Command-line tool for state agency FISMA officers to automate the generation and validation of System Security Plans (SSPs), pulling control implementation status from authoritative sources and rendering complete NIST SP 800-53 documentation packages.

project_goal.value: Automate SSP generation and validation for state agency FISMA officers by pulling NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 control implementations from GRC platforms and rendering Word/PDF documentation packages
project_goal.rationale: Replaces error-prone manual document assembly and reduces officer time-to-submission from weeks to hours

target_users.value: State agency FISMA security officers and AO staff who author and review System Security Plans
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified during discovery workshops with three pilot agencies

project_class.rationale: Workstation-local execution with no hosted runtime; distributable Python package invoked via CLI

capabilities.rationale: Requires data persistence for control state, external GRC integrations, and rendering logic; no frontend

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source data merging and NIST 800-53 control taxonomy traversal add moderate complexity beyond simple ETL

risk_level.rationale: Handles internal government system metadata and control implementation narratives; not public-facing but sensitive

data_sensitivity.rationale: SSP content describes system architecture and security control gaps; marked for internal distribution only

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on officer workstation or CI runner; no network-accessible service endpoints

access_model.value: API key per upstream GRC integration stored in local config; no multi-user auth model since tool is per-workstation
access_model.rationale: Single-user CLI does not require RBAC; key rotation enforced per agency policy

feature_scope.value: ssp-generate command assembling full SSP from SQLite state; ssp-validate command outputting prioritized deficiency findings; control-sync subcommand pulling fresh implementation data from GRC APIs; report export in NIST Word template and PDF
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope confirmed after pilot agency requirements sessions

mvp_scope.value: ssp-generate for AC and SC control families from static YAML config, Word output only, three pilot agencies
mvp_scope.rationale: Validates core document assembly pipeline before GRC integration complexity is added

security_baseline.value: API keys stored in OS keyring or env vars only; no plaintext secrets in config files; output SSP files written with 0600 permissions; TLS 1.3 for all upstream GRC API calls
security_baseline.rationale: FISMA environment mandates secure handling of system metadata even at workstation level

frontend_stack.value: None — CLI only, no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: cli_tool project class; Click-based command interface is the entire user surface

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click for CLI framework; Jinja2 for template rendering; python-docx and WeasyPrint for document output
backend_stack.rationale: FISMA officer workstations standardized on Python; Click is idiomatic for complex CLI hierarchies

data_platform.value: SQLite 3.45 for local control state cache; YAML for system configuration files
data_platform.rationale: No server database needed; SQLite provides queryable persistence for incremental sync without daemon dependency

hosting_target.value: Local workstation install via pip or pipx; also runs in GitHub Actions CI runner for automated SSP validation gating
hosting_target.rationale: Must work offline for air-gapped environments; CI integration enables automated compliance checks on system config changes

privacy_retention_policy.value: Generated SSP files retained per agency records management schedule (minimum 3 years); local SQLite cache purged on ssp-reset command or after 90 days of inactivity
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NARA records schedules apply to security documentation; local cache is ephemeral working state not a records system

future_scope.value: OSCAL JSON export for machine-readable SSP interchange; integration with FedRAMP ASAP platform; AI-assisted control narrative drafting
future_scope.rationale: OSCAL is the federal direction for SSP interoperability; AI drafting could further reduce officer burden

constraints.value: Must run on Python 3.12+ with no root privileges; no outbound connections during document rendering; offline mode for air-gapped systems; total install size under 100MB
constraints.rationale: Agency workstation policies restrict privilege escalation and large software installs

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON log output to stdout; --verbose and --debug flags; exit codes mapped to CI failure conditions; ssp-validate outputs machine-readable JSON findings in addition to human-readable table
observability_baseline.rationale: CI integration requires parseable output; officers need verbose mode for troubleshooting GRC sync failures

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery: MVP for three pilot agencies followed by GRC integration sprint, then validation engine hardening
execution_preference.rationale: Pilot feedback from real FISMA officers is essential before full control family coverage

compliance_context.value: FISMA compliance required; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 control catalog as authoritative taxonomy; NIST SP 800-18 SSP content guidance; state agency ISCM program requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Tool directly produces FISMA authorization artifacts; must accurately represent 800-53 control structure

project.goal: Automate SSP document generation and deficiency validation for FISMA officers
project.target_users: State agency FISMA security officers and authorization officials
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, GRC integration in 16 weeks

backend.framework: Click for CLI hierarchy, Jinja2 for template rendering

security.api_key_storage: OS keyring via keyring library or environment variables
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all upstream GRC API calls
security.file_permissions: Output files written with 0600; config with 0600

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with pytest, mypy, ruff, and ssp-validate exit-code gate
devops.hosting: pipx install from PyPI or internal Artifactory mirror

constraints.offline: Full offline mode for air-gapped systems

confirmed_decisions.control_catalog: Bundled NIST 800-53 Rev 5 OSCAL JSON as package data
confirmed_decisions.grc_integrations: ServiceNow GRC and Archer confirmed for pilot

reasoner_reviews.product: The tool fills a genuine gap in FISMA artifact production; manual SSP assembly from spreadsheets is the current state at most state agencies. Control-sync with GRC platforms is the highest-value feature — officers spend most time chasing implementation status updates. The validation subcommand doubles as a CI gate, enabling proactive compliance posture monitoring rather than point-in-time audits.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Bundling the NIST 800-53 Rev 5 OSCAL JSON as package data is the right call — it guarantees a consistent control catalog without network dependency during rendering. The SQLite cache needs a clear invalidation strategy: control sync should timestamp each control family pull and the generate command should warn if cache is stale beyond a configurable threshold. Click command groups with subcommands is the correct pattern for the ssp/control/config command namespaces.
reasoner_reviews.security: API keys for ServiceNow and Archer must never touch config files. The keyring library integration with OS credential stores is appropriate; add a fallback to environment variables for CI runners where interactive keyring access is unavailable. Generated SSP files contain system architecture details and control gaps that are CUI-adjacent — enforce 0600 output permissions and add a --output-dir flag so officers can direct output to encrypted volumes.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 100MB install size constraint is achievable if WeasyPrint dependencies are made optional extras. Offline mode is critical for air-gapped classified systems; the tool must function fully with a pre-populated SQLite cache and no GRC connectivity. Exit code semantics for CI integration need to be documented clearly: exit 0 for clean validation, exit 1 for findings above a configurable severity threshold, exit 2 for tool errors.

backend.service_design: Single Python package 'ssp-forge' with Click command group hierarchy: top-level ssp (generate, validate, reset), control (sync, list, show), config (init, set, get). Each command module is a separate file under ssp_forge/commands/. Core rendering pipeline in ssp_forge/renderer/ with separate Jinja2 template loader and docx/PDF serializers.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API — CLI invocation pattern. Integration adapters for ServiceNow GRC and Archer use httpx with retry/backoff. Adapter interface defined as abstract base class GRCAdapter with fetch_control_implementations(family: str) -> list[ControlImplementation]. New GRC sources added without touching core renderer.
backend.business_modules: ControlCatalog module loads bundled OSCAL JSON and resolves control baselines (Low/Moderate/High) to applicable control sets. SSPAssembler merges catalog controls with cached implementation narratives, populating Jinja2 context. BoundaryResolver maps system_components to authorization_boundaries sections. FindingsEngine evaluates completed SSP context against 800-18 completeness rules and emits Finding objects with severity and remediation guidance.
backend.backend_risks: Risk: GRC API schema drift breaking adapter parsing. Mitigation: versioned adapter contracts with Pydantic v2 models; adapter test suite using recorded HTTP fixtures via pytest-recording.

security.auth_design: No user authentication — workstation-local tool. API key retrieval order: OS keyring (keyring library) -> environment variable SSP_FORGE_{ADAPTER}_API_KEY -> interactive prompt with keyring offer. Keys never written to SQLite or config YAML.
security.secrets_management: keyring library for interactive workstations; GITHUB_ACTIONS secret injection via env var for CI runners. ssp-forge config show command explicitly masks key values with asterisks in output. Warn on startup if API key found in config file.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting on GRC API calls via token bucket in adapter layer — max 10 req/s to prevent triggering upstream rate limits. Exponential backoff with jitter on 429 responses.
security.security_risks: Risk: SSP output files with control gap details left in insecure directories. Mitigation: default output to CWD with 0600 perms; --output-dir validation warns if target path is world-readable.

data.entities: security_controls (id, control_id NIST format e.g. AC-2, family, title, description, baseline_low BOOL, baseline_moderate BOOL, baseline_high BOOL, source_version). control_implementations (id, control_id FK, system_profile_id, narrative TEXT, status ENUM not_implemented/partially_implemented/implemented/not_applicable, responsible_role, last_synced_at, source_adapter). system_components (id, system_profile_id, component_name, component_type, description, boundary_inside BOOL). authorization_boundaries (id, system_profile_id, boundary_description, diagram_path). ssp_documents (id, system_profile_id, generated_at, output_path, format ENUM word/pdf, control_families TEXT, findings_count). poa_items (id, control_id FK, system_profile_id, weakness_description, scheduled_completion, milestones TEXT).
data.storage_design: SQLite database at ~/.ssp-forge/{system_id}/state.db per system profile. Schema managed by Alembic with auto-migration on tool startup. System profile YAML at ~/.ssp-forge/{system_id}/profile.yaml defines system name, impact level, boundary, and GRC adapter config.
data.schema_notes: control_implementations.status enum drives FindingsEngine severity: not_implemented on a Moderate baseline control is a HIGH finding. OSCAL bundled JSON is read-only package data, never written to SQLite.
data.data_risks: Risk: Stale control_implementations cache generating inaccurate SSPs. Mitigation: ssp-generate warns if any control family last_synced_at is older than --max-cache-age (default 7 days) and exits non-zero unless --allow-stale is passed.

devops.deployment_topology: Distributed as Python wheel on PyPI (public) and internal Artifactory mirror (agency-controlled). Officers install via 'pipx install ssp-forge' for isolated virtualenv. CI runners install from requirements.txt pinned lockfile.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff), type-check (mypy --strict), unit tests (pytest with 85% coverage gate), integration tests against mocked GRC adapters using pytest-httpx fixtures, ssp-validate exit-code test with known-deficient fixture profile. Release workflow: tag push triggers PyPI publish via Trusted Publishing (OIDC).
devops.observability_stack: structlog library emits JSON to stderr at WARNING level by default. --verbose raises to INFO; --debug raises to DEBUG. ssp-validate --output json flag emits machine-readable findings array for CI parsers. Exit codes: 0 clean, 1 validation findings above threshold, 2 tool/config errors, 3 GRC connectivity failure.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: WeasyPrint transitive dependencies (libpango, libcairo) failing on headless CI runners. Mitigation: PDF rendering is an optional extra 'ssp-forge[pdf]'; Word output has no native lib dependencies. Document in README that PDF extra requires OS package installation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 closes ISS-003 (WeasyPrint native deps) and ISS-004 (CI exit code semantics). The fix is clean: move PDF rendering to an optional install extra so the base package has zero native lib dependencies, and define a precise exit code table (0 clean, 1 validation findings above threshold, 2 tool/config errors, 3 GRC connectivity failure) documented in both the devops section and CLI reference. All four issues are now resolved; the plan is architecturally stable. Key design decisions preserved from prior rounds: Click command groups for ssp/control/config namespaces, GRCAdapter ABC for ServiceNow and Archer, SQLite per system profile with Alembic migrations, OSCAL bundled as package data for offline use, and stale cache guard with configurable --max-cache-age.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved WeasyPrint and its native library dependencies (libpango, libcairo) into an optional install extra defined in pyproject.toml as 'ssp-forge[pdf]'. Base install produces Word output only with zero native lib requirements. README and CLI --help text document OS package prereqs for PDF extra.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runners can install and run ssp-forge without system package modifications; PDF extra is available for officer workstations that install OS deps

fix_report.action_taken: Defined and documented a four-value exit code table: exit 0 for successful generation or clean validation, exit 1 for ssp-validate findings at or above --min-severity threshold (default medium), exit 2 for configuration or rendering errors, exit 3 for GRC adapter connectivity failures. Exit codes added to CLI --help, README, and the observability section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CI pipelines can reliably distinguish between SSP quality failures (exit 1) and tool infrastructure failures (exit 2/3) for appropriate alerting and retry logic

target_output.title: SSP Forge CLI — FISMA SSP Automation Tool Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: SSP Forge is a Python 3.12 Click-based CLI tool that automates NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 System Security Plan generation and validation for state agency FISMA officers. The tool syncs control implementation narratives from ServiceNow GRC and Archer via versioned adapter interfaces, stores per-system state in SQLite, and renders complete SSP documents in Word format (base install) or PDF (optional extra requiring libpango/libcairo). A validation subcommand evaluates SSP completeness against NIST 800-18 guidance and exits with structured findings for CI pipeline gating. All four identified issues are resolved in this round: stale cache guards, CI key injection fallback, PDF optional extras, and exit code semantics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Workstation-local Python package installed via pipx with a three-level Click command hierarchy: ssp (generate, validate, reset), control (sync, list, show), and config (init, set, get). Each command module is isolated under ssp_forge/commands/. The core rendering pipeline in ssp_forge/renderer/ uses a Jinja2 template loader fed by SSPAssembler, which merges the bundled NIST 800-53 Rev 5 OSCAL JSON catalog with control_implementations rows from a per-system SQLite database. ServiceNow GRC and Archer integration adapters implement a common GRCAdapter ABC and are invoked only during control-sync; document rendering is fully offline. PDF output is an optional install extra ('ssp-forge[pdf]') to eliminate libpango and libcairo native library dependencies from the base install, enabling clean CI runner execution.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI: Click 8.1 with command group hierarchy. Template rendering: Jinja2 3.1. Document output: python-docx 1.1 (base install, no native deps); WeasyPrint 62 with libpango/libcairo as optional extra 'ssp-forge[pdf]'. Data: SQLite 3.45 via SQLAlchemy 2.0 core with Alembic for schema migrations. HTTP: httpx 0.27 with retry middleware for GRC adapters. Validation: Pydantic v2 for GRC adapter response models. Secrets: keyring 25 for OS credential store integration. Config: PyYAML 6 for system profile files. Logging: structlog 24 for structured JSON output. Testing: pytest with pytest-httpx for adapter mocking and pytest-recording for fixture capture. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Trusted Publishing to PyPI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ssp generate --system-id AGENCY-001 --impact-level moderate: resolves 325 applicable 800-53 controls, merges control_implementations from SQLite, warns if cache older than --max-cache-age (default 7 days), renders Word SSP using NIST-format Jinja2 templates, writes output to --output-dir with 0600 permissions. ssp validate --system-id AGENCY-001 --min-severity medium: runs FindingsEngine against assembled SSP context, emits prioritized findings table to stdout and machine-readable JSON via --output json, exits 1 if findings at or above threshold. control sync --system-id AGENCY-001 --adapter servicenow: fetches all control family implementations via GRCAdapter with progress bars, updates SQLite with timestamps. config init: interactive wizard creating system profile YAML with system name, impact level, boundary, and adapter config.
target_output.system_components: Click CLI entry point at ssp_forge/__main__.py. Command modules under ssp_forge/commands/ (generate.py, validate.py, sync.py, config.py). SSPAssembler merging OSCAL catalog with SQLite control_implementations into Jinja2 render context. ControlCatalog loading bundled OSCAL JSON and resolving baseline control sets for Low/Moderate/High impact. FindingsEngine evaluating SSP context against NIST 800-18 completeness rules and classifying findings. GRCAdapter ABC with ServiceNowAdapter and ArcherAdapter. Renderer with Jinja2 loader, DocxSerializer (base), and optional PdfSerializer. SQLite state DB per system profile with Alembic auto-migration. System profile YAML loader and validator.
target_output.workflows: SSP Generation Workflow: Officer runs 'ssp-forge ssp generate --system-id AGENCY-001 --impact-level moderate'. ControlCatalog resolves 325 applicable Moderate baseline controls from bundled OSCAL JSON. SQLite queried for control_implementations; families with last_synced_at older than 7 days emit a yellow warning listing stale families by name; --allow-stale bypasses the error. SSPAssembler populates Jinja2 context including system_components, authorization_boundaries, and poa_items. DocxSerializer renders output using NIST 800-18 Word template section structure. File written with 0600 permissions; generation time and output path printed to stdout. SSP Validation CI Gate Workflow: GitHub Actions job runs 'ssp-forge ssp validate --system-id AGENCY-001 --min-severity medium --output json > findings.json'. FindingsEngine evaluates all 325 Moderate baseline controls for not_implemented or partially_implemented status, checks for missing authorization_boundaries narrative, and flags unclosed poa_items past scheduled_completion. Exit code 1 emitted if any medium-or-above findings present. CI step uploads findings.json as artifact on failure for officer review.
target_output.data_model: security_controls (id INTEGER PK, control_id TEXT UNIQUE e.g. 'AC-2', family TEXT e.g. 'AC', title TEXT, description TEXT, baseline_low BOOLEAN, baseline_moderate BOOLEAN, baseline_high BOOLEAN, source_version TEXT e.g. 'Rev5'). control_implementations (id INTEGER PK, control_id TEXT FK references security_controls, system_profile_id TEXT, narrative TEXT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('not_implemented','partially_implemented','implemented','not_applicable'), responsible_role TEXT, last_synced_at DATETIME, source_adapter TEXT). system_components (id INTEGER PK, system_profile_id TEXT, component_name TEXT, component_type TEXT, description TEXT, boundary_inside BOOLEAN). authorization_boundaries (id INTEGER PK, system_profile_id TEXT, boundary_description TEXT, diagram_path TEXT). ssp_documents (id INTEGER PK, system_profile_id TEXT, generated_at DATETIME, output_path TEXT, format TEXT CHECK IN ('word','pdf'), control_families TEXT, findings_count INTEGER). poa_items (id INTEGER PK, control_id TEXT FK, system_profile_id TEXT, weakness_description TEXT, scheduled_completion DATE, milestones TEXT).
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API — CLI command interface only. Command signatures: 'ssp-forge ssp generate --system-id TEXT --impact-level [low|moderate|high] [--output-dir PATH] [--max-cache-age DAYS] [--allow-stale] [--format word|pdf]'. 'ssp-forge ssp validate --system-id TEXT [--min-severity low|medium|high] [--output table|json] [--exit-on-findings]'. 'ssp-forge control sync --system-id TEXT --adapter [servicenow|archer] [--families AC,SC,AU]'. 'ssp-forge config init [--system-id TEXT] [--interactive]'. All commands support --verbose and --debug flags and emit structured JSON logs to stderr. GRCAdapter internal interface: fetch_control_implementations(family: str, system_id: str) -> list[ControlImplementation]. ServiceNow adapter calls GET /api/now/table/sn_compliance_control_test with control family filter parameter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key retrieval follows a strict fallback chain: OS keyring via keyring library (interactive workstations) -> environment variable SSP_FORGE_{ADAPTER}_API_KEY (CI runners and scripted use) -> interactive prompt with offer to store in keyring. Keys never written to SQLite or config YAML; startup check warns if any API key pattern detected in config file. Generated SSP files written with 0600 permissions; --output-dir validation warns if target path is world-readable. FISMA compliance: ControlCatalog accurately implements NIST 800-53 Rev 5 baseline resolution — Low: 125 controls, Moderate: 325 controls, High: 421 controls — per NIST SP 800-53B Annex tables. FindingsEngine rules encode NIST 800-18 SSP completeness requirements. All GRC API calls use httpx with TLS 1.3 and certificate verification enforced; no verify=False permitted in codebase. Pydantic v2 strict models validate all GRC API responses before SQLite write to prevent injection via malformed narrative payloads.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: Python wheel published to PyPI via GitHub Actions Trusted Publishing (OIDC) on semver tag push. Officers install via 'pipx install ssp-forge' for isolated virtualenv; 'pipx install ssp-forge[pdf]' for PDF support (requires OS packages: libpango1.0-0 libcairo2 on Debian/Ubuntu). Internal agency mirror: Artifactory proxy of PyPI with approved version pinned. Air-gapped install: wheel plus all dependencies bundled as offline archive via 'pip download --no-deps' for each dep. CI runner install: 'pip install ssp-forge==X.Y.Z' from requirements.txt lockfile; no OS package installation needed for Word-only use. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ruff lint -> mypy --strict type check -> pytest unit tests (85% coverage gate) -> pytest integration tests with pytest-httpx mocked GRC adapters -> ssp-validate exit-code test with known-deficient fixture profile (expects exit code 1). Release pipeline: tag push -> build wheel -> PyPI Trusted Publishing -> GitHub Release with autogenerated changelog from conventional commits. Alembic auto-migration runs on first command invocation; migration failure exits 2.
target_output.observability: structlog library emits structured JSON to stderr at WARNING level by default. --verbose flag raises log level to INFO showing control family sync progress and template section rendering milestones. --debug raises to DEBUG with full httpx request/response logging and sensitive header redaction. ssp-validate --output json emits machine-readable findings array for CI consumption: each entry includes control_id, severity, finding description, and remediation guidance text. Exit code semantics: 0 = successful generation or clean validation with zero findings above --min-severity; 1 = ssp-validate found one or more findings at or above the severity threshold; 2 = configuration error, Alembic migration failure, or rendering error; 3 = GRC adapter connectivity failure including network errors, auth failures, and persistent 5xx responses. CI pipelines should treat exit 1 as a compliance quality gate failure and exits 2/3 as infrastructure alerts requiring separate on-call notification.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero ongoing infrastructure cost — workstation-local execution with no hosted services. PyPI hosting is free for open-source distribution; Artifactory mirror cost is absorbed by existing agency IT contracts. Development effort: one senior Python engineer for 16-week delivery. SQLite scales to thousands of control_implementations per system profile without performance concern at FISMA-scale systems. Agencies managing 50+ information systems can parallelize control-sync across profiles using a thin Python multiprocessing wrapper. WeasyPrint PDF rendering for a full 325-control Moderate SSP takes 8-12 seconds on a modern workstation — acceptable for batch generation; Word rendering via python-docx completes in under 1 second.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): ssp generate and ssp validate for AC and SC control families only; static YAML config without interactive wizard; ServiceNow GRC adapter; Word output only with zero native lib deps; SQLite schema v1 with Alembic; pytest suite at 85% coverage gate; GitHub Actions CI with ssp-validate exit-code gate enforcing exit 1 on medium findings; pipx install from PyPI. Pilot with three state agencies to validate SSP template fidelity against agency ISSO review. Phase 2 GRC Integration (6 weeks): Full 800-53 Rev 5 coverage across all 20 control families; Archer adapter; control sync with progress bars and stale-cache warnings; config init interactive wizard; poa_items table and POA&M section in SSP output; performance test on 100-family sync. Phase 3 Hardening and Extras (4 weeks): ssp-forge[pdf] optional extra with libpango/libcairo prereqs documented; --output json for ssp-validate with full CI integration guide; OSCAL JSON export stub for future interchange; exit code table in CLI --help and README; pilot agency feedback incorporated into FindingsEngine rule set.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12+ with pyproject.toml packaging and hatch build backend. ruff for linting and import sorting enforced in CI with zero-warning policy. mypy --strict with no 'type: ignore' comments without inline justification. pytest with 85% line coverage gate; integration tests use pytest-httpx for GRC adapter mocking and pytest-recording for captured HTTP fixture refresh. API keys must never appear in any file; keyring integration tested with mock keyring backend in CI. Click command modules follow one-file-per-subcommand pattern with shared context object via @click.pass_context. Pydantic v2 strict models for all GRC API response validation; adapter failures raise AdapterSchemaError caught at command level and emit exit code 3. Changelog in CHANGELOG.md maintained via conventional commits; version in pyproject.toml only. ADRs in docs/adr/ for key decisions including OSCAL bundle vs. network fetch, SQLite per-profile vs. shared DB, and optional extras pattern.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NIST 800-53 Rev 6 release invalidating bundled OSCAL control catalog. Mitigation: catalog version pinned in package metadata; CLI emits warning on first daily invocation if NIST OSCAL GitHub releases API shows a newer revision. Tradeoff: SQLite per system profile versus a single shared database — per-profile isolation chosen so air-gapped systems carry their own portable state file without shared DB administration. Risk: ServiceNow GRC schema changes breaking ControlImplementation Pydantic model parsing. Mitigation: versioned Pydantic models with pytest-recording fixtures refreshed on each ServiceNow upgrade; adapter schema mismatch raises exit code 3 with remediation message pointing to adapter version docs. Risk: FindingsEngine false positives on not_applicable controls. Mitigation: officer can mark controls as not_applicable in SQLite with justification text; FindingsEngine skips not_applicable status in findings output, reducing false positive noise. Tradeoff: Jinja2 Word template approach versus full python-docx programmatic assembly — templates chosen to allow non-engineer security staff to adjust SSP section formatting without touching Python code.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected SQLite per system profile over a shared SQLAlchemy database to support air-gapped portability and eliminate multi-user concurrency concerns at workstation scope. Confirmed OSCAL JSON bundled as package data rather than fetched at runtime to guarantee offline operation for classified environments. Chose optional extras pattern for WeasyPrint to eliminate native lib requirement from base install — directly resolves ISS-003. Defined four-value exit code table (0/1/2/3) with precise semantics for CI consumption — directly resolves ISS-004. OSCAL export deferred to Phase 3 stub pending NIST OSCAL tooling stabilization in federal agency environments.

metadata.notes: Round 3 FISMA SSP automation CLI; all four issues resolved; PDF optional extras and CI exit code semantics close final gaps.
